jobs.title: Service Coordinator/Administrators
jobs.description: Come and join Electra today. Electra Lifts is an elevator company who have been in operation for over 45 years. We specialize in Lift Maintenance, Modernizations, Replacements and New Installations. We maintain lifts all across Sydney and have a well-structured team to support our continued growth and expansion with main office located in Mascot, NSW. About You Come and join Electra today. Electra Lifts is an elevator company who have been in operation for over 45 years. We specialize in Lift Maintenance, Modernizations, Replacements and New Installations. We maintain lifts all across Sydney and have a well-structured team to support our continued growth and expansion with main office located in Mascot, NSW. About You We are looking for a number of Service Coordinators. These exciting permanent roles will be based in our Mascot, NSW office, and report to the Electra Office Manager. You are customer-focused and responsible for representing Electra Lifts positively to existing and prospective customers. As a results-focused individual, you will continually seek to improve the provision of products/services to customers. Your role includes the ability to positively influence the customer primarily through strong customer service focus and delivery of premium service to enhance our position as a market leader. Key responsibilities. Weekly checking and preparation of timesheets for Service Manager approval. Processing of chargeable calls (Bill without Cost - BWOC) ensuring that invoice and charging descriptions and are very clear. This will include obtaining POs from some customers to enable invoicing Book T work jobs on the system upon order receipt and close T jobs on the system when the job is completed. This will include managing staged invoicing on larger T jobs. Prepare customer reports as required (daily/monthly/quarterly) and ad-hoc admin work as required by the customers and the Electra team. Upload and maintain customer portals ensuring that the information required to be uploaded is uploaded and the work orders within the portals are closed out efficiently. Work with other Administrators, Sales Reps, Project and Service Managers to ensure customer requirements are being met. Assist Sales Reps and Service/Project Managers with debt collection efforts. Prepare credits (ECN’s) within the system as authorized by management. Provides Annual Maintenance Statements and lift registrations as required for customers. Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes What you will need to be successful Higher School Certificate (HSC)or relevant tertiary education. Administration experience within the construction, engineering or elevator industry will be an added advantage. Have 3-5 years experience in project coordination, customer service or administrative positions. Strong verbal and written communication skills. Good knowledge and aptitude on Microsoft and other IT platforms. Team player, agile and adaptable Has good time management abilities and able to multi-task. Apply now and help us build what's next

jobs.title: Supply Chain and Procurement Lead
jobs.description: About the Role This is a critical role responsible for ensuring supply chain performance directly supports on-time installations. We operate with long international lead times (16+ weeks) and a fast-moving install pipeline (as short as 4 weeks). This role exists to bring structure, accuracy, and control to how we forecast, order, and deliver. Reporting to the State Manager (Victoria), you will own end-to-end supply chain operations across Next Level Elevators, Compact Lifts, and Next Level Compact Lifts. This role is suited to a high performer looking for their next challenge—someone who prefers ownership, accountability, and impact over managing a large team. What You’ll Be Doing Supply Chain Ownership Manage long-lead international supply (16+ weeks) against a fast-moving install pipeline Ensure product availability aligns with project schedules and operational demand Take full accountability for supply chain performance and outcomes Procurement & Supplier Management Manage ordering of lifts and parts from key international suppliers Negotiate pricing, lead times, and commercial outcomes Actively manage supplier performance to reduce delays and improve reliability International Logistics & Freight Plan and manage international logistics across sea and air freight Coordinate freight forwarding, import duties, and GST requirements Control container planning and reduce idle time between manufacture and dispatch Planning, Forecasting & Inventory Work closely with sales teams to translate pipeline into accurate demand forecasts Maintain optimal stock levels across all locations Monitor and manage lead times to ensure alignment with installs Systems, Process & Reporting Maintain accurate supply chain data within Simpro Drive process improvements across forecasting, ordering, and inventory Deliver detailed reporting on performance, risks, and opportunities Cross-Functional Coordination Work directly with national operations teams to align installs with supply constraints Act as the link between sales, projects, warehouse, and suppliers Proactively communicate risks, delays, and solutions What We’re Looking For You’ve worked in a role where supply delays directly impacted operations You are comfortable chasing suppliers, solving problems, and owning outcomes You prefer being accountable for results rather than managing a large team Strong experience in international logistics (sea/air freight, freight forwarding, imports) Proven ability to negotiate better commercial and operational outcomes High attention to detail with strong reporting and analytical capability Process-driven mindset with the ability to bring structure and control Experience in supply chain, procurement, or logistics within an SME environment ERP system experience (Simpro highly regarded) Why Join Us Take ownership of a business-critical function where your decisions directly impact installs, revenue, and customer outcomes Step into a true “next challenge” role – build structure, improve systems, and elevate supply chain performance Work in a growing, entrepreneurial business where speed, accountability, and results are valued over hierarchy High visibility and impact – work closely with leadership and influence how the business scales Autonomy without bureaucracy – you’ll be trusted to own problems, make decisions, and drive outcomes Perks that matter – $250 annual wellbeing allowance & $50 birthday voucher

jobs.title: Service Account Representative - Sunshine Coast, QLD
jobs.description: At Otis, it’s our people who make us different. Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights. About You We are currently looking for a sales-focused Service Account Representative to be based in our Sunshine Coast office in QLD In this role, you would be responsible for selling the OTIS lift and escalator range of Maintenance products and services and maintaining good customer relationships for all accounts assigned to your portfolio with the aim of growing the Service business in the respective area. You are excited about proactively maintaining regular face-to-face contact with Otis Customers and ensuring that all selling activities, including prospecting, presenting, proposing, tendering, securing, and processing, meet the OTIS standards and customer requirements. Permanent/Full-Time role Reports to Queensland Branch Manager As a Service Account Representative, your key responsibilities are to Achieve "O" and "T" Service/Maintenance sales targets in accordance with the individual plan and defined submission activities. Proactively maintain regular face-to-face contact with Otis Customers. Achieves in-warranty conversion (NIS) targets and unit growth objectives to expand the portfolio unit count and revenue base. Provide daily, professional, value-based account management to key and national accounts within the portfolio and product proposals. Organize, prepare, and deliver Customer presentations promoting the features and benefits of Otis’s products and services, resulting in direct sales with new and existing customers. Provide timely, accurate forecasts and reports to management and ensure the designated service portfolio has reduced exposure to out-of-tenure agreements. Acts as a facilitator between the customers and the Service Operations team and assists with developing and implementing solutions to customers' vertical transport needs. Support the wider business requirements by identifying, actively introducing, and collaborating for Modernisation sales success within the portfolio.​ What you will need to be successful Have a minimum of 4-5 years’ experience in Frontline Sales or Key Account Management. Those with experience in the Elevator and Escalator industry will be preferred. Hold a valid current driver's license. Have strong business acumen and is results oriented. Able to manage several accounts, be customer-focused, and anticipate client needs. Works well in a team. Has the ability to liaise and influence all levels of stakeholders within the organization and possesses strong relationship-building skills. Good knowledge of using technology, including mobile applications and computer-based reporting systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet, database management) to analyze data and trends. Strong verbal and written communication skills. What We Offer Competitive base salary and car allowance Service Sales incentive Access to funded study schemes such as our industry-leading Employee Scholarship Program A culture that encourages innovative ideas and appreciates our talent is the key to our success. Come and join OTIS today and help us Build What's Next. Apply now! About us We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio. We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move. Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion We “lift” our people to their highest potential. Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world. We are

jobs.title: Lift Electrician - Installs & Maintenance
jobs.description: Are you an experienced A-Grade Electrician with lift installation or maintenance experience under your belt? Hamilton Elevators (QLD) Pty Ltd is an Australian, family-owned Elevator & Car Lift Company.  We are a close nit friendly fun team. We are looking for an A-Grade Electrician (lift experience preferred yet not necessary) to join our team on a full-time basis to assist with our installation of a variety of lifts into building in and around Brisbane & Gold Coast. We are looking for someone for the long term to train and grow with the company. Full Training will be provided and a long term position for the right candidate. What will you do: Reading and interpreting blueprints to determine the layout of the systems components. Installing electrical componentry in the correct order including shaft, car and controller. Assembling, installing, testing and tuning elevators using hand and power tools, and testing devices such as test lamps, ammeters, and voltmeters. An understanding of lift code requirements Experience on PLC's Responding to breakdowns in a timely manner to minimise downtime Monitor and contribute to developing new maintenance schedules for equipment, plant and other relevant data to identify faults and take action as necessary Performing scheduled and ad-hoc safety checks as per instructions Investigating failures and making recommendations to reduce risk and improve systems / processes. Handover assistance and training of the installation to the end user We are looking for a candidate who has; A-grade Licence essential Become a Qualified Technical Person with ESO Excellent troubleshooting and root cause analysis skills; Strong communication skills both written and verbal; Solid knowledge and focus on quality; A “can do” attitude Drivers Licence Physically fit – manual handling Eagerness to learn and develop skills. Computer skills Can travel to sites in and around Brisbane & Gold Coast In return we will offer you; Full time employment Vehicle provided or allowance Job stability Full product and job training Specialised tools supplied Immediate start Applicants must either be an Australian citizen or hold a Permanent Residency Visa for Australia with no work restrictions. Only short-listed candidates only will be contacted.

jobs.title: Lift Service Technician - Home Lifts/Elevators
jobs.description: About us - We are fast growing ambitious Home Lift company with motivation to change the Home Lift sector for the better. Disrupting the status quo and driving change through cutting edge products and unmatched customer service. About the opportunity - We are looking for another Lift Service Technician to join our amazing team. Travelling to our customers' homes or businesses to perform equipment and machine repairs. The Service Technician's responsibilities include traveling to customers' locations, accessing equipment and performing routine maintenance and repairs. You should be proficient in fault finding problem solving. Service Technician Responsibilities: Carrying out scheduled service Installing and testing products, equipment, and machinery and providing instructions to customers on the proper usage of their product. Using diagnostic tools to identify issues. Fixing defective or broken components or ordering the necessary parts to replace faulty or worn-out parts. Servicing is mainly residential so must be able to present well in a customer's home Understanding of hydraulics. Advanced electrical knowledge and fault finding. Use of company iPad to integrate site status with the main system and assist in the free flow of information Managing a fully stocked company van incl allocation of parts to jobs Maintaining your technical and mechanical skills. Service Technician Requirements: Experience within in the lift industry An Electrical A-Grade (Desirable) or sound knowledge of Electrical and Electronic components Full driving license The ability to work independently. Managing time on service calls with patience and not rushing the job. Excellent customer service and communication skills. The ability to diagnose and solve problems based on customers' non-technical descriptions. Strong working knowledge of equipment and tools used in the industry Package on offer: Hourly rate will be depending upon experience though above industry standard with fully maintained vehicle or vehicle allowance. We would love to hear from you - Apply now via SEEK with your resume and cover letter.

jobs.title: Lift Technician / Electrician
jobs.description:
Company Overview Direct Lifts specialises in supply and install of modern, eye-catching lifts and elevators throughout the east coast of Australia. Working closely with architects, builders and property owners we collaborate
and design lifts to suit our clients’ requirements and decor. Role Overview Due to an expanding portfolio of projects, we are seeking dedicated, qualified professionals to join our team. Available roles include: Lift Installers/Technician – Responsible for installing and assembling lift systems, including both electrical and mechanical components. You will work on a range of projects, ensuring all installations meet safety and quality standards. Service Technician – Carry out regular maintenance, troubleshooting and repairs on lift systems to ensure reliable performance and safety. Qualified Electricians – Utilise your expertise to ensure safe and efficient electrical systems and lift installations. This position offers the opportunity to expand your skills in a dynamic environment
while working with the latest lift technology Role Requirements Mechanical or industry related field experience (preferred) Demonstrated commitment to safety policies and procedures Self-starter who can work efficiently both independently and within a team Excellent communication skills A current Australian driver's license Electrical Qualifications (essential for relevant roles) Benefits We ensure all employees feel included, supported and valued and offer a range of benefits, including: Attractive salary and incentives Access to comprehensive learning and development programs covering a range of practical and professional skills Mentorship and support from experienced teams Opportunities to grow and advance your career Application Process If any these roles sound like you please submit your application on the link or directly to hr@directlifts.com.au

Lift Technician / Electrician
jobs.description:
Lift Technician / Electrician  
Frontline Recruitment Group  
Marrickville, NSW  
Permanent  
Full-time  
6 hours ago  

About the Role  
An exciting opportunity is available for a skilled Lift Technician or qualified Electrician to join a well-established organisation within the lift and accessibility sector. This role involves the installation, servicing and repair of a diverse range of lift and access systems across residential and commercial environments.  

The position offers stable, full-time hours (7:30am - 4:15pm), one RDO per calendar month, optional overtime, and long-term career progression for the right candidate.  

Key Responsibilities  
Install, service and repair platform lifts, stair-lifts and accessibility equipment  
Diagnose and fault-find electrical and mechanical control systems  
Review and interpret technical plans, drawings and specifications  
Test, commission and check PLCs and motor control systems  

Apply Now

Provide professional, customer-focused service on site

Key Skills & Experience

Electrical trade qualification (essential)  
Current electrical licence (preferred)  
Experience in lift technology, PLCs and motor control systems (highly regarded)  
Strong fault-finding and diagnostic capability in control circuits  
Applicants from electrical or metal trades with strong mechanical aptitude are encouraged to apply  
Ability to work autonomously and as part of a collaborative team  
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills  
Commitment to safety, quality and customer satisfaction  
Full training provided for candidates transitioning into the lift sector  

Benefits

Competitive salary package based on experience  
Company vehicle included  
Overtime available with penalty rates  
Monthly RDO  
Secure, long-term role with career development opportunities  
Comprehensive training and ongoing support  

Apply Now

Our client is a long-standing Australian organisation and a recognised leader in the lift and accessibility sector. They specialise in platform lifts, stair-lifts, residential and commercial elevators, and wheelchair access solutions.

Renowned for their high-quality products and exceptional after-sales service, the business has built a strong reputation for reliability, safety and long-term client relationships. jobs.title: EWP Parts Assembler
jobs.description: Nifty-Lift is an industry leader in the design and manufacture of EWP’s (Elevating work platforms). Now part of the Altec Worldwide group of companies, we continue to pride ourselves on providing our customers with a premium-engineered and manufactured product; delivered to schedule and supported by professional after sales service and advice. ABOUT THE ROLE We are currently seeking a motivated and reliable Parts Assembler to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for assembling, fitting, and installing components used in the manufacture and maintenance of Elevated Work Platforms (EWPs). You will play a key role in ensuring all components are assembled accurately, safely, and in line with engineering specifications and company standards. RESPONSABILITIES OF THE ROLE WILL INCLUDE: ·       Assemble EWP components and sub-assemblies ·       Read and interpret technical drawings ·       Assist with repairs, rebuilds, and servicing ·       Perform basic fabrication/welding (as required) ·       Operate equipment and overhead cranes (if licensed/trained) TO BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS POSITION YOU WILL HAVE: ·       Strong mechanical aptitude ·       Ability to read and interpret technical drawings ·       Previous experience in a manufacturing or workshop environment ·       Forklift licence (preferred) ·       Hydraulic knowledge (advantageous) ·       Experience with heavy accessory/body components (highly regarded) WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT A clean workshop Laundered uniforms/uniform allowance after your probationary period Competitive remuneration (reviewed annually) Permanent employment with regular hours, and the possibility to work overtime as required. A staff referral program A workplace culture that values and recognises effort and contribution Regular team lunches Guaranteed time off over Christmas and New Years each year HOW TO APPLY To apply for this role, click 'apply' on this page or email your application to careers@nifty-lift.com.au Applications can also be handed to reception in person at 188 Colchester Rd, Bayswater North 3153. Questions in relation to this vacancy can be directed to Human Resources on (03) 9725 0077 . Please note: Successful candidates will be required to complete a pre-employment medical which includes a drug and alcohol screen.

jobs.title: Lifting Engineer
jobs.description: Lifting Engineer – Join Us in Lifting the Weight Off Your Shoulders. Safely. At Freo Group, our people make the difference. We are committed to working safely, supporting our communities, and delivering quality outcomes for our clients. Backed by Marmon Group and Berkshire Hathaway, we combine the strength of a global organisation with the agility of a locally focused business. We support major customers across mining, resources, infrastructure and energy, including BHP, BMA, Glencore, Rio Tinto, South32, Northern Star Resources, Newmont, Santos and Alcoa. With operations nationwide, Freo Group offers the opportunity to grow your career while contributing to some of Australia’s most significant projects. What we’re looking for: We are seeking a Lift Engineer with 2–3 years’ experience to join our Engineering team. This opportunity is suited to a driven and capable professional looking to further develop their technical expertise within a dynamic, operationally focused environment. In this role, you will partner closely with operational teams to support informed, practical decision-making, balancing safety, technical integrity, and commercial outcomes. You will contribute to the planning and execution of lifting activities, ensuring solutions are fit-for-purpose, compliant, and aligned with both project and client requirements. What you’ll bring to the team: You’re a technically capable and motivated engineer with a strong foundation and a practical, solutions-focused mindset. You bring a balance of engineering rigour and operational awareness, with the ability to contribute meaningfully in a fast-paced environment. Tertiary qualification in Mechanical or Civil Engineering with a strong foundation in engineering analysis and design Exposure to crane and rigging, or related mining, heavy civil, or mechanical environments, with an understanding of operational delivery Foundational experience in lift planning, engineering calculations, and practical problem-solving Strong risk awareness with a commitment to safe, compliant, and fit-for-purpose engineering outcomes Effective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage across operations and stakeholders Proactive and accountable approach, with the ability to manage priorities and deliver outcomes Adaptable mindset with a willingness to learn and develop in a dynamic environment What you’ll be doing: You’ll play a key role in supporting safe, efficient, and commercially sound lifting operations, working closely with both engineering and operations teams. This role combines technical delivery with practical application, ensuring solutions are fit-for-purpose and aligned to real-world execution. Contribute to lift studies, transport drawings, method statements, and crane comparisons aligned to project requirements Prepare and maintain engineering calculation spreadsheets to support lift planning and technical decision-making Develop and maintain dynamic 3D models for cranes and rigging to support design and visualisation Provide technical input into incident investigations, including analysis and practical recommendations Review and optimise lift designs and methodologies based on equipment, site conditions, and constraints Support crane selection and configuration, providing recommendations to inform capital decisions Collaborate with operations to ensure engineering solutions are practical and executable Drive continuous improvement through enhancements to engineering processes, tools, and standards Why You’ll Love Working with Us: At Freo, we believe in Doing Things That Matter, Leading at Every Level and Winning a Better Way. Here’s what that means for you: Support & Growth: Opportunities to develop your skills, progress your career within Freo Group, or grow across Marmon’s global network of 120+ companies. Meaningful Work: Your work contributes to supporting some of Australia’s largest mining, infrastructure and industrial projects. Great Team Culture: A su

jobs.title: Workshop Mechanic/Technician
jobs.company: All Lift Forklifts and Access Equipment
jobs.description: Workshop Mechanic / Technician All Lift Forklifts & Access Equipment – Rydalmere, NSW Join a Growing Industry Leader – National Distributor of Premium Equipment Brands About Us All Lift Forklifts & Access Equipment is a proudly family-owned and operated business, with operations across NSW and other eastern states. We specialise in the rental, sales, and service of forklifts, telehandlers, and access equipment. As the appointed national distributor for Magni Telescopic Handlers and Taylor Heavy Forklifts , the business has experienced significant growth. To support our continued expansion, we’re looking for a Qualified Workshop Mechanic/Technician to join our dedicated service team at our Rydalemre workshop. The Role You’ll play a key part in ensuring our fleet is maintained to the highest standard. This hands-on role includes: Diagnosing mechanical, electrical and hydraulic faults Servicing and maintaining a range of lifting and access equipment Pre-hire and off-hire inspections Supporting breakdown and repair works as needed You’ll work closely with a team of skilled technicians, delivering quality service across a wide range of industry-leading equipment. What We’re Looking For Mechanical Trade Qualification (Diesel, Forklift, Heavy Vehicle, Light Vehicle, or Mobile Plant) Solid understanding of diesel, electrical and hydraulic systems Current driver’s licence Forklift licence (desirable) Ability to work efficiently, with a strong sense of urgency and pride in workmanship About You You’re more than just mechanically skilled — you’re a hands-on problem solver who thrives in a team environment. With a solid mechanical background and a passion for getting things done right, you take pride in your work and always put the customer first. Self-motivated, dependable, and driven to contribute, you’re ready to be a key player in a supportive business that values your expertise. What We Offer Competitive wages, aligned with experience Stable, full-time employment with a growing national company A friendly, supportive team culture Ongoing development opportunities within a high-demand industry Ready to take the next step in your mechanical career? Apply now and be part of a team that values your skills and supports your growth.

jobs.title: Vertical Transportation Team Leader - Building Services
jobs.description:
Vertical Transportation Team Leader - Building Services  
CSA Recruitment  
Brisbane, QLD  
Permanent  
Full-time  
3 hours ago  

Vertical Transportation Team Leader – Building Services Brisbane, Queensland Competitive Salary Package + Benefits  

Lead the future of vertical transportation in Brisbane’s booming skyline.  
We are seeking an experienced Vertical Transportation Team Leader to head up our growing Vertical Transportation (VT) team in Brisbane.  

This is a senior leadership role for a passionate engineer who wants to deliver high-quality lift, escalator, and moving walkway solutions on some of South East Queensland’s most exciting commercial, residential, healthcare, and mixed-use developments.  

About the Role  

As Team Leader, you will drive the technical excellence and commercial success of the Vertical Transportation discipline. You’ll manage a team of engineers and designers while playing a hands-on role in major projects from concept design through to construction and handover.  

Key Responsibilities  

- Lead and mentor a team of Vertical Transportation Engineers and Designers  
- Oversee the design and specification of lifts, escalators, and moving walkways  
- Ensure compliance with relevant Australian Standards and international best practices

Support business development activities and contribute to winning new work  
Drive innovation in sustainable and smart vertical transportation solutions  
Collaborate closely with MEP, fire, structural, and architectural teams using BIM/Revit workflows  

What You’ll Bring  
- Bachelor of Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, or related discipline)  
- 8+ years’ experience in Vertical Transportation / Lift Engineering within the Australian building services industry  
- Proven leadership experience managing a team of engineers  
- Strong technical knowledge of lift traffic analysis, system design, and performance specifications  
- Experience with major projects (high-rise, hospitals, airports, or large-scale developments)  
- Proficiency in design software, including Revit MEP, AutoCAD, and specialist VT tools  
- Excellent communication, client management, and commercial acumen  
- CPEng or eligibility for chartered status is highly regarded  

Why Join Us?  
- Lead a growing Vertical Transportation team in one of Australia’s fastest-growing cities  
- Work on landmark Brisbane and South East Queensland projects that shape the city’s skyline  
- Genuine autonomy and opportunity to build and grow the discipline  
- Attractive remuneration package including performance bonus, professional development, and flexible/hybrid working  
- Supportive, collaborative culture with a strong focus on work-life balance  

If you’re a technical leader with a passion for vertical transportation and ready to take the next step in your career, this is your opportunity to make a significant impact in Brisbane’s building services sector.  

Why CSA Recruitment?  
Craig Andresen has been a professional Recruitment Consultant since 2006 and has been deeply embedded in the Australian Building, Civil & Structural Engineering industries since 2012, so he's exceptionally well-positioned to assist you with your next career move.  

If you would like to contact Craig Andresen regarding this role or to submit your skills & experience, please feel free to do so by following the details below:  
Apply Now

This senior role requires an experienced engineer with strong leadership skills, technical expertise, and a commitment to innovation and compliance in the vertical transportation field.

jobs.title: NSW Construction Manager
jobs.description: Established in 1996 following the acquisition of City Elevator, TK Elevator Australia, building on the over 200 years of global engineering excellence of our German founding companies Thyssen and Krupp, install, maintain and modernize elevators and escalators in Australia. We have a strong presence in various segments, such as office, education and healthcare sectors, and are committed to making cities the best places to live by being safety and environmentally focused while fostering a strong safety culture. We are currently seeking a highly motivated and well‑organised Construction Manager to join our NSW Branch. This role reports directly to the Branch Manager and is responsible for overseeing all Lift Modernisation and Construction sites within the region. The successful candidate will have extensive experience in the Construction industry, with demonstrated capability in project management, scheduling, and financial reporting. Prior experience within the Construction sector is essential. You will also need strong communication and computer skills and maintain a professional presentation at all times. We offer excellent working conditions and a negotiable salary package reflective of the successful candidate’s experience. The key responsibilities of the successful applicant will include but not be limited to: Labour control, project financial control & forecasting Direct site management and site meeting attendance Programs & planning using Microsoft project and excel Contract administration Providing all site & company documentation Estimating / calculation of project variations Maintain an excellent team culture Conduct toolbox talks in conjunction with the field supervisor Conduct site safety leadership audits The successful candidate will also need to possess the following skills and attributes: Minimum 5 years proven experience in Project Management Ability to read, plan and execute “scope of works” in accordance with specifications and contractual obligations Ability to read and understand engineering drawings Microsoft Office proficient in Word, Excel and Project Well presented Excellent attention to detail and accuracy Strong communication skills Ability to build a strong rapport with clients and colleagues alike SAP knowledge and experience will be highly desirable What we offer you At TK Elevator you will be able to extend your horizon – thanks to flexible working time models, international teams and a wide range of individual training courses.  A collaborative approach and treating each other with respect have been central to what we do for more than 200 years. If that's as important to you as it is to us, then apply now! All suitable applicants should apply in writing to the HR department. To be eligible, you must submit a cover letter outlining your skills and experience in line with the role and a current CV. Please Note: Only successful candidates will be contacted We value diversity and therefore welcome all applications, irrespective of gender, nationality, ethnic and social background, religion and beliefs, disability, age, or sexual orientation and identity. TKE Australia is an ISO certified Company.

jobs.title: Senior Project Manager (Lifts / Elevators)
jobs.description: We are seeking a dedicated, highly organised Project Manager to oversee lift projects and ensure smooth operations from completion of sale to handover. This senior role requires interaction with multiple internal teams, engagement with external stakeholders, and managing several moving project parts simultaneously. Key Responsibilities: Project Coordination: Schedule and manage lift projects, ensuring seamless coordination of works and materials with the scheduling team. Team Management: Lead installation teams, ensuring optimum efficiency while maintaining high morale and productivity. Procurement & Logistics: Work closely with the procurement team to ensure products are available on time and in full. Deadline Management: Oversee the progress of projects, adapting work plans as needed to meet deadlines and ensure quality delivery. Client & Stakeholder Communication: Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and internal teams (Installation, Sales, Procurement, Accounts & Senior Management) Quality Control: Review and sign off on building works before installation, maintaining a high level of quality control throughout the project lifecycle. Documentation & Reporting: Oversee all incoming and outgoing project documentation, and prepare progress reports for senior management. Process Improvement: Continuously optimize processes and approaches to enhance efficiency and quality. Safety & Compliance: Ensure installation teams adhere to company OHS policies and maintain a safe working environment. Growth & Development: Identify opportunities for growth, new projects, and initiate actions to expand business opportunities. Required Personal Qualities: Professional "can-do" attitude with excellent communication skills. Ability to manage high-stress situations while maintaining clear, calm leadership. Strong multitasking skills with the capacity to handle multiple project components simultaneously. Leadership ability with a big-picture vision and strong attention to detail. Conflict resolution skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving. Experience 3 years plus experience within in the Lift / Elevator industry Managing multiple projects, ideally in construction or engineering or residential build projects Proven leadership in budgets, and project timelines. Experience with installation projects and construction-related work and understanding of building processes and safety protocols. High level of familiarity with project management or CRM software for scheduling and reporting. Experience in managing project budgets and working with procurement teams to ensure timely material availability. Ability to identify risks, adapt project plans, and solve issues to keep projects on track. Skilled in managing client relationships, communicating effectively, and ensuring project satisfaction. Experience enforcing safety protocols and ensuring OHS compliance on-site. Strong communication, conflict resolution, and leadership skills to maintain team morale and resolve issues. What We Offer: $130K base + Super + Car $5K bonus per quarter based on successful install KPI's achieved ($20K per annum) Opportunity to work in a senior, dynamic role within a fast-growing company. Exposure to a wide range of projects and the chance to lead teams to success. A collaborative, positive work environment focused on professional growth.

jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description: About the Role Australian Lifting Centre Pty Ltd is seeking a driven and highly organized Project Coordinator to join our team. This is a hybrid role requiring strong project management skills, day-to-day task coordination, and people management, especially across teams involved in manufacturing, lifting equipment, or construction environments. The ideal candidate will also bring hands-on experience or solid understanding of CAD/CAM, technical drafting, or similar engineering workflows to help bridge the gap between technical design and project execution. Key Responsibilities • Manage and coordinate end-to-end project delivery, including timelines, resources, and client communications • Assign daily tasks and priorities to workshop and site teams • Oversee and manage team productivity and performance, fostering accountability and collaboration • Liaise between design/drafting teams and operations to ensure clear understanding of technical drawings and deliverables • Track project milestones, risks, and variations using project management software • Prepare and maintain documentation including work schedules, risk assessments, compliance reports, and progress updates • Support procurement and inventory planning for ongoing projects • Ensure all operations meet safety, compliance, and quality standards, particularly in lifting or construction-related work • Review technical drawings and provide feedback or clarification where needed • Contribute to process improvement and efficiency initiatives across teams Skills & Qualifications • Proven experience in project management, operations coordination, or team leadership • Relevant degree in Engineering or Project Management • Background or strong working knowledge in CAD/CAM, AutoCAD or similar drafting software • Experience in manufacturing, lifting gear, mechanical workshop, or construction environments is highly regarded • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills • Ability to manage multiple tasks under pressure and keep stakeholders aligned • Strong organizational and time management abilities • Proficiency in MS Office and project tracking tools Desirable Attributes •Knowledge of lifting and rigging product used at construction site. •Familiar with WHS and Australian safety standards. •Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate problems into well-engineered solutions. What We Offer • The opportunity to shape the development of advanced mechanical solutions in a specialist equipment environment. • Strong involvement in end-to-end projects from early design through to validation and implementation. • A supportive and technically focused environment where innovation and are valued. • Competitive salary and long-term career prospects within a growing organisation.

jobs.title: Heavy Lift Engineer or Designer (Early Career)
jobs.description: About John Holland At John Holland, our purpose is simple - we transform lives with everything we do. We've always known that infrastructure is about people — our customers, our employees, and the communities in which we work every day. That's our difference. Deep experience and capability with a genuine care about creating better lives for people along the way. Be part of the team that's up for the challenge of transforming lives for good. Expression of Interest As a result of continued growth and a strong pipeline of projects across New South Wales, we are seeking Expressions of Interest from candidates with limited experience for an upcoming Heavy Lift role as part of our Sydney based Lift & Shift team . This opportunity is suited to candidates at the early stages of their career who are seeking to develop technical capability in heavy lift design, lift planning and operational construction support within a structured and well supported environment. Working at John Holland You will have the opportunity to work alongside experienced Lift & Shift Engineers, Designers and Specialists, supporting the planning and the delivery of lifting operations on some of Australia's largest and most complex construction projects. John Holland's Lift & Shift team provides specialist engineering and operational support across cranes, rigging, heavy haulage and specialised lifting equipment, from tender development through to project delivery. In this role, you will assist with the preparation of lift studies and associated documentation, participate in design reviews, and support construction teams by contributing to safe, compliant and cost-effective lifting solutions. Over time, you will progressively develop your technical skills, industry knowledge and confidence under the guidance of senior team members. A structured development pathway will be provided, including training and mentoring About You We are looking for candidates who demonstrate a strong interest in lifting operations and construction engineering, and who are motivated to build a career within the heavy lift and lift planning discipline. You will typically be: A recent graduate or near completion student in Mechanical, Civil or Structural Engineering, or A candidate with early career construction or lifting exposure seeking to transition into a design focused role You will also demonstrate: A developing understanding of lifting operations, cranes, rigging and associated equipment Willingness to learn and apply relevant Australian Standards, legislation, Codes of Practice and industry guidelines Basic drafting skills and commitment to further developing knowledge of AutoCAD and crane specific software for lift planning Ability to read and interpret design documentation and follow established procedures and instructions Strong attention to detail, a safety focused and risk management mindset, and a proactive approach to learning Good communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively within multidisciplinary project teams As part of the team, you help us deliver on our promise to transform lives. Your success is reflected in ours, so we are committed to being an employer of choice. We pride ourselves on having a diverse and inclusive workplace, as different perspectives and ideas will deliver our long-term success. What's in it for you We're about connecting your sense of purpose to ours. We understand that your career is one of the biggest ways to have an impact on the world. We also believe in supporting you as a whole person, not just an employee. We reward and support our people in so many ways. This starts with being flexible about how different people like to work. jobs.title: Full time Lift Installer / Mechanical Fitter
jobs.description: Lift Installer/Mechanical Fitter About us Goldenhome Lifts is a fast-growing, premium elevator supply and installation company operating across Victoria and New South Wales. We specialize in delivering high-quality, customized lift solutions for residential and commercial projects, including luxury homes, townhouses, apartments, warehouses, and shopping centres. Rigger certification (required) Valid driver's license and access to a reliable personal vehicle (required). Construction White Card (required). Prior success in managing projects within the lift/elevator industry (highly desirable). Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English. jobs.title: Lift Installer (Training Provided)
jobs.description: The Company Going Up is one of Australia's largest independent, Australian-owned elevator companies — proudly manufacturing, installing, servicing, and modernising lifts along the east coast. With a strong pipeline of projects and a great team culture, we're expanding our Sydney installation team and looking for Lift Installers — including qualified Electricians, Mechanics, or Fitters who want to move into a specialised, long-term career in the elevator and lift industry . Whether you’re an experienced tradesperson or ready to transition into lift installation, Going Up provides a supportive team, structured training, and a strong Sydney project pipeline. You'll work alongside experienced technicians, developing skills in one of Australia's most stable and rewarding trades. Key Responsibilities Install and fit out lift systems on a variety of projects Learn from experienced supervisors and colleagues Support testing, commissioning, and site handovers Maintain safety and quality standards About You Trade qualified or experienced in electrical, mechanical or construction work. Mechanically or electrically minded, with a strong interest in technical hands-on work. Reliable, organised and a strong team player. Commitment to safety and professional workmanship. Willingness to learn and develop new skills Lift or elevator experience is great — but not essential ; full training is provided. What We Offer Competitive pay with opportunities for overtime. Comprehensive on-the-job training with experienced installers. Support to build a recognised career in the vertical transport / elevator industry . Friendly, supportive and professional team environment. Uniform provided. Immediate start and a stable, full-time permanent role. Ready to elevate your career? If you're an Electrician, Mechanic, or trade-qualified professional looking for a new challenge and long-term stability, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now via Seek. No recruiters please.

jobs.description: Electrical Elevator Service Technician Going Up Elevators Pty Ltd Sydney NSW Full-time Salary: $80,000 – $90,000 per year Going Up Elevators Pty Ltd is an Australian-owned independent elevator company specialising in the design, manufacture, installation, servicing, and modernisation of lift systems across the east coast of Australia. jobs.title: Lift Engineer / Vertical Transportation (VT) Engineer - Building Services
jobs.description: Our client is a well-established Tier 2 multidisciplinary engineering consultancy with a busy and growing Vertical Transportation (VT) team in Brisbane. If you are currently working as a Lift Engineer, Elevator Engineer or Vertical Transportation Engineer, this role offers the opportunity to work on major building projects across Queensland, including hospitals, large-scale infrastructure and Olympic-related developments. Why This Role • Join a busy and established VT team with a strong project pipeline • Work on major hospitals, Olympic-related infrastructure and landmark developments • Be part of a growing multidisciplinary engineering consultancy • Offices across Brisbane and the Gold Coast, with Sunshine Coast expansion planned • Strong salary package and long-term career opportunity This is a fantastic opportunity to join a highly regarded consultancy delivering high-quality building projects across Southeast Queensland. The Role Working as part of a busy local VT team, you will be involved in: • Lift and escalator traffic analysis and concept design • Preparing VT design briefs and specifications • Coordinating with architects, developers and project teams • Supporting projects from early concept through tender and delivery • Documentation and layouts using AutoCAD and BIM tools Projects include major hospitals, large-scale infrastructure, commercial towers, residential developments and Olympic-related work. The Ideal Candidate This role would suit someone with vertical transportation or lift engineering experience, either from a consulting environment or from the lift manufacturing / OEM side of the industry. You may have experience in: • Vertical transportation consulting within a building services firm • Lift or escalator engineering with manufacturers such as KONE, Otis, Schindler or TK Elevator • Knowledge of NCC, AS1735 and lift design standards • Working with architects, developers and multidisciplinary engineering teams • AutoCAD experience (Revit/BIM exposure beneficial) This is an excellent opportunity for a Lift Engineer / VT Engineer looking to work on landmark projects in a collaborative Tier 2 consultancy environment. This opportunity may also suit Mechanical Engineers, Building Services Engineers or Lift Design Engineers who have experience working with vertical transportation systems including lifts, elevators or escalators. Search Keywords: Lift Engineer, Elevator Engineer, Vertical Transportation Engineer, Escalator Engineer and Vertical Transport opportunities within building services consulting. Experience with lift design, lift traffic analysis, AS1735 standards, NCC / BCA compliance, AutoCAD and BIM or Revit modelling within vertical transport engineering or lift manufacturer environments such as KONE, Otis, Schindler or TK Elevator will be highly regarded. jobs.title: Inspector - Crane / Lifting and rigging equipment
jobs.description: Lifting Equipment Inspector Lucon Industries | Full-Time | Williamstown / Port Melbourne About Us Lucon Industries is a leading specialist in industrial maintenance and repair, providing services to major ports, Quarries and industrial facilities across Australia. We pride ourselves on delivering high-quality solutions for our clients, with a focus on safety, efficiency, and reliability. We’re expanding and looking for an experienced Lifting Equipment Inspector to join our expert team. About the Role As a Lifting Equipment Inspector at Lucon Industries, you’ll be responsible for inspecting and testing a variety of lifting equipment, ensuring compliance with Australian safety standards. This is an exciting opportunity to work across different industrial environments and collaborate with a highly skilled team. Key Responsibilities: Perform inspections and functional tests on lifting equipment (chains, slings, shackles, hooks, cranes, hoists). Inspect flexible steel wire rope systems on various types of cranes and equipment Utilize start of the art NTD tooling and reporting software Ensure all lifting gear meets safety standards and regulatory compliance. Log, track and share inspection reports. Analyze data and trends to identify potential issues and ensure continuous safety improvement. Coordinate with maintenance teams to schedule regular inspections. Prepare and submit detailed inspection reports with recommendations for repairs or replacements. Maintain accurate documentation and equipment certification records. Skills & Experience: Previous experience in lifting equipment inspections is essential. Dogman/ intermediate rigging tickets Proficiency in using computer systems for managing records, inspections, and data analysis. Strong report writing skills with attention to detail. Knowledge of relevant safety standards and industry regulations. What We Offer: Competitive salary package. Overtime and weekend work available Opportunities to travel interstate and internationally Ongoing training and development opportunities. Supportive team environment with a focus on safety and quality. Exposure to a wide range of high-profile industrial projects. How to Apply: Ready to join a dynamic team at Lucon Industries? If you meet the above criteria, apply now with your resume and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience.

jobs.title: Offshore Wind Install/Service - Heavy Lift Supervision
jobs.description: About Us Cosmic Group Services Pty Ltd is an experienced provider of wind turbine installation and services across all Asia Pacific, with the resources and expertise to deliver efficient and cost-effective mechanical and electrical solutions. Our goal at Cosmic is to provide project based Heavy Lift support in a safe and harm-free manner, working closely with global leading turbine manufacturing companies to deliver high-quality, cost-effective, and efficient services. We offer a range of exceptional career opportunities and are proud to be a company invested in its people — encouraging individual development, focusing on employee engagement, and creating a learning environment. We always aim for the Golden Tower… The Opportunity Due to continuous growth and upcoming projects across APAC, we are opening an Expressions of Interest campaign for experienced Heavy Lift Supervisors to join our team. If you're a Heavy Lift Supervisor with wind industry experience, we want to hear from you. Extensive heavy lift supervision experience within wind turbine environments Proven experience supporting Main Component Exchange (MCE) operations Strong crane coordination and rigging oversight capability Experience supervising large nacelle component lifts (generator, gearbox, hub or blades) Strong understanding of lift planning, load management and exclusion zones Ability to lead multi-disciplinary teams during critical lifts Excellent communication and safety leadership skills Experience working under OEM procedures and engineered lift plans A bit about you You will be a key member of our Offshore Install and Service team, supporting turbine installation, inspection, and servicing on a variety of client turbines. You’re a safety-first technician with a track record of delivering quality work at height. You must hold: Valid GWO Basic Safety Training (BST) – in date Proven heavy lift supervision experience Recognised Lifting Supervisor or Appointed Person certification Working at Heights certification LOTO competency Rigging or lifting qualification (preferred) Medical fitness for turbine climbing Valid passport / working rights for deployment region As part of the recruitment process, you may be required to undertake pre-employment checks including police clearance, medical assessment and/or drug and alcohol testing. We are an equal opportunity employer that supports your career, growth, and development — and we can’t wait to welcome the next group of high performers into the fold.

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician
jobs.description: The Company Going Up is one of the largest independent Australian-owned and operated elevator companies, manufacturing, installing, servicing, and modernising lifts along the east coast of Australia. We have a great community of team members and are looking for skilled Elevator Service Technicians to join our team. As a Service Technician, you'll have the opportunity to apply your technical expertise in a friendly and supportive working environment. Sites are located across Sydney , with our office based in Brookvale NSW. The Role Conduct routine maintenance, servicing, and repairs on elevators to ensure optimal performance and safety. Diagnose faults and carry out repairs efficiently. Install and modernise lift equipment as required. Coordinate with the service team and management for job scheduling and reporting. Maintain compliance with all relevant safety and industry regulations. Ensure high-quality standards in all work performed. Full-time, permanent role. About You Qualified or experienced in the elevator or mechanical trades industry (Cert III in Electrotechnology or equivalent preferred). Strong problem-solving skills with a mechanical and electrical aptitude. Previous experience working with lift systems is highly regarded. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Organised and self-motivated. High standard of workmanship and attention to detail. Commitment to workplace safety and compliance. Benefits Competitive wage. Company vehicle and phone. Ongoing training and development opportunities. Full uniform provided excluding boots. Work within a supportive and dynamic team environment. Immediate start available. If you are ready to take the next step in your career in the elevator industry, this is the opportunity for you! Apply now for an immediate start and become a valued member of the Going Up team. Apply via Seek. No recruiters. Please note this is a full-time, permanent role.

jobs.title: Lift Installer - Bonuses on Offer!!
jobs.description: Are you ready to elevate your career in Melbourne? Join a nationally recognised home elevator company that sets the industry standard for excellence. We're proud to feature market-leading electric traction lifts, working exclusively with a top-tier supplier, ensuring top-quality products and unmatched installation support and training. Position Overview: We are searching for Lead Elevator Installers who are not just looking for a job but an opportunity to thrive. What sets us apart is our industry-leading bonus structure, paid quarterly, providing the potential to boost your annual earnings by up to 20%. If you back yourself to be the best in the industry, we offer the support and incentives to help you shine. Key Highlights: Market-leading electric traction lifts. Exclusive partnership with a single supplier, ensuring top-quality products. Exceptional installation support and training. Industry-leading bonus structure, paid quarterly, with the potential to increase your annual earnings by up to 20%. An unmatched installation incentive, setting new standards in the industry. Requirements: Proven experience as an Elevator Installer. Commitment to excellence and a drive to be the best. Strong work ethic and attention to detail. A passion for innovation and quality. How to Apply: Ready to take your career to new heights? If you're motivated by our industry-leading bonus structure and want to excel in the elevator industry, click apply now below. Join us in revolutionising the elevator industry and enjoy the financial rewards that come with it. Elevate your career with us today!

jobs.title: Service Technician/Mechanical Fitter
jobs.description: Come and join Otis today, and be part of the OTIS Family and Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers About you We are seeking a Service Technician/Mechanical Fitters based in the Brisbane, Queensland. In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators. Permanent role Brisbane portfolio Reports to the Service Manager Key Responsibilities for You Performs Service, Maintenance and Fitter work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals. Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging. Stand by and provide after-call-back coverage as needed. Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to meet customer satisfaction. Support apprentices and subcontractors with Electrical and Mechanical training Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and /or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement. Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications. Key working relationships - Internally and Externally Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers. Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters. Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public. Sales Team: Sales Account Representatives, Managers, and Coordinators (where relevant). External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers. What You Will Need To Be Successful Have an Electrical license or a Cert III in Electrotechnology Hold a valid driver's license. Minimum five (5) years’ experience within the Lift Industry. Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training. Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training and demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills. Good English language skills, both verbal and written Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet). A committed professional, with safety as priority Detailed oriented and is results-driven, Curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team Apply today to join us and build what’s next!. About us We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio. We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings a

jobs.title: Electrician (Elevator/Lift Installer)
jobs.description: Are you an Electrician that gets things done, likes a short work week, and would like to work in a niche industry? If so, keep reading! We are a small, high-performance team based in Brisbane, Queensland , specializing as the preferred subcontractors for several elevator companies. Our reputation for excellence means we are in high demand across the Greater Brisbane area, the Gold Coast, Sunshine Coast, Toowoomba, and beyond. What We Are Looking For: We need someone who takes pride in their work. You’ll need: Licensing: A current, unrestricted Electrical Licence. Safety: A valid Construction Induction (White) Card. Technical Literacy: The ability to accurately read and interpret layout drawings and electrical diagrams. Aptitude: Exceptional electrical skills paired with strong mechanical "know-how." Standards: A commitment to neat, tidy, and compliant workmanship. Bonus Points (Advantageous Licences): While not mandatory, we value candidates who bring extra skills to the table: Dogman’s or Basic Rigger’s Licence. Material and Personnel Hoist (HP) or Basic Scaffolding Licence. Forklift Licence. Blue Card: Working with Children Check (Required, or must be willing to obtain). Industry Experience. What You’ll Get: We believe in rewarding high-quality work with high-quality benefits. Joining our team means: Above Award Pay: Compensation that reflects your specialized skills. 4-Day Work Week: Reclaim your long weekends and enjoy a genuine work-life balance. A "Test Drive" Period: Start in a casual position so you can "try us out" first. Enjoy the flexibility of seeing if the industry is the right fit for you before committing long-term. Niche Industry Career Path: Expert experience in a specialized field with massive growth potential. Full Training: We provide all the specialized industry training required for the role. If you're an Electrician ready to move into the elevator industry (or already in the industry) and enjoy more time off, we want to hear from you. If you're happy with all the above, please apply (also email your resume/CV with a brief cover letter to admin@capstoneelectrical.com.au )

jobs.title: Crane & Heavy Lift Engineer
jobs.description: Cranecorp Australia is seeking an experienced Senior Heavy Lift Engineer to lead our engineering function and play a critical role in delivering safe, innovative, and commercially sound heavy lift solutions across Australia. This is a key position within the business, ensuring exceptional customer service and best‑in‑class safety outcomes that consistently exceed client expectations. About the Role Reporting to senior leadership, you will work closely with Operations, Business Development, and project stakeholders to deliver complex heavy lift and transport solutions. You will also contribute to business growth, technology advancement, and the development of industry‑leading engineering standards and tools. Key Responsibilities Collaborate with project managers, engineers, and clients to develop compliant heavy lift plans in accordance with relevant Australian Standards Communicate strategy, planning, outcomes, and issues clearly to senior management and the wider business Build and maintain strong working relationships across the business as a key member of the Technical team Evaluate and implement new and emerging engineering technologies related to cranes, rigging, load monitoring, and heavy lift operation Develop, coach, and support team members to enhance capability and succession planning Develop tools, systems, and training to ensure compliance with world’s best practice and to set new industry benchmarks Provide engineering support outside normal business hours as required Ensure compliance with the latest Australian Standards and Codes of Practice nationwide Support Business Development with technical input for tenders, proposals, and client presentations Assist with engineering design including lifting devices, load support beams, stools, transport equipment, load restraint diagrams, lifting studies, and alternative lift and transport methodologies About You You are a technically strong and commercially aware engineer with a passion for safety, leadership, and continuous improvement. You thrive in a dynamic environment and are confident working with clients, stakeholders, and multidisciplinary teams. Essential Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical or Structural Engineering Minimum 3 years’ experience working as an engineer (heavy lift or related industry preferred) Strong background in construction, cranes, lifting, and transportation Proficiency in AutoCAD or similar engineering design software C‑Class Driver’s Licence National Police Clearance Ability to pass a pre‑employment medical and Drug & Alcohol screening Why Join Cranecorp? Lead complex, high‑profile heavy lift projects across Australia Work with a respected and growing industry leader Opportunity to influence engineering standards and innovation Supportive leadership team with a strong focus on safety and excellence If you are ready to take the next step in your engineering leadership career and make a real impact in the heavy lift industry, we would love to hear from

jobs.title: Lifting Equipment Testing & Production Supervisor
jobs.description: Specialised Lifting Service (SLS Cranes) is a proudly Western Australian-owned company specialising in the supply, inspection, testing, and servicing of lifting and rigging equipment. With over 40 years of industry leadership, we are recognised as a trusted name in overhead crane manufacturing, repairs, and lifting solutions. We are seeking an experienced and motivated Lifting Equipment Testing & Production Supervisor to join our Lifting & Rigging Equipment Department. This is a hands-on leadership role combining inspection, load testing, workshop coordination, and customer support. Key Responsibilities Assist with inspection, testing, and load testing of lifting and rigging equipment, including height safety systems Supervise and coordinate workshop production activities Ensure compliance with relevant Australian Standards Manage inspection documentation and certification records Liaise with customers regarding servicing and equipment requirements Receive, check, store, and dispatch goods Operate plant, machinery, and vehicles safely Skills & Experience Lifting and rigging industry experience Strong understanding of load testing and inspection procedures Current C-Class Driver’s Licence (essential) Forklift Licence (desirable) Strong communication and organisational skills Computer literacy for reporting and record management Ability to lead workflow while remaining hands-on This is a fantastic opportunity to join a family-owned business that values its team and offers ongoing training and development. If you are passionate about lifting equipment and enjoy a role that combines technical and administrative responsibilities, we want to hear from you! To apply, please submit your resume via SEEK or email it to sls@slscranes.com.au

jobs.title: Lifting Equipment Inspector
jobs.description: Lifting Equipment Inspection Coordinator Sydney $65,000 – $85,000 + Super + Company Vehicle We are currently seeking a Lifting Equipment Inspection Coordinator to join a growing team working across multiple industrial and construction sites throughout Sydney. This is a hands-on, field-based role where you will travel to construction projects, tunnels, factories, and industrial sites to inspect lifting equipment and ensure compliance with safety standards. This role is ideal for a Rigger or Dogman looking to step off the tools into a long-term, stable position within the industry. Key Responsibilities Travel to client sites across Sydney to conduct inspections Inspect lifting equipment including slings, chains, lifting gear, and towing equipment Identify damaged, worn, or non-compliant equipment Tag and record inspection results in line with safety and compliance standards Complete clear and accurate inspection reports Liaise with site teams during inspections Requirements Rigger or Dogman ticket (essential) Experience working with lifting equipment or in heavy industry environments Strong safety awareness and attention to detail Ability to work independently across multiple sites Current driver’s licence What’s on Offer $65,000 – $85,000 + superannuation Company vehicle provided Full training provided Long-term, stable role with career development opportunities Exposure to a variety of industrial and construction projects

We are seeking an experienced
Escalator and Elevator Technician
to join our
Melbourne CBD.

. This role is predominantly focused on maintaining Escalator products, however, experience maintaining lifts is also beneficial.

Highly competitive base
- EBA

regular maintenance
Answer

and design lifts to suit our clients’ requirements and decor. jobs.title: Lift Fitter
jobs.description: Lift Fitter (Year-Round) Resort: Falls Creek Employment Type: Permanent, Full Time Remuneration: $47.00 – $56.88 per hour (dependent on experience & qualifications) Agreement: Falls Creek and Hotham Trades Maintenance Agreement 2025 Applications Close: 22nd March 2026 Falls Creek is seeking experienced, trade-qualified Lift Fitters to join our Lift Maintenance team on a full-time permanent contract . This position is ideal for mechanically minded trades professionals who enjoy hands-on work, problem-solving, and maintaining complex lift systems in a dynamic alpine environment. The Opportunity As a Lift Fitter, you will support the maintenance and safe operation of Falls Creek’s extensive lift network, including fixed grip and detachable chairlifts, surface lifts, conveyors, and associated mechanical systems. Working closely with the Lift Maintenance Manager, Supervisors and broader maintenance team, you will perform scheduled servicing, fault diagnostics, mechanical repairs, component replacements, and system testing to ensure lift reliability and compliance with regulatory standards. This role requires a high level of mechanical aptitude, adherence to safe work practices, and the ability to work efficiently in variable weather conditions, including during peak winter operations . Lift Fitters are employed under the Falls Creek and Hotham Trades Maintenance Agreement 2025 with a base hourly rate of $47.00 – $56.88 per hour (dependent on experience & qualifications) What You’ll Be Doing Perform maintenance, repair, and installation of ski lift and ropeway equipment Conduct daily, weekly, and monthly inspections to ensure safe and reliable lift operations Diagnose and troubleshoot mechanical, hydraulic, and extra-low-voltage control issues Support tower maintenance, sheave train alignment, and tensioning systems Carry out scheduled preventative maintenance and maintain accurate service records Respond promptly to lift breakdowns and operational issues during resort hours Operate heavy equipment and hand tools safely and efficiently Assist with lift construction, upgrades, and decommissioning projects as required Support snow clearing and access preparation for lift maintenance areas Assist vehicle maintenance staff as required The Expertise You Bring A recognised Mechanical Trade qualification (Fitter & Turner, Mechanical Fitter, or equivalent) Minimum 2 years’ post-apprenticeship experience in mechanical maintenance or fitting roles, including working at heights and following safe work procedures Strong mechanical diagnostics skills and ability to identify and resolve faults Current Driver’s Licence Ability to work effectively in alpine and winter conditions. Highly Desirable: Hydraulic qualification High-risk work licences (dogging, rigging, EWP, forklift) First Aid qualification Skiing or snowboarding ability Why Join Falls Creek Work with unique alpine lift systems and machinery offering real technical variety Grow your career in a permanent, f

jobs.title: Installation Manager

Previous experience with scheduling. Demonstrated knowledge of Construction Industry Standards and Safe Work Practices. Strong analytical, communication and problem solving skills. Previous experience with emails and competent using company systems. Highly motivated and positive attitude towards all tasks. Excellent attention to detail. Previous lift installation, service or testing experience would be preferred and an advantage. Experience in a similar role or industry will also be beneficial and considered. We will teach you the necessary skills if you have the right attitude! HOW TO APPLY We're looking for an energetic, inventive and smart individual who knows how to make an impression. To be considered for this position we want to see how your skills and talents are linked to the opportunity. On offer is a great package and opportunity. Join a company who pride themselves on exceeding client expectations with their exceptional people, products and services. Don't miss out on this great opportunity which includes long term secure full-time employment within a very successful team! All applications must include a cover letter & CV outlining your suitability for the role. What are you waiting for! Click on the QUICK APPLY button now!

jobs.title: Lift Service Technician
jobs.description: Established in 1996 following the acquisition of City Elevator, TK Elevator Australia, building on the over 200 years of global engineering excellence of our German founding companies Thyssen and Krupp, install, maintain and modernize elevators and escalators in Australia. We have a strong presence in various segments, such as office, education and healthcare sectors, and are committed to making cities the best places to live by being safety and environmentally focused while fostering a strong safety culture. We currently have a permanent position available within our Service Department in our NSW Branch. Reporting to the Service Area Manager you will be required to manage the day to day running of your specified area within NSW, ensuring all work is completed efficiently and safely. The main duties will be to attend and reduce breakdowns and maintain the service run. The successful applicant will hold a current electrical licence, and be able to service, repair and attend to breakdowns on various lift systems. You must also have the ability to work independently. The key responsibilities of the successful applicant will include but not be limited to: Follow management instructions and comply with company procedures Action customer complaints or enquiries Participate in After-hours call-out roster Take remedial action to rectify shutdowns Repairs / technical site-specific work Annual safety testing Call rate/maintenance visits and missed maintenance Procurement of parts where required Maintenance statement testing Ensure GSM is adhered to The successful candidate will need to possess the following skills and qualifications: Previous lift experience preferred although not essential A current Electrical Licence Excellent communication skills Ability to work independently Full Driving licence What we offer you At TK Elevator you will be able to extend your horizon – thanks to flexible working time models, international teams and a wide range of individual training courses.  A collaborative approach and treating each other with respect have been central to what we do for more than 200 years. If that's as important to you as it is to us, then apply now! Please Note: Only successful candidates will be contacted We value diversity and therefore welcome all applications, irrespective of gender, nationality, ethnic and social background, religion and beliefs, disability, age, or sexual orientation and identity. TKE Australia is an ISO certified Company.
