jobs.description:
Overview:
Vertical Transport Solutions Pty Ltd (VTS) is a leading provider of escalator and elevator installations, servicing, and modifications. We also specialise in security systems, home automation, and CCTV. Due to our continued growth, we are seeking a skilled Electronic Equipment Technician/Trades Worker to join our team.
This role is critical in supporting the electronic and control system functionality of vertical transport mechanisms. It involves installing, calibrating, testing, troubleshooting, repairing, and maintaining electronic components and control systems of vertical transport equipment to ensure optimal performance, reliability, and compliance with safety and industry standards.
Key Responsibilities:
Install, calibrate, and adjust electronic equipment and control systems specifically for lifts and escalators to meet required technical and safety standards.
Test and troubleshoot electronic circuits, sensors, controllers, and interface systems within lift machinery to identify and resolve faults.
Repair and replace faulty electronic components, wiring, control panels, and system parts to ensure the continuous operation of lift systems.
Perform routine servicing and preventative maintenance on electronic and control components of vertical transport systems.
Read and interpret technical drawings, wiring diagrams, and schematics relevant to lift installation and electronic control systems.
Work with PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers), variable speed drives, and motor control systems integrated in modern elevator technology.
Conduct electrical safety checks and compliance testing according to Australian lift industry standards and WHS requirements.
Collaborate with lift mechanics, electricians, and technicians to enhance service procedures and technical performance.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
Certificate III or IV in Electronics, Computer Hardware Servicing, Electrical Fitting, or equivalent qualifications relevant to the lift industry.
Hands-on experience with electronic and electrical control systems used in elevator and escalator operations.
Strong troubleshooting and diagnostic skills with lift electronics, sensors, control boards, and automation systems.
Proven ability to read and interpret lift control schematics, layout diagrams, and circuit drawings.
Familiarity with PLCs, safety relays, motor drives, and logic control used in elevator operations.
Sound knowledge of workplace safety procedures, risk assessment, and electrical compliance practices.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to work independently or as part of a lift service and installation team.
What We Offer:
Competitive salary and industry-aligned benefits
Ongoing training, professional development, and certification opportunities in the lift industry
A supportive, safety-focused, and collaborative team environment
Job Type: Full-time
Pay: $80,000.00 – $90,000.00 per year
Benefits:
Professional development assistance
Salary packaging
Work Authorisation:
Australia (Preferred)
Work Location: In person

Our client KLEEMANN Elevators Australia is seeking a Lift Repairs Technician to join their team!
Why this role?
This role is your chance to step into a pivotal role that combines technical expertise with
customer service. You will be responsible for the full spectrum of elevator, escalator, and walkway
maintenance, breakdowns, and repairs. At Kleemann, you will enjoy a work environment that
prioritizes quality maintenance, allowing you to focus on delivering excellence without the
pressure of tight schedules.
About Our Client
At KLEEMANN Elevators Australia, innovation and engineering excellence are at the heart of
everything we do. As a leader in elevator services for the low to mid-rise sector, we are
committed to providing top-notch solutions and supporting our employees with a friendly team
culture, a safe work environment, and opportunities for development and training.
What you'll be doing?
Conduct maintenance: Perform full spectrum elevator, escalator, and walkway maintenance.
Manage breakdowns: Undertake high-level fault finding and resolve breakdowns.
Customer support: Provide after-hours call-out support and release trapped passengers.
Preventative measures: Conduct preventative maintenance to ensure reliability and safety.
Who are they looking for?
Technical skills: Must have an electrical license and a good electrical aptitude.
Experience: Minimum 1-2 years of electrical experience required.
Safety commitment: Demonstrate an uncompromising commitment to safety.
Team player: Must be reliable and able to work well within a small team.
Additional requirements: Valid driver's license, White Card, and basic computer skills are
necessary.
Industry experience: Lift industry experience is favorable but not required.
Why should you consider this opportunity?
Our client offers an attractive remuneration package and other benefits, such as:
A Friendly Team Culture
A Safe Work Environment
Development and Training Opportunities
How to apply
Ready to join this role? Click Apply now to submit your resume and share your availability and
expected salary with us!
We welcome all applicants, regardless of experience or qualifications.
All information received will be kept strictly confidential and will be used only for employment-

Lift Technician / Electrician
Adapt Lifts & Electrical – Gold Coast / South East QLD
Adapt Lifts & Electrical is seeking a motivated and reliable technician to join our growing team
working across lift and escalator installations, repairs and upgrades.
We are ideally looking for someone with experience in the lift industry, however this is not
essential.
If you are not currently working in the lift industry, you MUST be a qualified electrician for your
application to be considered.
Candidates Ideally hold either a Trade Certificate or Licensed Electrician qualification to be
considered for this position.
If you are currently working in the lift industry, we will review applications even if you are not an
electrician.
About the Role
You will work across a wide variety of projects including:
Lift and escalator repairs
Installations and upgrades
Fault finding and electrical works
Working across commercial buildings, hotels and infrastructure projects
The role offers strong variety in the work, exposure to different equipment and the opportunity
to develop within the vertical transportation industry.
Work may occasionally involve:
Night works
Travel
Cleaning
Employment Structure
This position will commence on a casual basis.
This allows both parties to get to know each other before moving to a permanent full-time
position.
For the right candidate we are flexible and can initially offer trial days here and there so you do
not need to immediately leave your current role.
However, please note we have consistent work available and can provide full-time hours (5 days
per week) for the right person.
Mandatory Requirements
Applications will not be considered unless the following criteria are met:
Full working rights in Australia (non-negotiable)
Any valid driver's licence is acceptable for this position
If not currently in the lift industry – qualified electrician
Advantageous (but not essential)
Lift industry experience
Rigging ticket
Dogging ticket
What We Offer
Strong pipeline of work
A supportive and skilled team environment
Opportunity to build a long-term career in the lift industry
Variety of work across multiple projects
A company that rewards hard work and loyalty
If you are looking to join a growing and respected company in the lift industry, we encourage
you to apply.

jobs.description:
Management Consultant  
Next Level Elevators Pty Ltd  View all jobs  
Rosebery, Sydney NSW  
Management & Change Consulting (Consulting & Strategy)  
Full time  
Add expected salary to your profile for insights  
Posted 14d ago  

Quick apply  
Save

About the Business Next Level Elevators is an Australian-owned company specialising in the design, supply, installation and servicing of premium residential and light commercial elevators across New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland. The company delivers customised, all-electric lift solutions incorporating advanced European technology, energy-efficient systems and modern architectural integration for residential and commercial developments across Australia.

Due to continued national growth and increasing operational complexity, Next Level Elevators seeks an experienced Management Consultant to support strategic expansion, operational efficiency, and business performance improvement initiatives.

About the Role

The Management Consultant provides advisory services to senior C-level management, reporting on organisational performance and making strategic recommendations to improve efficiency, service delivery, and commercial outcomes across our residential elevator business. The role focuses on investigating and analysing organisational systems, operational workflows, and management practices, and delivering evidence-based advice to support executive decision-making and long-term business objectives.

Key Responsibilities

• Analyse end-to-end business processes relating to residential elevator sales, design coordination,

Management Consultant  
Next Level Elevators Pty Ltd  

- Analyse end-to-end business processes relating to residential elevator sales, design coordination, manufacturing scheduling, installation and servicing workflows, and breakdown response management.  

- Review organisational workflows between sales, product design, manufacturing, electrical, logistics, and site construction teams to identify inefficiencies, duplication, and process gaps.  

- Evaluate lead times, capacity constraints, and resource utilisation across elevator installations, scheduled servicing, and reactive repair activities.  

- Provide ongoing executive advisory services to senior C-level management, including the Chief Operating Officer and State leadership team, on organisational performance, operational effectiveness, and strategic priorities.  

- Prepare structured reports for executive leadership analysing business performance across lift installations, servicing, and repair operations.  

- Report on organisational performance against agreed objectives, KPIs, and strategic plans, highlighting risks, constraints, and improvement opportunities.  

- Make strategic recommendations to senior management based on quantitative analysis, operational data, and business trends.

jobs.description:
Project Manager Lifts / Elevators  
Next Level Elevators Pty Ltd - Brisbane QLD  
$20,000 - $130,000 a year  
Full time  
23d ago, from seek.com.au  

We are seeking a dedicated, highly organised Project Manager to oversee lift projects and ensure smooth operations from completion of sale to handover. This senior role requires interaction with multiple internal teams, engagement with external stakeholders, and managing several moving project parts simultaneously.

Key Responsibilities:  
• Project Coordination: Schedule and manage lift projects, ensuring seamless coordination of works and materials with the scheduling team.  
• Team Management: Lead installation teams, ensuring optimum efficiency while maintaining high morale and productivity.  
• Procurement & Logistics: Work closely with the procurement team to ensure products are available on time and in full.  
• Deadline Management: Oversee the progress of projects, adapting work plans as needed to meet deadlines and ensure quality delivery.

- Procurement & Logistics: Work closely with the procurement team to ensure products are available on time and in full.

- Deadline Management: Oversee the progress of projects, adapting work plans as needed to meet deadlines and ensure quality delivery.

- Client & Stakeholder Communication: Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and internal teams (Installation, Sales, Procurement, Accounts & Senior Management)

- Quality Control: Review and sign off on building works before installation, maintaining a high level of quality control throughout the project lifecycle.

- Documentation & Reporting: Oversee all incoming and outgoing project documentation, and prepare progress reports for senior management.

- Process Improvement: Continuously optimize processes and approaches to enhance efficiency and quality.

- Safety & Compliance: Ensure installation teams adhere to company OHS policies and maintain a safe working environment.

- Growth & Development: Identify opportunities for growth, new projects, and initiate actions to expand business opportunities.

Required Personal Qualities:

- Professional "can-do" attitude with excellent communication skills.

- Ability to manage high-stress situations while maintaining clear, calm leadership.

- Strong multitasking skills with the capacity to handle multiple project components simultaneously.

- Leadership ability with a big-picture vision and strong attention to detail.

- Conflict resolution skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

Experience

- Leadership ability with a big-picture vision and strong attention to detail.
- Conflict resolution skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

Experience
- 3 years plus experience within in the Lift / Elevator industry
- Managing multiple projects, ideally in construction or engineering or residential build projects
- Proven leadership in budgets, and project timelines.
- Experience with installation projects and construction-related work and understanding of building processes and safety protocols.
- High level of familiarity with project management or CRM software for scheduling and reporting.
- Experience in managing project budgets and working with procurement teams to ensure timely material availability.
- Ability to identify risks, adapt project plans, and solve issues to keep projects on track.
- Skilled in managing client relationships, communicating effectively, and ensuring project satisfaction.
- Experience enforcing safety protocols and ensuring OHS compliance on-site.
- Strong communication, conflict resolution, and leadership skills to maintain team morale and resolve issues.

What We Offer:

Bullivants

Sales Representative (Heavy Lifting & Rigging)  
Bullivants

Save

Enhancing the customer centric mentality, the Internal Sales Representative will support activities related to direct customer sales support and service. In addition to the face-to-face element of the role you will also be interacting with the internal and external sales team to resolve relevant queries.

Bullivants are leaders in lifting, rigging and safety related products with a national presence providing a customer first approach into the Mining and industrial market sectors. Product sales and ongoing service and support is a key part of our market offer. At the Newcastle branch, you’ll be joining a high performing, engaged team who celebrated their win as Branch of the Year! We are looking for the next great addition to nurture ongoing success!

This is a permanent, full-time opportunity and will be based onsite at Bullivants Newcastle.

Some of the tasks:
- Support with client sales and showroom retail activities
- Resolve customer queries either face-to-face or via phone and email
- Support with ordering to ensure delivery timelines are adhered to including any overdue deliveries
- Interact with relevant internal departments to ensure overall customer satisfaction
- Undertake ad-hoc sales duties as requested

Some of the skills:
- Experience in the lifting, rigging and safety industry highly regarded
- Previous experience in a customer service role in Mining or industrial sales environment
- Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment
- High level written and verbal communication skills
- A curious mindset and a willingness to learn
- Proactive nature with a focus on problem solving to achieve an outcome

Next Steps

Sales Representative (Heavy Lifting & Rigging)  
Bullivants  
  
Next Steps  
  
If you meet most of the criteria listed above and would like to be considered for this opportunity, please send your application today. We will be contacting suitable candidates as we receive them.  
  
As part of our recruitment process and commitment to safety, you may be required to undertake background checks (which may include a police check, a pre-employment medical assessment; and/or drug & alcohol testing) as part of your application process.  
  
Bullivants also promotes diversity and inclusion across our workplaces and encourages applications from all backgrounds, including people from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities, the LGBTQI+ community and people with disabilities.

jobs.title: Lift Installations - Carpentry Expertise (Perth)
jobs.description: Carpenter or Cabinet Maker? Apply Your Skills in a New Industry! If you’re in Perth and have experience in carpentry or cabinet-making, we have an exciting opportunity where you can put your skills to work while learning an exciting new trade! We’re a well-established lift company offering long-term career opportunities, ongoing training, and a clear path to a financially rewarding and specialised trade. No lift experience? No problem! We provide full training and a clear career path in a growing industry. Carpenters have valuable skills that can easily transition into the lift industry, allowing you to apply your carpentry or cabinet-making knowledge to achieve high-quality results. The Role: Assisting with the installation and assembly of lift systems with precision and care. Carrying out maintenance tasks and troubleshooting to keep lifts running smoothly. Working in a team to meet project deadlines and deliver outstanding service. Following strict safety protocols – we take safety seriously. About You: Experience in carpentry, cabinet making, or a similar hands-on trade. Skilled with hand and power tools, with a strong eye for detail. Ability to read and follow technical instructions. Must have a valid driver’s license and white card. What’s in it for you: Full training to transition into a new, in-demand trade. Potential for bonuses and company incentives after the training period. One extra rostered day off per month. The opportunity to move into an Electrical Apprenticeship if it’s the right fit. Take your skills beyond the workshop and into an industry with unmatched career growth opportunities. Apply today to start your next chapter!

jobs.title: Heavy Lift Engineer
jobs.company: Advanced Cranes and Rigging Pty Ltd
jobs.description: Heavy Lift Engineer A fantastic opportunity exists for an experienced Lift Engineer to join our business. The Company: With over 20 years of experience, Advanced Cranes and Rigging is a national leader in lifting solutions. We support Tier 1 government and private sector projects with a modern fleet ranging from 3t to 800+ tonnes. Safety, innovation, and quality are the pillars of our business—underpinned by ISO45001 accreditation and a practical, values-driven culture. Role Overview: This role requires a qualified Lift Engineer with extensive experience in the Mobile Crane industry. In this role, you will be a valued member of the Engineering team, and responsible for lift planning, site inspections and logistical arrangements of lift plans. Lift plans will be for Tier One projects, along with other infrastructure and commercial projects. Role is based out of our North Brisbane depot. Key Responsibilities: Collaborate closely with Project Managers / Safety / Operations team and other stakeholders to ensure seamless project execution. Plan and coordinate lift studies in a timely fashion. Foundation for success: Bachelor, Diploma, or Post-graduate degree in Civil/ Mechanical Engineering or similar related field. 3 years’ experience in a similar engineer role (exp in rail, wind or infrastructure projects highly regarded) Proficient in CAD software, 2D AUTOCAD in particular, 3D advantageous.  Proficient in LICCON Work Planner highly desirable. Familiarity with mobile, crawler and pick & carry cranes; Liebherr and Terex/Franna in particular. Excellent Office365 skills (Word, Excel, MS Projects, Teams). Familiar with safety and quality standards in the construction/crane industry. Customer relationship management experience. Experience in project management advantageous but not essential. Organisational, communication and time management skills. Excellent verbal and written communication skills. An attractive package will be tailored for the best candidate. Apply now and join Australia’s most trusted name in lifting and rigging. Please submit a cover letter and resume by clicking 'Apply'

jobs.title: Crane Lift Study Planner
jobs.description: About WA Universal WA Universal is a family-owned, third-generation Western Australian business and trusted and preferred provider of rigging and crane solutions. We deliver safe and efficient crane services across tier one construction, infrastructure, and industrial projects. As our Tower Crane and Mobile Crane operations continue to expand, we are seeking experienced technical professionals to support project planning, compliance, and execution. Role Overview This role sits within WA Universal’s technical and planning function and is responsible for supporting crane operations from concept through to execution. The position involves technical planning, crane selection, documentation, and close collaboration with internal teams, clients, manufacturers, and site personnel to ensure lifting operations are compliant with Australian standards and site-specific requirements. Key Responsibilities Develop and review technical drawings, lift studies and method statements for tower and mobile crane operations. Select appropriate crane types, capacities and configurations based on load, radius, site constraints and manufacturer guidelines. Perform load calculations, crane chart interpretation, stability and foundation/ground bearing checks. Prepare and manage technical documentation including lift studies, SWMS/JSA, risk assessments, permits and compliance records. Interpret crane manuals and manufacturer guidelines. Conduct site assessments and collaborate with clients, engineers, riggers, operators and project teams. Ensure compliance with WorkSafe, CraneSafe, Australian Standards (including AS 2550 series) and internal procedures. Provide technical support during mobilisations, lifts and demobilisations. Manage and maintain crane data, drawings, certifications and technical records. Skills & Experience Proven experience in crane planning, technical coordination, or lift study preparation. Strong understanding of crane operations, rigging methodology and safety systems. Proficiency in AutoCAD / CAD software; experience with lift planning software and 3D tools advantageous. Ability to interpret technical manuals, standards and engineering information. Excellent communication, coordination and problem-solving skills. Relevant engineering, rigging, or lift planning qualifications preferred. Strong safety focus and ability to work independently and within a team. What We Offer Competitive salary. Career development and training opportunities. Supportive, safety-first work environment. Exposure to complex and high-profile construction projects across WA.

jobs.title: AUS Inds Lifting Team Manager
jobs.description: As a Lifting Technician, you'll be responsible for inspecting, testing, repairing, and maintaining lifting equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards and regulations. You'll work closely with customers to provide expert advice and solutions, ensuring their lifting equipment is reliable and safe to use. If you have rigging, engineering or hands-on experience working at heights. We want to hear from you! IF you don't have these skills, that is no problem we'll train and teach THE RIGHT PERSON! What we will require from you is: a keen eye for detail, a can do attitude, a team player and above all a keenness to learn. It's a physical roll to some degree, a customer facing roll and an exciting technical roll once you are trained and competent. Join us in ensuring the safety and reliability of lifting equipment for industry is safe, whilst building a rewarding career with a Global company. Key Responsibilities Perform inspections, testing, and certification of lifting equipment, including chains, slings, hoists, and other lifting gear. Conduct repairs and maintenance on lifting equipment to ensure optimal performance and compliance with safety standards. Provide technical advice and support to customers regarding lifting solutions and equipment. Maintain accurate records of inspections, repairs, and certifications. Ensure all work complies with relevant Australian Standards and safety regulations. Collaborate with the sales team to identify customer needs and recommend appropriate lifting solutions. Uphold a strong commitment to workplace safety and quality standards. Who are you: Can do attitude with a strong work ethic Basic understanding of Australian Standards. This is your chance to be part of a passionate team that thrives on delivering exceptional service and building strong relationships with our va

jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer Product Development
jobs.description: About The Role Australian Lifting Centre Pty Ltd is a leading provider of access and lifting equipment, supporting construction, industrial and commercial customers with reliable machinery and tailored technical solutions. As we expand our product engineering capability, we are seeking an innovative Mechanical Engineer with strong expertise in mechanical design, advanced prototyping, mechatronic systems and R&D-driven product development. This position is ideal for an engineer with experience in precision mechanisms, robotics-inspired design principles, structural integrity analysis and development of engineered solutions for real-world applications. You will contribute to the enhancement, optimisation and customisation of our lifting and access equipment, helping us deliver products with improved safety, durability and performance. Key Responsibilities * Lead mechanical design and analysis activities for new product concepts, system improvements and customised equipment solutions. * Apply advanced mechanical and mechatronic engineering principles to enhance equipment capabilities. * Conduct structural and mechanical performance assessments, including simulations, safety evaluations and compliance checks with Australian Standards. * Develop prototypes, perform hands-on experimental testing, evaluate results and implement iterative improvements. * Prepare detailed engineering drawings, specifications, technical files and documentation for both internal teams and external manufacturing partners. * Contribute to R&D initiatives aimed at improving reliability, reducing failure rates and enhancing lifecycle performance of access and lifting machinery. * Investigate complex mechanical problems, carry out root-cause analysis and implement engineering corrective actions. * Work closely with service technicians, operations staff and external suppliers to ensure seamless integration of engineered improvements. * Support custom engineering requests by translating client requirements into practical, engineered solutions. Skills & Experience * Bachelor’s degree or higher in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics or a closely related field; higher research qualifications (e.g., Master or PHD) will be highly regarded. * Proven experience in product development, R&D engineering, robotics, mechanism designs or complex electromechanical systems. * Strong capability in 3D CAD modelling, mechanical analysis, prototype development and experimental validation. * Solid understanding of materials behavior, structural design, load paths, fatigue, safety engineering and mechanical reliability. * Experience in taking a concept from research/ideation through to prototype testing and final engineering documentation. * Ability to work independently on technically demanding tasks while collaborating effectively with multidisciplinary teams. Desirable Attributes * Background in designing or improving machinery, lifting systems, articulated mechanisms or automated devices. * Knowl

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician
jobs.description:
Elevator Service Technician

Otis – Darwin NT  
3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK  
Full time  
3d ago, from seek.com.au  

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World’s Best Employers!  

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

About you  

We are looking for a Service Technician to based in Darwin, Northern Territory. In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.  

- Permanent role  
- Reports to the Branch Manager  

Key responsibilities for YOU  

- Performs Service and Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals.  
- Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers.  
- Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

- Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.

- Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and/or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement.

- Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

- Standby and provide after-call-back coverage from time to time.

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally

- Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

- Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.

- Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.

- Sales Team: Sales Account Representatives, Managers, and Coordinators (where relevant).

- External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers.

What YOU will need to be successful

- Have Electrical and/or Fitter qualifications as relevant to the role and Lift apprenticeship or other industry-accredited training program (desirable)

- Minimum two (2) to five (5) years' experience within the Lift Industry.

- Hold a valid driver's license and Electrical license.

- Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

- Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training and demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills.

- Strong English language skills, both verbal and written

- Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet).

- You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority

- Has attention to detail and is results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team.

```
• You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority

• Has attention to detail and is results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!

We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion
We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity; Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation

jobs.description:
Open app

Service Manager
Schindler Lifts Australia 
3.1
34 reviews
·
View all jobs
Belmont, Perth WA
General/Business Unit Manager (CEO & General Management)
Full time
Competitive Base + 12% S + $28K Bonus + $18 Car A.
Posted 11d ago
WHY join Schindler?
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their
full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their
area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and
professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our
employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our
business in the coming years.
About the role:
We have an opportunity has arisen for an experienced Service Manager to join our team based
in Perth. Reporting directly to the area Operations Manager, you will have approximately 10
direct reports and P&L responsibilities. The role is office based as well as spending time in the
field with technician direct reports and attending customer meetings. You will also be expected
to carry out the monthly toolbox meetings and be responsible for driving consistent and reliable
financial performance.
As the successful candidate, you play a crucial role in our business as you are often the first point
of contact for our customers, to deliver responsive and high-quality services and solutions. You
will also be responsible for providing coaching, mentoring and leadership to your team of service
technicians driving open communication and a focus on a safety culture.
 
This package includes:
Highly Competitive base + 12% Super
$28,000 Bonus incentive
$18,000 car allowance
Access to discounted partner company offers.
Funding for further educational support upon completion of 1 year of employment
Birthday day off
Key Accountabilities
Improve operational budgets, including portfolio growth, material and labour cost control.
Constantly analyse results & take effective remedial action quickly.
Visit the site and record contact & other site details
Determine manpower capacity needed to service the contracted portfolio.
Reconcile fulfilment of maintenance visits and act as required
Make regular audits to ensure the quality of maintenance.
Allocate resources to attend to shutdowns
Monitor callback history and allocate resources to attend to sick lifts
Set and attend team meetings.
Identify & plan for Service training needs.
Document and solve customer complaints.
Requirements for the role:
Ideally past experience as a Maintenance/ Repairs/ Service Manager working in
the Lift industry or within Fire, HVAC, Security or a similar industry.
Previous experience managing Field Technicians
Qualification in building, engineering, electrical or mechanical disciplines will be looked upon
favourably
Proven experience in driving business growth and minimizing labour costs
Strong organization and delegation skills, with proven ability to coordinate staff productivity.
Exceptional communication skills
Strong awareness of safety ideally with a qualification
Past experience forecasting, budgeting and contract administration 
About Schindler?
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the
world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the
largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing
over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for
almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology
and innovative people.
How to apply:
If you are seeking an opportunity to join a Global Industry Leader and believe that you possess
the required skills and qualifications to succeed in this role, click on the "Apply" button and
complete our online application form.
We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait
Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply.
Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes
those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a
manager.

About the role
Simplex Elevators Pty Ltd is seeking an experienced Service Manager to oversee the
maintenance and repair of our elevator systems across Queensland. As a full-time position based
in Salisbury, QLD, this role is crucial in ensuring the safe and reliable operation of our equipment
for our valued clients.
Australian citizenship is required for this position.
What you'll be doing
1. Manage and coordinate a team of skilled elevator technicians to deliver exceptional service
and maintenance
2. Liaise with clients to understand their needs and assist the scheduling team / maintenance
and repair activities accordingly
3. Provide technical guidance and support to the service team, including troubleshooting
complex issues
4. Ensure all service work is completed to the highest safety and quality standards
5. Maintain detailed records and provide regular reports on service activities
6. Support the sales team by providing technical expertise and recommendations for new
installations
What we're looking for
1. Significant experience (5+ years) in an elevator maintenance or service management role
2. Strong technical knowledge of elevator systems
3. Experience with all elevator types including hydraulic, traction, and machine room-less
systems is essential
4. Excellent communication and customer service skills to liaise effectively with clients
5. Proven ability to lead and motivate a team of technicians
6. Highly organised with a focus on safety, quality and efficiency
7. Relevant trade qualifications
About us
Simplex Elevators is a leading provider of elevator services in Queensland. With over 30 years of
experience, we are committed to delivering safe, reliable and innovative elevator solutions to our
clients. Our team of dedicated professionals work hard to maintain the highest standards of
service and customer satisfaction.
Apply now to join our team as Service Manager and play a vital role in the success of our growing
business.

Workshop Mechanic / Technician
All Lift Forklifts & Access Equipment – Hallam, VIC
Join a Growing Industry Leader – National Distributor of Premium Equipment Brands
About Us
All Lift Forklifts & Access Equipment is a proudly family-owned and operated business, with
operations across VIC and other eastern states. We specialise in the rental, sales, and service of
forklifts, telehandlers, and access equipment. As the appointed national distributor for Magni
Telescopic Handlers and Taylor Heavy Forklifts, the business has experienced significant growth.
To support our continued expansion, we’re looking for a Qualified Workshop
Mechanic/Technician to join our dedicated service team at our Hallam workshop.
The Role
You’ll play a key part in ensuring our fleet is maintained to the highest standard. This hands-on
role includes:
Diagnosing mechanical, electrical and hydraulic faults
Servicing and maintaining a range of lifting and access equipment
Pre-hire and off-hire inspections
Supporting breakdown and repair works as needed
You’ll work closely with a team of skilled technicians, delivering quality service across a wide
range of industry-leading equipment.
What We’re Looking For
Mechanical Trade Qualification (Diesel, Forklift, Heavy Vehicle, Light Vehicle, or Mobile Plant)
Solid understanding of diesel, electrical and hydraulic systems
Current driver’s licence
Forklift licence (desirable)
Ability to work efficiently, with a strong sense of urgency and pride in workmanship
About You
You’re more than just mechanically skilled — you’re a hands-on problem solver who thrives in a
team environment. With a solid mechanical background and a passion for getting things done
right, you take pride in your work and always put the customer first. Self-motivated, dependable,
and driven to contribute, you’re ready to be a key player in a supportive business that values your
expertise.
What We Offer
Competitive above-award wages, aligned with experience
Stable, full-time employment with a growing national company
A friendly, supportive team culture
Ongoing development opportunities within a high-demand industry
Ready to take the next step in your mechanical career?
Apply now and be part of a team that values your skills and supports your growth.

jobs.title: Lift Fitter
jobs.description:
Job Title: Lift Fitter  
Company Name: Elevate Mechanical Pty Ltd  
Location: Sydney CBD, NSW  

Job Description / Responsibilities:  
- Installation of lift systems
- Installation of the car structure
- Installation of rails
- Installation of landing doors
- Finalisation of levelling and door lock systems
- Liaise with builders and other trades to assist sub-contractors
- Includes electrical, maintenance, and repairs of electric and hydraulic passenger and freight lifts, including working alongside other trades
- Examining technical drawings, wiring diagrams and specifications to determine sequences and operations of product
- Measurement, testing and calibration of lift interface components
- Selecting, cutting and connecting wires and leads to terminals and connectors
- Using electrical and electronic infrastructure to trace and diagnose faults
- Repairing and replacing faulty wiring and electric parts
- Positioning and installing electrical substations
- Connecting electrical systems to power supply
- Testing functionality of lifts
- Installing, testing and adjusting electrical and mechanical parts of lifts
- Building the mechanical component of lifts
- Disassembling the mechanical components of the lift
- Running wireways
- Installing electronic components of lifts
- Wiring and repairs of interface and complete electrical and electronic circuitry  

Requirements / Qualifications:  
- Candidates considering employment must have been trained in and understand the installation methodology
- Must be qualified in an electrical/mechanical trade and hold a relevant height and power ticket
- Able to read mechanical components of the lift as per manufacturer guidelines and LEV-130 ticket

jobs.title: Vertical Transportation (VT) Consultant
jobs.description:
Electricians & Lift Fitters

Company: Vertical Transport Solutions Pty Limited (VTS)

Location: Sydney CBD, NSW

Job Description

Vertical Transport Solutions Pty Limited (VTS) specializes in escalator and elevator installations, modernizations, and removals.

Due to our constant growth, we are seeking highly motivated experienced electrical and elevator installers as well as experienced electricians and apprentice electricians to join our team.

Responsibilities include the following:

- Manage the electrical installation environment during installation activities and monitor escalator and elevator projects throughout the installation process.
- Examine blueprints, wiring diagrams, and specifications to determine sequences and phases of installation.
- Measure and lay out installation reference points.
- Install and connect wiring, power supplies, and terminals to connectors.
- Using various elevator and escalator industry tools.

Other responsibilities include:

- Troubleshooting electrical circuits related to the lifts and elevators.
- Mechanical and Electrical Repairs.
- Installing, maintaining, and adjusting electrical and mechanical parts of lifts.
- Designing, installation, maintenance, servicing, and repair of electric and hydraulic passenger and freight lifts, including support and customer feedback to the installation supervisor.
- Responsible for site documentation relating to the job/keeping, quality feedback, etc.
- May be required by the supervisor to give training/coaching to trainees and helpers.

Skills and Experience Required:

- Must have a white card.
- Must have or willing to obtain working safely at heights.
- Ideally 1-year experience in the lift industry but not mandatory.
- Electrical license for Electrical positions.
- Good team player, attention to detail and strong work ethic.
- Current Driver’s License.
- Ability to speak and understand English.

WHY join Schindler?
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their
full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their
area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and
professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our
employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our
business in the coming years.
About the role:
We have an opportunity has arisen for an experienced Service Manager to join our team based
in Perth. Reporting directly to the area Operations Manager, you will have approximately 10
direct reports and P&L responsibilities. The role is office based as well as spending time in the
field with technician direct reports and attending customer meetings. You will also be expected
to carry out the monthly toolbox meetings and be responsible for driving consistent and reliable
financial performance.
As the successful candidate, you play a crucial role in our business as you are often the first point
of contact for our customers, to deliver responsive and high-quality services and solutions. You
will also be responsible for providing coaching, mentoring and leadership to your team of service
technicians driving open communication and a focus on a safety culture.
 
This package includes:
Highly Competitive base + 12% Super
$28,000 Bonus incentive
$18,000 car allowance
Access to discounted partner company offers.
Funding for further educational support upon completion of 1 year of employment
Birthday day off
Key Accountabilities
Improve operational budgets, including portfolio growth, material and labour cost control.
Constantly analyse results & take effective remedial action quickly.
Visit the site and record contact & other site details
Determine manpower capacity needed to service the contracted portfolio.
Reconcile fulfilment of maintenance visits and act as required
Make regular audits to ensure the quality of maintenance.
Allocate resources to attend to shutdowns
Monitor callback history and allocate resources to attend to sick lifts
Set and attend team meetings.
Identify & plan for Service training needs.
Document and solve customer complaints.
Requirements for the role:
Ideally past experience as a Maintenance/ Repairs/ Service Manager working in
the Lift industry or within Fire, HVAC, Security or a similar industry.
Previous experience managing Field Technicians
Qualification in building, engineering, electrical or mechanical disciplines will be looked upon
favourably
Proven experience in driving business growth and minimizing labour costs
Strong organization and delegation skills, with proven ability to coordinate staff productivity.
Exceptional communication skills
Strong awareness of safety ideally with a qualification
Past experience forecasting, budgeting and contract administration 
About Schindler?
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the
world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the
largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing
over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for
almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology
and innovative people.

We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait

jobs.title: Junior Project Manager
jobs.description:
Junior Project Manager  
Velocity Home Lifts – Jamisontown NSW  
$95,000 - $100,000 a year  
Full time  
1d ago, from seek.com.au  

Apply on company site  

🔆 Ready to Step Into a Project Management Role That Builds Your Future?  
This isn’t your typical Project Management gig.

At Velocity Home Lifts, we’re elevating the standard of residential living, literally. We design and install high-end, custom-built lift solutions that help Aussies stay in the homes they love, longer.  

As we continue to scale across NSW, we’re on the hunt for a proactive, detail-driven Junior Project Manager who’s ready to roll up their sleeves, hit the road, and help make every installation seamless.  

👷‍♂️ What You’ll Be Doing  
- Head to site to measure lift shafts and confirm builder works are completed correctly  
- Be the on-the-ground support for the Project Manager, from pre-install checks to job booking

**What You’ll Be Doing**

- Head to site to measure lift shafts and confirm builder works are completed correctly
- Be the on-the-ground support for the Project Manager, from pre-install checks to job booking
- Liaise with clients, builders, and our install crew to keep timelines tight and jobs running smoothly
- Help track progress, troubleshoot hiccups, and flag anything that could impact quality or deadlines
- Maintain accurate project data and updates across our internal systems

**Why This Role is Built for You**

- You’re ready to grow and want hands-on project experience in a booming niche
- You’re not afraid of a bit of mud on your boots, you thrive in both office and site environments
- You love getting the details right and keeping things moving
- You’re a clear communicator and a team player who can hold your own with builders and clients
- You want to be part of a tight-knit, fast-moving team where your work actually matters

**What You Bring**

- Experience in construction, installation, or coordination-based roles (1–2 years ideal)
- Strong communication and planning skills, you can juggle tasks and manage site visits
- Confidence in reading plans or technical specs (lift or building knowledge = bonus)
- Valid driver’s licence, this is a mobile role
- Self-motivation, a sharp eye for detail, and a sense of humour

**What You Get**

- Valid driver’s licence, this is a mobile role
- Self-motivation, a sharp eye for detail, and a sense of humour

🎁 What You Get
- $95-100K
  - super
  - vehicle
  - phone
  - laptop
  - monthly RDO

- A unique mix of site work, project coordination, and client liaison
- Support from a team that actually supports you, no micromanagement, just collaboration
- Access to some of the most interesting home transformation projects in NSW
- A role where your input is seen, valued, and appreciated

🌟 Sound Like You?
Don’t sit still, hit apply now

Whether you're stepping up from a trades role or stepping across from a junior PM position, this is a unique opportunity to learn, lead, and lift your career with Velocity.

Velocity Home Lifts, Where Careers (and Homes) Go Up. 🏠📈

Apply on company site
Be careful - Don't provide your bank or credit card details when applying for jobs. Don't transfer any money or complete suspicious online surveys. If you see something suspicious, report this job ad.

KLEEMANN Elevators Australia is dedicated to delivering cutting-edge, custom-engineered lifts

excellence and customer satisfaction.
What you'll be doing?
Build Relationships: Establish and maintain trusted partnerships with clients and industry
leaders.
Drive Sales: Identify and pursue sales opportunities, tailoring solutions to meet client needs.
Engage Prospects: Proactively seek out new business opportunities and engage potential
customers.
Prepare Proposals: Draft compelling proposals, budgets, and tenders that align with market
standards.
Manage Pipeline: Oversee your sales pipeline, ensuring consistent follow-ups and customer
engagement.
Close Deals: Negotiate and finalize sales, ensuring alignment with company objectives.
Achieve Targets: Meet and exceed sales targets, contributing to the company's growth.
Who are they looking for?
Experienced Sales Professional: At least 3 years in sales, ideally in the lift industry, with a
proven track record.
Technical Knowledge: Understanding of the lift industry and customer needs is essential.
CRM Proficiency: Comfortable using CRM systems to manage sales activities.
Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to engage with
clients.
Mechanical Background: A background in mechanical engineering or relevant qualifications is
preferred.
Team Player: Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team.
Travel Ready: A valid driver's license and willingness to travel as required.
Why should you consider this opportunity?
Our client offers an attractive remuneration package and other benefits, such as:
Competitive base salary with a rewarding commission structure.
Option for a company vehicle or allowance.
Continuous training and career advancement opportunities.
A supportive and dynamic team culture.
Growth potential within a global organization.

expected salary with us!
We welcome candidates from all backgrounds to apply and are committed to providing an
inclusive environment.
All information received will be kept strictly confidential and will be used only for employment-

jobs.description:
Customer Service / Scheduler  
Next Level Elevators Pty Ltd  
View all jobs  
Rosebery, Sydney NSW  
Customer Service - Call Centre (Call Centre & Customer Service)  
Full time  
$75,000 plus Sup  
Posted 4d ago  

Quick apply  
Save

$75,000 plus Sup

Posted 4d ago

Company Overview:

Join our dynamic and growing team at Next Level Elevators, a leading organisation in the home lift industry.

The role

Due to our rapid expansion, we are in need of a Customer Service Representative & Service Scheduler.

This is a fast-paced role that requires someone who thrives under pressure and can remain calm and composed in challenging situations. As a key member of our service team, you will be responsible for dealing with customers enquiries and also scheduling / job management for the Service Team to ensure smooth workflow and successful business outcomes.

Your Responsibilities

- Liaise with customers and service team over the phone to confirm and update job details.
- Proactively manage appointments, deal with any client concerns and pricing queries with professionalism and empathy.
- Create, update, and prioritise job plans and maintenance schedules in the works management system (Sim-pro).
- Identify and coordinate all resources—including labour—required to perform work efficiently and cost-effectively.
- Review the work delivery forecast in conjunction with managers and supervisors.
- Prepare and process ad hoc reports as required.

The Person

- Business to customer telephone experience highly regarded.
- Demonstrated time management and organisation skills with the ability to prioritise and complete tasks with competing deadlines.

Customer Service / Scheduler  
Next Level Elevators Pty Ltd  

- Demonstrated time management and organisation skills with the ability to prioritise and complete tasks with competing deadlines.  
- Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to handle challenging conversations calmly and constructively.  
- Emotionally resilient, with a confident and grounded approach to managing stressful or confrontational situations.  
- A collaborative team player with the ability to build strong working relationships with both internal teams and customers.  
- Lateral thinker with well-developed analytical and problem-solving abilities.  
- Proactive in gathering and collating information from key sources.  
- Excellent attention to detail.  
- Ambition to build your career in a dynamic organization and growing industry.  

Benefits  
- Excellent salary of up to $75K base plus Super  
- Opportunity to join a growing industry with an innovative and ambitious company

The role involves managing customer inquiries, scheduling appointments, and coordinating resources while excelling in a fast-paced environment.

jobs.title: Occupational Therapist, Physiotherapist, Speech Therapist
jobs.description:
Occupational Therapist, Physiotherapist, Speech Therapist  
Lift Health Group  
Adelaide SA  
Physiotherapy, OT & Rehabilitation (Healthcare & Medical)  
Contract/Temp  
$600/day (additional $ packages for assessments)  
Posted 9d ago

Occupational Therapist/Speech Therapist/Physiotherapist

Lift Health Group

Location: Adelaide, SA  
Employment Type: Contract  
Pay: $600 per day (additional pay packages for assessments)

About Lift Health Group

Lift Health Group is a growing allied health organisation delivering high-quality, person-centred care across metropolitan, regional, and remote Australia. We work closely with individuals, families, and communities through the NDIS, providing consistent, well-governed, and clinically sound services.

Our multidisciplinary team supports people across the lifespan, with a strong focus on meaningful outcomes, sustainability, and supporting clinicians to do their best work.

Why Join Lift Health Group?
- 1–4 flying clinical days, delivering services to regional, rural, and remote communities. No overnights & home every afternoon.
- If you enjoy working alongside the team, our Parkside headquarters overlooking the park is available as a shared workspace, though contractors are equally welcome to work remotely

Day-trip flying model  
All regional travel is completed as day trips. Flights typically depart Adelaide Airport around 7:30am, with return by approximately 4:00pm.

Purposeful rural work  
Deliver high-impact therapy and assessments to communities with genuine need, while expanding your clinical skill set in a supported environment.

Clinical variety and development  
You will have:  
- Generalist caseload across paediatric, adolescent, and adult participants  
- Opportunities to shape your caseload around your clinical interests where possible

Occupational Therapist, Physiotherapist, Speech Therapist  
Lift Health Group  

The Role  
This role suits a rural-generalist Physiotherapist/Occupational Therapist/Speech Therapist, who enjoys variety, autonomy, and working across different environments.  
We welcome applications from qualified Occupational Therapists, Speech Therapists, or Physiotherapists.  
You will:  
• Deliver therapy and/or assessments across adult and paediatric participants  
• Work within schools, homes, and community settings  
• Travel to regional and remote SA as part of scheduled day trips  
• Complete high-quality clinical reports (if interested) with protected admin time  
• Collaborate closely with our multidisciplinary team through regular case discussions, shared planning, and coordinated care to support strong, participant-centred outcomes  

About You  
You will have:  
• AHPRA registration as an Occupational Therapist/Physiotherapist (no restrictions)  
• Speech Therapists must hold relevant professional body registration  
• Minimum of 2 years clinical experience  
• Experience working with NDIS participants, or a strong understanding of the scheme  
• Experience across paediatric and adult caseloads, or willingness to work across both  
• Strong communication, organisation, and time-management skills  
• A current driver's licence  
• Australian citizenship is required for this position  

What We Offer  
• Competitive salary with paid travel time  
• A structured, flexible work model that prioritises sustainability  
• Genuine work-life balance with no overnight travel
jobs.description_summary: Lift Health Group is seeking qualified Occupational Therapists, Physiotherapists, and Speech Therapists in Adelaide on a contract basis, offering $600 per day plus additional pay for assessments. The role involves delivering therapy in various settings, primarily to NDIS participants in regional and remote areas, with a focus on collaboration and meaningful outcomes.

Next Level Elevators is a leading Australian supplier and installer of premium electric home elevators. We focus on delivering elegant, sustainable, and innovative solutions that improve accessibility and add luxury to homes.

jobs.title: Sales and Key Account Representative
jobs.description:
Come and join Electra today. Electra Lift is an elevator company who have been in operation for over 45 years.

We specialize in Lift Maintenance, Modernizations, Replacements and New Installations. We maintain lifts all across Sydney and have a well-structured team to support our continued growth and expansion with our main office located in Mascot, NSW.

As we continue to expand, we are seeking an experienced Sales & Key Account Representative to join our growing team based in Mascot, NSW.

- 12 months fixed term contract
- Reports to the Operations Manager

About You

As a Sales & Key Account Representative you will work closely with our Key Accounts Team to quote urgent lift repairs/minor upgrades, quote proactive equipment improvements and support the administrative tasks required with our key account portals.

Key Responsibilities

- Deliver revenue performance: Consistently achieve (and exceed) assigned sales revenue and margin targets in line with the approved business plan, with clear accountability for results.
- Maximise speed-to-lead and conversion: Action all repair leads from every source immediately on receipt, applying disciplined follow-up to maximise contact and conversion rates.
- Qualify needs and build demand: Partner with the field technicians to confirm scope/requirements, then engage the customer to validate needs, create urgency, and progress opportunities through the buying cycle.
- Drive campaign-led pipeline: Actively participate in all repair product campaigns, generating pipeline contribution and supporting the Key Account team by providing responsive, accurate quoting and commercial support.
- Own proposal development end-to-end: Produce accurate estimates and secure required internal approvals to create a compliant Service Works Proposal, then submit to the customer within agreed turnaround times.
- Execute a structured close plan: Proactively follow up every submission using a structured, cyclical call-closing cadence to progress objections, confirm decision steps, and secure approval/booking.

Sales and Key Account Representative  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  

- Forecast with accuracy. Keep CRM/sales systems current, report activity consistently, and provide accurate forecasting against plan (pipeline, probability, timing, value).

What you will need to be successful  
- Minimum 2-3 years’ experience in Tele Sales, Frontline Sales, or Account Management, with a results-driven mindset (Preferred).  
- Exposure to technical or solution-based sales would be an added advantage  
- Proven ability to work independently and collaborate cross-functionally, building trusted relationships to drive outcomes.  
- Strong communications – verbal and written.  
- Attention to detail and accurate record-keeping are important for success  
- Sound knowledge of Microsoft 365 and the ability to learn and frequently switch between inhouse systems/databases is required  
- A desire to learn and understand the elevator industry and the technology involved  

Apply today and help us build what's next!  

Company profile  
OTIS
jobs.description_summary: OTIS Elevator Company is seeking an experienced Sales and Key Account Representative for a 12-month fixed-term contract based in Mascot, NSW, to drive sales performance and support key accounts. Candidates should have 2-3 years of experience in sales or account management, strong communication skills, and a results-driven mindset.

jobs.title: Heavy Lift Engineers, Designers & Specialists
jobs.description:
Open app
Sign in
Heavy Lift Engineers, Designers & Specialists
John Holland 
3.8
292 reviews
·
View all jobs
Fortitude Valley, Brisbane QLD
Civil/Structural Engineering (Engineering)
Full time
Add expected salary to your profile for insights
Posted 9d ago
About John Holland
At John Holland, our purpose is simple - we transform lives with everything we do.
We've always known that infrastructure is about people — our customers, our employees, and
the communities in which we work every day.
That's our difference. Deep experience and capability with a genuine care about creating better
lives for people along the way.
Be part of the team that's up for the challenge of transforming lives for good.
Expression of Interest
As a result of work in hand coupled with a strong pipeline of projects, we are interested in
engaging with experienced Heavy Lift Engineers, Designers and Specialists for an upcoming
Brisbane-based opportunity within our national Construction Engineering group.
Working at John Holland
You will have a multitude of opportunities to work on some of the largest and most complex
construction projects that are being delivered in Australia today.
John Holland's Lift & Shift team provides full lifecycle support to precontract and project delivery
teams across all aspects of lift and shift, including cranes, rigging, heavy haulage and specialised
heavy lift/shift equipment.
With a focus on developing tailored and cost-effective solutions, the Lift & Shift team supports
John Holland's construction teams to optimise construction methods, including equipment
selection, and ensure compliance requirements are met.
The team draws on its extensive experience across many industry sectors and extensive library of
construction methods to provide onsite and offsite support.
Should you be interested in learning more about how these opportunities could benefit your
career, please apply with your latest resume today.
About You
A Heavy Lift Engineer, Designer or Heavy Lift Specialist position with John Holland will require
you to hold proven experience and technical competency in planning and delivering lift and shift
operations, together with a degree qualification in Mechanical or Civil/Structural Engineering, or
a High-Risk Work Licence in Rigging or Cranes as applicable to the specific role.
Proficiency in using AutoCAD & Crane Specialist Software for preparing Lift/Transport plan/s,
along with the ability to nurture client relationships and utilise your excellent stakeholder
management skills is also essential.
As part of the team, you help us deliver on our promise to transform lives.
Your success is reflected in ours, so we're committed to being an employer of choice. We pride
ourselves on having a diverse and inclusive workplace, as different perspectives and ideas will
deliver our long-term success.
We want you to be with us for the long-term, so providing you with rich career experiences and
ongoing development is our priority.
What's in it for you
We're about connecting your sense of purpose to ours. We understand that your career is one of
the biggest ways to have an impact on the world. We also believe in supporting you as a whole
person, not just an employee.
We reward and support our people in so many ways. This starts with being flexible about how
different people like to work. From generous leave policies to wellbeing and relationship support,
your needs sit at the heart of our employee benefits.
We offer:
Competitive remuneration with salary continuance, and salary sacrifice options.
Great leave benefits including 18 weeks paid parental leave with super and up to an extra 18
weeks of super paid on further periods of unpaid parental leave, multicultural leave exchange and
two extra days to prioritise your wellbeing.
Career progression underpinned by our exciting pipeline of work means you'll have the
opportunity to work on iconic projects that are shaping cities and communities.
Learning and development opportunities where you'll have access to emerging talent programs,
building your career through clear career pathways, plus technical and leadership training and
development opportunities.
Inclusion, diversity and equity is part of how we work. We want everyone at John Holland to feel
that they belong - that's why we're working hard every day to foster a more inclusive culture,
backed by a business-wide inclusion strategy to bring about meaningful change. We've also got
active employee resource groups that support our commitments including those around gender
equality and reconciliation.
Flexibility means different things to different people. Whether it's flexi-hours, flexi-parenting,
flexi-leave, flexi-shifts or flexi-work, we're committed to helping our people work flexibly.

We are currently seeking experienced Rear lift, hook lift and front lift drivers to join our client based out of Kilburn, SA.  
These roles involve the safe collection, transport and disposal of waste using heavy vehicles in accordance with safety and environmental standards.  
If you’re an early riser who enjoys a steady routine, working independently, and being part of a supportive team – this opportunity is for you.

This is an ongoing role with consistent hours, a great team environment, and opportunities for career development.  
Hours & Roster:  
- All roles are based for Kilburn location  
- Shifts: Planned around 10 hours (occasional 12-hour shifts during busy periods)  
- Mon- Fri with occasional overtime offered. $43.40 Ord time/Penalties.  
- Front Lift Drivers:  
  Start between 1:00am – 4:30am depending on the route  
- Rear Lift Drivers:  
  Start times between 1:00am – 4:30am depending on route  
- Hook Lift Drivers:  
  Start times from 4:00am  
- Average shift length:  
  Around 10 hours (occasional 12-hour shifts during busy periods)  

To be successful, you will have:

Driver - Rear lift Operators  
CoreStaff Broken Hill  

To be successful, you will have:  
- Current Heavy Rigid (HR) Licence  
- White Card, or the ability to obtain one  
- National Police Clearance (or ability to obtain)

- Physically fit with a strong safety focus  
- Good communication and customer service skills  
- Reliability and ability to work autonomously  

Desirable:  
- Certificate III in Waste Management or equivalent experience  

Benefits:  
- Immediate start available  
- Weekly pays  
- Ongoing, stable work hours  
- Supportive and safety-focused team  
- Access to CoreStaff’s employee benefits platform with discounts at over 60 major Australian retailers  

CoreStaff is a proud leader in workplace diversity. We encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, women seeking non-traditional roles, and applicants with disabilities.

jobs.title: Lift Service Technician
jobs.description: Responsibilities Complete scheduled services, repairs and call out (breakdown) visits effectively and efficiently; Participate in After Hours On-Call Roster (approx 1 week in 5 on-call); Effectively plan workload, in conjunction with team of service technicians; Be a good communicator and maintain excellent customer relations; Ensure all work is carried out in line with company WH&S policy and procedures; Works within quality standards and procedures; Ensure true and accurate reports of activities and work carried out; Present a strong customer focus. Ensure that Company Vehicle is maintained in accordance with manufacturers schedule and company policies. Supervise and train apprentices as and when required (Eletech is committed to training and development of apprentices). Education and Qualifications Required Queensland or other Australian equivalent Electrical fitter/mechanic license; Manual Driver's License; Construction White Card. Experience Experience in the lift industry preferred; Electrical licence required; Familiarity with PLC and VF Drives; Mechanical experience and competence. Requirements: Candidates will be required to obtain a National Police Certificate and Working with Children Check in the course of their employment.
