jobs.title: Lift Repairs Technician
jobs.description: Our client KLEEMANN Elevators Australia is seeking a Lift Repairs Technician to join their team! Why this role? This role is your chance to step into a pivotal role that combines technical expertise with customer service. You will be responsible for the full spectrum of elevator, escalator, and walkway maintenance, breakdowns, and repairs. At Kleemann, you will enjoy a work environment that prioritizes quality maintenance, allowing you to focus on delivering excellence without the pressure of tight schedules. About Our Client At KLEEMANN Elevators Australia, innovation and engineering excellence are at the heart of everything we do. As a leader in elevator services for the low to mid-rise sector, we are committed to providing top-notch solutions and supporting our employees with a friendly team culture, a safe work environment, and opportunities for development and training. What you'll be doing? Conduct maintenance: Perform full spectrum elevator, escalator, and walkway maintenance. Manage breakdowns: Undertake high-level fault finding and resolve breakdowns. Customer support: Provide after-hours call-out support and release trapped passengers. Preventative measures: Conduct preventative maintenance to ensure reliability and safety. Who are they looking for? Technical skills: Must have an electrical license and a good electrical aptitude. Experience: Minimum 1-2 years of electrical experience required. Safety commitment: Demonstrate an uncompromising commitment to safety. Team player: Must be reliable and able to work well within a small team. Additional requirements: Valid driver's license, White Card, and basic computer skills are necessary. Industry experience: Lift industry experience is favorable but not required. Why should you consider this opportunity? Our client offers an attractive remuneration package and other benefits, such as: A Friendly Team Culture A Safe Work Environment Development and Training Opportunities How to apply Ready to join this role? Click Apply now to submit your resume and share your availability and expected salary with us! We welcome all applicants, regardless of experience or qualifications. All information received will be kept strictly confidential and will be used only for employment-related purposes.

jobs.title: Lift Mechanic Service Technician
jobs.description: The role involves maintaining and repairing lift systems across a range of residential and commercial buildings throughout the Sydney metropolitan area. Our Sydney operations are based in Brookvale NSW , with service sites located across the wider Sydney region. Key Responsibilities Carry out routine maintenance and servicing of lift and elevator systems Diagnose faults and complete electrical and mechanical repairs Attend lift breakdowns and emergency call-outs including passenger entrapments Complete service reports and maintain accurate maintenance documentation Ensure all work is completed in accordance with safety regulations and company procedures Liaise with supervisors, service coordinators, and building representatives as required Skills and Experience To be successful in this role you will have: Demonstrated experience servicing and maintaining elevator or lift systems A relevant trade qualification (Electrical or Mechanical trade preferred) Strong diagnostic and troubleshooting skills Ability to work independently while managing a service route Strong focus on safety and compliance Good communication and organisational skills Current driver’s licence What We Offer Full-time permanent position Salary range $80,000 – $90,000 per year , depending on experience Company vehicle and mobile phone Overtime and on-call allowances Ongoing training and support Opportunity to work within a well-established Australian company Employer: Going Up Elevators Pty Ltd Location: Sydney NSW (Brookvale office) Applications are invited from candidates with the relevant skills and experience for this role.

jobs.title: Elevator Technician
jobs.description: The role: You will undertake the full spectrum of elevator, escalator and walkway maintenance, breakdowns and repairs. You will excel with your attention to detail, superior technical knowledge and outstanding customer service. What sets us apart: At Kleemann, we pride ourselves on allocating significantly more hours to maintenance than our competitors. This commitment to quality maintenance ensures our portfolio maintains excellent reliability, giving you the time to do the job properly rather than rushing from site to site. How You will make a difference: Living our Culture through our values of Safety, Passion for People, Trust & Breakthrough Working autonomously and managing your daily workload Conducting preventative maintenance Undertaking high level fault finding Resolving elevator, escalator and walkway breakdowns / repairs Releasing trapped passengers Providing after-hours call out support About You: Enthusiastic, ready to show your high-quality work, attention to detail and customer first focus. Demonstrating an uncompromising commitment to Safety. Reliable and able to work well within a small team. To triumph in this role, you will need: An electrical license preferred Police Clearance A good electrical aptitude Minimum 1-2 years electrical experience required Valid driver's license White Card Basic computer skills Lift Industry Experience is favourable but not required About the company: Elevator Services Group is a wholly owned subsidiary of Kleemann Elevators Australia. We are Australia's leading specialist in maintaining and servicing Kleemann elevators, escalators and moving walk solutions for the low to mid-rise sector. We have been doing so for over 23 years. Our niche and specialty mean we have quality control over all of our works. We are a company who genuinely believes in and supports our employees and provide: A Friendly Team Culture A Safe Work Environment Development and Training To Apply Don't wait! Apply now as we will appoint as soon as we find the right person. So, submit your resume via the "apply now" button.

jobs.title: Lifting Technician
jobs.description: Key Responsibilities Perform inspections, testing, and certification of lifting equipment, including chains, slings, hoists, and other lifting gear. Conduct repairs and maintenance on lifting equipment to ensure optimal performance and compliance with safety standards. Provide technical advice and support to customers regarding lifting solutions and equipment. Maintain accurate records of inspections, repairs, and certifications. Ensure all work complies with relevant Australian Standards and safety regulations. Collaborate with the sales team to identify customer needs and recommend appropriate lifting solutions. Uphold a strong commitment to workplace safety and quality standards. Who are you: Can do attitude with a strong work ethic Basic understanding of Australian Standards. Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Excellent communication and customer service skills. High attention to detai l and commitment to quality. Current driver's license is essential A commitment to safety and compliance. What We Offer New Ford Ranger vehicle, laptop, ipad and phone. NATA & LEEA national certifcations. A supportive and collaborative team environment. Full day induction to help you settle into the business and position. Access to employee benefits , including discounts on products and services. Opportunities for career growth and development within the GPC network. Uniforms supplied - feel like a team. Recognition programs and bonus scheme for your performance. 1 paid Volunteer Leave Day per calendar year Parental leave top-up allowance Employee Assistance Program with access to mental health and well-being support initiatives including confidential counselling services About Motion: As part of the Motion Australia family, Motion (Websterbsc) is a trusted name in industrial solutions, offering top-quality products and services to keep our customers moving. This is your chance to be part of a passionate team that thrives on delivering exceptional service and building strong relationships with our valued customers. Join us on this exciting journey of success and growth and let us help you empower your career with us! MOTION is an equal opportunity employer You must be either an Australian Citizen or a holder of an Australian Permanent Residency Visa to be eligible to apply for this position. If this sounds like the opportunity for you, click "Apply for this job" and submit both your resume and cover letter. Please note only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

jobs.title: Experienced Service Technician
jobs.description: The Role: Service, maintain, and upgrade electric/hydraulic residential lift systems to top standards. Diagnose and repair electrical/mechanical faults efficiently. Read and interpret technical schematics and drawings. Deliver clear, professional on-site service and client updates. What We Offer: Full training on premium lift systems for career growth. Competitive pay with performance incentives. Job security from ongoing residential demand. Extra leave entitlements beyond standard. All tools, company vehicle, phone, tablet, PPE, and uniforms provided. Supportive team environment. What We're Looking For: Valid driver's licence (essential) and White Card. Electrical trade qualification or relevant hands-on experience. Strong mechanical aptitude and troubleshooting skills. Lift installation/maintenance experience preferred. Excellent customer-facing communication. Meticulous attention to detail, positive attitude, and solid time management. If you're a hands-on tech ready to apply your skills in Australia's booming residential lift sector, this is your chance for stability, development, and satisfaction.

jobs.title: Lift Repair Technician – Electrician
jobs.description: The Role: We’re seeking a skilled Lift Repair Electrician who thrives on hands-on, ground-up repair work. This role suits someone who enjoys diagnosing faults without relying on a guide—using their electrical and mechanical expertise to restore lift systems with precision. You’ll carry out detailed inspections, testing, troubleshooting, and repairs on lift equipment, working both on the bench and onsite across Greater Sydney. Key Responsibilities Diagnose, troubleshoot, and repair lift electrical and mechanical faults Complete bench repairs, electrical testing, and component rebuilds Conduct mechanical inspections to ensure safety and compliance Interpret wiring diagrams, schematics, and technical drawings Repair or replace faulty parts with accuracy and care Adhere to WHS and industry safety standards Work independently while supporting the team when needed About You We’re looking for a Lift Repair Mechanic / Electrician  who genuinely enjoys repairing lifts not just routine maintenance. You love problem-solving, fault-finding, tinkering and bringing complex systems back to life. You’ll bring: A valid NSW Electrical Licence (must-have) Experience in lift, hoist, crane, or similar mechanical/electrical repair work 3+ years mechanical/electrical fault-finding experience Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to resolve issues without guidance Ability to read schematics and understand motor control systems NSW Driver’s Licence Strong independence, initiative, and a safety-first mindset we want someone who is a self starter, critical thinker and gets the job done. What’s on Offer EBA Rate $59.30 per hour + Super Company car Phone + tools provided Stable permanent full-time role covering our clients vertical transport in Greater Sydney Supportive Training Career growth within the business take your career wherever you want it to go! Supportive, experienced repair team, our team has the best culture in the business we have each others backs and support each other its a great place to work and we love what we do! Ready to Apply? If you’re passionate about lift repairs and want a role where your skills are truly valued, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now! Email me your CV to [email protected] or for a confidential chat give me a call 0439 811 172

jobs.description: About the Role You will work across a wide variety of projects including: Lift and escalator repairs Installations and upgrades Fault finding and electrical works Working across commercial buildings, hotels and infrastructure projects The role offers strong variety in the work , exposure to different equipment and the opportunity to develop within the vertical transportation industry. The company covers: Flights Accommodation Associated travel expenses Meals where required You will have the opportunity to work in a variety of locations across North Queensland and remote regions. About You We are looking for someone who is: Hardworking and reliable Physically fit and comfortable with manual work Willing to learn new skills Able to follow instructions and work in a team Comfortable travelling for work Must have valid Australian work rights Preferred but not essential: Construction or mechanical experience Basic knowledge of tools White Card Driver's licence What We Offer Opportunity to learn a specialised industry Hands-on experience with lift installation and servicing Paid travel to unique locations across Queensland Supportive small team environment Potential long-term career opportunities as the business grows Apply Now If you're reliable, hardworking, and interested in building practical skills in a growing industry, we'd love to hear from you. jobs.title: Elevator Service Technicians
jobs.description:
Elevator Service Technician

Otis – Darwin NT  
⭐ 3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK  
Full time  
3d ago, from seek.com.au  

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World’s Best Employers!

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

About you

We are looking for a Service Technician to based in Darwin, Northern Territory. In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.

- Permanent role  
- Reports to the Branch Manager  

Key responsibilities for YOU

- Performs Service and Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals.  
- Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers.  
- Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

- Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

- Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.

- Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and/or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement.

- Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

- Standby and provide after-call-back coverage from time to time.

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally

- Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

- Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.

- Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.

- Sales Team: Sales Account Representatives, Managers, and Coordinators (where relevant).

- External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers.

What YOU will need to be successful

- Have Electrical and/or Fitter qualifications as relevant to the role and Lift apprenticeship or other industry-accredited training program (desirable)

- Minimum two (2) to five (5) years’ experience within the Lift Industry.

- Hold a valid driver’s license and Electrical license.

- Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

- Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training and demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills.

- Strong English language skills, both verbal and written

- Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet).

- You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority

- Has determination to detail and results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team.

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!

We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion  
We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation

Due to an expanding portfolio of projects, our client is currently seeking a dedicated and qualified Electrician / Lift Technician to join their team. Skills and Experience

- Practical, field-based electrical experience will be highly regarded  
- A strong commitment to safety protocols and procedures  
- The ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team

jobs.title: Vertical Transportation (VT) Consultant
jobs.description:
About the Role
We are currently recruiting for a Vertical Transportation (VT) Consultant to be based in our Sydney office.
This is an exciting opportunity for an enthusiastic person who has proven experience working in the Vertical Transportation industry to join Stantec and participate on a wide range of project types including commercial, retail, healthcare, educational and industrial. You will ideally have strong vertical transportation design knowledge and experience coupled with a sound knowledge of mechanical, electrical and controls systems for elevators, escalators and moving walks.
Knowledge of installation methodology for vertical transportation systems would be a distinct advantage.
Our dynamic work environment provides the optimal setting to take your career to the next level.
What you’ll be doing:
Design and engineering of Vertical transportation systems for a variety of projects.
Work collaboratively with other Engineers and Project Engineers to represent the Company and interact with clients, contractors and other project team members.
Collaborate with the design team on the preparation of fee proposals and review of associated project conditions of contract
Perform design calculations including Elevate for lift designs
Select appropriate VT equipment for use in buildings
Report writing including project briefs and design reports
Preparation of VT project specifications and tender documents
Site Inspections
Inspection of existing VT installations and condition reporting
What We’re Looking For:
Certification as a Design Practitioner or ability to gain the certification.
Strong technical knowledge of vertical transportation systems to primarily include elevators, escalators, moving walks. Knowledge of other vertical transportation systems like dumbwaiters, platform lifts and hoists will be also highly regarded.
Experience in using Peters Research Elevate software or similar for VT simulations will be highly regarded
Strong interpersonal and relationship building skills with the ability to develop strong relationships with team members and clients
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Previous experience in a similar client facing role is highly desirable
About Stantec
The Stantec community unites approximately 34,000 employees working in over 450 locations across six continents. We collaborate across disciplines and industries to bring buildings, energy, resources, water, environmental, transport and infrastructure projects to life.
Our work from initial project concept and planning through design, construction, and commissioning begins at the intersection of community, creativity, and client relationships.
What we offer
Genuine Flexible Working Arrangements - Including work from home opportunities, provision for school term contracts, job sharing and part time employment.
Option to purchase up to 4 weeks additional leave.
Learning and Talent Development Programs - Including Global programmes, online learning and on-the-job learning
Professional Memberships, Salary Continuance Insurance (SCI), Service Recognition Awards
Mental Health, Employee Assistance Program and Wellbeing Programs

About the Role
We are seeking an
experienced Elevator Service Technician
with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at
BOSS Elevators
. In this role, you will play a crucial part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sunshine Coast to Discovery Coast region.
As an expert technician, you’ll be responsible for conducting routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs, with a focus on quickly diagnosing and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator components. Your ability to think critically and implement effective solutions will be essential to ensuring optimal functionality and compliance with AS1735 standards.
You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to
"Leading the Change"
in elevator maintenance.
This role is suited for someone who is not only technically skilled but also values quality workmanship, safety, and client satisfaction. If you take pride in overcoming challenges and delivering high-quality results, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.
What you'll be doing
Routine Maintenance:
Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing
Diagnose & Troubleshoot Issues:
Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as motors, controllers, and safety devices. Your expertise will be pivotal in delivering swift, accurate solutions
Carry Out Repair Work:
Conduct precise repairs, replacing worn or faulty parts as needed to restore full elevator functionality and reliability.
Maintain Detailed Records:
Keep thorough, accurate records of all maintenance, service, and repair activities, ensuring clear documentation for compliance and future reference.
Ensure Safety & Compliance:
Maintain strict adherence to all safety standards and regulations, upholding safety as a non-negotiable priority in every task.
Provide On-Call Support:
Be prepared to respond to emergency situations as part of our rotational standby roster, ensuring timely support and effective resolution of urgent issues.
Liaise with Clients:
Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.
Continuous Skill Development:
Participate in training events and workshops.
What we're looking for
Positive, proactive attitude
and a commitment to safety and quality service.
Minimum 5 years of experience
in the elevator service industry, with
superior fault-finding abilities
.
Queensland Electrical Licence (no restrictions).
White card.
Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).
Rigging and Dogging certification (favourable but not essential).
Certificate IV in WHS (favourable but not essential).
Must have the right to work in Australia
.
Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.
What we offer
Competitive Remuneration:
Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.
Coastal Lifestyle:
Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most sought-after coastal regions.
Motivating Team Culture:
We believe in creating a positive work environment where your skills are valued, and your career can thrive.
Vehicle and Tools:
The whole kit and caboodle

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician / Electrician – Maintenance & Repairs
jobs.description:
Job Title: Elevator Service Technician  
Company: BOSS Elevators  
Location: Australia  

Job Description:  

About the role:  
We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at BOSS Elevators. You will play a key role in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevators across the Sydney and Central Coast regions.  

You will directly work with clients to provide routine maintenance, repairs, and inspections, while also improving operational efficiency and ensuring compliance with our industry's safety regulations.  

You will also need to communicate effectively with team members, clients, and other stakeholders to provide high-quality service that reflects our commitment to "Leading the Charge."  

What you’ll be doing:  
- Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual, and 5-yearly testing.  
- Technical Support: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components and equipment, including controls, electronics, and hydraulic systems.  
- Safety and Compliance: Maintain high standards of compliance and safety regulations.  
- Conduct inspections: Provide technical support to clients, ensuring lifts are in operational condition to prevent breakdowns.  
- Collaborate and communicate: Work closely with coworkers and stakeholders to ensure prompt and effective resolution of issues.  

What we’re looking for:  
- Flexible, proactive attitude towards safety and quality service.  
- Minimum 3 years of experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.  
- Qualified Electrical License preferred.  
- Knowledge of relevant Australian standards (preferable but not essential).  
- Current Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).  
- Certification in CPR and First Aid (favourable but not essential).  
- Clean driving record and driver's license.  

What we offer:  
- Competitive Remuneration based on your experience and qualifications.  
- Lifestyle flexibility: Be part of a team that genuinely cares about work-life balance.  
- You’ll be joining an industry leader, with one of the largest footprints in the country and a strong backing to support your development.  

Please apply if you meet the above criteria and are keen to be a part of a team committed to excellence in the elevator industry.

jobs.title: Construction Technicians
jobs.description:
Electrical Technician  
Marble Group – Sydney NSW  
$145,000 - $145,000 a year  
Permanent  
3mo ago, from Marble Group  

Apply on company site  

The opportunity and why apply  
This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
• $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
• Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)  
• OEM Training and development on offer to become an expert in your field.  
• Working in a technical and challenging role on automated stacker cranes  

The company  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
• Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
• People focused management team with a strong team orientated culture  
• Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities  

Roster and hours  
You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

Roster and hours

You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

- 12-hour shifts with a paid lunch and smoko
- Day shift: 6am - 6pm
- Night shift: 6pm - 6am
- Occasional overtime available on the end of your swing for those looking for more hours.

The role

As a highly skilled electrician, you’ll be responsible for all electrical maintenance and repair at one of Australia’s biggest logistic parks. All of your work will be completed at the well-equipped site.

- Electrical and mechanical fault finding, preventative maintenance and emergency repair work on a range of rail mounted automated stacker cranes
- Work on a range of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives and crane management software
- Working on variable speed drives, control logic on wiring diagrams, and off grid generator systems.

Your value

You will be a trade qualified electrician with a well-rounded knowledge of electrical fault finding and repairs on mobile plant and/or cranes.

- Electrical trade qualification (Cert III Electrotechnology)
- Hydraulic and mechanical knowledge (highly regarded)
- An excellent attitude towards work with proven ability to work both autonomously and in a team environment.
- Working knowledge and use of electrical schematics and symbols
- Must demonstrate working knowledge of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives
- Be able to pass a pre-employment medical and national police check

jobs.description:
Lift Service Technician

Schindler Lifts – Canberra ACT  
3.2 from 34 reviews at SEEK  
Full time  
19d ago, from seek.com.au

WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential by careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.

About the role:

We are seeking an experienced Lift & Escalator Technicians to join our Service Team in Canberra. Reporting to the Service Manager, you will be responsible for minimising breakdowns and ensuring that all lifts and escalators perform to customer expectations.

This role covers both our lift and escalator products, we are looking for an individual who preferably has demonstrated experience in dealing with the maintenance and service of both lifts and escalators.

Benefits and Perks:

- Highly competitive package - EBA
- Permanent position
- A supportive team.

- Permanent position

- A supportive team.

- Fitness passport with access to over 1,700 fitness facilities across Australia
- A company that promotes from within

Key Accountabilities:

- Carrying out regular maintenance

- Carrying out repair work to restore lift/escalator to full service

- Answer all calls promptly and provide an accurate ETA, in accordance with response terms

- In accordance with established procedures shut down a lift or escalator which is regarded as unsafe and unable to be made to function properly

- Propose material alterations where it is felt the performance of the escalator can be improved by doing so

- Depending on work requirements there is some flexibility required to be available to work overtime or on weekends.

Requirements for the role:

- Electrical Trade qualification

- A current electrical license

- A current driver’s license

- Excellent interpersonal, communication skills and customer service etiquette

- Excellent organisation skills, with the ability to work under pressure

- An uncompromising commitment to quality workmanship and safety policies and procedures

About us:

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

Further Information & how to apply:

jobs.title: Construction Technicians
jobs.description:
Electrical Technician  
Marble Group – Sydney NSW  
$145,000 - $146,000 a year  
Permanent  
2mo ago, from Marble Group  

The opportunity and why apply  
This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
- $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
- Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)  
- OEM Training and development on offer to become an expert in your field.  
- Working in a technical and challenging role on automated stacker cranes  

The company  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
- Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
- People focused management team with a strong team orientated culture  
- Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities  

Roster and hours  
You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

Roster and hours

You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

- 12-hour shifts with a paid lunch and smoko
- Day shift: 6am - 6pm
- Night shift: 6pm - 6am
- Occasional overtime available on the end of your swing for those looking for more hours.

The role

As a highly skilled electrician, you'll be responsible for all electrical maintenance and repair at one of Australia’s biggest logistic parks. All of your work will be completed at the well-equipped site.

- Electrical and mechanical fault finding, preventative maintenance and emergency repair work on a range of rail mounted automated stacker cranes
- Work on a range of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives and crane management software
- Working on variable speed drives, control logic on wiring diagrams, and off grid generator systems.

Your value

You will be a trade qualified electrician with a well-rounded knowledge of electrical fault finding and repairs on mobile plant and/or cranes.

- Electrical trade qualification (Cert III Electrotechnology)
- Hydraulic and mechanical knowledge (highly regarded)
- An excellent attitude towards work with proven ability to work both autonomously and in a team environment.
- Working knowledge and use of electrical schematics and symbols
- Must demonstrate working knowledge of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives
- Be able to pass a pre-employment medical and national police check

This is a fantastic opportunity to secure a long-term permanent position. Apply now for more information or call Matt Grainger on 0448 551 ***

In the past 5 years we have been extremely humbled to have placed over 14,000 people into meaningful employment. This equates to 21 lives enhanced every day!

Matt Grainger | *********@marble.com.au | 02 8116 2*** 

By submitting your CV you agree to have read: marble.com.au/privacy

$110,000 - $115,000 a year Full time, Permanent

28d ago, from Astrum Recruitment

Local Representative - Newcastle & Forster Region  
Location: Newcastle & Forster, NSW  
Position Type: Permanent  
Reporting To: Service Branch Manager, Newcastle NSW  
Package: $110k-$115k + Super + Car Allowance + Bonus  
Vehicle: Fully maintained company vehicle and fuel card provided  

About the Role:  
We are seeking an experienced Electrician/ Lift Technician to take on a Local Area Representative role within a global lift company. This role will cover the Newcastle and Forster regions.

In this role, you will oversee field operations, customer relationships, maintenance and business development initiatives across your designated area.

With a balance of field and office responsibilities 85% Field work on the tools 15% Office split, you'll play a key role in driving operational efficiency, safety compliance, and service excellence.

Key Responsibilities Field-Based Duties 85%:  
- Reduce call-outs through proactive asset management and scheduled maintenance on vertical transport, lifts, elevators etc.  
- Achieve maintenance targets in accordance with contract requirements.  
- Minimise equipment downtime through timely repair coordination and estimate submissions to Sales.  

- Maintain regular work with customers and subcontractors.

Achieve maintenance targets in accordance with contract requirements.

Minimise equipment downtime through timely repair coordination and estimate submissions to Sales.

Manage repair works with customers and subcontractors.

Build trusted customer relationships to support retention and growth.

Conduct field audits and ensure subcontractor compliance with safety standards.

On Call Work every other weekend.

Office-Based Duties 15%:
- Manage routine maintenance scheduling and meet contractual obligations.
- Monitor repair budgets and support achievement of new business targets.
- Oversee procurement and stock control of parts and equipment.
- Respond to customer queries and concerns in a prompt, solution-focused manner.
- Attend customer meetings and provide technical input.
- Assist with tenders, modernisation projects, and service-related sales opportunities.
- Provide regular reporting and identify process improvement opportunities.

What You'll Need
- NSW Electrical License (mandatory).
- Trade qualification as an Electrician/Fitter/Mechanic. Additional studies in Electronics are highly regarded.
- Lift Experience an understanding in vertical transportation.
- IT proficiency and team leadership experience.
- Valid driver's license and clean driving record.
- Commercial mindset and ability to collaborate with Sales teams.
- Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
- Proven ability to manage end-to-end projects and meet KPIs.
- Team-oriented with a proactive and positive attitude.

Why Join Us
- Competitive salary package + quarterly bonuses.

- 12 Annual Rostered Days Off (RDOs).

The Company  
Going Up is one of Australia's largest independent, Australian-owned elevator companies — proudly manufacturing, installing, servicing, and modernising lifts along the east coast.  

With a strong pipeline of projects and a great team culture, we're expanding our Sydney installation team and looking for Lift Installers — including qualified Electricians, Mechanics, or Fitters who want to move into a specialised, long-term career in the elevator and lift industry.  

Whether you’re an experienced tradesperson or ready to transition into lift installation, Going Up provides a supportive team, structured training, and a strong Sydney project pipeline. Join a smaller, Australian-owned company that values its people.  

Our worksites are located across Sydney, with our office based in Brookvale.  

The Role  
As a Lift Installer, you'll be involved in the full installation process — assembling, wiring and fitting out new lift systems from start to finish. You'll work alongside experienced technicians, developing skills in one of Australia's most stable and rewarding trades.  

Key Responsibilities  
- Install and fit out lift systems on a variety of projects  
- Learn from experienced supervisors and colleagues  
- Support testing, commissioning, and site handovers  
- Maintain safety and quality standards  

About You  
- Trade qualified or experienced in electrical, mechanical or construction work.  
- Mechanically or electrically minded, with a strong interest in technical hands-on work.  
- Reliable, organised and a strong team player.

service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units
worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.
We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest

jobs.description:
Expressions of Interest - Apprentice Program 2027

Schindler Lifts Australia

Brisbane, Queensland

Register your interest for our Apprentice Program 2027. In August 2026 we will reach out with the application links.

WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on identifying potential through careful selection and constant development. We offer the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally. We work with a diverse range of people. We offer job stability to our employees and we are excited about the trajectory of our business in the coming years.

About the role:

Our apprentices are trained in all the skills required to service lifts, escalators and moving walkways. If you join our program you will rotate across our business to help you decide what you want to specialise in after completing your apprenticeship.

As an apprentice, you will get to work closely with our customers and develop exceptional customer service skills. One of the many bonuses is that we will provide you with the chance to work full-time and study at TAFE / Trade School to receive your trade qualification in Electrotechnology Certificate III.

What’s in it for you:

- An indentured apprenticeship - 4 years
- Career in a fast-growing electrical industry
- 9-day fortnight (RDO)

We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
• $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
• Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)

The company  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
• Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
• People focused management team with a strong team orientated culture  
• Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities

You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

Roster and hours

- 12-hour shifts with a paid lunch and smoko
- Day shift: 6am - 6pm
- Night shift: 6pm - 6am

The role

As a highly skilled electrician, you'll be responsible for all electrical maintenance and repair at one of Australia's biggest logistic parks. All of your work will be completed at the well-equipped site.

- Electrical and mechanical fault finding, preventative maintenance and emergency repair work on a range of rail mounted automated stacker cranes
- Work on a range of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives and crane management software
- Working on variable speed drives, control logic on wiring diagrams, and off grid generator systems.

Your value

You will be a trade qualified electrician with a well-rounded knowledge of electrical fault finding and repairs on mobile plant and/or cranes.

- Electrical trade qualification (Cert III Electrotechnology)

jobs.description:
Sales and Key Account Representative

Otis Elevator Company  
Mascot New South Wales  

**Date Posted:**  
2026-02-22  

**Country:**  
Australia  

**Location:**  
LOC7038 : 9 & 13/4 Bronti Street, Mascot NSW 2019  

Come and join Electra today. Electra Lift is an elevator company who have been in operation for over 45 years.  

We specialize in Lift Maintenance, Modernizations, Replacements and New Installations. We maintain lifts all across Sydney and have a well-structured team to support our continued growth and expansion with our main office located in Mascot, NSW.  

As we continue to expand, we are seeking an experienced **Sales & Key Account Representative** to join our growing team based in **Mascot, NSW.**  

+ 12 months fixed term contract

**Reports to the Operations Manager**

**About You**

As a **Sales & Key Account Representative** you will work closely with our Key Accounts Team to quote urgent lift repairs/minor upgrades, quote proactive equipment improvements and support the administrative tasks required with our key account portals.

**Key Responsibilities**

+ **Deliver revenue performance:** Consistently achieve (and exceed) assigned sales revenue and margin targets in line with the approved business plan, working collaboratively to ensure customer satisfaction.

+ **Maximise speed-to-lead and conversion:** Accelerate the sales process from receipt, applying disciplined follow-up to maximise conversion.

+ **Qualify needs and build demand:** Partner with the customer to understand their needs, then engage the customer to validate needs, create urgency and progress the opportunity through the lifecycle.

+ **Drive campaign-led pipeline:** Actively participate in key marketing initiatives to drive contribution and supporting the Key Account team with pricing and commercial support.

+ **Own proposal development end-to-end:** Progress submissions through various approvals to create a compliant Service Works Proposal in timely manner.

+ **Execute a structured close plan:** Proactively follow up every submission using a structured, cyclical call-closing cadence to progress objections, confirm decision steps, and secure approval/booking.

+ **Build and maintain a robust pipeline:** Maintain a healthy opportunity funnel that supports target attainment, including ongoing prospecting, opportunity progression, and clear next steps at every stage.

+ **Forecast with accuracy:** Keep CRM/sales systems current, report activity consistently, and provide accurate forecasting against plan (pipeline, probability, timing, value).

**What you will need to be successful**

**Sales and Key Account Representative jobs in Mascot**

+ Minimum 2-3 years' experience in Tele Sales, Frontline Sales, or Account Management, with a results-driven mindset (Preferred).

+ Exposure to technical or solution-based sales would be an added advantage

+ Proven ability to work independently and collaborate cross-functionally, building trusted relationships to drive outcomes.

+ Strong communications - verbal and written.

+ Attention to detail and accurate record-keeping skills.

+ Sound knowledge of Microsoft 365 and the ability to use various systems/databases is required

+ A desire to learn and understand the elevator industry.

Sounds like a great role? Apply Today!

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a hand on your career journey.

Otis is the world's leading elevator and escalator manufacturer. We move 2.4 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.1 million elevators around the world's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world's most iconic buildings, like the Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company.

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You'll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you'll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge.

+ Performs either Installation or Maintenance work and refers to relevant service bulletins, articles, and operations manuals.

+ Stand by and provide after-call-back coverage as required.

+ Identify process improvement opportunities and actions to enhance customer satisfaction.

+ Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices.

+ Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs to vertical transportation systems, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.

+ Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with relevant Australian Standards, including AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and/or agreement.

+ Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

**Key working relationships - Internally and Externally**

+ Work closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

+ Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.

+ Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.

**What You Will Need To Be Successful**

+ Have an Electrical license or a Cert III in Electrotechnology

+ Hold a valid driver's license.

+ Minimum 3-5 years' experience within the elevator industry.

+ Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge with the ability to knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

+ Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer of engineering principles with demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills.

+ Good English language skills, both verbal and written.

+ Intermediate knowledge and experience using the Microsoft Office Suite, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, etc.).

+ A committed professional, with safety as priority.

+ Has attention to detail and is results-driven, continuously seeking improvement.

**Benefits**

+ You will receive a long-term employment contract with the world market leader in a crisis-proof industry.

+ We offer you remuneration in accordance with the local EBA and you will be working 36 hours weekly.

+ We will train you intensively in the areas of technology, processes & soft skills and you can exchange ideas with experienced colleagues at any time.

+ You will receive notice, new work clothes, your own iPhone and a tool of trade.

WHY join Schindler?
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their
full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their
area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and
professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our
employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our
business in the coming years.
About the role:
We have an opportunity has arisen for an experienced Service Manager to join our team based
in Perth. Reporting directly to the area Operations Manager, you will have approximately 10
direct reports and P&L responsibilities. The role is office based as well as spending time in the
field with technician direct reports and attending customer meetings. You will also be expected

As the successful candidate, you play a crucial role in our business as you are often the first point
of contact for our customers, to deliver responsive and high-quality services and solutions. You
will also be responsible for providing coaching, mentoring and leadership to your team of service
technicians driving open communication and a focus on a safety culture.

3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK

3d ago, from seek.com.au
Apply on company site

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers! At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. 

Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights. 

We are looking for Construction Technicians to be based in and around Adelaide, South Australia.

About you 

You will be the face of the OTIS brand and responsible for installing lifts, escalators, and travelators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures. 

This permanent role will be reporting to the Project Manager and working closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters & Field Engineers as well as Construction Coordinators. 

Permanent role

Reports to the Construction Project Manager

Key Responsibilities 

Install and Modernise lifts, escalators, and travelators and work to the highest safety and environmental standards while meeting service targets. 

Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in coordination with the Project Manager. 

Occasional travel to regional Adelaide.

Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes. 

Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property. 

Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards and maintain and work to the Quality System. 

Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures, including reporting any conditions that may impact on the environment or any employee. 

What you will need to be successful 

Have a Electrical trade qualification or equivalent.

Min 5 years work experience as a Construction Technician.

Have Working at Heights, Rigging and Dogging experience.

Hold a valid driver's license

Be available for occasional travel to regional Adelaide and able to work unsupervised. 

Enjoys communicating with various stakeholders at different levels and working closely with others in the field. 

Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training. 

Able to plan and prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner. 

Key Benefits

Industry-leading compensation and benefits. 

Be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers.

Meaningful career opportunities for people to grow through excellent Learning programs.

Becoming part of the largest vertical transportation company in the world and gaining exposure to the benefits and opportunities offered.

About us

We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other protected class according to applicable law. Otis are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive place to work. We strongly encourage applications from women, and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people

For more information: https://www.otis.com/en/au/our-company/diversity-and-inclusion

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician
jobs.description:
What
Where
Search jobs
Elevator Service Technician
BOSS Elevators
–
Birtinya QLD
Full time
23h ago, from seek.com.au
Apply on company site

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will play a crucial part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sunshine Coast to Discovery Coast region.

As an expert technician, you’ll be responsible for conducting routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs, with a focus on quickly diagnosing and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator components. Your ability to think critically and implement effective solutions will be essential to ensuring optimal functionality and compliance with AS1735 standards.

You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to "Leading the Change" in elevator maintenance.

This role is suited for someone who is not only technically skilled but also values quality workmanship, safety, and client satisfaction. If you take pride in overcoming challenges and delivering high-quality results, this could be the perfect opportunity for you. 

What you'll be doing

Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing

Diagnose & Troubleshoot Issues: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as motors, controllers, and safety devices. Your expertise will be pivotal in delivering swift, accurate solutions

Carry Out Repair Work: Conduct precise repairs, replacing worn or faulty parts as needed to restore full elevator functionality and reliability.

Maintain Detailed Records: Keep thorough, accurate records of all maintenance, service, and repair activities, ensuring clear documentation for compliance and future reference.

Ensure Safety & Compliance: Maintain strict adherence to all safety standards and regulations, upholding safety as a non-negotiable priority in every task.

Provide On-Call Support: Be prepared to respond to emergency situations as part of our rotational standby roster, ensuring timely support and effective resolution of urgent issues.

Liaise with Clients: Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.

Continuous Skill Development: Participate in training events and workshops. 

What we're looking for

Positive, proactive attitude and a commitment to safety and quality service.

Minimum 5 years of experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.

Queensland Electrical Licence (no restrictions).

White card.

Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).

Rigging and Dogging certification (favourable but not essential).

Certificate IV in WHS (favourable but not essential).

Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer

Competitive Remuneration: Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications. 

Coastal Lifestyle: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most sought-after coastal regions.

Motivating Team Culture: We believe in creating a positive work environment where your skills are valued, and your career can thrive.

Vehicle and Tools: The whole kit and caboodle 

Application Process

Please submit your resume, outlining your experience, qualifications, and referees, by 15 March 2026

Applications must also include a cover letter explaining why you are an ideal candidate for this role.

Shortlisted applicants will be contacted by 15 March 2026.

Interviews will be completed by 20 March 2026, with an employment offer extended to the successful candidate shortly thereafter.

Stand by and provide after-call-back coverage as needed.

Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to meet customer satisfaction.

Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and subcontractors.

Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.

Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and /or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement.

Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally

Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.

Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.

Sales Team: Sales Account Representatives, Managers, and Coordinators (where relevant).

External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers.​

What You Will Need To Be Successful

Have an Electrical license or a Cert III in Electrotechnology

Hold a valid driver's license.

Minimum five (5) years’ experience within the Lift Industry.

Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training and demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills.

Good English language skills, both verbal and written

Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet).

A committed professional, with safety as priority

Has attention to detail and is results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!.

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.

Otis is the world’s leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2.4 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.4 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company.

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You’ll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you’ll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge. 

We provide opportunities, training, and resources, that build leadership and capabilities in Sales, Field, Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Scholar Program is a notable point of pride, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certification programs.

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people – passengers, customers, and colleagues – at the center of everything we do. We are guided by our values that we call our Three Absolutes – prioritizing Safety, Ethics, Quality in all that we do. If you would like to learn more about environmental, social and governance (ESG) at Otis click here.

Become a part of the Otis team and help us #Buildwhatsnext!

jobs.title: Branch Administrator - VIC
jobs.description:
WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years

At Schindler, We Elevate...

As a leading employer in the urban mobility industry, we look for highly engaging professionals who are driven to grow and advance their careers at a first-class organisation. Our goal is to have a highly motivated and engaged workforce led by talented management in a work environment that is stimulating and attractive to both current and prospective employees.

About the role:

As our Administrator, you will be responsible for timesheet management, rosters, ordering PPE, handling customer enquiries, and supporting field staff with a range of tasks, including technological issues and a vast range of other administrative duties. The role is a fast-paced, highly reactive role that reports to an experienced Branch Support Lead and will see you working closely with our team of skilled administrators. Our Melbourne team is based in Port Melbourne. You will liaise with key stakeholders and customers to ensure a high level of customer service is provided.

Previous experience working as a Service Coordinator, Service Administrator, Branch Administrator or similar in an industry such as Lift, HVAC, Fire, Security, Electrical, Mechanical or Plumbing will be highly regarded

What’s in it for you:

$72K base + 12% Super
5% annual Bonus
Excellent opportunity for you to expand your experience at world-leading Lift company
Permanent full-time role
A work/life balance organisation - culture-first approach
A great learning environment with a local and global team
Access to the Employee Benefits Discount Scheme
A wide variety of social activities – even a day off for your birthday!
Fitness passport with access to over 1,700 fitness facilities across Australia
Office Based in - Port Melbourne

Key Accountabilities:

Ensuring all work orders are captured in the system
Organising customer inductions and medicals for field staff
Updating rosters
Handling customer enquiries and escalating when required
Raising purchase requests for uniforms, stationary, parts, labour etc.
Providing customers with periodic performance reports
Maintaining and reviewing timesheets for payroll
Customer invoicing
Adhoc administrative tasks

What we are looking for:

Previous experience in an administration role, preferably within a service-based industry
Demonstrated customer service or service coordinator experience
Strong attention to detail and an ability to multitask multiple priorities
Intermediate MS office skills
Experience with SAP or similar ERP systems
Good written and verbal communication skills and the ability to work in a team
A desire for growth and to expand your knowledge and skillset

About Schindler:

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

HOW to apply:

We are constantly looking for passionate and enthusiastic people with the energy and drive to build relationships and networks. At Schindler, you will find a highly motivated and engaged working environment that lets you grow as an individual and allows you to plan your career.

If you are seeking an opportunity to join a Global Industry Leader and believe that you possess the required skills and qualifications to succeed in this role, click on the "" button

If you are an existing Schindler employee, please ensure you have discussed your application with your line manager prior to applying.

We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply. Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a manager.

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician – Sydney (Hands-On & Training)
jobs.description:
Title: Elevator Service Technician  
Company: DOSS Elevators  
Location: Not specified in the provided text.  

Job Description:  
About the role  
We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at DOSS Elevators. In this role, you will assist in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sydney and Country Coast region.  

You will work directly with clients to provide routine maintenance, repairs, and inspections, which factors heavily on quality assurance and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator systems. Your responsibilities will also include demonstrating your technical proficiency and commitment to customer satisfaction.

What you’ll be doing  
- Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual, and 5-year testing.  
- Maintenance & Technical Issues: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as electrical works, doors, and control systems. Experience in bush and urban settings is valuable.  
- Conduct Site Audit: Conduct periodic inspections, ensure communication, and deliver professional documentation, while working both independently and as part of a team.  
- Effectively Communicate: Liaise with clients regarding emergency procedures, ongoing maintenance, and repairs, while maintaining adherence to compliance and safety standards.  
- Elevators Safety & Compliance: Maintain high standards of safety and compliance and understand the latest industry regulations and technology.  

What we’re looking for  
- Flexible, proactive attitude: High levels of integrity and quality service.  
- Minimum 5 years’ experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault finding abilities.  
- Qualified Electrical License (restricted).  
- Trade Certificate: Must hold a recognised qualification.  
- Working at heights certification (favorable but not essential).  
- First Aid certificate (favorable but not essential).  
- Current Driver's License: Must have a valid license.  
- Customer-centric mindset: An ability to work effectively with clients.  

Other Info:  
- Competitive Remuneration: Our pay will be determined based on your experience and qualifications.  
- Cultural Lifestyle: Step into a great culture and enjoy living in one of the best locations in Australia.  

Please note: No further details about the application process or deadlines have been provided in the text.

Lift Maintenance / Installation / Electrician - Residential Lift Industry (SYD)

Calling all experienced Lift Installers & Maintenance Technicians looking for direct employment or subcontracting opportunities in Sydney!

About Us

We’re Australia’s largest premium home lift specialist, with over 220 full‑time staff across Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth and Los Angeles. We believe in hiring smart people, trusting them to do their best, and backing them with the tools, systems, and support to thrive. Check us out: www.liftshop.com.au or our USA site www.elevatorboutique.com.

Why Lift Shop?

Join a team, not a machine. Lift Shop isn’t an engine that uses people, it’s a business built by people. Our culture starts with connection.
Grow with us, not just grind for us. Your ambition has a place. Whether you want to lead installs, train others, or build a business, we’ll help you move forward.
You’re not just installing lifts. You’re building Lift Shop. The people on site shape the culture, reflecting who we are and who you are.
About The Role

Installing electric and hydraulic residential lifts to the highest standards.
Performing servicing, fault finding, repairs, and modernisations.
Delivering precise, code‑compliant work with pride.
Communicating professionally with clients and project teams.
Maintaining a safe, clean, and efficient worksite.
Helping shape how we do things, not just following a manual.
The Perks

Weekly pay. No surprises. No delays.
Bonuses that reward speed, precision, and pride.
Perks: access to our in‑house gym, sauna and recreational games area.
10 extra days off each year, because rest fuels quality.
We provide a company vehicle, full tool kit, phone, iPad, PPE, shirts.
A real path forward: leadership, specialisation, team building.
A team that listens. A system that works. A future you can grow with.
About You

Are you a licensed electrician, a lift tech with years of grit, or a talented carpenter ready to step up? You’ll shine here.
Clear communicator, tidy operator, calm under pressure.
White card and driver’s licence essential.
Subbies welcome, must have Public Liability & Workers Comp (if applicable)

If you’ve worked for the big players, you know how it feels to be a cog. This is different – we’ve built the business around you, not over you. We’ll show you exactly how we run things, and why techs stay. Apply now!

Come and join Otis today, and be part of the OTIS Family and Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers

About you
We are seeking a the following mechanics/technicians to be based in Tasmania
- Construction Mechanic - Hobart
- Service Technician - Hobart
- Service Technician - Launceston

In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.
- Permanent roles
- Reports to the Tasmania Branch Manager

Key Responsibilities for You
- Performs either Installation or Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals.
- Stand by and provide after-call-back coverage as needed.
- Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to meet customer satisfaction.
- Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and subcontractors.
- Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.
- Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

What You Will Need To Be Successful

Construction Mechanic and Service Technicians (Hobart and Launceston, ...  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  

What You Will Need To Be Successful  
- Have an Electrical license or a Cert III in Electrotechnology  
- Minimum 3-5 years’ experience within the elevator Industry with extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment  
- Hold a valid driver's license  
- Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer through training and demonstrated fault-finding, problem-solving skills  
- Good English language skills, both verbal and written  
- Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet)  
- A committed professional, with safety as priority  
- Has attention to detail and is results-driven  
- Curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team  

Benefits  
- You will receive a long-term employment contract with the world market leader in a crisis-proof industry.  
- We offer you remuneration in accordance with the local EBA and you will be working 36 hours weekly  
- We will train you intensively in the areas of technology, processes & soft skills and you can exchange ideas with experienced colleagues at any time.  
- You will receive modern and high-quality work clothes, your own tools, an iPhone and a tool of trade (depending on area of coverage)  

Apply Now and help us build what's next  

About us  
We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.  

You can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation

jobs.description:
What
Where
