Lift Maintenance / Installation / Electrician - Residential Lift Industry (SYD)
Lift Shop | Elevator Boutique
–
City of Sydney NSW
Full time
4d ago, from Talent AU
Apply on company site

Whether you want to lead, specialise, or build your own crew, we’ll help you get there. At Lift Shop, you’re not a number – you’re a professional.

We’re not like the corporates who treat you like hired hands. You’re problem‑solvers, partners, and the face of everything we build. We trust your judgement, pay you on time, and give you the space to do great work by giving back your autonomy.

About Us

We’re Australia’s largest premium home lift specialist, with over 220 full‑time staff across Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth and Los Angeles. But size isn’t the story, respect is. We believe in hiring smart people, trusting them to do their best, and backing them with the tools, systems, and support to thrive.

Check us out: www.liftshop.com.au or our USA site www.elevatorboutique.com

Why Lift Shop?

Join a team, not a machine. Lift Shop isn’t an engine that uses people, it’s a business built by people. Our culture starts with connection.
Grow with us, not just grind for us. At Lift Shop, your ambition has a place. Whether you want to lead installs, train others, or build a business, we’ll help you move forward.
You’re not just installing lifts. You’re building Lift Shop. Here, the people on site shape the culture, not just the people in head office. Every lift you build reflects who we are, and who you are.
About the Role

Installing electric and hydraulic residential lifts to the highest standards.
Performing servicing, fault finding, repairs, and modernisations.
Delivering precise, code‑compliant work with pride.
Communicating professionally with clients and project teams.
Maintaining safe, clean, and efficient worksites.
Helping shape how we do things, not just following a manual.
The Perks

Weekly pay. No surprises. No delays.
Bonuses that reward speed, precision, and pride.
Perks: access to our in‑house gym, sauna and recreational games area.
10 extra days off each year, because rest fuels quality.
We provide a company vehicle, full tool kit, phone, iPad, PPE, shirts.
A real path forward: leadership, specialisation, team building.
A team that listens. A system that works. A future you can grow with.
About You

Are you a licensed electrician, a lift tech with years of grit, or a talented carpenter ready to step up? You’ll shine here.
Clear communicator, tidy operator, calm under pressure.
White card and driver’s licence essential.
Subbies welcome, must have Public Liability & Workers Comp (if applicable).

If you’ve worked for the big players, you know how it feels to be a cog. This is different, as we’ve built the business around you, not over you.

We’ll show you exactly how we run things, and why techs stay. Apply now!

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers!

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

About you  
We are looking for a Service Technician to based in Darwin, Northern Territory. In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.  
- Permanent role  
- Reports to the Branch Manager  

Key responsibilities for YOU  
- Performs Service and Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals.  
- Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers.  
- Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.  
- Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.  
- Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and /or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement.  
- Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.  
- Standby and provide after-call-back coverage from time to time.  

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally  
- Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

Elevator Service Technician  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally  

- Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.  
- Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.  
- Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.  
- Sales Team: Sales Account Representatives, Managers, and Coordinators (where relevant).  
- External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers.  

What YOU will need to be successful  

- Have Electrical and/or Fitter qualifications as relevant to the role and Lift apprenticeship or other industry-accredited training program (desirable)  
- Minimum two (2) to five (5) years’ experience within the Lift Industry.  
- Hold a valid driver's license and Electrical license.  
- Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.  
- Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training and demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills.  
- Strong English language skills, both verbal and written  
- Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet).  
- You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority  
- Has attention to detail and is results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team  

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!  

We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.  

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.  

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician – CBD Brisbane
jobs.description:
Job Title: Elevator Service Technician

Company: BOSS Elevators

Location: Australia

About the Role:
We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving in our customer-focused BOSS Elevators team. This role is critical in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sydney and Sydney Coast region.

Responsibilities:
- You will directly work with clients responsible for providing routine maintenance, repairs, and fixes that focus on quality assurance and monitoring systems that ensure safe elevator operations.
- Conduct scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual, and 5-year testing.
- Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as automatic doors, electronic systems, and hydraulics.
- Plan your day effectively and communicate with clients regarding schedules, providing excellent customer service.
- Troubleshoot electrical and mechanical issues and make adjustments or repairs as necessary.
- Maintain detailed records for thorough documentation and compliance for audits.
- Understand Safety & Compliance: maintain high standards in compliance with industry regulations and safety requirements.
- Link with clients: be responsive and provide clear communication to empower clients regarding their elevator systems and services.
- Provide high-quality service with a focus on customer satisfaction.
- Commit to professional development to maintain and enhance skills.

Requirements:
- Familiarity with the elevator industry and quality services.
- Minimum 5 years’ experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.
- Qualified Electrical License (restricted).
- White Card.
- Working at heights certification (favorable but not essential).
- Current Driver’s License.
- Certificate in Work (favorable but not essential).
- Competitive Remuneration based on your experience and qualifications.
- Lifestyle flexibility: great work-life balance with the option to work in one of the most beautiful locations in Australia.

jobs.title: Construction Mechanic/Electrician (Casual)
jobs.description:
**Date Posted:**

2025-09-30

**Country:**

Australia

**Location:**

LOC7013 : 2-12 Montague Street, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the TIME 2025 World's Best Employers! At Otis, it's our people that make us different.

Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

We are looking for **Lift Electricians** and **Mechanics** to be based in Melbourne, Victoria.

+ Casual role based on project demands

+ Classification and hourly rate per the latest EBA

+ Opportunity to work on prime projects

**About you**

You will be the face of the OTIS brand and responsible for installing lifts, escalators, and travelators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures.

This role will be reporting to the Project Manager and working closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters & Field Engineers, as well as Construction Coordinators.

**Key Responsibilities**

+ Install and Modernise lifts, escalators, and travelators and work to the highest safety and environmental standards while meeting service targets.

+ Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in coordination with the Project Manager.

+ Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes.

+ Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property.

+ Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards and maintain and work to the Quality System.

+ Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures, including reporting any conditions that may impact on the environment or any employee.

**What you will need to be successful**

+ Have a Fitting/Electrical/Mechanical trade qualification or equivalent experience _._

+ Hold a valid drivers' license

+ Enjoys communicating with various stakeholders at different levels and working closely with others in the field.

+ Solutions-oriented, independent, and well-organized.

+ Willing to travel in & out of the region and able to work unsupervised.

+ Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

+ Able to plan and prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner.

**Apply today and help us build what's next!**

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.

Otis is the world's leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2.4 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.4 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world's most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company.

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You'll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you'll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge.?

We provide opportunities, training, and resources, that build leadership and capabilities in Sales, Field, Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Scholar Program is a notable point of pride, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certification programs.

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people - passengers, customers, and colleagues - at the center of everything we do. We are guided by our values that we call our Three Absolutes - prioritizing Safety, Ethics, Quality in all that we do. If you would like to learn more about environmental, social and governance (ESG) at Otis click here (https://www.otis.com/en/us/our-company/esg) .

Become a part of the Otis team and help us #Buildwhatsnext!

_Otis is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other protected class according to applicable law. To request an accommodation in completing an employment application due to a special need or a disability, please contact us at careers@otis.com._

**Privacy Policy and Terms:**

Click on this link (https://www.otis.com/corporate/privacy-policy/Job-Applicant/) to read the Policy and Terms

We go to great lengths to hire and develop the best people, and offer a supportive environment where employees are motivated and empowered to perform at their full potential. Today, we continue pushing the boundaries of what's possible to thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential by careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.

About the role:

We are seeking an experienced Lift & Escalator Technicians to join our Service Team in Canberra. Reporting to the Service Manager, you will be responsible for minimising breakdowns and ensuring that all lifts and escalators perform to customer expectations.

This role covers both our lift and escalator products, we are looking for an individual who preferably has demonstrated experience in dealing with the maintenance and service of both lifts and escalators. 

Benefits and Perks:

Highly competitive package - EBA

Permanent position

A supportive team.

Fitness passport with access to over 1,700 fitness facilities across Australia
A company that promotes from within 

Key Accountabilities:

Carrying out regular maintenance

Carrying out repair work to restore lift/escalator to full service

Answer all calls promptly and provide an accurate ETA, in accordance with response terms

In accordance with established procedures shut down a lift or escalator which is regarded as unsafe and unable to be made to function properly

Propose material alterations where it is felt the performance of the escalator can be improved by doing so

Depending on work requirements there is some flexibility required to be available to work overtime or on weekends.

Requirements for the role:

Electrical Trade qualification

A current electrical license

A current driver's license

Excellent interpersonal, communication skills and customer service etiquette

Excellent organisation skills, with the ability to work under pressure

An uncompromising commitment to quality workmanship and safety policies and procedures

About us:

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

Further Information & how to apply:

If you are passionate about a career with us, please click on the “Apply” button and complete our online application form.

We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply. Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a manager.

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician (Developing or Experienced)
jobs.description:
Elevator Service Technician

BOSS Elevators – Birtinya QLD

Full time

13d ago, from seek.com.au

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will play a crucial part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sunshine Coast to Discovery Coast region.

As an expert technician, you’ll be responsible for conducting routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs, with a focus on quickly diagnosing and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator components. Your ability to think critically and implement effective solutions will be essential to ensuring optimal functionality and compliance with AS1735 standards.

You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to "Leading the Change" in elevator maintenance.

This role is suited for someone who is not only technically skilled but also values quality workmanship, safety, and client satisfaction. If you take pride in overcoming challenges and delivering high-quality results, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

What you’ll be doing

- Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing.

What you’ll be doing

• Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing

• Diagnose & Troubleshoot Issues: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as motors, controllers, and safety devices. Your expertise will be pivotal in delivering swift, accurate solutions

• Carry Out Repair Work: Conduct precise repairs, replacing worn or faulty parts as needed to restore full elevator functionality and reliability.

• Maintain Detailed Records: Keep thorough, accurate records of all maintenance, service, and repair activities, ensuring clear documentation for compliance and future reference.

• Ensure Safety & Compliance: Maintain strict adherence to all safety standards and regulations, upholding safety as a non-negotiable priority in every task.

• Provide On-Call Support: Be prepared to respond to emergency situations as part of our rotational standby roster, ensuring timely support and effective resolution of urgent issues.

• Liaise with Clients: Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.

• Continuous Skill Development: Participate in training events and workshops.

What we’re looking for

• Positive, proactive attitude and a commitment to safety and quality service.

• Minimum 5 years of experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.

• Queensland Electrical Licence (no restrictions).

• White card.

• Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).

• Rigging and Dogging certification (favourable but not essential).

• Certificate IV in WHS (favourable but not essential).

• Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer

• Competitive Remuneration: Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.

• Coastal Lifestyle: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most

Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer
• Competitive Remuneration: Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.
• Coastal Lifestyle: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most sought-after coastal regions.
• Motivating Team Culture: We believe in creating a positive work environment where your skills are valued, and your career can thrive.
• Vehicle and Tools: The whole kit and caboodle

Application Process
Please submit your resume, outlining your experience, qualifications, and referees, by 15 March 2026

Applications must also include a cover letter explaining why you are an ideal candidate for this role.

Shortlisted applicants will be contacted by 15 March 2026.

Interviews will be completed by 20 March 2026, with an employment offer extended to the successful candidate shortly thereafter.

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician – Sydney (36h, Training & Tools Provided)
jobs.description:
Title: Elevator Service Technician  
Company: BOSS Elevators  
Location: Australia

Responsibilities:  
- Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems, meeting standards for monthly, annual, and five-year testing.  
- Troubleshooting & Problem Solving: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components of elevators, including controllers, motors, and safety devices.  
- Conduct Daily Reports: Conduct daily inspections, prepare communication, and deliver reports thoroughly documenting any findings related to safety, efficiency, or operational standards.  
- Material Deficiency Research: Proactively identify and report issues with maintenance required for compliance and future repairs.  
- Elevators Safety & Compliance: Maintain adherence to all local regulatory requirements and industry standards, ensuring compliance of the elevators under your care.  
- Customer Service: Build and maintain relationships with clients and stakeholders, providing timely, effective solutions that contribute to customer satisfaction.  
- Confidential Staff Relations: Fulfill confidential responsibilities pertaining to internal workplace matters.

Requirements:  
- Experience: Minimum 5 years of experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.  
- Qualifications:  
   - Qualifying Electrical License (restricted) preferred.  
   - Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).  
   - Certificate III in Lift Engineering (favourable but not essential).  
- Reliable: Must possess keen attention to detail, being thorough and precise in work.  
- Adaptable: Must be willing to work independently and as part of a team.  
- Lifestyle Fit: Strong consideration will be given to candidates who reside within or near the area of operation.  

Additional Notes:  
- Competitive Remuneration: Discussed in proportion to experience and qualifications.  
- Call out duties: May be part of the role where necessary.

encourage interstate applicants to apply as assistance with relocation costs is available to the right candidate. City Lift Services
specialises in the areas of service repairs and modernisation of elevator systems within Western Australia. We are a family-owned
business with our head office based in WA. Our steady growth is attributed to superior customer service and a dedicated team with a
strong focus on team support and work life balance. Why City Lift Services? Are you tired of not being able to fulfill your job role
easily?, Make your own decisions? provide input on how things can be made easier to be done? Then this is a great opportunity for
you to join a successful established business in the privately owned sector. Your work will have a direct impact on the business and
will allow you to develop your skills, while you have the support of a solid business foundation and industry experienced team.
Purpose of the position The Lift Mechanic role is an integral role in our team and will be responsible for Servicing and Repairs on
elevators and access solutions within our business. The key focus of the role is to ensure City Lift Services work procedures are
followed and work is carried out safely, effectively, efficiently and ethically within City Lift Services policies and practices. Our
technicians are the face of our business and are the key to our success. Salary and Conditions: The role is a full-time position. This
position will at times require a reasonable amount of overtime and after-hours roster or shift work will be a required part of your roster.
This position requires providing support and training to apprentices. Provision of Company telephone, uniform and vehicle is provided.
The salary is Negotiable to the right candidate and is above award rates with EBA rates being matched. The position will undertake a
performance review according to the position description. Hours Full time - minimum hours required to reflect service team operation
hours 8:00am ? 4:30pm. Key tasks and responsibilities include, but are not limited to the following key areas: Service, repair, and
maintain elevators, escalators, moving sidewalks, disability access equipment and dumbwaiters. Use hand and power tools, testing
devices, welders, workshop equipment such as drill press, cutting equipment and assembly equipment. Locate malfunctions in
brakes, motors, machines and moving automated equipment. Identify and order parts for required jobs. Adjust safety controls,
counterweights, door mechanisms, and components such as valves, ratchets, seals, and brake linings. Inspect door installations and
alignments, clearances of cars and hoist ways, platforms and stairway lifts to ensure that equipment will operate properly.
Disassemble defective units, repair or replace parts such as locks, gears and cables. Maintain electronic and written log books that
detail all repairs and checks performed. Participate in additional training to keep skills up-to-date. Keep workshop/vehicle/sites
organised, clean, functional and safe. To be successful you will: Need to have post trade experience in mechanical fitting/similar roles
Have a strong mechanical aptitude and skills set with attention to detail Have previous experience with machinery fixed/mobile plant
industry Have a professional attitude to customer relationships Have excellent communication both written and oral Have an excellent
business acumen with self-motivation and self-management skills Be able to prioritise activities Experience in maintenance,

jobs.description:
Lift Technician / Electrician  
Frontline Construction – Sydney CBD NSW  
$90,000 - $110,000 a year  
Permanent  
26d ago, from Frontline Construction Australia  

**About the Role**  
An exciting opportunity is available for a skilled Lift Technician or qualified Electrician to join a well-established organisation within the lift and accessibility sector. This role involves the installation, servicing and repair of a diverse range of lift and access systems across residential and commercial environments.

The position offers stable, full-time hours (7:30am - 4:15pm), one RDO per calendar month, optional overtime, and long-term career progression for the right candidate.

**Key Responsibilities**  
- Install, service and repair platform lifts, stair-lifts and accessibility equipment  
- Diagnose and fault-find electrical and mechanical control systems  
- Review and interpret technical plans, drawings and specifications  
- Test, commission and check PLCs and motor control systems  
- Align mechanical components and integrate with electrical switches  
- Ensure all work complies with safety standards and quality expectations  
- Provide professional, customer-focused service on site  

**Key Skills & Experience**  
- Electrical trade qualification (essential)

- Provide professional, customer-focused service on site

**Key Skills & Experience**
- Electrical trade qualification (essential)
- Current electrical licence (preferred)
- Experience in lift technology, PLCs and motor control systems (highly regarded)
- Strong fault-finding and diagnostic capability in control circuits
- Applicants from electrical or metal trades with strong mechanical aptitude are encouraged to apply
- Ability to work autonomously and as part of a collaborative team
- Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
- Commitment to safety, quality and customer satisfaction
- Full training provided for candidates transitioning into the lift sector

**Benefits**
- Competitive salary package based on experience
- Company vehicle included
- Overtime available with penalty rates
- Monthly RDO
- Secure, long-term role with career development opportunities
- Comprehensive training and ongoing support
- Supportive, team-oriented workplace culture

**About the Company**
Our client is a long-standing Australian organisation and a recognised leader in the lift and accessibility sector. They specialise in platform lifts, stair-lifts, residential and commercial elevators, and wheelchair access solutions.

Renowned for their high-quality products and exceptional after-sales service, the business has built a strong reputation for reliability, safety and long-term client relationships. Their continued growth is driven by a people-first culture that values teamwork, professionalism and continuous improvement.

General/Business Unit Manager (CEO & General Management)
Full time
Competitive Base + 12% S + $28K Bonus + $18 Car A.
Posted 9d ago
WHY join Schindler?
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their
full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their
area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and
professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our
employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our
business in the coming years.
About the role:
We have an opportunity has arisen for an experienced Service Manager to join our team based
in Perth. Reporting directly to the area Operations Manager, you will have approximately 10
direct reports and P&L responsibilities. The role is office based as well as spending time in the
field with technician direct reports and attending customer meetings. You will also be expected
to carry out the monthly toolbox meetings and be responsible for driving consistent and reliable
financial performance.
As the successful candidate, you play a crucial role in our business as you are often the first point
of contact for our customers, to deliver responsive and high-quality services and solutions. You
will also be responsible for providing coaching, mentoring and leadership to your team of service
technicians driving open communication and a focus on a safety culture.
 
This package includes:
Highly Competitive base + 12% Super
$28,000 Bonus incentive
$18,000 car allowance
Access to discounted partner company offers.
Funding for further educational support upon completion of 1 year of employment
Birthday day off
Key Accountabilities
Improve operational budgets, including portfolio growth, material and labour cost control.
Constantly analyse results & take effective remedial action quickly.
Visit the site and record contact & other site details
Determine manpower capacity needed to service the contracted portfolio.
Reconcile fulfilment of maintenance visits and act as required
Make regular audits to ensure the quality of maintenance.
Allocate resources to attend to shutdowns
Monitor callback history and allocate resources to attend to sick lifts
Set and attend team meetings.
Identify & plan for Service training needs.
Document and solve customer complaints.
Requirements for the role:
Ideally past experience as a Maintenance/ Repairs/ Service Manager working in
the Lift industry or within Fire, HVAC, Security or a similar industry.
Previous experience managing Field Technicians
Qualification in building, engineering, electrical or mechanical disciplines will be looked upon
favourably
Proven experience in driving business growth and minimizing labour costs
Strong organization and delegation skills, with proven ability to coordinate staff productivity.
Exceptional communication skills
Strong awareness of safety ideally with a qualification
Past experience forecasting, budgeting and contract administration 
About Schindler?
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the
world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the
largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing
over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for
almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology
and innovative people.
How to apply:
If you are seeking an opportunity to join a Global Industry Leader and believe that you possess
the required skills and qualifications to succeed in this role, click on the "Apply" button and
complete our online application form.
We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait
Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply.
Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes
those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a
manager.

jobs.title: Electrician (General)
jobs.location: Australia, Western Australia, Perth Region, Perth, Perth CBD
jobs.description:
Job Info

Location: NEDLANDS, WA

Salary: Award

Job Type: Permanent position | Full time position

Job Description

About the Role: We are currently seeking a qualified and dependable Electrician (General) to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for installing, maintaining, testing, and repairing a variety of electrical systems and equipment across commercial, industrial, and domestic settings.

This position requires a licensed professional with a strong understanding of electrical safety standards and installation protocols. Key Responsibilities: Interpret electrical blueprints, wiring diagrams, and technical specifications to determine installation requirements

Measure and mark reference points for wiring and equipment installation Select, cut, and connect electrical wires and cables to panels, switchboards, and systems Use electrical testing equipment to trace faults, diagnose malfunctions, and verify system performance

Repair or replace defective wiring, components, and electrical systems as needed Install and position electrical switchboards, control panels, and other related systems Connect electrical networks and systems to the main power supply in accordance with safety codes

Test the continuity of circuits and grounding to ensure safe operation Install, test, and commission solar photovoltaic (PV) systems in line with standards Service and maintain electric and mechanical components of lifts and related equipment

Preferred Candidate Profile: AQF Certificate III in Electrotechnology Electrician or equivalent Licensed and registered to perform electrical work as required by relevant regulatory authorities

Minimum of 2 years of relevant on-the-job experience post-certification Proficient in using diagnostic tools and interpreting test results Strong understanding of electrical codes, safety standards, and compliance requirements

Ability to work independently or as part of a team in a fast-paced environment Strong troubleshooting skills and attention to detail Pay: A$70,000.00 - A$120,000.00 per year

jobs.title: Installation Mechanics based in Brisbane/Gold Coast
jobs.description:
**Date Posted:**

2025-11-24

**Country:**

Australia

**Location:**

LOC7060: Level 4, 19 Lang Parade, Milton, QLD 4064

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the TIME 2025 World's Best Employers!

At Otis, it's our people that make us different. Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

We are currently looking for **Lift Installation Mechanics** to be based in **Brisbane or Gold Coast, Queensland.**

**About you**

You will be the face of the Otis brand and responsible for installing Lifts, Escalators, and Travellators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures.

+ 12 months fixed-term contract

+ Reports to the Project Manager

+ This role falls under the local EBA, and your hourly rates are determined by your classification.

**Key Responsibilities**

+ Installation of New Equipment and Modernisation lifts and working to the highest safety and environmental standards while ensuring service targets are met.

+ Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in coordination with the Project Manager.

+ Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property.

+ Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards.

+ Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures, including reporting any conditions that may impact the environment or any employee.

+ Maintain and work on the Quality System.

+ Attend customer handing over inspection/service inspections on installation and modernization projects.

+ Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes.

**What you will need to be successful**

+ Experience in Adjustment / Commissioning work would be beneficial.

+ Working with heights, Basic Rigging and hold a valid driver's license

+ Hold full working rights in Australia

+ Has relevant Lift experience and proven ability to work safely as part of a team or independently, depending on requirements.

+ Enjoys communicating with various levels of stakeholders and working closely with others in the field.

+ Solutions-oriented, independent, and well organized.

+ Willing to travel in & out of the region and able to work unsupervised.

+ Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

+ Able to plan and prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner.

**Key Benefits**

+ Opportunity to work on projects of a wide spectrum.

+ Classification and hourly rate per the latest EBA

+ Opportunity to work on latest technology with the industry leader with highest safety and quality standards.

**?Apply today to join us and build what's next!.**

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.

Otis is the world's leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2.4 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.4 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world's most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company.

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You'll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you'll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge.?

We provide opportunities, training, and resources, that build leadership and capabilities in Sales, Field, Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Scholar Program is a notable point of pride, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certification programs.

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people - passengers, customers, and colleagues - at the center of everything we do. We are guided by our values that we call our Three Absolutes - prioritizing Safety, Ethics, Quality in all that we do. If you would like to learn more about environmental, social and governance (ESG) at Otis click here (https://www.otis.com/en/us/our-company/esg) .

Become a part of the Otis team and help us #Buildwhatsnext!

About the role

Simplex Elevators Pty Ltd is seeking an experienced Service Manager to oversee the maintenance and repair of our elevator systems across Queensland. As a full-time position based in Salisbury, QLD, this role is crucial in ensuring the safe and reliable operation of our equipment for our valued clients.

What you'll be doing

Manage and coordinate a team of skilled elevator technicians to deliver exceptional service and maintenance

Liaise with clients to understand their needs and assist the scheduling team / maintenance and repair activities accordingly

Provide technical guidance and support to the service team, including troubleshooting complex issues

Ensure all service work is completed to the highest safety and quality standards

Maintain detailed records and provide regular reports on service activities

Support the sales team by providing technical expertise and recommendations for new installations

What we're looking for

Significant experience (5+ years) in an elevator maintenance or service management role

Strong technical knowledge of elevator systems

Excellent communication and customer service skills to liaise effectively with clients

Proven ability to lead and motivate a team of technicians

Highly organised with a focus on safety, quality and efficiency

Relevant trade qualifications

About us

Simplex Elevators is a leading provider of elevator services in Queensland. With over 30 years of experience, we are committed to delivering safe, reliable and innovative elevator solutions to our clients. Our team of dedicated professionals work hard to maintain the highest standards of service and customer satisfaction.

Apply now to join our team as Service Manager and play a vital role in the success of our growing business.

Permanent role

Reports to the Service Managers

Key Responsibilities for You

Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to meet customer satisfaction.

Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and subcontractors.

You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority

Has attention to detail and is results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!.

About us

We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. Lift Service Technician  
Schindler Lifts – Canberra ACT  
Full time  
26d ago, from seek.com.au  

Apply on company site  

WHY join Schindler?  
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential by careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.  

About the role:  
We are seeking an experienced Lift & Escalator Technicians to join our Service Team in Canberra. Reporting to the Service Manager, you will be responsible for minimising breakdowns and ensuring that all lifts and escalators perform to customer expectations.  

This role covers both our lift and escalator products, we are looking for an individual who

We are seeking an experienced Lift & Escalator Technicians to join our Service Team in Canberra. Reporting to the Service Manager, you will be responsible for minimising breakdowns and ensuring that all lifts and escalators perform to customer expectations.

This role covers both our lift and escalator products, we are looking for an individual who preferably has demonstrated experience in dealing with the maintenance and service of both lifts and escalators.

Benefits and Perks:
- Highly competitive package - EBA
- Permanent position
- A supportive team.
- Fitness passport with access to over 1,700 fitness facilities across Australia
- A company that promotes from within

Key Accountabilities:
- Carrying out regular maintenance
- Carrying out repair work to restore lift/escalator to full service
- Answer all calls promptly and provide an accurate ETA, in accordance with response terms
- In accordance with established procedures shut down a lift or escalator which is regarded as unsafe and unable to be made to function properly
- Propose material alterations where it is felt the performance of the escalator can be improved by doing so
- Depending on work requirements there is some flexibility required to be available to work overtime or on weekends.

Requirements for the role:
- Electrical Trade qualification

- Depending on work requirements there is some flexibility required to be available to work overtime or on weekends.

Requirements for the role:

- Electrical Trade qualification
- A current electrical license
- A current driver's license
- Excellent interpersonal, communication skills and customer service etiquette
- Excellent organisation skills, with the ability to work under pressure
- An uncompromising commitment to quality workmanship and safety policies and procedures

About us:
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

Further Information & how to apply:
If you are passionate about a career with us, please click on the “Apply” button and complete our online application form.

jobs.title: Elevator Fitter (AF214)
jobs.description:
Job Brief:

● Full-Time position

● 38 hours per week, Monday to Friday

● AUD 85,000 to 115,00 per year + superannuation

● Location of Employment: Caringbah, Sydney, NSW 2229

About Us:

Old Mate Elevators (OME) is an established company in Sydney that has been in operation for more than 25 years. We provide a highly supportive environment for all our employees.

The successful applicant MUST be a motivated and self-driven individual who is seeking a long-term, full-time position. You will be working with a team that has over 60 years of combined experience and knowledge that you will be able to leverage information and learn from.

The Role

We are seeking a talented and passionate Elevator Fitter to join our team. In this role, you will be working in conjunction with the Installation Project Manager and Service Coordinator and reporting to the Operations Manager to provide best practice in the installation of lift components, maintenance, servicing, and troubleshooting of all vertical transport products and related equipment supplied or maintained by Old Mate Elevators Pty Ltd (OME). Working with the Installation Project Manager and Service Coordinator to streamline and improve efficiencies within the departments and meet KPIs on a quarterly basis.

Responsibilities:

The Elevator Fitter will be responsible for the following tasks:

· Reviewing elevator blueprints, wiring diagrams, and technical specifications to determine project requirements, identify necessary materials, and plan the sequence of elevator installation, wiring, and operational testing.

· Devise, implement, and maintain best practice installation solutions.

· Fitting and securing manufactured metal components and subassemblies, such as guide rails, car frames, counterweights, and machinery, within lift shafts and machine rooms.

· Verifying the accuracy, clearance, and proper fit of installed mechanical and structural parts using precision measuring tools such as vernier calipers, micrometers, dial indicators, feeler gauges, laser alignment tools, and spirit levels to ensure compliance with strict safety and operational standards.

· Ensure project scope is adhered to throughout the installation process.

· Adjusting elevator guide rails, stops, and control systems during installation to ensure proper alignment and smooth operation. Installing and configuring specialized tools and equipment required for on-site modifications to elevator components.

· Precisely forming metal components and assemblies using techniques like cutting, grinding, and drilling to achieve required tolerances for proper alignment and integration within the lift system.

· Cutting, threading, bending, and installing hydraulic and pneumatic pipes and lines that power lift doors, braking systems, and other auxiliary functions.

· Setting up and adjusting the mechanical and electromechanical components that govern the operation and safety features of the lift.

· Diagnosing mechanical and hydraulic faults in installed lifts, performing routine operational maintenance, and carrying out overhauls and repairs on mechanical parts and fluid power equipment.

· Rectify breakdown issues.

· Procure the required materials to resolve the breakdown.

· Work in a timely and conscientious manner to carry out extensive checks of all critical safety apparatus on the vertical transport product, including emergency telephone systems.

· Assembling and installing complete lift systems on-site in both residential and commercial buildings, ensuring all mechanical components are correctly integrated and ready for electrical connection and testing.

· Ensure that operational H&S requirements as per Old Mate Elevators (OME) H&S Policy & Procedure document are met and adhered to.

Promptly report all accidents, hazards, near-miss events, and property damage by advising the office at your first opportunity.

Skills and Qualifications Required:

Successful applicants must have:

● Certificate III in Engineering - Mechanical Trade, which covers fitting, machining, maintenance, and repair, Certificate IV in Engineering, or its equivalent. However, at least three years of relevant experience may substitute for the formal qualifications.

● Vertical Transport industry knowledge

● AS1735 Lift knowledge

● White Card

● Ability to work as part of a team to share knowledge, especially in the up-skilling of apprentices.

● Ability to work independently (self-motivated) to achieve company targets.

● High level of initiative.

● Ability to work under pressure and achieve deadlines.

● Strong customer service focus.

● Personal integrity, well organised.

● Confident and articulate.

Ready to take the next step in your career? Submit your application today and become part of a company that values creativity, technical expertise, and professional growth.

We encourage all qualified applicants to apply and are committed to providing equal opportunity to everyone. However, to comply with relevant regulations, preference will be given to Australian citizens and permanent residents.

jobs.title: International careers
jobs.description:
Home
Careers
Work and grow at Schindler
International careers
International careers
Broadening horizons

Accepting an international assignment allows you to grow your career while experiencing professional life abroad.

Apply now
See the world at Schindler

Thanks to our presence in over 100 countries, we offer many opportunities to work internationally at Schindler. The Elevator & Escalator industry is expanding at a rapid pace, and we need people who are open to working in cultures and regions beyond their own.

Seize the opportunity

Besides being exciting, challenging and oftentimes fun, international assignments are a key part of how we develop people at Schindler. Shifting your perspective inevitably enriches how you perceive and relate to both our customers and your colleagues. Professional experience abroad can accelerate your promotion prospects, too.

The power of networks

Having a broad and deep professional network is one of the best ways to build a satisfying career – especially when you feel that you’ve learned enough in your role and are ready to try something new.

Comprehensive support

Whether you are on your own or part of a family, Schindler takes care of you during your time abroad. If you accept a position outside your home country, we’ll do our best to help ease the transition for you and your inner circle, including identifying possible housing and school options. We’ll also provide hands-on support for navigating the necessary logistics (immigration documents, taxes, etc.).

Visit our global Job Search page to see the latest opportunities in elevator service, as well as jobs in related disciplines and support functions.

Why Schindler

Our Employer Value Proposition
We Elevate... Your Career

Grow a thriving career and build sustainable mobility solutions that enhance the quality of urban living.


Enhancing quality of life

Our elevators, escalators, and moving walks move more than 2 billion of us safely up and down buildings and across transportation hubs, every day. ​

With passion and precision, we develop innovative solutions that make places more accessible, inclusive, and sustainable. Your contribution will keep the world moving and enhance the quality of life for different communities.

Your growth path

We're all about helping you grow and thrive, giving you the support to develop your skills and enable you to be at your best for our customers, every day.​​

We open doors for you to explore diverse career paths across functions and organizational levels, whether it's within your country or beyond borders. This means access to fresh and rewarding opportunities for a fulfilling career journey.

Together, we’re Team Schindler

Our core values are the foundation of how we collaborate and lead at Schindler, and respect and care are our guiding principles. When you join us, you’re not just part of our success story; you’re shaping the future and carrying forward our proud legacy that started back in 1874.​

Together, we embrace diverse perspectives and ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to share ideas. This enables us to keep pushing boundaries and reaching new heights. Together, we’re Team Schindler.

jobs.title: Inclusion and diversity
jobs.description:
Home
Responsibility
People
Inclusion & Diversity
Inclusion & Diversity
"We are all in!"

Our global presence gives us access to the full spectrum of human diversity – reinforcing our ability to adapt and innovate.

We believe that where we work – both physical and virtual – must be inclusive in all aspects. It must be a place where we welcome people of all backgrounds and empower them to achieve great things - a place where we practice equity.


We appreciate the full scope of human differences, including ethnic background, gender, culture, religion, nationality, age, disability or health status, race, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, as well as personality, preferences and working styles. All of this, and more, sway how we see each other as similar or different.

And we like different. After all, Inclusive and diverse teams drive more innovative ideas and thinking, which is good for all of us at Schindler and our customers.


Inclusion is key to create an environment that welcomes all aspects of diversity and ensures the organization benefits from each person’s full contribution and unique perspective.

Wensi Wang, Head Global Inclusion & Diversity

Our Inclusion and Diversity Commitment

We commit to providing an inclusive work environment at Schindler, where diverse people feel welcomed to come together as they are to do their best work and to serve the over 2 billion people using our elevators, escalators, and moving walks every day. 

Download our complete commitment statement (PDF, 2 MB) ➝

Gender

Gender matters. We are committed to the UN Women's Empowerment Principles and feel strongly that our industry and our organization should reflect the mix of genders in society. Beyond our values, we know that mixed gender teams collaborate better and make better decisions. Through targeted development programs, training on unconscious bias, we’re working hard to build a mix of genders across Schindler. Our CEO reviews our progress regularly to make sure we stay on track. Also, our global equal pay initiative ensures that everyone receives fair pay for their role, no matter what their gender.

Over 300 women have taken part in leadership & advancement programs.
Diverse abilities

Mobility solutions are our business, and our technicians think inclusively every day when installing elevators, escalators, or moving walks. Abilities come in many different packages so, through initiatives like our Valuable 500 commitment, we are working to improve how we recruit and include people with disabilities. Several local programs have already helped increase the number of people with disabilities in our workforce.

Generations

We celebrate that there are up to five generations working together at Schindler, offering fantastic opportunities to learn from each other. We encourage every generation to remain open, curious, and ready to learn from others.

Sexual orientation & Gender identity

We know that our personal lives intersect more and more with our work lives. All of us need to feel safe at work to be able to be our true selves. Through training, we encourage all leaders and employees to step up and act as allies when something isn’t safe, or if someone is not treated with respect. We support the UN Standards of Conduct for Business Tackling Discrimination Against LGBTI People.

Schindler Switzerland proud to have been awarded the Swiss LGBTI* label
Ethnicity and Culture

We do not tolerate discrimination and are proud to do our part to fight biases to help make the world more just for all ethnicities. It is only natural that, as a global organization, we reflect the ethnic diversity found in the places where we operate. One way we do this is to promote international mobility, helping to build teams with diverse nationalities around the world.  

Employee inclusion networks

We actively work with our inclusion networks for gender, LGBTQIA+, multicultural /ethnicity, generations, diverse abilities and mental wellbeing, and U.S. veterans to shape our culture so that all colleagues feel part of the team and have a work environment where they feel they belong.

Equal Pay

We are committed to embracing the full scope of human diversity and fostering an inclusive working environment. We work strategically to eliminate biases and inequalities in order to ensure equal opportunities and equal access for all. Equal Pay is part of our inclusion and diversity strategy, and we ensure equal pay for equal work.

Marina S. and Mario F. from the Schindler team
Our Diversity at a glance
Over 69,000 employees
Over 130 nationalities
Over 1,000 branch offices in over 100 countries
Gender distribution of employees :
At the close of 2023, 13% female and 87% male*
 

*Even though globally databases are binary by government-requirement, we recognize that gender is not binary.

How we’re creating more inclusion
Together, as leaders and colleagues, we create an inclusive work environment that enables everyone to be their authentic selves at work. We want to make sure that everyone feels free to speak up and contribute to their fullest. 
Find out more about what we're doing
What we can do
Collectively, at Schindler we can all make the world more accessible for everyone by applying an inclusive mindset when we create our mobility solutions.
Read more about our inclusive mindset
Why it matters for all of us
Our values tell us that fostering inclusion and diversity is the right thing to do.
Learn more about why it matters
Reset Filter

Otis

3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the TIME 2025 World's Best Employers!

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

We are currently looking for Lift Installation Mechanics to be based in Brisbane, Queensland.

About you

You will be the face of the Otis brand and responsible for installing Lifts, Escalators, and Travellators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures.

12 months fixed-term contract

Reports to the Project Manager

This role falls under the local EBA, and your hourly rates are determined by your classification.

Key Responsibilities

Installation of New Equipment and Modernisation lifts and working to the highest safety and environmental standards while ensuring service targets are met.

Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in coordination with the Project Manager.

Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property.

Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards.

Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures, including reporting any conditions that may impact the environment or any employee.

Maintain and work on the Quality System. 

Attend customer handing over inspection/service inspections on installation and modernization projects.

Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes.

What you will need to be successful

Experience in Adjustment / Commissioning work would be beneficial.

Working with heights, Basic Rigging and hold a valid driver’s license

Hold full working rights in Australia

Has relevant Lift experience and proven ability to work safely as part of a team or independently, depending on requirements.

Enjoys communicating with various levels of stakeholders and working closely with others in the field.

Solutions-oriented, independent, and well organized.

Willing to travel in & out of the region and able to work unsupervised.

Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

Able to plan and prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner.

Key Benefits

Opportunity to work on projects of a wide spectrum.

Classification and hourly rate per the latest EBA

Opportunity to work on latest technology with the industry leader with highest safety and quality standards.

​Apply today to join us and build what’s next!.

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day. 

Otis is the world’s leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.2 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.  

You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 69,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company.  

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You’ll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you’ll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge.  

We provide opportunities, training, and resources, that build leadership and capabilities in Sales, Field, Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Scholar Program is a notable point of pride, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certification programs.   

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people – passengers, customers, and colleagues – at the center of everything we do.  We are guided by our values that we call our Three Absolutes – prioritizing Safety, Ethics, Quality in all that we do. If you would like to learn more about environmental, social and governance (ESG) at Otis click here.  

Become a part of the Otis team and help us #Buildwhatsnext! 

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And, a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other protected class according to applicable law. Otis are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive place to work. We strongly encourage applications from women, and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people

For more information: https://www.otis.com/en/au/our-company/diversity-and-inclusion

The Lift Mechanic role is an integral role in our team and will be responsible for Servicing and Repairs on elevators and access solutions within our business. The key focus of the role is to ensure City Lift Services work procedures are followed and work is carried out safely, effectively, efficiently and ethically within City Lift Services policies and practices. Our technicians are the face of our business and are the key to our success.

This position will at times require a reasonable amount of overtime and after-hours roster or shift work will be a required part of your roster.

Inspect door installations and alignments, clearances of cars and hoist ways, platforms and stairway lifts to ensure that equipment will operate properly.

Participate in additional training to keep skills up-to-date.

Keep workshop/vehicle/sites organised, clean, functional and safe.

Need to have post trade experience in mechanical fitting/similar roles

Have a strong mechanical aptitude and skills set with attention to detail

Have previous experience with machinery fixed/mobile plant industry

Have a professional attitude to customer relationships

Have excellent communication both written and oral

Be able to prioritise activities

Experience in maintenance, installations within this or heavy industry

Demonstrate good basic computer skills

Drivers licence

Experience using work scheduling software and device, but not essential

KLEEMANN Elevators Australia – Elevate Your Career!

At KLEEMANN Elevators Australia, we’re not just in the lift business—we’re in the business of innovation, engineering excellence, and customer-first solutions. As a leading provider of world-class vertical transportation systems, we deliver cutting-edge, custom-engineered lifts for a diverse range of industries, from commercial skyscrapers to high-end residential developments.

With a strong presence across NSW, QLD, VIC, SA, and ACT, we’re backed by the global strength of KLEEMANN and the specialist expertise of Elevator Services Group (ESG), ensuring seamless service, maintenance, and ongoing support. Our success is driven by our people—dedicated professionals across engineering, project management, sales, operations, customer service, and technical fields.

If you’re looking for a career that challenges, excites, and rewards, KLEEMANN is the place to be. Join a team that values innovation, collaboration, and excellence, and help us shape the future of vertical mobility in Australia.

About the Opportunity

KLEEMANN Elevators Australia is hiring full-time Lift Commissioner / Tester in QLD.

Key Responsibilities:

- Fine Tuning and Testing: Ensure all components of Kleemann elevators are finely tuned and tested before passing into service.
- Defect Identification and Resolution: Identify, escalate, and repair defects; align all doors and openings; conduct audits and corrective actions on lift car/landing finishes.
- Safety and Quality Compliance: Adhere to safety, productivity, quality, and customer experience standards.
- Reporting and Documentation: Report status daily to the Installation Manager; energize lifts and complete test reports.
- Training and Support: Train field staff in test and commissioning processes; assist with technical issues on-site and over the phone.
 

Qualifications:

- Minimum 4 years of experience in Vertical Transportation.
- Electrical Trade certification.
- High-level communication and problem-solving skills.
- Ability to read and interpret electrical and mechanical documentation.
- Valid driver’s license and White Card.
- Strong technical aptitude and team player mentality.

About You

To qualify, you will need a valid electrical license (trade qualification). Preferably, you have 4 years of experience in the lift industry or a related field.

Additionally, the following skills and background will be highly valued:

Experience in servicing and maintaining lift systems

Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills

Ability to work independently and manage workload efficiently

Excellent communication and customer service skills

Commitment to workplace safety and compliance

Training and professional development opportunities, including safety certifications

The Role – A-Grade Lift Technician

You’ll lead the technical delivery of lift projects and service work, with responsibilities including:

Servicing, maintaining, and fault diagnostics (on-site and remote)

Occasionally installing and commissioning a wide range of lift systems (hydraulic, traction, screw, MRL, platform)

Supervising and training apprentices as well as trade assistants

Overseeing apprentice profiling and progress

Overseeing site safety, compliance and documentation

Communicating with homeowners, builders, project managers & relevant parties

Ensuring all work meets OH&S, Australian Standards, and lift code compliance

You Must Have

Current A-Grade Victorian Electrical Licence with 5 years of proven interaction with ESV

Construction White Card

Experience:

vertical transportation systems. This is a critical role in ensuring the safe and reliable operation of these essential systems within
commercial and residential buildings.
As an Elevator Installation Technician, you will be responsible for the installation, repair and modernization of elevators, escalators,
and other vertical transportation systems. This is a critical role in ensuring the safe and reliable operation of these essential systems
within commercial and residential buildings.
Join Octagon Lifts
At Octagon Lifts, we know that great elevators are built by skilled technicians who care about their craft. As our business continues to

supports its people.

residential clients. Our team of dedicated professionals is passionate about ensuring the safe and efficient operation of these
essential systems, and we take pride in our commitment to customer satisfaction.

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will play a crucial part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sunshine Coast to Discovery Coast region.

As an expert technician, you'll be responsible for conducting routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs, with a focus on quickly diagnosing and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator components. Your ability to think critically and implement effective solutions will be essential to ensuring optimal functionality and compliance with AS1735 standards.

You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to "Leading the Change" in elevator maintenance.

This role is suited for someone who is not only technically skilled but also values quality workmanship, safety, and client satisfaction. If you take pride in overcoming challenges and delivering high-quality results, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

What you’ll be doing

• Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing

What you’ll be doing

• Diagnose & Troubleshoot Issues: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as motors, controllers, and safety devices. Your expertise will be pivotal in delivering swift, accurate solutions

• Carry Out Repair Work: Conduct precise repairs, replacing worn or faulty parts as needed to restore full elevator functionality and reliability.

• Maintain Detailed Records: Keep thorough, accurate records of all maintenance, service, and repair activities, ensuring clear documentation for compliance and future reference.

• Ensure Safety & Compliance: Maintain strict adherence to all safety standards and regulations, upholding safety as a non-negotiable priority in every task.

• Provide On-Call Support: Be prepared to respond to emergency situations as part of our rotational standby roster, ensuring timely support and effective resolution of urgent issues.

• Liaise with Clients: Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.

• Continuous Skill Development: Participate in training events and workshops.

What we’re looking for

• Positive, proactive attitude and a commitment to safety and quality service.

• Minimum 5 years of experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.

• Queensland Electrical Licence (no restrictions).

• White card.

• Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).

• Rigging and Dogging certification (favourable but not essential).

• Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer

• Competitive Remuneration: Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.

• Coastal Lifestyle: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most

What we offer

• Coastal Lifestyle: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most sought-after coastal regions.

• Motivating Team Culture: We believe in creating a positive work environment where your skills are valued, and your career can thrive.

Application Process

2025-11-24

Australia
