Come and join OTIS today and be part of the TIME 2025 World's Best Employers!

At Otis, it's our people that make us different. Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

We are currently looking for **Lift Installation Mechanics** to be based in **Brisbane or Gold Coast, Queensland.**

**About you**

You will be the face of the Otis brand and responsible for installing Lifts, Escalators, and Travellators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures.

+ 12 months fixed-term contract

+ Reports to the Project Manager

+ This role falls under the local EBA, and your hourly rates are determined by your classification.

**Key Responsibilities**

+ Installation of New Equipment and Modernisation lifts and working to the highest safety and environmental standards while ensuring service targets are met.

+ Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in coordination with the Project Manager.

+ Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property.

+ Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards.

+ Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures, including reporting any conditions that may impact the environment or any employee.

+ Maintain and work on the Quality System.

+ Attend customer handing over inspection/service inspections on installation and modernization projects.

+ Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes.

**What you will need to be successful**

+ Experience in Adjustment / Commissioning work would be beneficial.

+ Working with heights, Basic Rigging and hold a valid driver's license

+ Hold full working rights in Australia

+ Has relevant Lift experience and proven ability to work safely as part of a team or independently, depending on requirements.

+ Enjoys communicating with various levels of stakeholders and working closely with others in the field.

+ Solutions-oriented, independent, and well organized.

+ Willing to travel in & out of the region and able to work unsupervised.

+ Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

+ Able to plan and prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner.

**Key Benefits**

+ Opportunity to work on projects of a wide spectrum.

+ Classification and hourly rate per the latest EBA

+ Opportunity to work on latest technology with the industry leader with highest safety and quality standards.

**?Apply today to join us and build what's next!.**

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.

Otis is the world's leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2.4 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.4 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world's most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company.

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You'll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you'll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge.?

We provide opportunities, training, and resources, that build leadership and capabilities in Sales, Field, Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Scholar Program is a notable point of pride, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certification programs.

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people - passengers, customers, and colleagues - at the center of everything we do. We are guided by our values that we call our Three Absolutes - prioritizing Safety, Ethics, Quality in all that we do. If you would like to learn more about environmental, social and governance (ESG) at Otis click here (https://www.otis.com/en/us/our-company/esg) .

Become a part of the Otis team and help us #Buildwhatsnext!

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

We are currently looking for a Lift Installation Mechanic to be based in Brisbane, Queensland.

About you

You will be the face of the Otis brand and responsible for installing Lifts, Escalators, and Travellators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures.

12 months fixed-term contract

Reports to the Project Manager

This role falls under the local EBA, and your hourly rates are determined by your classification.

Key Responsibilities

Installation of New Equipment and Modernisation lifts and working to the highest safety and environmental standards while ensuring service targets are met.

Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in coordination with the Project Manager.

Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property.

Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards.

Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures, including reporting any conditions that may impact the environment or any employee.

Maintain and work on the Quality System.

Attend customer handing over inspection/service inspections on installation and modernization projects.

Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes.

What you will need to be successful

Experience in Adjustment / Commissioning work would be beneficial.

Working with heights, Basic Rigging and hold a valid driver’s license

Hold full working rights in Australia

Has relevant Lift experience and proven ability to work safely as part of a team or independently, depending on requirements.

Enjoys communicating with various levels of stakeholders and working closely with others in the field.

Solutions-oriented, independent, and well organized.

Willing to travel in & out of the region and able to work unsupervised.

Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

Able to plan and prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner.

Key Benefits

Classification and hourly rate per the latest EBA

Exciting and a wide spectrum of projects to work

Opportunity to work on the latest technology with a global industry leader with the highest safety and quality standards.

​

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!.

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.

Otis is the world’s leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2.4 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.4 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company.

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You’ll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you’ll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge. 

We provide opportunities, training, and resources, that build leadership and capabilities in Sales, Field, Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Scholar Program is a notable point of pride, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certification programs.

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people – passengers, customers, and colleagues – at the center of everything we do. We are guided by our values that we call our Three Absolutes – prioritizing Safety, Ethics, Quality in all that we do. Become a part of the Otis team and help us #Buildwhatsnext!

jobs.location: Australia, Victoria, Melbourne Region, Port Phillip, Middle Park
jobs.description:
Expression of Interest – Lift Industry ECW3 Mechanical Fitters

Location: Various Projects Across Melbourne, VIC
Employment Type: Casual / Project-Based

About People 2U

People 2U partners with some of Melbourne’s leading construction and lift industry companies, connecting skilled tradespeople with quality projects across the city. We work closely with our clients to provide experienced Mechanical Fitters who contribute to the safe, efficient, and high-quality installation of lift systems.

As demand continues to grow across multiple projects, we’re inviting Expressions of Interest from qualified ECW3 Mechanical Fitters who would like to be considered for upcoming and ongoing opportunities.

About the Opportunities

We regularly support lift installation projects across Melbourne and are keen to connect with experienced Mechanical Fitters for current and future work. These roles typically involve hands-on mechanical installation, alignment, and commissioning support across a variety of commercial sites.

Registering your interest means you’ll be considered as projects become available, with the opportunity to work alongside established lift industry professionals on high-profile developments.

Typical Responsibilities May Include

Assembling and installing lift mechanical components in accordance with drawings and specifications
Alignment, adjustment, and testing of lift systems to ensure smooth operation
Working closely with site supervisors and project teams to meet program requirements
Maintaining a safe, clean work environment in line with OH&S standards
Providing clear communication around progress and any site issues

What We’re Looking For

Mechanical Fitter trade qualification (essential)
Previous experience in lift installation or maintenance (highly regarded)
Ability to read and interpret technical drawings
Strong attention to detail and a safety-first mindset
White Card and current driver’s license
Flexibility to travel to different project locations across Melbourne

Why Register Your Interest with People 2U?

Access to competitive ECW3 EBA rates
Opportunities with reputable lift industry companies
Consistent communication and support from experienced labour hire consultants
Exposure to a variety of commercial projects across Melbourne
Potential for ongoing and repeat project work

Register Your Expression of Interest

If you’re an ECW3 Mechanical Fitter in the lift industry and would like to be considered for upcoming opportunities, we’d love to hear from you. Submit your details to join the People 2U talent pool and be notified as suitable projects arise.

People 2U acknowledges the Traditional Owners of Country on which we live and work throughout Australia. We pay our respects to Elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all backgrounds.

jobs.location: Australia, Western Australia, Perth Region, Perth
jobs.description:
Lift Mechanic

Job Responsibilities:

Install, maintain, repair, and test various types of lifts and elevators

Perform routine inspections to ensure lift safety and functionality

Troubleshoot issues and accurately diagnose problems with lift components

Follow safety protocols and regulations while working on lifts

Interact with customers to understand and address their lift concerns

Essential Qualifications:

Recent full-time experience in this line of work. (presently employed)

Completion of a relevant lift mechanic apprenticeship or vocational training

Valid elevator mechanic license or certification

Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills

Excellent attention to detail and the ability to follow technical manuals

Desired Experience:

Minimum of 5 years of experience working as a lift mechanic

Experience with a variety of lift systems and brands

Knowledge of electrical and hydraulic systems related to lifts

Ability to work independently and as part of a team

Salary & Benefits:

The annual salary for this full-time position ranges from $74,000 to $85,000.

Location:

City: Perth

State: Western Australia

Country: Australia

Note: Other clients with workplaces in regional Australia potentially available.

Visa:

Sponsored working visa class 482

2 year visa, extendable.

Lift Mechanic 341113 MLTSSL

Future Benefits:

Subsequent entrant visa for family. (generally, after 6 months work)

A pathway to PR. Permanent Residency. (Depending on age and English level)

English level needed:

An IELTS score of 5,5,5,5 + or equivalent in PTE, OET.

Future requirements:

After a pre-screening interview, If we are interested in connecting you to a client for a work-place interview, then we would first need:

Proof of Employment.

Video’s showing skill-set/work. (in a google-drive shared folder)

Passport copy, Showing 1 year + validity

Police clearance

Financial requirements:

There ​​​​​​​is NO show-money needed to be seen by immigration when applying for a working visa to Australia.

Personal requirements:

Happy, confident, outgoing.

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician
jobs.location: Australia, New South Wales, Sydney Region, Sydney, The Rocks
jobs.description:
Elevator Technician

The Company

Going Up is one of the largest independent Australian-owned and operated elevator companies, manufacturing, installing, servicing, and modernising lifts along the east coast of Australia.

We have a great community of team members and are looking for skilled Elevator Service Technicians to join our team.

As a Service Technician, you'll have the opportunity to apply your technical expertise in a friendly and supportive working environment.

Sites are located across Sydney, with our office based in Brookvale NSW.

The Role

Conduct routine maintenance, servicing, and repairs on elevators to ensure optimal performance and safety.

Diagnose faults and carry out repairs efficiently.

Coordinate with the service team and management for job scheduling and reporting.

Maintain compliance with all relevant safety and industry regulations.

Ensure high-quality standards in all work performed.

Full-time, permanent role.

About You

Qualified or experienced in the elevator or mechanical trades industry (Cert III in Electrotechnology or equivalent preferred).

Strong problem-solving skills with a mechanical and electrical aptitude.

Previous experience working with lift systems is highly regarded.

Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Organised and self-motivated.

High standard of workmanship and attention to detail.

Commitment to workplace safety and compliance.

Benefits

Competitive wage.

Company vehicle.

Ongoing training and development opportunities.

Full uniform provided excluding boots.

Work within a supportive and dynamic team environment.

Immediate start available.

If you are ready to take the next step in your career in the elevator industry, this is the opportunity for you! Apply now for an immediate start and become a valued member of the Going Up team.

Apply via Seek. No recruiters. Please note this is a full-time, permanent role.

jobs.title: Elevator Technician
jobs.location: Australia, Western Australia, Perth Region, Perth, Perth CBD
jobs.description:
About the role

We are looking for Electricians/Elevator Technicians.

If you have an Electrical Workers License and experience with electromechanical systems, please apply.

Full-time Elevator Maintenance and Installation Technician roles available.

Based in Perth, Western Australia.

As an Elevator Maintenance Technician, you will be responsible for the maintenance and repair of elevators, escalators, and other vertical transportation systems. This is a critical role in ensuring the safe and reliable operation of these essential systems within commercial and residential buildings.

As an Elevator Installation Technician, you will be responsible for the installation, repair and modernization of elevators, escalators, and other vertical transportation systems. This is a critical role in ensuring the safe and reliable operation of these essential systems within commercial and residential buildings.

Join Octagon Lifts

At Octagon Lifts, we know that great elevators are built by skilled technicians who care about their craft. As our business continues to grow, we're looking for experienced elevator technicians who want consistent work, quality projects, and a company that genuinely supports its people.

You'll be joining a professional, close‑knit team delivering high‑quality installation and service work across reputable projects. Our management understands the trade and respects your experience, giving you the trust, planning, and resources needed to do the job properly.

Why work with Octagon Lifts?

Steady, ongoing work with well‑organized projects

Supportive management who understands site realities

Quality equipment and a focus on doing things right the first time

A team culture built on respect, safety, and pride in workmanship

Opportunity to grow with a company that values long‑term careers

If you're an elevator technician looking for stability, respect, and a workplace where your skills are recognized, we'd like to hear from you.

What we're looking for

Must hold a current electrical worker's license.

Proven experience as an Elevator Technician or Industrial Electrician.

Strong technical knowledge of electromechanical control systems.

Excellent problem-solving and fault-finding skills to quickly identify and resolve issues

Good physical fitness and dexterity to access and work on equipment in confined spaces

Effective communication skills to liaise with clients and colleagues

A valid driver's license and ability to travel to job sites as required

Australian citizenship or permanent residency is required for this position

What we offer

At Octagon Lifts, we are committed to providing a supportive and collaborative work environment that empowers our team to grow and succeed. You will have access to ongoing training and development opportunities, a competitive salary and benefits package, and the opportunity to be part of a dynamic and customer-focused organization.

About us

Octagon Lifts Pty Ltd is a leading provider of elevator and escalator services in Western Australia. Established in 1985, we have built a reputation for delivering high-quality, reliable, and innovative vertical transportation solutions to a wide range of commercial and residential clients. Our team of dedicated professionals is passionate about ensuring the safe and efficient operation of these essential systems, and we take pride in our commitment to customer satisfaction.

Apply now for this exciting opportunity to join the Octagon Lifts team as an Elevator Technician.

jobs.title: Electrician Lift Repairs
jobs.location: Australia, New South Wales, Sydney Region, Sydney
jobs.description:
Lift Repair Technician – Electrician

Chullora & Greater Sydney | Full-Time |EBA rates $59.30 p/h + Super Company Car + Phone + Tools + Overtime + Allowances
NSW Electrical Licence Essential!!

The company:
Our client is the world’s largest elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service business. They serve customers in over 200 countries and territories around the world and maintain more than ~2.4 million elevators and escalators. They have more than 72,000 employees globally, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians. A proud global team that continues to rise to the challenges of a fast-moving, high-performance company.

Just imagine where you can take your career in a company this size!

The Role:

We’re seeking a skilled Lift Repair Electrician who thrives on hands-on, ground-up repair work. This role suits someone who enjoys diagnosing faults without relying on a guide—using their electrical and mechanical expertise to restore lift systems with precision.

You’ll carry out detailed inspections, testing, troubleshooting, and repairs on lift equipment, working both on the bench and onsite across Greater Sydney.

Key Responsibilities

Diagnose, troubleshoot, and repair lift electrical and mechanical faults
Complete bench repairs, electrical testing, and component rebuilds
Conduct mechanical inspections to ensure safety and compliance
Interpret wiring diagrams, schematics, and technical drawings
Repair or replace faulty parts with accuracy and care
Adhere to WHS and industry safety standards
Work independently while supporting the team when needed
About You
We’re looking for a Lift Repair Mechanic / Electrician who genuinely enjoys repairing lifts not just routine maintenance. You love problem-solving, fault-finding, tinkering and bringing complex systems back to life.

You’ll bring:

A valid NSW Electrical Licence (must-have)
Experience in lift, hoist, crane, or similar mechanical/electrical repair work
3+ years mechanical/electrical fault-finding experience
Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to resolve issues without guidance
Ability to read schematics and understand motor control systems
NSW Driver’s Licence
Strong independence, initiative, and a safety-first mindset we want someone who is a self starter, critical thinker and gets the job done.
What’s on Offer

EBA Rate $59.30 per hour + Super
Company car
Phone + tools provided
Stable permanent full-time role covering our clients vertical transport in Greater Sydney
Supportive Training
Career growth within the business take your career wherever you want it to go!
Supportive, experienced repair team, our team has the best culture in the business we have each others backs and support each other its a great place to work and we love what we do!
Ready to Apply?
If you’re passionate about lift repairs and want a role where your skills are truly valued, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now!

Email me your CV to natalie.iglesias@astrumrecruitment.com or for a confidential chat give me a call 0439 811 172

jobs.title: Technical Field Support - NSW
jobs.location: Australia, New South Wales, Sydney Region, Sydney
jobs.description:
WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential by careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.

About the role:

We have an opportunity for an experienced individual in Technical Field Support to join our team based in Sydney. Reporting to the Branch Technical Support Manager. The role will require extensive Knowledge of the lift industry as you will be providing technical advice to technicians directly on-site and over the phone.

This role will be ideally suited to an experienced, Technical Field Support, Lift Technical support, Technical Services Group (TSG), Technical Field Engineer or a highly experienced Lift Service Technician looking to step up in their career.

Benefits and Perks:

Highly competitive package - EBA

Permanent position

A supportive team.

A company that promotes from within

Key Responsibilities:

Providing technical advice to technicians directly (onsite) and indirectly (via phone and remote diagnostic platform)

Assisting in identifying specific training requirements for field personnel and facilitate both in-class and onsite training & mentoring

Providing technical support for Schindler-installed units & third party units maintained by Schindler

Driving reliability improvements

Analysing data and provide technical support and/or suggest improvements

Documenting technical problems together with their respective solutions in the technical database and then communicate this information with the relevant personnel

What we are looking for:

Electrical Trade qualification (recognised in Australia)

Good knowledge of lift codes (EN81 and AS1735) along with related standards

Thorough, proven technical fault-finding ability, with an ability to apply to both Schindler and third party products

Excellent understanding of installation and maintenance of lifts & escalators with the ability to disseminate that information to a broad audience

Excellent organisation skills, with the ability to work under pressure

An uncompromising commitment to quality workmanship and safety policies and procedures

Good knowledge of Microsoft Office and SAP beneficial (however training provided)

Ability to work within a responsive team in a dynamic environment

Clear communication skills (verbal and written)

About us:

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

Further Information & how to apply:

If you are passionate about a career with us, please click on the “” button and complete our online application form.

We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply. Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a manager.

Electra Lift is a proudly Australian-owned elevator company with more than 45 years of proven industry experience.

We specialise in lift maintenance, modernisations, replacements, and new installations across Sydney. With our head office based in Mascot, NSW, we operate with a strong people-first culture, high technical expertise, and an unwavering commitment to safety and quality.

About you

As we continue to expand, we are seeking a dedicated and skilled Lift Service Technicians to join our growing team based in Mascot, NSW

As a Lift Service Technician with Electra Lift, you will play a critical role in ensuring the safe and reliable operation of our customers’ assets.

You’ll be supported by a well‑structured Service team, access to quality training, and a positive working environment that values professionalism, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

Benefits

Competitive package per the EBA

Permanent role

Join a company focused on safety, quality, and ethics

Excellent learning opportunities and career growth

Key Responsibilities for You

Conduct routine service and maintenance in accordance with procedures, technical manuals, and safety standards.

Attend lift breakdowns promptly and carry out necessary repairs.

Communicate effectively with clients and internal teams to uphold our strong customer service culture.

Complete service reports, Job Hazard Forms, and track service activities accurately.

Identify and recommend upgrade opportunities that may attract incentive payments for technicians.

What You Will Need To Be Successful

Hold a current Australian driver’s license.

Have an Electrical trade license or relevant trade certification

Previous experience within the Lift industry is required.

Strong communication skills, a positive attitude, and a commitment to safe work practices.

Motivation to contribute to a supportive team environment.

Apply today to join us and build what’s next !.

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.

Otis is the world’s leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2.4 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.4 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company.

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You’ll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you’ll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge. 

We provide opportunities, training, and resources, that build leadership and capabilities in Sales, Field, Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Scholar Program is a notable point of pride, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certification programs.

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people – passengers, customers, and colleagues – at the center of everything we do. We are guided by our values that we call our Three Absolutes – prioritizing Safety, Ethics, Quality in all that we do. If you would like to learn more about environmental, social and governance (ESG) at Otis click here.

Become a part of the Otis team and help us #Buildwhatsnext!

Otis is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other protected class according to applicable law. To request an accommodation in completing an employment application due to a special need or a disability, please contact us at careers@otis.com.

jobs.title: Service Technician - QLD
jobs.description:
WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.

About the role:

We are seeking an experienced Service Technician to join our Brisbane CBD Service Team. The role will be reporting to an experienced Service Manager, and you will be responsible for minimising breakdowns and ensuring that all equipment performs to our customers' expectations.

We are looking for an individual who has demonstrated experience in dealing with the maintenance and service of both lifts and escalators.

What’s in it for you:

Highly competitive base - EBA
Excellent opportunity for you to expand your career at a multinational organisation
Potential for real growth both personally and professionally
Opportunity to access educational support, access to staff discount scheme, and social activities

Key Accountabilities:

Carrying out regular maintenance
Answer all calls promptly and provide an accurate ETA, in accordance with response terms
Ensure that an appropriate person on site is kept informed of the arrival, departure, and status of the Equipment.
In accordance with established procedures be able to shut down an escalator or elevator which is regarded as unsafe and unable to be made to function properly
Propose material alterations where it is felt the performance of the escalator can be improved by doing so
Ensure your work sites are kept tidy and that all paperwork is up to date

What we are looking for:

A current electrical license
Previous experience as a Lift Service Technician or a Lift Repairs Technician will be highly regarded
A current driver's license
Outstanding communication skills and customer service etiquette
Excellent organisation skills, with the ability to work under pressure
An uncompromising commitment to quality workmanship and safety policies and procedures

About Schindler:

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

How to apply:

We are constantly looking for passionate and enthusiastic people with the energy and drive to build relationships and networks. At Schindler, you will find a highly motivated and engaged working environment that lets you grow as an individual and allows you to plan your career.

If you are seeking an opportunity to join a Global Industry Leader and believe that you possess the required skills and qualifications to succeed in this role, click on the "APPLY" button and complete our online application form

If you are an existing Schindler employee, please ensure you have discussed your application with your line manager prior to applying.

We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders, and people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply. Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a manager.

jobs.title: Lift Service Technician
jobs.location: Australia, Victoria, Melbourne Region, Port Phillip, Middle Park
jobs.description:
Lift Service Technician | Melbourne, VIC | Full-time position

About Us
Tesla Elevators is a proudly Australian-owned and operated independent lift company, providing service and maintenance across residential, commercial and industrial sites. Our growth has been built on exceptional customer service, a dedicated technical team, and a strong commitment to work-life balance. We’re serious about quality, safety and professionalism, and we value a supportive culture and enjoying what we do.

The Role
We are seeking an experienced Lift Service Technician to join our Melbourne-based Service Team. Reporting to the Maintenance Coordinator, you will take ownership of a scheduled maintenance run and be responsible for servicing, repairs and breakdown response across a range of lift systems including hydraulic, MRL and platform lifts. You will work on our own products as well as a variety of third-party equipment.

This is a hands-on role for someone who takes pride in clean, compliant work, communicates well with customers, and can fault-find confidently to reduce repeat callouts.

What We Offer

· Company vehicle

· iPhone

· On-call allowance (rostered on-call)

· 36-hour work week

· 26 RDOs per annum

· Competitive remuneration with overtime opportunities

· Training, support and a team that values your contribution

Key Responsibilities

· Plan and execute your workload effectively in coordination with the Maintenance Coordinator

· Perform scheduled maintenance, repairs, breakdown response and call-out visits with care and attention to detail

· Diagnose faults, troubleshoot equipment and complete inspections and diagnostic testing to ensure reliability and performance

· Complete all work to manufacturer specifications, Australian Standards and regulatory requirements

· Maintain strict compliance with WHS policies and maintain safe operating conditions at all times

· Maintain a safe, tidy and professional worksite, with strong care and respect in customers’ homes and workplaces

· Communicate professionally with customers, keeping them informed and providing practical guidance on lift operation and care

· Accurately document work completed, including maintenance logs, breakdown reports, repair reports and emails, in clear language that non-technical customers and internal staff can understand

· Monitor your calendar daily for schedule updates and upcoming work

· Contribute to continuous improvement by helping refine workflows and service processes

What We’re Looking For

Qualifications (essential)

· Australian Electrical A-Grade Licence

· Construction White Card

· Current manual Driver’s Licence

· Full working rights in Australia (citizen or permanent resident)

Experience and technical capability

· Minimum 5 years’ experience as a lift technician (service and repairs focused preferred)

· Strong electrical and mechanical fault-finding skills

· Experience with hydraulic and traction drive systems (MRL and hydraulic)

· Ability to read and interpret electrical schematics

· Confidence with PLCs, microprocessors and VF drives is highly regarded

Personal attributes

· Self-motivated, solution-focused and team-oriented

· Organised, reliable and professional with customers

· Takes pride in doing the job properly, including clear reporting and clean workmanship

· Flexible and willing to travel within our service area as required

Checks

· Prepared to provide a National Police Check and Working with Children Check

Ready to Join Us?
If you’re ready to elevate your career with Tesla Elevators, apply now. We’re looking for a technician who wants stability, great conditions, and a team that backs quality work.

As a Lifting Technician, you'll be responsible for inspecting, testing, repairing, and maintaining lifting equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards and regulations. You'll work closely with customers to provide expert advice and solutions, ensuring their lifting equipment is reliable and safe to use.  

If you have a LEEA Diploma, engineering certification, or hands-on experience as a Lifting Technician? We want to hear from you! We also encourage EX ADF personal to apply as we value your experience, skillset, discipline and work ethic that ex - ADF personal bring to the workforce.

Cranecorp Australia is seeking an experienced Senior Heavy Lift Engineer to lead our engineering
function and play a critical role in delivering safe, innovative, and commercially sound heavy lift
solutions across Australia.
This is a key position within the business, ensuring exceptional customer service and best‑in‑class
safety outcomes that consistently exceed client expectations.
About the Role
Reporting to senior leadership, you will work closely with Operations, Business Development,
and project stakeholders to deliver complex heavy lift and transport solutions. You will also
contribute to business growth, technology advancement, and the development of
industry‑leading engineering standards and tools.
Key Responsibilities
Collaborate with project managers, engineers, and clients to develop compliant heavy lift
plans in accordance with relevant Australian Standards
Communicate strategy, planning, outcomes, and issues clearly to senior management and the
wider business
Build and maintain strong working relationships across the business as a key member of the
Technical team
Evaluate and implement new and emerging engineering technologies related to cranes,
rigging, load monitoring, and heavy lift operation
Develop, coach, and support team members to enhance capability and succession planning
Develop tools, systems, and training to ensure compliance with world’s best practice and to
set new industry benchmarks
Provide engineering support outside normal business hours as required
Ensure compliance with the latest Australian Standards and Codes of Practice nationwide
Support Business Development with technical input for tenders, proposals, and client
presentations
Assist with engineering design including lifting devices, load support beams, stools, transport
equipment, load restraint diagrams, lifting studies, and alternative lift and transport
methodologies
About You
You are a technically strong and commercially aware engineer with a passion for safety,
leadership, and continuous improvement. You thrive in a dynamic environment and are confident
working with clients, stakeholders, and multidisciplinary teams.
Essential Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical or Structural Engineering
Minimum 3 years’ experience working as an engineer (heavy lift or related industry preferred)
Strong background in construction, cranes, lifting, and transportation
Proficiency in AutoCAD or similar engineering design software
C‑Class Driver’s Licence
National Police Clearance
Ability to pass a pre‑employment medical and Drug & Alcohol screening
Why Join Cranecorp?
Lead complex, high‑profile heavy lift projects across Australia
Work with a respected and growing industry leader
Opportunity to influence engineering standards and innovation
Supportive leadership team with a strong focus on safety and excellence
If you are ready to take the next step in your engineering leadership career and make a real
impact in the heavy lift industry, we would love to hear from you.
Apply now with your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your suitability for the role.

jobs.title: Elevator Installation Technician
jobs.description:
Electrical Technician  
Marble Group – Sydney NSW  
$145,000 - $145,000 a year  
Permanent  
3mo ago, from Marble Group  

**The opportunity and why apply**  
This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
- $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
- Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)  
- OEM Training and development on offer to become an expert in your field.  
- Working in a technical and challenging role on automated stacker cranes  

**The company**  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
- Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
- People focused management team with a strong team orientated culture  
- Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities  

**Roster and hours**  
You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

Roster and hours

You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

- 12-hour shifts with a paid lunch and smoko
- Day shift: 6am - 6pm
- Night shift: 6pm - 6am
- Occasional overtime available on the end of your swing for those looking for more hours.

The role

As a highly skilled electrician, you'll be responsible for all electrical maintenance and repair at one of Australia's biggest logistic parks. All of your work will be completed at the well-equipped site.

- Electrical and mechanical fault finding, preventative maintenance and emergency repair work on a range of rail mounted automated stacker cranes
- Work on a range of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives and crane management software
- Working on variable speed drives, control logic on wiring diagrams, and off grid generator systems.

Your value

You will be a trade qualified electrician with a well-rounded knowledge of electrical fault finding and repairs on mobile plant and/or cranes.

- Electrical trade qualification (Cert III Electrotechnology)
- Hydraulic and mechanical knowledge (highly regarded)
- An excellent attitude towards work with proven ability to work both autonomously and in a team environment.
- Working knowledge and use of electrical schematics and symbols
- Must demonstrate working knowledge of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives
- Be able to pass a pre-employment medical and national police check

jobs.title: Sales and Key Account Representative
jobs.description:
Sales and Key Account Representative  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  
Mascot, Sydney NSW  
Sales Representatives/Consultants (Sales)  
Contract/Temp  
Posted 7d ago

Come and join Electra today. Electra Lift is an elevator company who have been in operation for over 45 years.

We specialize in Lift Maintenance, Modernizations, Replacements and New Installations. We maintain lifts all across Sydney and have a well-structured team to support our continued growth and expansion with our main office located in Mascot, NSW.

As we continue to expand, we are seeking an experienced Sales & Key Account Representative to join our growing team based in Mascot, NSW.

- 12 months fixed term contract
- Reports to the Operations Manager

About You

As a Sales & Key Account Representative you will work closely with our Key Accounts Team to quote urgent lift repairs/minor upgrades, quote proactive equipment improvements and support the administrative tasks required with our key account portals.

Key Responsibilities

- Deliver revenue performance: Consistently achieve (and exceed) assigned sales revenue and margin targets in line with the approved business plan, with clear accountability for results.
- Maximise speed-to-lead and conversion: Action all repair leads from every source immediately on receipt, applying disciplined follow-up to maximise contact and conversion rates.
- Qualify needs and build demand: Partner with the field technicians to confirm scope/requirements, then engage the customer to validate needs, create urgency, and progress opportunities through the buying cycle.
- Drive campaign-led pipeline: Actively participate in all repair product campaigns, generating pipeline contribution and supporting the Key Account team by providing responsive, accurate quoting and commercial support.

Sales and Key Account Representative  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  

- Drive campaign-led pipeline: Actively participate in all repair product campaigns, generating pipeline contribution and supporting the Key Account team by providing responsive, accurate quoting and commercial support.  
- Own proposal development end-to-end: Produce accurate estimates and secure required internal approvals to create a compliant Service Works Proposal, then submit to the customer within agreed turnaround times.  
- Execute a structured close plan: Proactively follow up every submission using a structured, cyclical call-closing cadence to progress objections, confirm decision steps, and secure approval/booking.  
- Build and maintain a robust pipeline: Maintain a healthy opportunity funnel that supports target attainment, including ongoing prospecting, opportunity progression, and clear next steps at every stage.  
- Forecast with accuracy: Keep CRM/sales systems current, report activity consistently, and provide accurate forecasting against plan (pipeline, probability, timing, value).  

What you will need to be successful  

- Minimum 2-3 years’ experience in Tele Sales, Frontline Sales, or Account Management, with a results-driven mindset (Preferred).  
- Exposure to technical or solution-based sales would be an added advantage  
- Proven ability to work independently and collaborate cross-functionally, building trusted relationships to drive outcomes.  
- Strong communications – verbal and written.  
- Attention to detail and accurate record-keeping are important for success  
- Sound knowledge of Microsoft 365 and the ability to learn and frequently switch between inhouse systems/databases is required  
- A desire to learn and understand the elevator industry and the technology involved  

Apply today and help us build what's next!

Service Lift Technician - NSW  

Join a supportive national team and build your career with Australia’s trusted independent lift company.  

About United Lifts  

United is a proudly Australian-owned, independent lift company. Established in Melbourne, we’ve grown into a national organisation with branches in NSW, ACT, QLD, and SA. We partner with a wide range of high-profile clients across numerous industries, providing expert service, exceptional modernisation, and new installation solutions.  

With over 200 passionate employees and a growing portfolio, we are looking to expand our team in NSW. This is an excellent opportunity to work alongside industry experts, develop your skills, and contribute to a thriving and dynamic business.

Service Lift Technician  
United Lifts AUST  

Why Join United?  
- A positive, professional team culture where your contributions are valued  
- Excellent career growth with ongoing training and development  
- Attractive remuneration package (negotiable based on experience)  
- Work-life balance with opportunities for overtime  
- Company vehicle, tools, and uniform provided  
- Be part of a company with a strong team culture and national presence  
- Work with experienced technicians on a variety of lift types and technologies  

The Role  
As a Service Lift Technician (NSW), you will report to our NSW Management Teams. You’ll take ownership of lift maintenance and repairs, respond to service calls, and carry out various lift-related tasks across our diverse client base.  

Your expertise will help us deliver exceptional service and maintain the high standards our clients expect.  

Key Responsibilities  
- Perform maintenance and repairs on lifts and related equipment  
- Respond to breakdowns and call-outs in a timely manner  
- Collaborate with the Service Team and Management to ensure high-quality outcomes  
- Provide excellent customer service on-site  
- Complete documentation and reporting as required

United Lifts AUST

• Current Electrical License (essential)

• Strong communication and interpersonal skills

• Team player with a proactive, growth-oriented mindset  
• Flexible and adaptable to business needs  

Ready to Take the Next Step?

jobs.description:
Lift Technician / Electrician  
Frontline Construction – Sydney CBD NSW  
$90,000 - $110,000 a year  
Permanent  
26d ago, from Frontline Construction Australia  

**About the Role**  
An exciting opportunity is available for a skilled Lift Technician or qualified Electrician to join a well-established organisation within the lift and accessibility sector. This role involves the installation, servicing and repair of a diverse range of lift and access systems across residential and commercial environments.

The position offers stable, full-time hours (7:30am - 4:15pm), one RDO per calendar month, optional overtime, and long-term career progression for the right candidate.  

**Key Responsibilities**  
- Install, service and repair platform lifts, stair-lifts and accessibility equipment  
- Diagnose and fault-find electrical and mechanical control systems  
- Review and interpret technical plans, drawings and specifications  
- Test, commission and check PLCs and motor control systems  
- Align mechanical components and integrate with electrical switches  
- Ensure all work complies with safety standards and quality expectations  
- Provide professional, customer-focused service on site  

**Key Skills & Experience**  
- Electrical trade qualification (essential)

```
• Provide professional, customer-focused service on site

Key Skills & Experience
• Electrical trade qualification (essential)
• Current electrical licence (preferred)
• Experience in lift technology, PLCs and motor control systems (highly regarded)
• Strong fault-finding and diagnostic capability in control circuits
• Applicants from electrical or metal trades with strong mechanical aptitude are encouraged to apply
• Ability to work autonomously and as part of a collaborative team
• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
• Commitment to safety, quality and customer satisfaction
• Full training provided for candidates transitioning into the lift sector

Benefits
• Competitive salary package based on experience
• Company vehicle included
• Overtime available with penalty rates
• Monthly RDO
• Secure, long-term role with career development opportunities
• Comprehensive training and ongoing support
• Supportive, team-oriented workplace culture

Our client is a long-standing Australian organisation and a recognised leader in the lift and accessibility sector. They specialise in platform lifts, stair-lifts, residential and commercial elevators, and wheelchair access solutions.

Renowned for their high-quality products and exceptional after-sales service, the business has built a strong reputation for reliability, safety and long-term client relationships. Their continued growth is driven by a people-first culture that values teamwork, professionalism and continuous improvement.

jobs.title: Area Administrator - Part time
jobs.description:
Sales Administrator  
Going Up Elevators  
Dee Why, Sydney NSW  
Client & Sales Administration (Administration & Office Support)  
Part time  
Posted 20d ago

Sales Administrator  
Part-Time | Dee Why Head Office | 18–24 hours per week  

About Us  
Going Up is one of the largest independent Australian owned and operated elevator companies, manufacturing, installing, servicing and modernising lifts along the east coast of Australia. We are a Tier 1 subcontractor specialising in premium, robust elevator solutions, with 98% of our business coming from repeat customers. Known for our excellent customer service, fast turnaround times, and products that don’t let clients down, we design and deliver lifts built for longevity and reliability.  

About the Role  
We are seeking a motivated Sales Administrator to join our team at our head office in Dee Why. Reporting to the Sales Manager, this role plays a key part in helping us meet our 24-hour turnaround commitment to clients. You’ll assist with tenders, quotes, and customer communication, ensuring smooth sales processes while providing a high level of service.  

This role offers variety, responsibility, and the opportunity to grow. Your attention to detail, initiative, and customer focus will set you up for success.  

Key Responsibilities  
- Monitor and manage sales enquiries and new tender opportunities.  
- Prepare and coordinate tender submissions, quotes, and pricing information.  
- Support the Sales Manager with day-to-day sales functions and customer communication.  
- Build and maintain strong client relationships.  
- Liaise with internal teams (design, drafting, construction, finance, service) to ensure seamless delivery.  
- Assist in identifying and reporting on new business opportunities.  

Key Criteria  
- Strong attention to detail and accuracy in all tasks.

Sales Administrator  
Going Up Elevators  

Key Criteria  
• Strong attention to detail and accuracy in all tasks.  
• Solid work ethic with the ability to work independently and in a team.  
• Proficiency with computers, office software, and tendering packages.  
• Organised, reliable, and eager to learn.  
• Strong communication skills to build lasting customer relationships.  

What We Offer  
• Part-time hours starting at 18–24 per week, worked across 3 days (with flexibility for the right candidate).  
• A supportive and professional team environment.  
• Opportunities for career progression within the business.  
• Exposure to varied and rewarding projects.  

How to Apply  
If you are ready to embark on an exciting journey in the elevator industry, this is the opportunity for you! Apply now for an immediate start and become a valued member of the Going Up team.  

Apply via Seek with your resume and a short cover letter explaining why this role is right for you. No recruiters.

jobs.title: Warehouse Technician
jobs.description:
Service Technician

Otis – South Melbourne VIC

Date Posted: 2026-02-17 Country: Australia Location: LOC7013 : 2-12 Montague Street, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia Would you like to join a truly international, talent-driven company that values Safety, Ethics, Quality, Innovation, and Employee Opportunity? Otis is growing and is looking for a Service Mechanic based in Melbourne, Victoria. Your priority is to ensure the safety of passengers and technical stakeholders by safely maintaining a portfolio of elevator and escalator units.

- Permanent role
- Coverage of Melbourne CBD portfolio
- Reports to the Service Manager

On a typical day you will..
- Carry out 100% of the elevator Maintenance and Service visits of the units within your portfolio.
- Work within your defined geographical area/territory, managing the portfolio of elevators.
- Respond proactively and quickly to fix any breakdowns, aiming to achieve first-time fixes.
- Carry out minor repairs to achieve continual operations.
- Undertake Upgrade & Modernisation works.
- You will be available to participate in an after-hours on-call roster
- Liaise directly with the customer while on site to build a good working relationship.

To be successful you will need to be successful
- Trade qualifications as an Electrical Fitter/Mechanic with an Electronics degree/diploma/certificate.

What you will need to be successful
- Trade qualifications as an Electrical Fitter/Mechanic with an Electronics degree/diploma/certificate.
- A minimum of four (4) years proven successful experience in elevator maintenance and/or installation.
- Have a current Electrical license.
- Hold valid driver's license.
- Self-motivated and able to operate without supervision.
- Good verbal and written communication skills.
- Has the ability to deal with and influence customers and colleagues/peers.

Benefits
- You will receive a long-term employment contract with the world market leader in a crisis-proof industry.
- We offer you remuneration in accordance with local EBA.
- You will be trained intensively in the areas of technology, processes & soft skills, and you can exchange ideas with experienced colleagues at any time.
- The health and safety of our employees is our top priority. We promote this through regular training on the subject of occupational health and safety as well as through an employee support program.

Apply today to join us and build what’s next! If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day. Otis is the world’s leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2.4 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.4 million customer units worldwide, the industry’s largest Service portfolio. You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performance company. When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative global industry leader with a resilient business model. You’ll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where your contributions, and the skills and capabilities you’ll gain working alongside the best and brightest, keep us connected and on the cutting edge. We provide opportunities, training, and resources, that build leadership and capabilities in Sales, Field, Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Scholar Program is a notable point of pride, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certification programs. Today, our focus is more than ever on its people. As a global, people-powered company, we put people – passengers, customers, and colleagues – at the center of everything we do. We are guided by our values that we call our Three Absolutes – prioritizing safety, getting the job done, and sustaining our environment, social and governance (ESG) at Otis. We promote a path to professional growth and a culture of inclusion where you can thrive.

You'll work closely with customers to provide expert advice and solutions, ensuring their lifting equipment is reliable and safe to use.  

If you have rigging, engineering or hands-on experience working at heights. We want to hear from you!  

Join us in ensuring the safety and reliability of lifting equipment while building a rewarding career in a growing industry.  

Key Responsibilities  

- Perform inspections, testing, and certification of lifting equipment, including chains, slings, hoists, and other lifting gear.  
- Conduct repairs and maintenance on lifting equipment to ensure optimal performance and compliance with safety standards.  
- Provide technical advice and support to customers regarding lifting solutions and equipment.  
- Maintain accurate records of inspections, repairs, and certifications.  
- Ensure all work complies with relevant Australian Standards and safety regulations.  
- Collaborate with the sales team to identify customer needs and recommend appropriate lifting solutions.  
- Uphold a strong commitment to workplace safety and quality standards.

Who are you:

- Can do attitude with a strong work ethic
- Basic understanding of Australian Standards.
- Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills.
- Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
- Excellent communication and customer service skills.
- High attention to detail and commitment to quality.
- Current driver's license is essential
- A commitment to safety and compliance.

What We Offer

- New company vehicle, laptop, iPad and phone.
- NATA & LEEA national certifications.
- A supportive and collaborative team environment.
- Full day induction to help you settle into the business and position.
- Access to employee benefits, including discounts on products and services.
- Opportunities for career growth and development within the GPC network.
- Uniforms supplied - feel like a team.
- Recognition programs and bonus scheme for your performance.
- 1 paid Volunteer Leave Day per calendar year

- Parental leave top-up allowance

the east coast of Australia.

across a range of residential and commercial buildings throughout the Sydney metropolitan area.

Sydney region.

jobs.description:
TRADE ASSISTANT - MECHANICAL ASSEMBLY - LIFTING INDUSTRY  
Recruit Inc Pte Ltd – Sydney NSW  
$30 - $40 an hour  
Permanent  
1mo ago, from Recruit Australia  

RECRUIT AUSTRALIA PROFESSIONALS  
SPECIALIST RECRUITERS - ENGINEERING, MANAGEMENT, SALES & ACCOUNTING.  

- REF NUMBER - 537  
- AFP & ASIC Checks apply  
- Must have current drivers Lic  
- Forklift licence ideal  
- Must have mechanical assembly work exp.  
- Maybe suitable for car mechanic looking for a change.  

This is a great opportunity for a Trade Assistant to join a successful Aviation Lifting

Equipment Specialist Company in Sydney. We are looking for an experienced TA with mechanical assembly experience or a trades person and forklift licence who can assist the technicians with carrying out inspections, testing and maintenance of lifting equipment and tooling within the Aviation Industry.

This new position will involve a variety of tasks, so it is necessary that you are flexible with your skills. Reporting to the Technical Engineers, in addition to working alongside the Inspection Technicians.

You must have a high attention to detail as well as experience with assembling and disassembling machinery. Interstate travel is a part of the role and a non-smoker is preferred as most sites are non smoking. You will also need to go through Federal police checks and medicals.

Role requirements:

- Drivers licence & own transport
- AFP & ASIC Checks required
- Medical assessment (to be completed prior to commencement of the role)
- Mechanical assembly experience
- Experience as a trade assistant or supporting technical role

- Full time permanent employment

- Excellent wage with growth

jobs.description:
Open app
Sign in
Installations & Service Technician
Acorn Stairlifts Pty Ltd 
View all jobs
Newcastle, Newcastle, Maitland & Hunter NSW
Technicians (Trades & Services)
Full time
$80,000 – $100,000 per year
Posted 5h ago
Acorn Stairlifts is the Australian division of the world's leading manufacturer and distributor of
stairlifts to help people with stairs in their homes. The home-care industry in Australia is currently
going through a continued period of growth, making it a very attractive career choice. Acorn, as
a leader in this field, continues to drive forward its growth throughout Australia. To jump on this
growth and our further plans for the future, we are looking for additional capable and enthusiastic
Installations and service Technicians to support our expanding Operations team. 
This fantastic opportunity will involve installing, servicing and repairing our stairlift installations in
the Newcastle and central coast area. There is also be the need for regular regional travel and
interstate travel for training. 
This is a Full time role with lots of overtime possibilities and you are paid door to door!
Technicians work an average 43 hour weeks.
You work from your home at various work sites across the Newcastle/central coast area. 
About You
The ideal candidate will:
Must have current Full Australian working rights
Clean Australian Drivers licence and Police check
Mechanically minded
Good physical fitness and able to lift heavy items on a regular basis. 
Good attention to detail.
Reliable and punctual.
Knowledge and experience in using powered tools desirable.
Able to work autonomously
A background in stairlifts is not essential as full training will be given.
The job requires work in the homes of our customers, many of whom are aged and therefore the
successful candidates will need to be well presented, tidy, honest and be able to demonstrate
pride in a job well done.
Why join us?
Competitive salary package including fully expensed company vehicle (van) and mobile
phone
On the road, change of scenery everyday!
2 - 4 weeks of PAID training
Get paid from when you leave home to return home, Door to Door!
Be part of a fun and enthusiastic team!
Put your technical abilities to good use helping other people live safely at home.
World market-leading company
Interesting and varied role
Potential for interstate travel
Your skills and customer service will be acknowledged and rewarded
How to Apply :
To apply for this role, please apply now via SEEK.
Any questions please email: recruitment@acornstairlifts.com.au
For more information about Acorn please visit www.acornstairlifts.com.au
Please note: Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

Ravinder (pictured above), a new equipment technician, was part of the Chantier École in France, an innovative recruiting and training program.

When Cheuk Lun joined Otis working in Hong Kong SAR, he wasn’t certain about his career choice, but having mentors early in his career helped guide him to success.

“I felt overwhelmed when I first started working in the industry. My mentors played a key role in helping me navigate this new world, grow, and finally foster a passion for pursuing a career in the elevator industry,” said Cheuk Lun, who now works as a service mechanic in Hong Kong.

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.  

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers!  

About You  

We are looking for a Project Manager based in Sydney, NSW. This is a full-time role and reports to the Construction Manager - NSW/ACT.  

In this role, you will be key in managing and administering Otis New Equipment (NE) and Modernization (MODs) contracts according to Otis ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).  

• Permanent role  
• Based in Sydney CBD

Modernization (MODs) contracts according to Otis ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).

• Permanent role

• Based in Sydney CBD

• Competitive base pay, car allowance and project management incentive

As a Project Manager, your key responsibilities will include

• Maintains a high-performance team by recruiting, developing, and motivating field labor, setting appropriate standards of performance, and coaching the team.

• Manage and Administer Otis New Equipment and Modernization contracts according to the Otis’ ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).

• Ensures that Installation teams maintain approved site programs, attend site meetings, and exercise line authority over all allocated installation field labor.

• Proactively reports project risk and deliverable issues.

• Sets objectives and priorities, assigns and reviews tasks, performance, and staffing requirements while maintaining the resource plan.

• Develop and maintain strong relationships with customers, government inspectors, consultants, and within Otis to understand business challenges and recommend innovative integrated business solutions.

• Ensures that reports, contract records, site diaries, accident reports, time sheets, and manpower charts are completed and submitted in a timely manner.

• Participates in monthly project reviews with the branch and head office.

What you will need to be successful

• A tertiary qualification in Building or Engineering and/or an Electrical or Mechanical Trade Certificate is preferred.

• Has 3-5 years experience of leading teams of mechanics, installers, technicians or sub-contractors.

- A tertiary qualification in Building or Engineering and/or an Electrical or Mechanical Trade Certificate is preferred.

- Has 3-5 years experience of leading teams of mechanics, installers, technicians or sub-contractors

- Be a self-starter who can work safely and efficiently in a dynamic environment.

- Committed to excellence and innovation, and have a good work ethics.

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.

Otis is the world’s leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.2 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician (Brisbane Based)
jobs.description:
Elevator Service Technician

BOSS Elevators – Birtinya QLD

Full time

13d ago, from seek.com.au

Apply on company site

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will play a crucial part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sunshine Coast to Discovery Coast region.

As an expert technician, you'll be responsible for conducting routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs, with a focus on quickly diagnosing and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator components. Your ability to think critically and implement effective solutions will be essential to ensuring optimal functionality and compliance with AS1735 standards.

You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to "Leading the Change" in elevator maintenance.

This role is suited for someone who is not only technically skilled but also values quality workmanship, safety, and client satisfaction. If you take pride in overcoming challenges and delivering high-quality results, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

What you'll be doing

• Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventive maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing

What you’ll be doing

- **Routine Maintenance**: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing
- **Diagnose & Troubleshoot Issues**: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as motors, controllers, and safety devices. Your expertise will be pivotal in delivering swift, accurate solutions
- **Carry Out Repair Work**: Conduct precise repairs, replacing worn or faulty parts as needed to restore full elevator functionality and reliability.
- **Maintain Detailed Records**: Keep thorough, accurate records of all maintenance, service, and repair activities, ensuring clear documentation for compliance and future reference.
- **Ensure Safety & Compliance**: Maintain strict adherence to all safety standards and regulations, upholding safety as a non-negotiable priority in every task.
- **Provide On-Call Support**: Be prepared to respond to emergency situations as part of our rotational standby roster, ensuring timely support and effective resolution of urgent issues.
- **Liaise with Clients**: Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.
- **Continuous Skill Development**: Participate in training events and workshops.

What we’re looking for

- **Positive, proactive attitude** and a commitment to safety and quality service.
- **Minimum 5 years of experience** in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.
- Queensland Electrical Licence (no restrictions).
- White card.
- Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).
- Rigging and Dogging certification (favourable but not essential).
- Certificate IV in WHS (favourable but not essential).
- Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer

- **Competitive Remuneration**: Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.
- **Coastal Lifestyle**: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most

Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer
- **Competitive Remuneration:** Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.
- **Coastal Lifestyle:** Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most sought-after coastal regions.
- **Motivating Team Culture:** We believe in creating a positive work environment where your skills are valued, and your career can thrive.
- **Vehicle and Tools:** The whole kit and caboodle

Application Process
Please submit your **resume**, outlining your experience, qualifications, and referees, by **15 March 2026**.

Applications must also include a **cover letter** explaining why you are an ideal candidate for this role.

Shortlisted applicants will be contacted by **15 March 2026**.

Interviews will be completed by **20 March 2026**, with an employment offer extended to the successful candidate shortly thereafter.

GPC Pacific Asia – Derwent Park TAS

As a Lifting Technician, you'll be responsible for inspecting, testing, repairing, and maintaining lifting equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards and regulations. You'll work closely with customers to provide expert advice and solutions, ensuring their lifting equipment is reliable and safe to use.  

If you have rigging, engineering or hands-on experience working at heights. We want to hear from you!  

IF YOU don't have these skills, that is no problem we'll train and teach THE RIGHT PERSON!  

What we will require from you is: a keen eye for detail, a can do attitude, a team player and above all a keenness to learn. It's a physical roll to some degree, a customer facing roll and an exciting technical roll once you are trained and competent.  

Join us in ensuring the safety and reliability of lifting equipment for industry is safe, whilst building a rewarding career with a Global company.

Join us in ensuring the safety and reliability of lifting equipment for industry is safe, whilst building a rewarding career with a Global company.

Key Responsibilities

- Perform inspections, testing, and certification of lifting equipment, including chains, slings, hoists, and other lifting gear.
- Conduct repairs and maintenance on lifting equipment to ensure optimal performance and compliance with safety standards.
- Provide technical advice and support to customers regarding lifting solutions and equipment.
