jobs.title: Electronic trades Worker - Vertical transportation
jobs.location: Australia, Tasmania, Hobart Region, Hobart, North Hobart
jobs.description:
Electronics Technician

VERTICAL TRANSPORT SOLUTIONS PTY LTD – Wyee NSW

✔ Verified employer

Full time

14h ago, from Jora

Quick apply

VERTICAL TRANSPORT SOLUTIONS PTY LTD is hiring a Full time Electronics Technician role in Wyee, NSW. Apply now to be part of our team.

Requirements for this role:
• Flexible hours available
• 2-3 years of relevant work experience required for this role

Company Overview:
Vertical Transport Solutions Pty Ltd (VTS) is a leading provider of escalator and elevator installations, servicing, and modifications. We also specialise in security systems, home automation, and CCTV. Due to our continued growth, we are seeking a skilled Electronic Equipment Technician/Trades Worker to join our team.

Vertical Transport Solutions Pty Ltd (VTS) is a leading provider of escalator and elevator installations, servicing, and modifications. We also specialise in security systems, home automation, and CCTV. Due to continued growth, we are seeking a skilled Electronic Equipment Technician/Trades Worker to join our team.

This role is critical in supporting the electronic and control system functionality of vertical transport mechanisms. It involves installing, calibrating, testing, troubleshooting, repairing, and maintaining electronic components and control systems of vertical transport equipment to ensure optimal performance, reliability, and compliance with safety and industry standards.

Key Responsibilities:

- Install, calibrate, and adjust electronic equipment and control systems specifically for lifts and escalators to meet required technical and safety standards.
- Test and troubleshoot electronic circuits, sensors, controllers, and interface systems within lift machinery to identify and resolve faults.
- Repair and replace faulty electronic components, wiring, control panels, and system parts to ensure the continuous operation of lift systems.
- Perform routine servicing and preventative maintenance on electronic and control components of vertical transport systems.
- Read and interpret technical drawings, wiring diagrams, and schematics relevant to lift installation and electronic control systems.
- Work with PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers), variable speed drives, and motor control systems integrated in modern elevator technology.
- Conduct electrical safety checks and compliance testing according to Australian lift industry standards and WHS requirements.
- Collaborate with lift mechanics, electricians, and technicians to enhance service procedures and technical performance.

Required Skills & Qualifications:

- Qualifications in Electrician or Electrical Engineering.
- Experience with elevator systems, motor drives, and control equipment.
- Strong troubleshooting skills and a solid understanding of electronic principles.
- Ability to read technical documentation and schematics.
- Knowledge of safety standards and regulations in the electrical industry.
- Excellent communication skills and a commitment to teamwork.

- Collaborate with lift mechanics, electricians, and technicians to enhance service procedures and technical performance.

Required Skills & Qualifications:

- Certificate III or IV in Electronics, Computer Hardware Servicing, Electrical Fitting, or equivalent qualifications relevant to the lift industry.

- Hands-on experience with electronic and electrical control systems used in elevator and escalator operations.

- Strong troubleshooting and diagnostic skills with lift electronics, sensors, control boards, and automation systems.

- Proven ability to read and interpret lift control schematics, layout diagrams, and circuit drawings.

- Familiarity with PLCs, safety relays, motor drives, and logic control used in elevator operations.

- Sound knowledge of workplace safety procedures, risk assessment, and electrical compliance practices.

- Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to work independently or as part of a lift service and installation team.

What We Offer:

- Competitive salary and industry-aligned benefits

- Ongoing training, professional development, and certification opportunities in the lift industry

- A supportive, safety-focused, and collaborative team environment
jobs.description_summary: Vertical Transport Solutions Pty Ltd is seeking a full-time Electronics Technician in Wyee, NSW, to install, test, and maintain electronic and control systems for elevators and escalators. Applicants should have 2-3 years of relevant experience, qualifications in Electrical Engineering or as an Electrician, and strong troubleshooting skills.

$47 – $56.88 per hour
Posted 22d ago
Lift Fitter (Year-Round)
Resort: Falls Creek
Employment Type: Permanent, Full Time
Remuneration: $47.00 – $56.88 per hour (dependent on experience & qualifications)
Agreement: Falls Creek and Hotham Trades Maintenance Agreement 2025
Applications Close: 22nd March 2026
Falls Creek is seeking experienced, trade-qualified Lift Fitters to join our Lift Maintenance team
on a full-time permanent contract. This position is ideal for mechanically minded trades
professionals who enjoy hands-on work, problem-solving, and maintaining complex lift systems
in a dynamic alpine environment.
The Opportunity
As a Lift Fitter, you will support the maintenance and safe operation of Falls Creek’s extensive lift
network, including fixed grip and detachable chairlifts, surface lifts, conveyors, and associated
mechanical systems. Working closely with the Lift Maintenance Manager, Supervisors and
broader maintenance team, you will perform scheduled servicing, fault diagnostics, mechanical
repairs, component replacements, and system testing to ensure lift reliability and compliance
with regulatory standards.
This role requires a high level of mechanical aptitude, adherence to safe work practices, and the
ability to work efficiently in variable weather conditions, including during peak winter
operations. Lift Fitters are employed under the Falls Creek and Hotham Trades Maintenance
Agreement 2025 with a base hourly rate of $47.00 – $56.88 per hour (dependent on experience
& qualifications)
What You’ll Be Doing
Perform maintenance, repair, and installation of ski lift and ropeway equipment
Conduct daily, weekly, and monthly inspections to ensure safe and reliable lift operations
Diagnose and troubleshoot mechanical, hydraulic, and extra-low-voltage control issues
Support tower maintenance, sheave train alignment, and tensioning systems
Carry out scheduled preventative maintenance and maintain accurate service records
Respond promptly to lift breakdowns and operational issues during resort hours
Operate heavy equipment and hand tools safely and efficiently
Assist with lift construction, upgrades, and decommissioning projects as required
Support snow clearing and access preparation for lift maintenance areas
Assist vehicle maintenance staff as required
The Expertise You Bring
A recognised Mechanical Trade qualification (Fitter & Turner, Mechanical Fitter, or equivalent)
Minimum 2 years’ post-apprenticeship experience in mechanical maintenance or fitting roles,
including working at heights and following safe work procedures
Strong mechanical diagnostics skills and ability to identify and resolve faults
Current Driver’s Licence
Ability to work effectively in alpine and winter conditions.
Highly Desirable:
Hydraulic qualification
High-risk work licences (dogging, rigging, EWP, forklift)
First Aid qualification
Skiing or snowboarding ability
Why Join Falls Creek
Work with unique alpine lift systems and machinery offering real technical variety
Grow your career in a permanent, full-time role with long-term stability
Join a skilled maintenance team focused on safety, precision, and quality
Vail Resorts Australia Staff Lift Pass for the season
Up to 40% discounts on food, retail, and rentals across Vail Resorts Australia
A supportive and inclusive team culture that celebrates fun, professionalism, and mountain life
How to Apply
Submit your application via our Career Center.
Our Commitment
At Vail Resorts Australia, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. If you
require support with your application or interview process, please contact us.
Our Core Values
At Vail Resorts, our values guide everything we do — Be Inclusive, Do Good, Be Safe, Have Fun,
Serve Others, Drive Value, and Do Right. We’re proud to foster a culture where everyone belongs
and thrives, reflecting the diversity of our guests and communities.
Build your trade career in the mountains—join the team that keeps Falls Creek moving.

jobs.title: MEWP Service Technician & Lift Specialist
jobs.description:
Service Technician - Lift and Escalator Industry  
Litronic – Brisbane QLD  
Full time  
29d ago, from seek.com.au  

About Us  
We are a leading independent company specializing in the design, installation, maintenance, and modernization of elevators and escalators. For over 40 years, we have delivered innovative and tailored vertical transport solutions to a diverse range of clients across Australia. With a strong focus on safety, quality, and customer satisfaction, we are proud to be a trusted partner to thousands of businesses and organizations.  

About the Role  
As a Service Technician, you will oversee the planning, coordination, and execution of lift and escalator service portfolio across Brisbane and surrounds. This is a hands-on role where you will work closely with the QLD Service Manager, other technicians, and clients to ensure high-quality, safe, and timely delivery of maintenance visits and reduce downtime of units.  

Key Responsibilities  
- Manage service run schedules, ensuring deadlines are met.  
- Conduct site inspections to maintain safety, compliance, and quality standards.  
- Act as the primary point of contact for clients, providing updates and addressing concerns.  
- Ensuring downtime of units is kept to a minimum

About You

To succeed in this role, you will need to demonstrate:

- A strong background in the lift and escalator industry,
- Experience in the lift and escalator industry favourable.
- Technical expertise in lift and escalator installations/service.
- Strong technical fault-finding abilities.
- Excellent organizational and time management skills.
- Strong communication abilities, with a focus on fostering a positive team environment.
- A commitment to safety, quality, and client satisfaction.
- A valid Driver’s license
- A valid Electrical license
- A valid White Card certification is required for this position.

What We Offer

- Competitive EBA rates including allowances.
- Company car, laptop, phone, uniform.
- Opportunities for overtime.
- Opportunities for professional development and career advancement.
- A supportive and collaborative work environment.

jobs.title: Elevator Technician
jobs.description:
Elevator Service Technician

Otis – Darwin NT  
3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK  
Full time  
3d ago, from seek.com.au  

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World’s Best Employers!

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

About you  
We are looking for a Service Technician to based in Darwin, Northern Territory. In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.

- Permanent role
- Reports to the Branch Manager

Key responsibilities for YOU  
- Performs Service and Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals.
- Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers.
- Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

- Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-ropeing, Hoisting, and Rigging.

- Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and/or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement.

- Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

- Standby and provide after-call-back coverage from time to time.

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally

- Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

- Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.

- Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.

- Sales Team: Sales Account Representatives, Managers, and Coordinators (where relevant).

- External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers.

What YOU will need to be successful

- Have Electrical and/or Fitter qualifications as relevant to the role and Lift apprenticeship or other industry-accredited training program (desirable)

- Minimum two (2) to five (5) years’ experience within the Lift Industry.

- Hold a valid driver’s license and Electrical license.

- Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

- Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training and demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills.

- Strong English language skills, both verbal and written

- Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet).

- You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority

- Has attention to detail and is results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team.

**Apply today to join us and build what’s next!**

We are the world's leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world's largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world's most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

**Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion**  
We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity; Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation

jobs.description:
Lifting Technician  
GPC Pacific Asia – Derwent Park TAS  
Permanent  
25d ago, from GPC Pacific Asia  

As a Lifting Technician, you'll be responsible for inspecting, testing, repairing, and maintaining lifting equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards and regulations. You'll work closely with customers to provide expert advice and solutions, ensuring their lifting equipment is reliable and safe to use.

If you have a LEEA Diploma, engineering certification, or hands-on experience as a Lifting Technician? We want to hear from you! We also encourage EX ADF personal to apply as we value your experience, skillset, discipline and work ethic that ex - ADF personal bring to the workforce.

Key Responsibilities  
- Perform inspections, testing, and certification of lifting equipment, including chains, slings, hoists, and other lifting gear.  
- Conduct repairs and maintenance on lifting equipment to ensure optimal performance and compliance with safety standards.  
- Provide technical advice and support to customers regarding lifting solutions and equipment.  
- Maintain accurate records of inspections, repairs, and certifications.  
- Ensure all work complies with relevant Australian Standards and safety regulations.  
- Collaborate with the sales team to identify customer needs and recommend appropriate lifting solutions.  
- Uphold a strong commitment to workplace safety and quality standards.

Who are you:

- Can do attitude with a strong work ethic
- Knowledge of Australian Standards related to lifting equipment and safety (e.g., AS 4991, AS 1418).
- LEEA Diploma (or equivalent established training in lifting equipment or Engineering certification)
- Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills.
- Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
- Excellent communication and customer service skills.
- High attention to detail and commitment to quality.
- Current driver’s license is essential
- A commitment to safety and compliance.

What We Offer:

- A supportive and collaborative team environment.
- Full day induction to help you settle into the business and position.
- Access to employee benefits, including discounts on products and services.
- Opportunities for career growth and development within the GPC network.
- Uniforms supplied - feel like a team.
- Recognition programs and bonus scheme for your performance.
- 1 paid Volunteer Leave Day per calendar year
- Free access to webinars, training events and courses; we promote a culture of continuous learning and assisting you to reach your full potentials.
- Parental leave top-up allowance
- Employee Assistance Program with access to mental health and well-being support initiatives including confidential counselling services
- Company vehicle available, depending on experience

About Motion:
We offer top-quality products and services to keep our customers moving. This is your chance to be part of a passionate team that thrives on delivering exceptional service and building strong relationships with our valued customers.

Join us on this exciting journey of success and growth and let us help you empower your career with us!

A fantastic opportunity exists for an experienced Lift Engineer to join our business.

With over 20 years of experience, Advanced Cranes and Rigging is a national leader in lifting
solutions. We support Tier 1 government and private sector projects with a modern fleet ranging
from 3t to 800+ tonnes. Safety, innovation, and quality are the pillars of our business—
underpinned by ISO45001 accreditation and a practical, values-driven culture.
 
Role Overview:
This role requires a qualified Lift Engineer with extensive experience in the Mobile Crane industry.
In this role, you will be a valued member of the Engineering team, and responsible for lift
planning, site inspections and logistical arrangements of lift plans. 
Lift plans will be for Tier One projects, along with other infrastructure and commercial projects.
Role is based out of our North Brisbane depot.
  
Key Responsibilities:
Collaborate closely with Project Managers / Safety / Operations team and other stakeholders
to ensure seamless project execution.
Plan and coordinate lift studies in a timely fashion.
 Foundation for success:
Bachelor, Diploma, or Post-graduate degree in Civil/ Mechanical Engineering or similar
related field.
3 years’ experience in a similar engineer role (exp in rail, wind or infrastructure projects highly
regarded)
Proficient in CAD software, 2D AUTOCAD in particular, 3D advantageous.  Proficient in
LICCON Work Planner highly desirable.
Familiarity with mobile, crawler and pick & carry cranes; Liebherr and Terex/Franna in
particular.
Excellent Office365 skills (Word, Excel, MS Projects, Teams).
Familiar with safety and quality standards in the construction/crane industry.
Customer relationship management experience.
Experience in project management advantageous but not essential.
Organisational, communication and time management skills.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
An attractive package will be tailored for the best candidate.

Service Technician - Lift and Escalator Industry  
Lifttronic – Brisbane QLD  
Full time  
25d ago, from seek.com.au  

About Us  
We are a leading independent company specializing in the design, installation, maintenance, and modernization of elevators and escalators. For over 40 years, we have delivered innovative and tailored vertical transport solutions to a diverse range of clients across Australia. With a strong focus on safety, quality, and customer satisfaction, we are proud to be a trusted partner to thousands of businesses and organizations.

About the Role  
As a Service Technician, you will oversee the planning, coordination, and execution of lift and escalator service portfolio across Brisbane and surrounds. This is a hands-on role where you will work closely with the QLD Service Manager, other technicians, and clients to ensure high-quality, safe, and timely delivery of maintenance visits and reduce downtime of units.

Key Responsibilities  
- Manage service run schedules, ensuring deadlines are met.  
- Conduct site inspections to maintain safety, compliance, and quality standards.  
- Act as the primary point of contact for clients, providing updates and addressing concerns.  
- Ensuring downtime of units is kept to a minimum

To succeed in this role, you will need to demonstrate:

- A strong background in the lift and escalator industry,

- Strong technical fault-finding abilities.
- Excellent organizational and time management skills.
- Strong communication abilities, with a focus on fostering a positive team environment.
- A commitment to safety, quality, and client satisfaction.
- A valid Driver’s license

Join a supportive team where you're valued, not just a number.  

Going Up is one of the largest independent Australian-owned and operated elevator companies, manufacturing, installing, servicing, and modernising lifts along the east coast of Australia.  

We have a great community of team members and are looking for skilled Elevator Service Technicians to join our team.  

Our service technicians work within a well-resourced team, with daily jobs scheduled by our admin staff and clear communication for any changes. Company vehicle, phone, paid overtime, and on-call work provided.

Workshop Mechanic / Technician
All Lift Forklifts & Access Equipment – Hallam, VIC
Join a Growing Industry Leader – National Distributor of Premium Equipment Brands
About Us
All Lift Forklifts & Access Equipment is a proudly family-owned and operated business, with
operations across VIC and other eastern states. We specialise in the rental, sales, and service of
forklifts, telehandlers, and access equipment. As the appointed national distributor for Magni
Telescopic Handlers and Taylor Heavy Forklifts, the business has experienced significant growth.
To support our continued expansion, we’re looking for a Qualified Workshop
Mechanic/Technician to join our dedicated service team at our Hallam workshop.
The Role
You’ll play a key part in ensuring our fleet is maintained to the highest standard. This hands-on
role includes:
Diagnosing mechanical, electrical and hydraulic faults
Servicing and maintaining a range of lifting and access equipment
Pre-hire and off-hire inspections
Supporting breakdown and repair works as needed
You’ll work closely with a team of skilled technicians, delivering quality service across a wide
range of industry-leading equipment.
What We’re Looking For
Mechanical Trade Qualification (Diesel, Forklift, Heavy Vehicle, Light Vehicle, or Mobile Plant)
Solid understanding of diesel, electrical and hydraulic systems
Current driver’s licence
Forklift licence (desirable)
Ability to work efficiently, with a strong sense of urgency and pride in workmanship
About You
You’re more than just mechanically skilled — you’re a hands-on problem solver who thrives in a
team environment. With a solid mechanical background and a passion for getting things done
right, you take pride in your work and always put the customer first. Self-motivated, dependable,
and driven to contribute, you’re ready to be a key player in a supportive business that values your
expertise.
What We Offer
Competitive above-award wages, aligned with experience
Stable, full-time employment with a growing national company
A friendly, supportive team culture
Ongoing development opportunities within a high-demand industry
Ready to take the next step in your mechanical career?
Apply now and be part of a team that values your skills and supports your growth.

jobs.title: Maintenance Electrician
jobs.description:
Maintenance Technician  
KONE – Perth WA  
2.3 from 83 reviews at SEEK  
Full time  
15d ago from KONE  

• Great benefits - 2 RDO’s per month, competitive base wage, overtime available  
• Work with industry - leading products and technology  
• Join a global organisation with a strong focus on training, safety and quality  

At KONE, we bring buildings to life.  

Through our technical expertise and digital connectivity, we provide an essential service to communities around the world - keep people moving no matter what.  

Our work will take you in different directions.  

We’re looking for a Maintenance Technician to join our high performing Service Team in Perth, Australia. No two days are the same as you manage the equipment installation of KONE’s elevator products, across low-rise housing through to the world’s tallest skyscrapers.  

What will you be doing?  
• Attending elevator and escalator breakdowns  
• Attending to maintenance activities and repairs  
• Carrying out all activities in accordance with KONE policies and procedures including

- Attending to maintenance activities and repairs

- Lift industry experience preferred but not essential

- Motivated and self-starter who can manage workload and competing priorities
- Commitment to working safely with a strong focus on quality

jobs.title: Forklift Technician / Mechanic – Workshop & Field Service
jobs.description:
Forklift Technician / Mechanic – Workshop & Field Service  
Hyworth Forklifts  
View all jobs  

Prestons, Sydney NSW  
Automotive Trades (Trades & Services)  
Full time  
$45 – $47 per hour + quarterly bonus  

Posted 1mo ago

About us:  
Hyworth Forklifts is proudly celebrating 20 years as a trusted specialist in forklift hire, sales, and service across Sydney's warehouse and logistics sector. Established in 2006, we operate a modern fleet including Lithium Electric Counterbalance Forklifts, Reach Trucks, Walk-Behind, LPG/Gas, and Pallet Movers. Our business is experiencing strong growth, and we're expanding our team to meet rising customer demand.  

About the role:  
As a Forklift Technician, you'll deliver high-quality service, repairs, fault diagnosis, and scheduled maintenance on our hire fleet of material handling equipment.  
- Primarily workshop-based at our modern Prestons facility  
- Occasional field service call-outs using a company-provided vehicle for overflow/urgent work. This is a hands-on role where you'll work on a diverse range of late-model forklifts in a supportive, growing environment.  

We prefer candidates with proven forklift industry experience, but full in-house training on our specific fleet and systems will be provided. A minimum of 1–2 years' relevant mechanical experience is required.  

You'll play an active part in improving our operations—your ideas and input will be valued and rewarded via our quarterly bonus scheme.  

About you:  
- Trade qualified in a relevant field (e.g., Forklift, Diesel, Heavy Vehicle, Automotive, Auto Electrical)  
- Full working rights in Australia (Australian citizenship or permanent residency preferred)  
- Solid understanding of mechanical, hydraulic, and electrical systems  
- Comfortable completing jobs digitally using online software (basic computer skills required)  
- Current Australian Driver's Licence (manual transmission) and valid High Risk Work Forklift Licence (LF)  
- Strong commitment to safety, excellent communication skills, and a proactive, self-motivated attitude  
- Well-developed mechanical aptitude, good time management, and reliable work ethic  

What's on offer?  
- Competitive hourly rate $45–$47 p/h (based on experience) + superannuation  
- Fully maintained work van with tools of trade provided

Forklift Technician / Mechanic – Workshop & Field Service  
Hyworth Forklifts  
- Ongoing upskilling, on-the-job training, and career development  
- Overtime opportunities  
- Loyalty reward: $5,000 fully funded dream trip after 5 years of continuous service—we truly value long-term team members!  
  
How to Apply  
A telephone interview will be arranged first, followed by an in-person interview for suitable candidates. Successful applicants will need to complete a pre-employment medical and background check.  
  
Click Apply below and attach your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your relevant experience.  
  
We look forward to hearing from you!  
  
Introduction to Hyworth Forklifts  
Hyworth Forklifts  
Watch on YouTube

jobs.title: Facilities Operations Manager
jobs.description:
Operations Manager  
Direct Lifts – Kingsgrove NSW  

Full time  
6d ago, from seek.com.au  

Apply on company site  

Company Overview  
Direct Lifts Australia specialises in supply and install of modern, eye-catching lifts and elevators throughout the east coast of Australia. Working closely with architects, builders and property owners we collaborate and design lifts to suit our clients’ requirements and decor.  

With a strong reputation for quality, compliance, and service excellence, we are seeking an experienced Operations Manager to lead our Installation and Service Division, based at our Kingsgrove office in Sydney.  

Position Summary  
As Operations Manager, you will take full accountability for the performance of the installation and service operations, ensuring projects are delivered safely, efficiently, on time, and on budget.  

Key Responsibilities

As Operations Manager, you will take full accountability for the performance of the installation and service operations, ensuring projects are delivered safely, efficiently, on time, and on budget.

Key Responsibilities

Reporting directly to the Managing Director, you will be responsible for driving performance, profitability, and team engagement across the division. Additional responsibilities include:

- Lead and manage day-to-day operations across lift installation and service teams.
- Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and financial performance to meet agreed KPIs and profit targets.
- Drive operational efficiencies and implement cost control strategies.
- Manage workforce planning, scheduling, and resource allocation.
- Supervise and support technicians, project managers, service coordinators, administration staff, and sales personnel.
- Maintain high standards of safety, compliance, and customer satisfaction.
- Manage technical lift inventory and spare parts for NSW operations.
- Strengthen relationships with builders, developers, and residential clients.
- Foster a culture of accountability, teamwork, and continuous improvement.

About You

You are a confident and capable operational leader with a strong understanding of construction or lift installation environments. You combine technical knowledge with commercial awareness and people leadership skills.

Skills & Experience

- Previous experience in lift installation, construction, or related technical industry (highly desirable)
- Proven leadership experience managing operational teams.
- Strong financial acumen with demonstrated experience managing budgets and improving profitability.

- Previous experience in lift installation, construction, or related technical industry (highly desirable)
- Proven leadership experience managing operational teams.
- Strong financial acumen with demonstrated experience managing budgets and improving profitability.
- Experience using operational/service management systems (AroFlo highly desirable).
- Experience with Microsoft Business Central (advantageous).
- Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams across B2B and B2C client bases.
- Strong organisational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
- A proactive, solutions-focused leadership style.
- High-level competency in Microsoft Office Suite.

Benefits
- Competitive base salary commensurate with experience.
- Company vehicle and tools of trade.
- Performance-based incentive structure.
- A genuine opportunity to shape and influence operational strategy.
- Supportive leadership and collaborative team culture
- Long-term career growth within a stable and growing Australian business.

Application Process
If you are ready to take ownership of operations and drive performance within a respected lift company, we look forward to hearing from you.

Apply now and take the next step in your leadership career with Direct Lifts Australia.

As a Service Technician, you’ll work with our commitment to safety, quality, and customer service, while enjoying ongoing development and exceptional opportunities for your career.

Responsibilities:  
- Service, maintain, and inspect lifting equipment to the highest standards.  
- Conduct repairs and perform inspections according to manufacturer's specifications.  
- Hold the ability to achieve LEIA Certification.  
- Troubleshoot and determine the root cause of equipment malfunctions.  

Requirements:  
- Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills.  
- Current driver’s license (manual).  
- Knowledge of lift and tower identity (desired).  
- Technical skills and relevant trade qualification (advantageous).  
- Willingness to learn and develop.  

What's On Offer:  
- Competitive pay rate: $50-$60 per hour (based on experience).  
- 12% superannuation plus other incentive opportunities.  
- Full-time position with the chance to achieve LEIA Certification.  
- Career growth in a supportive team.  

Additional Information:  
- Apply only if you meet the listed requirements and are ready to start your career as a Lifting Equipment Technician! For further information email Wendy at [company email].  
- Additional pay range: $51-$63 per hour + 12% super-ann.  
- Career growth in a supportive team-oriented environment.

Be a part of Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

We are looking for Construction Mechanics to be based in and around Sydney, New South Wales, Australia.

You will be the face of the OTIS brand and responsible for installing lifts, escalators, and travelators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures.

jobs.title: Management Consultant
jobs.description:
Management Consultant  
Next Level Elevators Pty Ltd – Rosebery NSW  
Full time  
11d ago, from seek.com.au  

About the Business  
Next Level Elevators is an Australian-owned company specialising in the design, supply, installation and servicing of premium residential and light commercial elevators across New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland. The company delivers customised, all-electric lift solutions incorporating advanced European technology, energy-efficient systems and modern architectural integration for residential and commercial developments across Australia.

Due to continued national growth and increasing operational complexity, Next Level Elevators seeks an experienced Management Consultant to support strategic expansion, operational efficiency, and business performance improvement initiatives.

About the Role  
The Management Consultant provides advisory services to senior C-level management, reporting on organisational performance and making strategic recommendations to improve efficiency, service delivery, and commercial outcomes across our residential elevator business. The role focuses on investigating and analysing organisational systems,

About the Role

The Management Consultant provides advisory services to senior C-level management, reporting on organisational performance and making strategic recommendations to improve efficiency, service delivery, and commercial outcomes across our residential elevator business. The role focuses on investigating and analysing organisational systems, operational workflows, and management practices, and delivering evidence-based advice to support executive decision-making and long-term business objectives.

Key Responsibilities

• Analyse end-to-end business processes relating to residential elevator sales, design coordination, manufacturing scheduling, installation and servicing workflows, and breakdown response management.

• Review organisational workflows between sales, product design, manufacturing, electrical, logistics, and site construction teams to identify inefficiencies, duplication, and process gaps.

• Evaluate lead times, capacity constraints, and resource utilisation across elevator installations, scheduled servicing, and reactive repair activities.

• Provide ongoing executive advisory services to senior C-level management, including the Chief Operating Officer and State leadership team, on organisational performance, operational effectiveness, and strategic priorities.

• Prepare structured reports for executive leadership analysing business performance across lift installations, servicing, and repair operations.

• Report on organisational performance against agreed objectives, KPIs, and strategic plans, highlighting risks, constraints, and improvement opportunities.

• Make strategic recommendations to senior management based on quantitative analysis, operational data, and business trends.

• Assess the effectiveness of business systems used for job management, scheduling, and financial reporting (including HubSpot, Simpro, Dashboard and Xero) and recommend improvements to system usage and reporting.

- Assess the effectiveness of business systems used for job management, scheduling, and financial reporting (including HubSpot, Simpro, Dashboard and Xero) and recommend improvements to system usage and reporting.

- Develop reporting frameworks and management dashboards to provide visibility of performance across installations, servicing, and repair workstreams.

Skills and Experience Required

- Bachelor’s degree or higher qualification in Business, Management, Engineering or 5 years of relevant experience in lieu of qualification (required).

- Postgraduate qualification such as a Master of Business Administration (MBA) is highly desirable.

- Experience working within construction, manufacturing, or technically-oriented service businesses is advantageous.

- Strong analytical, reporting, and stakeholder communication skills.

- Proficiency in business systems and reporting tools (experience with HubSpot, Simpro, Xero, or similar platforms is advantageous).

- Current and valid driver’s licence.

Benefits

- Competitive salary package

- Collaborative and innovative work environment

- Long-term career development opportunities

Application Process

Applications should include a current CV and cover letter outlining relevant experience.

Next Level Elevators is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applications from suitably qualified candidates.
jobs.description_summary: Next Level Elevators Pty Ltd is seeking a full-time Management Consultant to provide advisory services to senior management, focusing on improving operational efficiency and business performance in their residential elevator segment. The role involves analyzing business processes, identifying inefficiencies, and making strategic recommendations to enhance service delivery and organizational performance.

jobs.company: Ranger Lifting Rigging Safety Pty Ltd
jobs.description:
Ranger Lifting Rigging Safety is a leading specialist in lifting, rigging and height-safety solutions, supporting customers across construction, manufacturing, transport, infrastructure and utilities. We are seeking a hands-on Technical Services Fitter to join our Inspection & Technical Services Workshop team.

About the role

Reporting to the Technical Services Manager, you will work across installation, workshop production and technical inspections of lifting, rigging and height-safety equipment. This is a varied, practical role suited to a trade-qualified fitter who enjoys both workshop work and customer sites.

Key responsibilities

- Perform in-house and on-site inspections of lifting, rigging and height-safety equipment
- Carry out installation of manufactured systems and associated components on customer sites
- Fit and assemble fabricated metal parts into products, machines and equipment
- Set up and operate production machines (including pressing and cutting) as required
- Read and interpret drawings and specifications to determine materials, methods and sequence of work
- Check components and assemblies for accuracy, clearance and fit using precision measuring instruments
- Conduct testing in line with NATA/LEEA and relevant standards, maintaining accurate registers and records
- Assist with booking, receipting and scheduling of inspection and workshop jobs
- Provide technical support and product advice to Ranger customers
- Work safely at all times and comply with company policies, procedures and WHS requirements

About you

- Trade qualification – Fitter / Mechanical / related trade (Certificate III or IV)
- Experience in lifting, rigging, height-safety or a closely related mechanical environment
- Strong practical fitting, assembly and fault-finding skills
- Ability to read and interpret drawings and technical specifications
- Comfortable working both in the workshop and on customer sites
- Sound computer skills for basic data entry and reporting
- Current NSW driver’s licence
- Strong attention to detail, with a “can-do” attitude and commitment to safety and quality
- Excellent communication skills and a customer-focused approach

Ranger Lifting Rigging Safety Pty Ltd  

What we offer  
- Full-time, stable position with a growing specialist business  
- Variety of work across installation, production and inspection  
- Training and development opportunities, including industry certifications  
- Supportive team environment with a strong safety and quality culture

Lift Technician / Electrician
Adapt Lifts & Electrical – Gold Coast / South East QLD
Adapt Lifts & Electrical is seeking a motivated and reliable technician to join our growing team
working across lift and escalator installations, repairs and upgrades.
We are ideally looking for someone with experience in the lift industry, however this is not
essential.
If you are not currently working in the lift industry, you MUST be a qualified electrician for your
application to be considered.
Candidates Ideally hold either a Trade Certificate or Licensed Electrician qualification to be
considered for this position.
If you are currently working in the lift industry, we will review applications even if you are not an
electrician.
About the Role
You will work across a wide variety of projects including:
Lift and escalator repairs
Installations and upgrades
Fault finding and electrical works
Working across commercial buildings, hotels and infrastructure projects
The role offers strong variety in the work, exposure to different equipment and the opportunity
to develop within the vertical transportation industry.
Work may occasionally involve:
Night works
Travel
Cleaning
Employment Structure
This position will commence on a casual basis.
This allows both parties to get to know each other before moving to a permanent full-time
position.
For the right candidate we are flexible and can initially offer trial days here and there so you do
not need to immediately leave your current role.
However, please note we have consistent work available and can provide full-time hours (5 days
per week) for the right person.
Mandatory Requirements
Applications will not be considered unless the following criteria are met:
Full working rights in Australia (non-negotiable)
Any valid driver's licence is acceptable for this position
If not currently in the lift industry – qualified electrician
Advantageous (but not essential)
Lift industry experience
Rigging ticket
Dogging ticket
What We Offer
Strong pipeline of work
A supportive and skilled team environment
Opportunity to build a long-term career in the lift industry
Variety of work across multiple projects
A company that rewards hard work and loyalty
If you are looking to join a growing and respected company in the lift industry, we encourage
you to apply.

About the role
Simplex Elevators, a leading provider of elevator installation and maintenance services, is seeking
a skilled Maintenance Technician to join our team in Brisbane, Queensland. This full-time role is
responsible for the preventative maintenance and repair of elevators, ensuring our clients' vertical
transportation systems operate efficiently and safely.
What you'll be doing
1. Conduct regular inspections and servicing of elevators to identify and address any issues
2. Troubleshoot and repair malfunctioning elevator components, such as motors, control
systems, and safety devices
3. Maintain detailed records of all maintenance activities and repairs
4. Respond promptly to emergency callouts and work to resolve issues in a timely manner
5. Collaborate with the broader team to ensure the seamless operation of our clients' elevator
systems
6. Adhere to all relevant safety regulations and procedures to maintain a safe working
environment
What we're looking for
1. Minimum 2 years of experience as an elevator maintenance technician or similar role
2. Strong mechanical and electrical troubleshooting skills, with the ability to diagnose and repair
a wide range of elevator components
3. Familiarity with elevator control systems and the ability to interpret technical manuals and
schematics
4. A valid driver's licence and the ability to travel to client sites as required
5. Excellent customer service skills and the ability to communicate effectively with clients
6. Commitment to safety and a proactive approach to identifying and mitigating risks
What we offer
At Simplex Elevators, we are committed to the development and wellbeing of our employees. In
addition to a competitive salary, we offer a range of benefits, including:
1. Comprehensive training and ongoing professional development opportunities
2. Discounted health insurance and gym memberships
3. Flexible work arrangements to support a healthy work-life balance
4. A supportive and collaborative team environment
About us
Simplex Elevators is a leading provider of elevator installation, maintenance, and repair services in
Australia. With over 30 years of industry experience, we are dedicated to delivering exceptional
service and ensuring the safe and reliable operation of our clients' elevator systems. Join our
team and be a part of a dynamic and growing company that values its employees and is
committed to their success.
Apply now for this exciting opportunity to become a Maintenance Technician with Simplex
Elevators.

jobs.title: Construction Mechanics
jobs.description:
Construction Project Manager - Vertical Transportation  
Otis – Sydney CBD NSW  
3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK  
Full time  
1d ago, from seek.com.au  

Apply on company site  

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.  

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers!  

About You  
We are looking for a Project Manager based in Sydney, NSW. This is a full-time role and reports to the Construction Manager - NSW/ACT.  

In this role, you will be key in managing and administering Otis New Equipment (NE) and Modernization (MODs) contracts according to Otis ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).  

• Permanent role  
• Based in Sydney CBD

Modernization (MODs) contracts according to Otis ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).

- Permanent role
- Based in Sydney CBD
- Competitive base pay, car allowance and project management incentive

As a Project Manager, your key responsibilities will include

- Maintains a high-performance team by recruiting, developing, and motivating field labor, setting appropriate standards of performance, and coaching the team.
- Manage and Administer Otis New Equipment and Modernization contracts according to the Otis’ ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).
- Ensures that Installation teams maintain approved site programs, attend site meetings, and exercise line authority over all allocated installation field labor.
- Proactively reports project risk and deliverable issues.
- Sets objectives and priorities, assigns and reviews tasks, performance, and staffing requirements while maintaining the resource plan.
- Develop and maintain strong relationships with customers, government inspectors, consultants, and within Otis to understand business challenges and recommend innovative integrated business solutions.
- Ensures that reports, contract records, site diaries, accident reports, time sheets, and manpower charts are completed and submitted in a timely manner.
- Participates in monthly project reviews with the branch and head office.

What you will need to be successful

- A tertiary qualification in Building or Engineering and/or an Electrical or Mechanical Trade Certificate is preferred.
- Has 3-5 years experience of leading teams of mechanics, installers, technicians or sub-contractors.

- A tertiary qualification in Building or Engineering and/or an Electrical or Mechanical Trade Certificate is preferred.  
- Has 3-5 years experience of leading teams of mechanics, installers, technicians or sub-contractors  
- Minimum of five (5) years of experience in project management of Lift and Escalator installations.  
- Hold a valid driver's license  
- Able to engage frequently and effectively with various stakeholders within Sales, New Equipment, Contract Management, Builders, Consultants, and other project stakeholders.  
- Excellent English communication skills - written and verbal  
- Effective planning and organizational skills.  
- Be a self-starter who can work safely and efficiently in a dynamic environment.  
- Able to work autonomously and in a team with minimal supervision.  
- Committed to excellence and innovation, and have a good work ethics.  
  
About us  
  
If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.  
  
Otis is the world’s leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.2 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.  
  
You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 69,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation.
jobs.description_summary: Otis is seeking a full-time Construction Project Manager based in Sydney CBD to oversee New Equipment and Modernization contracts, ensuring high performance from the installation team and effective stakeholder engagement. The ideal candidate should have a tertiary qualification in Building or Engineering, 3-5 years of leadership experience in mechanical or installation teams, and a minimum of five years in project management within the lift and escalator industry.

jobs.title: Electrician | Clayton
jobs.description:
Open app
Sign in
Electrician/Lift Service Technician
Old Mate Elevators 
View all jobs
Sydney NSW
Electricians (Trades & Services)
Full time
$100,000 – $120,000 per year + Super
Posted 8d ago
Join one of Sydney's most trusted lift companies!
Old Mate Elevators (OME) is a leading provider of lift services in NSW, providing new
installations, modernisations, and maintenance for all types of elevators - from residential lifts to
big commercial systems. With over 25 years of industry experience, we take pride in our
reputation built on reliable service, quality workmanship and a genuine commitment to our
customers. We are constantly seeking talented individuals to join our team and contribute to our
continued success. We also maintain Limited Mobility Lifts, Hillside Inclined Lifts, and Car
Lifts/Stackers, so no two days are ever the same!
About the role
We are seeking a skilled and motivated Licensed Electrician to join our established elevator
service and modernisation team servicing the Greater Sydney region. This is a field-based role
suited to someone who enjoys autonomy, technical problem-solving and direct client interaction.
What you'll be doing
Performing scheduled and reactive maintenance on lift systems, including diagnostics,
troubleshooting and repairs.
Carrying out safety inspections and testing to identify and address any potential issues.
Responding promptly to customer call-outs and providing efficient on-site service.
Maintaining detailed records of all work performed and ensuring compliance with relevant
regulations and standards.
Collaborating with the wider team to share knowledge, provide support, and contribute to the
continuous improvement of our service delivery.
What we're looking for
Someone with previous experience as an Electrician or Lift Service Technician.
Strong technical knowledge and hands-on skills in the maintenance and repair of a wide range
of lift systems.
Minimum of 3+ years experience in lift servicing and fault-finding or qualified electrician of 4+
years.
Excellent problem-solving and troubleshooting abilities to diagnose and rectify issues
efficiently.
A keen eye for detail and a commitment to high-quality work and customer service.
Effective communication skills to liaise with customers and coordinate with the wider team.
A proactive and adaptable approach to your work, with the ability to work independently or as
part of a team.
Solid understanding of Australian Lift Codes and Safety Standards.
Current Australian Drivers Licence.
Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident.
What we offer
Competitive pay with regular reviews.
Company vehicle, phone + Super.
Supportive team that actually listens and helps out.
Work-life balance and respect for your time.
A chance to work on a variety of systems and projects - never boring!
If you're a reliable technician who takes pride in your work and wants to join a team that values
safety, quality, and good people - we'd love to hear from you!
If you're successful, we'll be in touch directly. If not, thank you for taking the time to apply - we
truly appreciate it.

🔧 Now Hiring: Lift Technicians
📍 Location: Australia | 🌍 Open to Skilled Filipino Tradespeople
Take your technical expertise to the next level with an opportunity to work in Australia’s fast-
growing building services and vertical transport industry. We are seeking experienced Lift
Technicians who are passionate about safety, precision, and delivering high-quality workmanship
in modern commercial and residential environments.
🧰 About the Role
As a Lift Technician, you will be responsible for installing, maintaining, and servicing lift systems
to ensure safe and efficient operation. This role plays a critical part in supporting infrastructure
and keeping people moving reliably across residential towers, commercial buildings, and
industrial facilities.
🛠️ Key Responsibilities:
➤ Install, test, and commission new lift and escalator systems
➤ Perform routine maintenance and preventive servicing
➤ Diagnose electrical and mechanical faults using appropriate testing tools
➤ Repair or replace defective components such as controllers, motors, cables, and safety
devices
➤ Ensure all work complies with safety regulations and industry standards
➤ Respond to service calls and emergency breakdowns efficiently
➤ Maintain accurate service reports and documentation
➤ Collaborate with team members and clients to deliver high-quality service
✅ To Qualify, You Must Have:
➤ Minimum 3–5 years of proven experience in lift installation, maintenance, or servicing
➤ Strong knowledge of electrical, mechanical, and control systems
➤ Experience working with lift controllers and diagnostic tools
➤ Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and schematics
➤ Good English communication skills for workplace safety and coordination
➤ Relevant technical training or certifications in electrical/mechanical fields preferred
➤ Physically fit and capable of working at heights or in confined spaces
➤ Must be ready with visa documentation (valid passport, police clearance, employment
evidence, and training/school records)
🌏 Why Choose Australia?
➤ Work with globally respected companies and modern technologies
➤ Clean, safe, and highly regulated work environments
➤ Competitive salaries plus superannuation benefits
➤ Opportunities for long-term employment and potential permanent residency
➤ Generous leave benefits including annual and sick leave
➤ Excellent quality of life with vibrant, safe, and diverse communities

About Us
Access Home Lifts Cairns is a growing lift installation and service company specialising in
residential and small commercial lifts across Queensland. Due to strong growth and an increasing
number of projects, we are looking for a Trades Assistant to join our team and support our Lift
Technician with installations and servicing.
This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys hands-on work, travel, and learning a
specialised trade.
The Role
You will work directly alongside our technician assisting with the installation, servicing, and
maintenance of home lifts, small commercial lifts, stairlifts, and vertical platform lifts.
While the role is based in Cairns, the position involves travel to project sites across Queensland,
from Bundaberg through to Thursday Island.
All travel expenses are covered.
Key Responsibilities
Assist with installation of home lifts, platform lifts, stairlifts, and small commercial lifts
Support servicing and maintenance work
Carry and organise tools and materials on site
Assist with mechanical and installation tasks
Maintain a safe and organised work environment
Help prepare equipment and materials for jobs
Travel to regional and remote locations as required
Travel
This role includes regular travel throughout Queensland.
Candidates must be available for overnight travel away from Cairns as needed based on project
requirements.
The company covers:
Flights
Accommodation
Associated travel expenses
Meals where required
You will have the opportunity to work in a variety of locations across North Queensland and
remote regions.
About You
We are looking for someone who is:
Hardworking and reliable
Physically fit and comfortable with manual work

jobs.description:
Open app

Installations & Service Technician
Acorn Stairlifts Pty Ltd 
View all jobs
Melbourne VIC
Technicians (Trades & Services)
Full time
$80,000 – $100,000 per year
Posted 27d ago
Acorn Stairlifts is the Australian division of the world's leading manufacturer and distributor of
stairlifts to help people with stairs in their homes. The home-care industry in Australia is currently
going through a continued period of growth, making it a very attractive career choice. Acorn, as
a leader in this field, continues to drive forward its growth throughout Australia. To capitalise on
this growth and our future plans, we are looking for additional capable and
enthusiastic Installations and Service Technicians to support our expanding Operations team.
This fantastic opportunity will involve installing, servicing and repairing our stairlift installations in
the Melbourne region. There may also be the need for regular regional and interstate travel for
training.
This is a Full time role with lots of overtime possibilities and you are paid for all the driving,
that means from when you leave home until you return back home. Technicians work an
average 45 hour weeks.
You take the van home with you, and access replenishment stock at a local storage facility set
up close to home.
About You
The ideal candidate will:
Have current full Australian working rights
Hold a clean Australian driver’s licence
Be mechanically minded
Have good physical fitness and be able to lift heavy items on a regular basis
Have good attention to detail
Be reliable and punctual
Have knowledge and experience using power tools (desirable)
Be able to work autonomously
A background in stairlifts is not essential as full training will be provided.
This role involves working in the homes of our customers, many of whom are elderly. Therefore,
the successful candidate must be well presented, tidy, honest and able to demonstrate pride in a
job well done.
Why join us?
Competitive salary package including fully expensed company vehicle (van) and mobile
phone
On the road, change of scenery everyday!
2 - 4 weeks of PAID training
Get paid from when you leave home to return home, Door to Door!
Be part of a fun and enthusiastic team!
Put your technical abilities to good use helping other people live safely at home.
World market-leading company
Interesting and varied role
Potential for interstate travel
Your skills and customer service will be acknowledged and rewarded

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician (Developing or Experienced)
jobs.description:
Elevator Service Technician

BOSS Elevators – Birtinya QLD

Full time

13d ago, from seek.com.au

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will play a crucial part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sunshine Coast to Discovery Coast region.

As an expert technician, you’ll be responsible for conducting routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs, with a focus on quickly diagnosing and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator components. Your ability to think critically and implement effective solutions will be essential to ensuring optimal functionality and compliance with AS1735 standards.

You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to "Leading the Change" in elevator maintenance.

This role is suited for someone who is not only technically skilled but also values quality workmanship, safety, and client satisfaction. If you take pride in overcoming challenges and delivering high-quality results, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

What you'll be doing

• Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing

What you’ll be doing

- **Routine Maintenance:** Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing

- **Diagnose & Troubleshoot Issues:** Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as motors, controllers, and safety devices. Your expertise will be pivotal in delivering swift, accurate solutions

- **Carry Out Repair Work:** Conduct precise repairs, replacing worn or faulty parts as needed to restore full elevator functionality and reliability.

- **Maintain Detailed Records:** Keep thorough, accurate records of all maintenance, service, and repair activities, ensuring clear documentation for compliance and future reference.

- **Ensure Safety & Compliance:** Maintain strict adherence to all safety standards and regulations, upholding safety as a non-negotiable priority in every task.

- **Provide On-Call Support:** Be prepared to respond to emergency situations as part of our rotational standby roster, ensuring timely support and effective resolution of urgent issues.

- **Liaise with Clients:** Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.

- **Continuous Skill Development:** Participate in training events and workshops.

What we’re looking for

- **Positive, proactive attitude** and a commitment to safety and quality service.

- **Minimum 5 years of experience** in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.

- Queensland Electrical Licence (no restrictions).

- White card.

- Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).

- Rigging and Dogging certification (favourable but not essential).

- Certificate IV in WHS (favourable but not essential).

- Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer

- **Competitive Remuneration:** Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.

- **Coastal Lifestyle:** Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most

```
• Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer
• Competitive Remuneration: Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.
• Coastal Lifestyle: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most sought-after coastal regions.
• Motivating Team Culture: We believe in creating a positive work environment where your skills are valued, and your career can thrive.
• Vehicle and Tools: The whole kit and caboodle

Application Process
Please submit your resume, outlining your experience, qualifications, and referees, by 15 March 2026

Applications must also include a cover letter explaining why you are an ideal candidate for this role.

Shortlisted applicants will be contacted by 15 March 2026.

Interviews will be completed by 20 March 2026, with an employment offer extended to the successful candidate shortly thereafter.

jobs.title: Lift Maintenance Technician
jobs.description:
Job title: Lift Maintenance Technician  
Company name: Simplex Elevators  
Location: Australia  

Job description / responsibilities:  
- Install, maintain, and repair various elevator components, such as mechanical systems, electrical systems, and safety devices.  
- Conduct regular maintenance of elevator systems and respond to service calls.  
- Maintain detailed records of maintenance activities and repairs.  
- Respond promptly to emergency calls and ensure user safety.  
- Collaborate with the broader team to ensure the seamless operation of client elevator systems.  

Requirements / qualifications:  
- Minimum of 2 years of experience in an elevator maintenance technician role or similar role.  
- Strong mechanical and electrical troubleshooting skills, with the ability to diagnose and repair a wide range of elevator components.  
- Familiarity with various control systems and the ability to interpret technical manuals and schematics.  
- Valid driver's license for travel to client sites.  
- Excellent customer service skills and the ability to communicate effectively with clients.  

What we offer:  
- Competitive salary and ongoing professional development opportunities.  
- Discounted health insurance and gym membership.  
- A supportive and collaborative team environment.  

About us:  
Simplex Elevators is a leading provider of elevator installation, maintenance, and repairs in Australia. With over 20 years of industry experience, we are dedicated to delivering convenient and safe elevator solutions that meet our clients' needs. We aim to foster a part of a thriving and engaging company that values its employees and their contributions.  

Apply now for the exciting opportunity to become a Maintenance Technician with Simplex Elevators.

jobs.title: Lift Technician (Electrician)
jobs.description:
Lift Technician / Electrician  
Frontline Construction – Sydney CBD NSW  
$90,000 - $110,000 a year  
Permanent  
26d ago, from Frontline Construction Australia  

About the Role  
An exciting opportunity is available for a skilled Lift Technician or qualified Electrician to join a well-established organisation within the lift and accessibility sector. This role involves the installation, servicing and repair of a diverse range of lift and access systems across residential and commercial environments.  

The position offers stable, full-time hours (7:30am - 4:15pm), one RDO per calendar month, optional overtime, and long-term career progression for the right candidate.  

Key Responsibilities  
- Install, service and repair platform lifts, stair-lifts and accessibility equipment  
- Diagnose and fault-find electrical and mechanical control systems  
- Review and interpret technical plans, drawings and specifications  
- Test, commission and check PLCs and motor control systems  
- Align mechanical components and integrate with electrical switches  
- Ensure all work complies with safety standards and quality expectations  
- Provide professional, customer-focused service on site  

Key Skills & Experience  
- Electrical trade qualification (essential)

```

• Applicants from electrical or metal trades with strong mechanical aptitude are encouraged to apply

jobs.title: AUS Inds Lifting Technician
jobs.location: Australia, Tasmania, Burnie-Devonport Region, Burnie Area, Burnie
jobs.description:
Open app
Sign in
Lifting Technician
Motion Asia Pacific 
2.6
33 reviews
·
View all jobs
Derwent Park, Hobart TAS
Technicians (Trades & Services)
Full time
Add expected salary to your profile for insights
Posted 7d ago
As a Lifting Technician, you'll be responsible for inspecting, testing, repairing, and maintaining
lifting equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards and regulations. You'll work closely
with customers to provide expert advice and solutions, ensuring their lifting equipment is reliable
and safe to use.
If you have rigging, engineering or hands-on experience working at heights. We want to hear
from you!
IF you don't have these skills, that is no problem we'll train and teach THE RIGHT PERSON!
What we will require from you is: a keen eye for detail, a can do attitude, a team player and above
all a keenness to learn. It's a physical roll to some degree, a customer facing roll and an exciting
technical roll once you are trained and competent.
Join us in ensuring the safety and reliability of lifting equipment for industry is safe, whilst
building a rewarding career with a Global company.
Key Responsibilities
Perform inspections, testing, and certification of lifting equipment, including chains, slings,
hoists, and other lifting gear.
Conduct repairs and maintenance on lifting equipment to ensure optimal performance and
compliance with safety standards.
Provide technical advice and support to customers regarding lifting solutions and equipment.
Maintain accurate records of inspections, repairs, and certifications.
Ensure all work complies with relevant Australian Standards and safety regulations.
Collaborate with the sales team to identify customer needs and recommend appropriate
lifting solutions.
Uphold a strong commitment to workplace safety and quality standards.
Who are you:
Can do attitude with a strong work ethic
Basic understanding of Australian Standards.
Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Excellent communication and customer service skills.
High attention to detail and commitment to quality.
Current driver's license is essential
A commitment to safety and compliance.
What We Offer
New Ford Ranger vehicle, laptop, ipad and phone.
NATA & LEEA national certifcations.
A supportive and collaborative team environment.
Full day induction to help you settle into the business and position.
Access to employee benefits, including discounts on products and services.
Opportunities for career growth and development within the GPC network.
Uniforms supplied - feel like a team.
Recognition programs and bonus scheme for your performance.
1 paid Volunteer Leave Day per calendar year
Parental leave top-up allowance
Employee Assistance Program with access to mental health and well-being support initiatives
including confidential counselling services
About Motion:
As part of the Motion Australia family, Motion (Websterbsc) is a trusted name in industrial
solutions, offering top-quality products and services to keep our customers moving. This is your
chance to be part of a passionate team that thrives on delivering exceptional service and building
strong relationships with our valued customers.
Join us on this exciting journey of success and growth and let us help you empower your career
with us!
MOTION is an equal opportunity employer
You must be either an Australian Citizen or a holder of an Australian Permanent Residency Visa
to be eligible to apply for this position.
If this sounds like the opportunity for you, click "Apply for this job" and submit both your resume
and cover letter. Please note only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

jobs.title: Service Technician (Mechanic)
jobs.description:
Job Title: Service Technician - Lifting Equipment  
Company: Service Now  
Location: [Not specified in the image]  

Job Description:  
Join our team as a dedicated and certified Lifting Equipment Technician with your acquired Lifting Industry Licenses and qualifications. If you're technically minded and ready to take your career to a rewarding future in a growing industry, we want you!

As a Service Technician, you will work from the commitment to safety, service quality, and customer service, providing support and development opportunities for the team.

Responsibilities:  
- Service, maintain, and inspect lifting equipment to the highest standards.  
- Conduct routine inspections, repairs, and certifications on both electrical and mechanical lifting equipment.  
- Travel across designated areas to perform on-site servicing.  
- Complete all necessary documentation, including reports and compliance paperwork.  
- Ensure that all work is carried out according to safety guidelines and industry regulations.  
- Assist in training new staff members in equipment handling and safety protocols.

Requirements:  
- Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills.  
- Current driver's license (manual).  
- An eye for detail and excellent communication skills.  
- Previous experience in a similar role is preferred.  
- Must hold relevant industry qualifications (e.g., Elevator Industry License, LEEA Certification).  
- A professional approach and commitment to customer service.  

What’s on Offer:  
- Competitive pay rate ($30-$40 per hour based on experience).  
- 12% superannuation plus other incentives.  
- Full training provided to achieve LEEA Certification.  
- Continuous career development opportunities.  

Additional Information:  
Apply online with your current resume and cover letter and start your journey toward becoming a certified Lifting Equipment Technician! For further information email [contact information]. 

Salary:   
- Entry-level position: $30-$34 per hour.  
- Experienced role: $35-$40 per hour.

Career growth in a supportive team: Monday to Friday schedule.

jobs.title: Service Technicians x 4

Job Title: Service Technician - Lifting Equipment  
Company: Seasoned Tech  
Location: Australia  

Responsibilities:  
- Service, maintain, and inspect lifting equipment to the highest standards.  
- Work with clients to ensure safety and compliance.  
- Assist with electrical and mechanical repairs.  
- Conduct inspections and provide reports.  
- Carry out routine and emergency service calls.  

Requirements:  
- Current driver's license.  
- Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills.  
- Proven experience in the lifting industry.  
- Required to obtain UEA Certification.  
- Electrical and hydraulic experience preferred.  
- Must have a willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies.  

What’s on Offer:  
- Competitive pay $55,000 - $84,000 per annum based on experience.  
- 12% superannuation plus other benefits.  
- Full-time position with trade-specific UEA Certification.  
- Career progression and development opportunities.  

Additional Information:  
- Apply only if you meet the requirements and are ready to start your journey in the elevator/lift industry.  
- Career growth in a supportive team - Monday to Friday schedule.

Company
Eletech Pty Ltd
Position available: Lift Service Technician
Be prepared to join a company with a positive work culture and attitude of success. Eletech
prides itself on being a leader in elevator installation, service, maintenance and repair.
*Competitive industry wage
*Dual cab vehicle provided
Duties and Responsibilities
Complete scheduled services, repairs and call out (breakdown) visits effectively and
efficiently;
Participate in After Hours On-Call Roster (approx 1 week in 5 on-call);
Effectively plan workload, in conjunction with team of service technicians;
Be a good communicator and maintain excellent customer relations;
Ensure all work is carried out in line with company WH&S policy and procedures;
Works within quality standards and procedures;
Ensure true and accurate reports of activities and work carried out;
Present a strong customer focus.
Ensure that Company Vehicle is maintained in accordance with manufacturers schedule and
company policies.
Supervise and train apprentices as and when required (Eletech is committed to training and
development of apprentices).
Education and Qualifications Required
Queensland or other Australian equivalent Electrical fitter/mechanic license;
Manual Driver's License;
Construction White Card.
Experience
Experience in the lift industry preferred;
Electrical licence required;
Familiarity with PLC and VF Drives;
Mechanical experience and competence.
Requirements:
Candidates will be required to obtain a National Police Certificate and Working with Children
Check in the course of their employment.
Candidates will be required to be flu-vaccinated due to Eletech's customer's requirements
(Health Care and Aged Care facilities).
*This position is based in Brisbane but can include travel to all areas in South East QLD.
Successful applicant will be flexible and self motivated, working within a team environment.
