Posted 25d ago
Come and join Otis today, and be part of the OTIS Family and Forbes 2025 World's Best
Employers
About you
We are seeking a Service Technician based in Launceston, Tasmania.
In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants,
Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.
Permanent role
Reports to the Tasmania Branch Manager
Key Responsibilities for You
Performs Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles,
and operations manuals.
Stand by and provide after-call-back coverage as needed.
Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to
meet customer satisfaction.
Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and subcontractors.
Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts
and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.
Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.
What You Will Need To Be Successful
Have an Electrical license or a Cert III in Electrotechnology
Minimum 3-5 years’ experience within the elevator Industry with extensive Electrical and
Mechanical knowledge across large equipment
Hold a valid driver's license
Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer through training and demonstrated fault-
finding, problem-solving skills
Good English language skills, both verbal and written
Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications,
applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet)
A committed professional, with safety as priority
Has attention to detail and is results-driven
Curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team
Benefits
You will receive a long-term employment contract with the world market leader in a crisis-
proof industry.
We offer you remuneration in accordance with the local EBA and you will be working 36 hours
weekly
We will train you intensively in the areas of technology, processes & soft skills and you can
exchange ideas with experienced colleagues at any time.
You will receive modern and high-quality work clothes, your own tools, an iPhone and a tool
of trade (depending on area of coverage)
Apply Now and help us build what's next
About us
We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and
service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units
worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.
We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest
transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.
Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion
We “lift” our people to their highest potential.
Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both
culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and
celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.
We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a
best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders,
sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity
of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice
feels safe, welcomed and heard.
We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career.
This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help
working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.
Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive
consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation,
gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other protected class
according to applicable law. Otis are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive place to work.
We strongly encourage applications from women, and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander
people
For more information: https://www.otis.com/en/au/our-company/diversity-and-inclusion

jobs.description:
**ACORN STAIRLIFTS**

Recruiting NOW

Installations & Service Technician

- Competitive salary package including company vehicle and mobile phone
- On the road - 2-4 weeks of PAID training - NO WEEKENDS!
- World market-leading company, interesting and varied role
- Potential for interstate travel

Full-time, Permanent, Competitive Salary, NO WEEKENDS
- Must have current full Australian working rights & clean Australian drivers licence
- Good physical fitness and able to lift heavy items on a regular basis.
- A background in stairlifts is not essential as full training will be given but you must have a trade background.

**Stairlift Technician**

Acorn Stairlifts Pty Ltd

Sydney NSW (Remote)

Technicians (Trades & Services)

Full time

$80,000 - $100,000 per year

Posted 27d ago

Quick apply  
Save

$80,000 - $100,000 per year

Posted 27d ago

Are you ready to make a real difference in people's lives while working with cutting-edge technology? Acorn Stairlifts is looking for enthusiastic and skilled Technicians to join our expanding team in the greater Sydney area. As the Australian division of the world’s leading manufacturer of stairlifts, we’re at the forefront of the home-care industry—a sector that’s rapidly growing and offers excellent career opportunities.

Why Acorn?  
As an industry leader, we’re committed to driving growth and innovation across Australia, and we want YOU to be a part of this exciting journey. By joining us, you’ll be helping improve the lives of customers by installing, servicing, and repairing our high-quality stairlifts. Whether it’s locally or further afield, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our clients can stay safe and comfortable in their homes.

What You'll Do:  
- Install, service, and repair stairlifts across the Greater Sydney area (with occasional travel for country and interstate jobs).  
- Enjoy the flexibility of working from home and across various work sites throughout the greater Sydney area.  
- This is a full-time role with plenty of opportunities for overtime, and you’ll get paid door-to-door—meaning you’re paid from the moment you leave home to when you return.

What We’re Looking For:  
- Full Australian working rights.  
- Clean Australian driver’s license.  
- A mechanically minded individual who enjoys working with tools and solving problems.  
- Good physical fitness and the ability to lift heavy items regularly.  
- Attention to detail, reliability, and punctuality.  
- Previous experience with powered tools is a plus but not essential.  
- A desire to work autonomously and take pride in delivering high-quality service.

Stairlift Technician  
Acorn Stairlifts Pty Ltd  

- Previous experience with powered tools is a plus but not essential.  
- Able to work autonomously and take pride in delivering high-quality service.  
- A clean, tidy, and professional appearance when interacting with our customers, many of whom are elderly.  

No experience with stairlifts? No problem! We provide full training to ensure you’re fully equipped for success.  

Why You’ll Love It Here:  

- Competitive salary + company vehicle (van) + mobile phone.  
- A fun and dynamic team environment where your skills are valued and rewarded.  
- 2-4 weeks of paid training to set you up for success.  
- Get paid from the moment you leave home, right through to when you return—door-to-door pay!  
- Opportunity to travel and see new places while doing meaningful work.  
- Be part of a world-leading company with a great reputation in the industry.  

Ready to take the next step in your career? If you’re passionate about helping others and eager to apply your technical skills in a rewarding field, we’d love to hear from you!  

Employer questions  
Your application will include the following questions:

3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers!  

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.  

About you  
We are looking for a Service Technician to based in Darwin, Northern Territory. In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.  

• Permanent role  
• Reports to the Branch Manager  

Key responsibilities for YOU  
• Perform Service and Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures

- Permanent role

- Reports to the Branch Manager

Key responsibilities for YOU

- Performs Service and Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals.

- Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers.

- Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

- Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.

- Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and/or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement.

- Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

- Standby and provide after-call-back coverage from time to time.

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally

- Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

- Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.

- Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.

- Sales Team: Sales Account Representatives, Managers, and Coordinators (where relevant).

- External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers.

What YOU will need to be successful

Have Electrical and/or Fitter qualifications as relevant to the role and Lift apprenticeship or other industry-accredited training program (desirable)

Minimum two (2) to five (5) years’ experience within the Lift Industry.

Hold a valid driver's license and Electrical license.

Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training and demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills.

Strong English language skills, both verbal and written

Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet).

You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority

Has attention to detail and is results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!

We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

Lift Technician | Electrician  
Frontline Construction – Sunshine Coast QLD  
Permanent  
6d ago, from Frontline Construction Australia  

About the role  
Due to an expanding portfolio of projects, our client is currently seeking a dedicated and qualified Electrician / Lift Technician to join their team. You will be responsible for installing and assembling lift systems, including both electrical and mechanical components. This position offers electricians the chance to broaden their skills in a dynamic environment while working with the latest lift technology.  

Tasks and Responsibilities  
- Install, align and commission lift and elevator equipment  
- Manage materials and tools on site.  
- Work closely with team leaders, supervisors, and builders.  
- Maintain a clean, safe and efficient work environment.  
- Complete projects on time and to a high standard.  

Skills and Experience  
- A registered electrical licence  
- Practical, field-based industry experience will be highly regarded  
- A strong commitment to safety policies and procedures  
- The ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team

Practical, field-based industry experience will be highly regarded  
A strong commitment to safety policies and procedures  
The ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team  
Excellent communication skills  
A valid Australian driver's license  

Benefits and Perks  
Excellent incentives and bonuses available on a per job basis to reward you for your efforts;  
Ongoing Learning and Development  
Competitive salary and benefits package  
Mentorship from experienced teams  
Fulltime, permanent ongoing role  
Family owned business where you are treated like part of the family  
Career progression as the business expands  
Clean, comfortable work environment  

About the Company  
Our client is a leading supplier and installer of lifts and elevators within commercial, residential, civil projects. They are committed to delivering high-quality projects on time and within budget. Their team is dedicated to excellence, innovation, and sustainability.

jobs.title: MOTOR MECHANIC / FORKLIFT TECHNICIAN
jobs.description:
Service Lift Technician

United Lift Services – Sydney CBD NSW

Full time

3d ago, from seek.com.au

Service Lift Technician – NSW

Join a supportive national team and build your career with Australia’s trusted independent lift company.

About United Lifts

United is a proudly Australian-owned, independent lift company. Established in Melbourne, we’ve grown into a national organisation with branches in NSW, ACT, QLD, and SA. We partner with a wide range of high-profile clients across numerous industries, providing expert service, exceptional modernisation, and new installation solutions.

With over 200 passionate employees and a growing portfolio, we are looking to expand our team in NSW. This is an excellent opportunity to work alongside industry experts, develop your skills, and contribute to a thriving and dynamic business.

Why Join United?

• A positive, professional team culture where your contributions are valued

• Excellent career growth with ongoing training and development

• Attractive remuneration package (negotiable based on experience)

• Work-life balance with opportunities for overtime

• Company vehicle, tools, and uniform provided

Work-life balance with opportunities for overtime

Company vehicle, tools, and uniform provided

Be part of a company with a strong team culture and national presence

Work with experienced technicians on a variety of lift types and technologies

The Role  
As a Service Lift Technician (NSW), you will report to our NSW Management Teams. You’ll take ownership of lift maintenance and repairs, respond to service calls, and carry out various lift-related tasks across our diverse client base.

Your expertise will help us deliver exceptional service and maintain the high standards our clients expect.

Key Responsibilities  
- Perform maintenance and repairs on lifts and related equipment  
- Respond to breakdowns and call-outs in a timely manner  
- Collaborate with the Service Team and Management to ensure high-quality outcomes  
- Provide excellent customer service on-site  
- Complete documentation and reporting as required  

About You

- Flexible and adaptable to business needs

jobs.title: Local Area Representative - Newcastle - Forster
jobs.description:
Local Area Representative - Newcastle - Forster  
Astrum Recruitment - Newcastle NSW  
$110,000 - $115,000 a year Full time, Permanent  
28d ago, from Astrum Recruitment  

Apply on company site  

Local Representative - Newcastle & Forster Region  
Location: Newcastle & Forster, NSW  
Position Type: Permanent  
Reporting To: Service Branch Manager, Newcastle NSW  
Package: $110k-$115k + Super + Car Allowance + Bonus  
Vehicle: Fully maintained company vehicle and fuel card provided  

About the Role:  
We are seeking an experienced Electrician/ Lift Technician to take on a Local Area Representative role within a global lift company. This role will cover the Newcastle and Forster regions.  

In this role, you will oversee field operations, customer relationships, maintenance and business development initiatives across your designated area.  

With a balance of field and office responsibilities 85% Field work on the tools %15 Office split, you'll play a key role in driving operational efficiency, safety compliance, and service excellence.  

Key Responsibilities Field-Based Duties 85%:  
- Reduce call-outs through proactive asset management and scheduled maintenance on vertical transport, lifts, elevators etc.  
- Achieve maintenance targets in accordance with contract requirements.  
- Minimise equipment downtime through timely repair coordination and estimate submissions to Sales.  

- Build strong relationships with customers and subcontractors.

Achieve maintenance targets in accordance with contract requirements.

Minimise equipment downtime through timely repair coordination and estimate submissions to Sales.

Manage repair works with customers and subcontractors.

Build trusted customer relationships to support retention and growth.

Conduct field audits and ensure subcontractor compliance with safety standards.

On Call Work every other weekend.

Office-Based Duties 15%:
- Manage routine maintenance scheduling and meet contractual obligations.
- Monitor repair budgets and support achievement of new business targets.
- Oversee procurement and stock control of parts and equipment.
- Respond to customer queries and concerns in a prompt, solution-focused manner.
- Attend customer meetings and provide technical input.
- Assist with tenders, modernisation projects, and service-related sales opportunities.
- Provide regular reporting and identify process improvement opportunities.

What You'll Need
- NSW Electrical License (mandatory).
- Trade qualification as an Electrician/Fitter/Mechanic. Additional studies in Electronics are highly regarded.
- Lift Experience an understanding in vertical transportation.
- IT proficiency and team leadership experience.
- Valid driver's license and clean driving record.
- Commercial mindset and ability to collaborate with Sales teams.
- Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
- Proven ability to manage end-to-end projects and meet KPIs.
- Team-oriented with a proactive and positive attitude.

Why Join Us
- Competitive salary package + quarterly bonuses.
- Fully maintained vehicle and fuel card.
- 12 Annual Rostered Days Off (RDOs).
- Flexible working arrangements and autonomy to manage your own area.
- Career development through world-class learning programs.
- Proud to be named in Forbes’ 2022 World’s Best Employers.

This is an exciting opportunity to join a global industry leader and drive service excellence across the Newcastle and Forbes region.

jobs.title: Service Mechanic
jobs.description:
Lift Service Technician
Company: Tesla Elevators
Source: https://www.adzuna.com.au/details/5615013627?utm_medium=api&utm_source=e5b1fd30
Captured: 15/03/2026
Job Description
Lift Service Technician | Melbourne, VIC | Full-time position About Us Tesla Elevators is a proudly Australian-owned and operated
independent lift company, providing service and maintenance across residential, commercial and industrial sites. Our growth has
been built on exceptional customer service, a dedicated technical team, and a strong commitment to work-life balance. We?re serious
about quality, safety and professionalism, and we value a supportive culture and enjoying what we do. The Role We are seeking an
experienced Lift Service Technician to join our Melbourne-based Service Team. Reporting to the Maintenance Coordinator, you will
take ownership of a scheduled maintenance run and be responsible for servicing, repairs and breakdown response across a range of
lift systems including hydraulic, MRL and platform lifts. You will work on our own products as well as a variety of third-party
equipment. This is a hands-on role for someone who takes pride in clean, compliant work, communicates well with customers, and
can fault-find confidently to reduce repeat callouts. What We Offer · Company vehicle · iPhone · On-call allowance (rostered on-call) ·
36-hour work week · 26 RDOs per annum · Competitive remuneration with overtime opportunities · Training, support and a team that
values your contribution Key Responsibilities · Plan and execute your workload effectively in coordination with the Maintenance
Coordinator · Perform scheduled maintenance, repairs, breakdown response and call-out visits with care and attention to detail ·
Diagnose faults, troubleshoot equipment and complete inspections and diagnostic testing to ensure reliability and performance ·
Complete all work to manufacturer specifications, Australian Standards and regulatory requirements · Maintain strict compliance with
WHS policies and maintain safe operating conditions at all times · Maintain a safe, tidy and professional worksite, with strong care
and respect in customers? homes and workplaces · Communicate professionally with customers, keeping them informed and
providing practical guidance on lift operation and care · Accurately document work completed, including maintenance logs, breakdown
reports, repair reports and emails, in clear language that non-technical customers and internal staff can understand · Monitor your
calendar daily for schedule updates and upcoming work · Contribute to continuous improvement by helping refine workflows and
service processes What We?re Looking For Qualifications (essential) · Australian Electrical A-Grade Licence · Construction White
Card · Current manual Driver?s Licence · Full working rights in Australia (citizen or permanent resident) Experience and technical
capability · Minimum 5 years? experience as a lift technician (service and repairs focused preferred) · Strong electrical and
mechanical fault-finding skills · Experience with hydraulic and traction drive systems (MRL and hydraulic) · Ability to read and interpret
electrical schematics · Confidence with PLCs, microprocessors and VF drives is highly regarded Personal attributes · Self-motivated,
solution-focused and team-oriented · Organised, reliable and professional with customers · Takes pride in doing the job properly,
including clear reporting and clean workmanship · Flexible and willing to travel within our service area as required Checks · Prepared
to provide a National Police Check and Working with Children Check Ready to Join Us? If you?re ready to elevate your career with
Tesla Elevators, apply now. We?re looking for a technician who wants stability, great conditions, and a team that backs quality work.

jobs.description:
Service Manager  
Schindler Lifts Australia  
3.1  
34 reviews  
· View all jobs  

Belmont, Perth WA  
General/Business Unit Manager (CEO & General Management)  
Full time  
Competitive Base + 12% S + $28K Bonus + $18 Car A.  

Posted 2d ago  

Apply  
Save

WHY join Schindler?  
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.

About the role:  
We have an opportunity has arisen for an experienced Service Manager to join our team based in Perth. Reporting directly to the area Operations Manager, you will have approximately 10 direct reports and P&L responsibilities. The role is office based as well as spending time in the field with technician direct reports and attending customer meetings. You will also be expected to carry out the monthly toolbox meetings and be responsible for driving consistent and reliable financial performance.

As the successful candidate, you play a crucial role in our business as you are often the first point of contact for our customers, to deliver responsive and high-quality services and solutions. You will also be responsible for providing coaching, mentoring and leadership to your team of service technicians driving open communication and a focus on a safety culture.

This package includes:  
- Highly Competitive base + 12% Super  
- $28,000 Bonus incentive  
- $18,000 car allowance  
- Access to discounted partner company offers.  
- Funding for further educational support upon completion of 1 year of employment  
- Birthday day off

Service Manager  
Schindler Lifts Australia  

- Funding for further educational support upon completion of 1 year of employment  
- Birthday day off  

Key Accountabilities  

- Improve operational budgets, including portfolio growth, material and labour cost control.  
- Constantly analyse results & take effective remedial action quickly.  
- Visit the site and record contact & other site details  
- Determine manpower capacity needed to service the contracted portfolio.  
- Reconcile fulfilment of maintenance visits and act as required  
- Make regular audits to ensure the quality of maintenance.  
- Allocate resources to attend to shutdowns  
- Monitor callback history and allocate resources to attend to sick lifts  
- Set and attend team meetings.  
- Identify & plan for Service training needs.  
- Document and solve customer complaints.  

Requirements for the role:  

- Ideally past experience as a Maintenance/ Repairs/ Service Manager working in the Lift industry or within Fire, HVAC, Security or a similar industry.  
- Previous experience managing Field Technicians  
- Qualification in building, engineering, electrical or mechanical disciplines will be looked upon favourably  
- Proven experience in driving business growth and minimizing labour costs  
- Strong organization and delegation skills, with proven ability to coordinate staff productivity.  
- Exceptional communication skills  
- Strong awareness of safety ideally with a qualification  
- Past experience forecasting, budgeting and contract administration  

About Schindler?  

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200.

The role offers a competitive salary, substantial bonuses, and opportunities for professional growth within a supportive company culture.

jobs.title: Head Mechanic | Forklift & Access Equipment
jobs.location: Australia, Victoria, Melbourne Region, Frankston Area, Frankston East
jobs.description:
Workshop Mechanic  
All Lift Forklifts and Access Equipment  
Salisbury, Brisbane QLD  
Automotive Trades (Trades & Services)  
Full time  
$45 - $51 per hour  
Posted 3d ago

All Lift Forklifts and Access Equipment

About Us
We are a family owned and run forklift and access equipment rental and sales company based in Brisbane, QLD and other eastern states. Due to rapid growth a full time position has become available to join our service department.

The Role
We are seeking a Forklift/Access Technician to be based at our workshop in Salisbury, diagnosis, servicing, pre hires and off hires on all our equipment. As part of a dedicated team of technicians, you will be responsible for delivering a high level of service to our customers specialising in our range of forklifts, telehandlers and access equipment covering breakdowns, fault finding and servicing.

To be considered for this role you must have the following:
1. Mechanical Trade Certificate (Forklift Mechanic / Diesel Mechanic / Heavy Vehicle Mechanic or light vehicle).
2. Current drivers licence
3. Forklift licence (desirable)
4. Solid understanding of diesel and hydraulics.
5. Sense of urgency and can work within time frames

About You
With your trade qualification or mechanical experience under your belt you enjoy being in a hands on customer facing role. Team player and motivated to help grow the business.

Workshop Mechanic  
All Lift Forklifts and Access Equipment  
customer facing role. Team player and motivated to help grow the business.  

The rewards  

Competitive above award wages are on offer in line with experience. In addition to this you will be part of a supportive friendly team!

jobs.title: Maintenance Technician
jobs.description:
Maintenance Technician  
KONE – Gold Coast QLD  
Full time  
17d ago, from KONE  

- Great benefits - 2 RDO's per month, competitive base wage, overtime available  
- Work with industry - leading products and technology  
- Join a global organisation with a strong focus on training, safety and quality  

At KONE, we bring buildings to life.  

Through our technical expertise and digital connectivity, we provide an essential service to communities around the world – keep people moving no matter what.  

Our work will take you in different directions.  

We're looking for a Maintenance Technician to join our high performing Service Team in Gold Coast. No two days are the same as you manage the equipment installation of KONE's elevator products, across low-rise housing through to the world's tallest skyscrapers.  

What will you be doing?  
- Attending breakdowns on Elevators and Escalators  
- Attending to maintenance activities and repairs  
- Carrying out all activities in accordance with KONE policies and procedures including health, quality and safety standards

Attending to maintenance activities and repairs

Carrying out all activities in accordance with KONE policies and procedures including health, quality and safety standards

Working to actively maintain customer relationships in the local region

What are we looking for?

Lift industry experience preferred but not essential

Qualified electrical licence required

Experiencing managing critical breakdowns and key stakeholder’s expectations

Motivated and self-starter who can manage workload and competing priorities

Commitment to working safely with a strong focus on quality

A customer service mindset

Excellent communication skills and ability to work as part of a team

There’s so much more to KONE than you may realise.

Enjoy career progression opportunities within a global organisation

Access a range of benefits that engage and motivate our employees and help us make KONE a great place to work

Grow with comprehensive learning and development programs covering a wide range of professional skills

A job at KONE is the start of an experience like no other. You’ll find a bunch of passionate “lifters”, and maybe even become one yourself! Apply today and help us to bring buildings to life.

At KONE, we are focused on creating an innovative and collaborative working culture where we value the contribution of each individual. Employee engagement is a key focus area for us and we encourage participation and the sharing of information and ideas. Sustainability is an integral part of our culture and the daily practice. We follow ethical business practices and we seek to develop a culture of working together where co-workers trust and respect each other and good performance is recognized. In being a great place to work, we are proud to offer a range of experiences and opportunities that will help you to achieve your career and personal goals and enable you to live a healthy and balanced life.

jobs.title: Lift Installer ( Fitter & Electrician )
jobs.description:
Lift Installer ( Fitter & Electrician )  
Electrical & Lift Solutions Pty Ltd  
Brisbane QLD  
Electricians (Trades & Services)  
Full time  
$58 - $67 per hour  
Posted 10d ago

We're looking for experienced Lift Installers to join us as direct employees on EBA rates. You'll be involved end-to-end across new installations and modernisations — the kind of work that keeps you sharp and lets you take real ownership.

Your day-to-day will include:
- Mechanical installation — rails, brackets, doors/entrances, car & counterweight components
- Electrical installation — containment, wiring, terminations (where applicable)
- Machine-room and MRL fit-out support
- Working from drawings, lift layouts, and manufacturer installation manuals
- Site coordination with builders and other trades
- QA checks, testing support, and completion documentation
- Maintaining a high safety standard on active construction sites

We hire character as much as competency. We want people who take pride in their craft, communicate straight, and want to grow alongside the business. Here's the profile:
- Demonstrated lift installation experience (construction environment)
- Strong mechanical aptitude — electrical experience is a real advantage
- Ability to read drawings and follow installation specifications precisely
- High attention to detail and a genuine safety-first mindset
- Construction White Card (essential) + Driver's licence (essential)
- Reliable, self-motivated, and easy to work with — team culture matters here

Lift Installer ( Fitter & Electrician )  
Electrical & Lift Solutions Pty Ltd  
- Construction White Card (essential) + Driver's licence (essential)  
- Reliable, self-motivated, and easy to work with — team culture matters here  

Tickets (Highly Regarded):  
- Working at Heights  
- Electrical trade qualification + current QLD electrical licence  
- Rigging  
- CPR/LVR  

If this sounds like the role you've been waiting for, we'd love to hear from you. Apply via SEEK or send your resume and a brief cover note directly. Tell us about your experience and — more importantly — why you'd be a great fit for a team built around quality and culture.  

Employer questions  
Your application will include the following questions:  
- Which of the following statements best describes your right to work in Australia?  
- How many years' experience do you have as a Lift Installer?

jobs.description:
Open app

Electrician Lift Repairs
Astrum

Electricians (Trades & Services)
Full time
EBA $59.30 + Super + Car + Phone + OT + Allowances
Posted 9d ago
Lift Repair Technician – Electrician
Chullora & Greater Sydney | Full-Time |EBA rates $59.30 p/h + Super Company Car + Phone +
Tools + Overtime + Allowances 
NSW Electrical Licence Essential!!
The company: 
Our client is the world’s largest elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service
business. They serve customers in over 200 countries and territories around the world and
maintain more than ~2.4 million elevators and escalators. They have more than 72,000
employees globally, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales and functional
specialists, as well as factory and field technicians. A proud global team that continues to rise to
the challenges of a fast-moving, high-performance company.
Just imagine where you can take your career in a company this size!
The Role:
We’re seeking a skilled Lift Repair Electrician who thrives on hands-on, ground-up repair work.
This role suits someone who enjoys diagnosing faults without relying on a guide—using their
electrical and mechanical expertise to restore lift systems with precision.
You’ll carry out detailed inspections, testing, troubleshooting, and repairs on lift equipment,
working both on the bench and onsite across Greater Sydney.
Key Responsibilities
Diagnose, troubleshoot, and repair lift electrical and mechanical faults
Complete bench repairs, electrical testing, and component rebuilds
Conduct mechanical inspections to ensure safety and compliance
Interpret wiring diagrams, schematics, and technical drawings
Repair or replace faulty parts with accuracy and care
Adhere to WHS and industry safety standards
Work independently while supporting the team when needed
About You
We’re looking for a Lift Repair Mechanic / Electrician  who genuinely enjoys repairing lifts not just
routine maintenance. You love problem-solving, fault-finding, tinkering and bringing complex
systems back to life.
You’ll bring:
A valid NSW Electrical Licence (must-have)
Experience in lift, hoist, crane, or similar mechanical/electrical repair work
3+ years mechanical/electrical fault-finding experience
Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to resolve issues without guidance
Ability to read schematics and understand motor control systems
NSW Driver’s Licence
Strong independence, initiative, and a safety-first mindset we want someone who is a self
starter, critical thinker and gets the job done. 
What’s on Offer
EBA Rate $59.30 per hour + Super
Company car
Phone + tools provided
Stable permanent full-time role covering our clients vertical transport in Greater Sydney
Supportive Training 
Career growth within the business take your career wherever you want it to go!
Supportive, experienced repair team, our team has the best culture in the business we have
each others backs and support each other its a great place to work and we love what we do!
Ready to Apply?
If you’re passionate about lift repairs and want a role where your skills are truly valued, we’d love
to hear from you.
Apply now!
Email me your CV to natalie.iglesias@astrumrecruitment.com or for a confidential chat give me a
call 0439 811 172

jobs.title: Construction Mechanic  and Service Technicians (Hobart and Launceston, Tasmania)
jobs.description:
Open app
Sign in
Service Technician (Launceston, Tasmania)
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY 
3.6
28 reviews
·
View all jobs
Launceston, Launceston & North East TAS
Technicians (Trades & Services)
Full time
Add expected salary to your profile for insights
Posted 24d ago
Come and join Otis today, and be part of the OTIS Family and Forbes 2025 World's Best
Employers
About you
We are seeking a Service Technician based in Launceston, Tasmania.
In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants,
Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.
Permanent role
Reports to the Tasmania Branch Manager
Key Responsibilities for You
Performs Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles,
and operations manuals.
Stand by and provide after-call-back coverage as needed.
Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to
meet customer satisfaction.
Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and subcontractors.
Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts
and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.
Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.
What You Will Need To Be Successful
Have an Electrical license or a Cert III in Electrotechnology
Minimum 3-5 years’ experience within the elevator Industry with extensive Electrical and
Mechanical knowledge across large equipment
Hold a valid driver's license
Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer through training and demonstrated fault-
finding, problem-solving skills
Good English language skills, both verbal and written
Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications,
applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet)
A committed professional, with safety as priority
Has attention to detail and is results-driven
Curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team
Benefits
You will receive a long-term employment contract with the world market leader in a crisis-
proof industry.

jobs.title: Elevator Field Technician — Flexible Hours (NSW License)
jobs.description:
Elevator Service Technician  
BOSS Elevators – Birtinya QLD  

Full time  
23d ago, from seek.com.au  

About the Role  

We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our dynamic team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will play a crucial part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sunshine Coast to Discovery Coast region.   

As an expert technician, you’ll be responsible for conducting routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs, with a focus on quickly diagnosing and resolving issues with a wide range of elevator components. Your ability to think critically and implement effective solutions will be essential to ensuring optimal functionality and compliance with AS1735 standards.  

You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to "Leading the Change" in elevator maintenance.

You will liaise directly with clients to understand their concerns, provide clear communication, and deliver exceptional service. You will have the opportunity to work both independently and as part of a skilled team committed to "Leading the Change" in elevator maintenance.

This role is suited for someone who is not only technically skilled but also values quality workmanship, safety, and client satisfaction. If you take pride in overcoming challenges and delivering high-quality results, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

What you'll be doing

• Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual and 5-yearly testing

• Diagnose & Troubleshoot Issues: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as motors, controllers, and safety devices. Your expertise will be pivotal in delivering swift, accurate solutions

• Carry Out Repair Work: Conduct precise repairs, replacing worn or faulty parts as needed to restore full elevator functionality and reliability.

• Maintain Detailed Records: Keep thorough, accurate records of all maintenance, service, and repair activities, ensuring clear documentation for compliance and future reference.

• Ensure Safety & Compliance: Maintain strict adherence to all safety standards and regulations, upholding safety as a non-negotiable priority in every task.

• Provide On-Call Support: Be prepared to respond to emergency situations as part of our rotational standby roster, ensuring timely support and effective resolution of urgent issues.

• Liaise with Clients: Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.

• Continuous Skill Development: Participate in training events and workshops.

- Liaise with Clients: Engage professionally with clients to understand their requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that reinforce our commitment to superior service.

- Continuous Skill Development: Participate in training events and workshops.

What we're looking for

- Positive, proactive attitude and a commitment to safety and quality service.

- Minimum 5 years of experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.

- Queensland Electrical Licence (no restrictions).

- White card.

- Working at Heights certification (favourable but not essential).

- Rigging and Dogging certification (favourable but not essential).

- Certificate IV in WHS (favourable but not essential).

- Must have the right to work in Australia. Please note: No sponsorship opportunities are available.

What we offer

- Competitive Remuneration: Get paid your worth based on experience and qualifications.

- Coastal Lifestyle: Skip the long commutes and work close to home in one of Australia’s most sought-after coastal regions.

- Motivating Team Culture: We believe in creating a positive work environment where your skills are valued, and your career can thrive.

- Vehicle and Tools: The whole kit and caboodle

Application Process

Please submit your resume, experience, qualifications, and references by 15

jobs.title: Construction Mechanics
jobs.description:
Construction Project Manager - Vertical Transportation  
Otis – Sydney CBD NSW  
3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK  
Full time  
2d ago, from seek.com.au  

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.  

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers!  

About You  
We are looking for a Project Manager based in Sydney, NSW. This is a full-time role and reports to the Construction Manager - NSW/ACT.  

In this role, you will be key in managing and administering Otis New Equipment (NE) and Modernization (MODs) contracts according to Otis ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).  
- Permanent role  
- Based in Sydney CBD

Modernization (MODs) contracts according to Otis ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).

- Permanent role
- Based in Sydney CBD
- Competitive base pay, car allowance and project management incentive

As a Project Manager, your key responsibilities will include

- Maintains a high-performance team by recruiting, developing, and motivating field labor, setting appropriate standards of performance, and coaching the team.
- Manage and Administer Otis New Equipment and Modernization contracts according to the Otis’ ‘Sales Installation Process’ (SIP).
- Ensures that Installation teams maintain approved site programs, attend site meetings, and exercise line authority over all allocated installation field labor.
- Proactively reports project risk and deliverable issues.
- Sets objectives and priorities, assigns and reviews tasks, performance, and staffing requirements while maintaining the resource plan.
- Develop and maintain strong relationships with customers, government inspectors, consultants, and within Otis to understand business challenges and recommend innovative integrated business solutions.
- Ensures that reports, contract records, site diaries, accident reports, time sheets, and manpower charts are completed and submitted in a timely manner.
- Participates in monthly project reviews with the branch and head office.

What you will need to be successful

- A tertiary qualification in Building or Engineering and/or an Electrical or Mechanical Trade Certificate is preferred.
- Has 3-5 years experience of leading teams of mechanics, installers, technicians or sub-contractors.

- A tertiary qualification in Building or Engineering and/or an Electrical or Mechanical Trade Certificate is preferred.

- Has 3-5 years experience of leading teams of mechanics, installers, technicians or sub-contractors

- Minimum of five (5) years of experience in project management of Lift and Escalator installations.

- Hold a valid driver's license

- Able to engage frequently and effectively with various stakeholders within Sales, New Equipment, Contract Management, Builders, Consultants, and other project stakeholders.

- Excellent English communication skills - written and verbal

- Effective planning and organizational skills.

- Be a self-starter who can work safely and efficiently in a dynamic environment.

- Able to work autonomously and in a team with minimal supervision.

- Committed to excellence and innovation, and have a good work ethics.

About us

If you live in a city, chances are we will give you a lift or play a role in keeping you moving every day.

Otis is the world’s leading elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation, and service company. We move 2 billion people every day and maintain approximately 2.2 million customer units worldwide, the industry's largest Service portfolio.

You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 69,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse, global team with a proven legacy of innovation

Alexandria, Sydney NSW

WHY join Schindler?  
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.  

About the role:  
We have an exciting opportunity for a Design Engineer to join our engineering team based in our new Head Office in Alexandria. As a key contributor to the project success of any new lift installation, you will be responsible for providing engineering and drafting support to ensure that any engineered solution fits customer requirements and is executable from a product compliance and efficiency perspective.  

What’s in it for you:  
- $92,000 base (negotiable depending on qualifications and experience)  
- 12% Super  
- 5% Annual Bonus  
- Excellent opportunity for you to expand your Engineering knowledge at a global leader  
- Potential for real growth both personally and professionally  
- Opportunity to access educational support, access to staff discount scheme, and social activities  
- You will even get a day off for your birthday!  
- Fitness passport with access to over 1,700 fitness facilities across Australia  
- New state-of-the-art office facility in Alexandria  

Key Accountabilities:  
- Liaise with sales teams nationally to determine customer requirements  
- Produce and facilitate escalator layouts and appointment drawings in AutoCAD / 3D Revit based on

Design Engineer  
Schindler Lifts Australia  

- Liaise with sales teams nationally to determine customer requirements  
- Produce lift and escalator layouts and appointment drawings in AutoCAD / 3D Revit based on customer specifications  
- Configure the final design specification in our configuration tool and manage the ordering of all equipment required  
- Support fulfilment teams to address unexpected site issues  
- Ensure compliance with QA procedures, current codes and regulations  

What we are looking for:  
- Proven technical ability with experience using AutoCAD / 3D Revit is essential  
- Tertiary qualifications in Engineering (or equivalent) for a related discipline or appropriate level of relevant industry experience  
- Exposure to 3D Modelling e.g. BIM and configuration tools  
- Excellent communication skills and ability to communicate at all levels  
- Be highly organised with strong attention to detail  
- Ability to work autonomously and within a team environment  
- High level of enthusiasm and a willingness to learn!  

About Schindler:  
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 67,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.  

HOW TO apply:

jobs.title: Elevator Service Tech & Electrician — Grow Your Career
jobs.description:
Job Title: Elevator Service Technician  
Company: BOSS Elevators  
Location: Australia  

About the Role:  
We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will play a vital part in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sydney and Country Coast region.

You will be directly accountable for responding to critical routine maintenance, repairs, and service calls that focus primarily on upholding and improving standards of electrical engineering. You will also take part in project management and HSE (Health, Safety, and Environment) standards.

What You'll Be Doing:  
- Routine Maintenance: Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual, and 5-year testing.  
- Industry Standards & Troubleshooting: Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as elevator control systems, electronic fixtures, and hydraulic systems.  
- Carry Out Repairs: Conduct rapid responsive repairs, ensure clear communication, and deliver high-quality work standards to meet the range of elevator service needs.  
- Technical Expertise: Provide expert technical support and offer recommendations on service enhancements.  
- Methodical Detailed Record Keeping: Thoroughly record all maintenance and service actions undertaken, adhering to documentation compliance and future reference.  
- Workplace Safety & Compliance: Maintain high compliance with local and national standards, updating daily reports and addressing any compliance issues.  
- Customer Liaison: Act as the primary point of contact for customers, ensuring they are informed and updated on service requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that communicate respect and commitment to superior services.  
- Contingent Skills Development: Participate in training sessions as needed.  

What We’re Looking For:  
- Flexible, proactive attitude with a focus on safety and quality services.  
- Minimum 5 years’ experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.  
- Qualified Electrical License (restricted).  
- Current Working at Heights qualification (favorable but not essential).  
- Certificate in GWO (favorable but not essential).  
- Resilience and the ability to work autonomously as well as part of a team.  
- Competitive Remuneration based on your experience and qualifications.  
- Community Lifestyle: Skip the commute and enjoy living in one of the most desirable areas in Australia.  

Please apply if you meet the requirements and are looking for an exciting opportunity to develop your career in the elevator service industry.

Operations Manager  
Direct Lifts  

Company Overview  
Direct Lifts Australia specialises in supply and install of modern, eye-catching lifts and elevators throughout the east coast of Australia. Working closely with architects, builders and property owners we collaborate and design lifts to suit our clients’ requirements and decor.  

With a strong reputation for quality, compliance, and service excellence, we are seeking an experienced Operations Manager to lead our Installation and Service Division, based at our Kingsgrove office in Sydney.  

Position Summary  
As Operations Manager, you will take full accountability for the performance of the installation and service operations, ensuring projects are delivered safely, efficiently, on time, and on budget.  

Key Responsibilities  
Reporting directly to the Managing Director, you will be responsible for driving performance, profitability, and team engagement across the division. Additional responsibilities include:  
- Lead and manage day-to-day operations across lift installation and service teams.  
- Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and financial performance to meet agreed KPIs and profit targets.  
- Drive operational efficiencies and implement cost control strategies.  
- Manage workforce planning, scheduling, and resource allocation.  
- Supervise and support technicians, project managers, service coordinators, administration staff, and sales personnel.  
- Maintain high standards of safety, compliance, and customer satisfaction.  
- Manage technical lift inventory and spare parts for NSW operations.  
- Strengthen relationships with builders, developers, and residential clients.  
- Foster a culture of accountability, continuous improvement.

Operations Manager  
Direct Lifts  

- Manage technical lift inventory and spare parts for NSW operations.  
- Strengthen relationships with builders, developers, and residential clients.  
- Foster a culture of accountability, teamwork, and continuous improvement.  

About You  

You are a confident and capable operational leader with a strong understanding of construction or lift installation environments. You combine technical knowledge with commercial awareness and people leadership skills.  

Skills & Experience  

- Previous experience in lift installation, construction, or related technical industry (highly desirable)  
- Proven leadership experience managing operational teams.  
- Strong financial acumen with demonstrated experience managing budgets and improving profitability.  
- Experience using operational/service management systems (AroFlo highly desirable).  
- Experience with Microsoft Business Central (advantageous).  
- Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams across B2B and B2C client bases.  
- Strong organisational, communication, and problem-solving skills.  
- A proactive, solutions-focused leadership style.  
- High-level competency in Microsoft Office Suite.  

Benefits  

- Competitive base salary commensurate with experience.  
- Company vehicle and tools of trade.  
- Performance-based incentive structure.  
- A genuine opportunity to shape and influence operational strategy.  
- Supportive leadership and collaborative team culture.  
- Long-term career growth within a stable and growing Australian business.  

Application Process  

If you are ready to take ownership of operations and drive performance within a respected lift company, we look forward to hearing from you.

jobs.title: Expressions of Interest - Apprentice Program 2027
jobs.description:
Expressions of Interest - Apprentice Program 2027

Schindler Lifts Australia  
Gold Coast , Queensland  

Apply for this job  

Register your interest for our Apprentice Program 2027. In August 2026 we will reach out with the application details.  

WHY join Schindler?  
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We foster the potential through careful selection and constant development. We provide the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally. We offer job stability to our employees and are well-positioned for growth with the trajectory of our business in the coming years.  

About the role:  
Our apprentices are trained in all the skills required to service elevators, escalators and moving walkways. If you join our program you will rotate across our business to help you decide what you want to specialise in after completing your apprenticeship.  

As an apprentice, you will get to work closely with our customers and develop exceptional customer service skills. One of the many bonuses is that we will provide you with the chance to work full-time and study at TAFE / Trade School to receive your trade qualification in Electrotechnology Certificate III.  

What’s in it for you:  
- An indentured apprenticeship - 4 years  
- Career in a fast-growing electrical industry  
- 9-day fortnight (RDO)

Expressions of Interest - Apprentice Program 2027 jobs in Gold Coast Region

Leave us your email address and we'll send you similar new jobs

To be successful:
- Completed school studies to Year 10 or above
- Ideally have completed Certificate II in Electrotechnology
- Excellent safety awareness
- Hands-on approach to enjoy mechanical and electrical training
- An interest in working with electrical systems
- Good communication and the ability to work in a team
- Enthusiasm and a strong work ethic
- Previous work experience will also be highly regarded

About the application process:
- August - Applications open

About us:
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

Further Information & how to apply:
We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply. Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a manager.

This is a registration only - Applications open July/ August 2025 & We will reach out to you with the application link.

jobs.title: AUS Inds Lifting Team Manager

jobs.description:
LIFTING TECHNICIAN

Motion APAC

Derwent Park, Tasmania

Apply for this job

As a Lifting Technician, you’ll be responsible for inspecting lifting equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards and will work closely with customers to provide expert advice and solutions, ensuring their equipment is safe and operational.

If you have rigging, engineering or hands-on experience, we want to hear from you!

IF YOU DON’T HAVE THESE SKILLS, THAT IS NO PROBLEM!

What we will require from you is: a keen eye for detail, a strong work ethic, and a keenness to learn. It’s a physical role to some degree, but we can provide all the training you need once you are trained and competent.

Join us in ensuring the safety and reliability of lifting equipment. Take the next step in your rewarding career with a Global company.

Key Responsibilities

- Perform inspections, testing, and certification of lifting equipment, including chains, slings, hoists, and other lifting gear.
- Conduct repairs and maintenance on lifting equipment to ensure optimal performance and compliance with safety standards.
- Provide technical advice and support to customers regarding lifting solutions and equipment.
- Maintain accurate records of inspections, repairs, and certifications.
- Ensure all work complies with relevant Australian Standards and safety regulations.
- Collaborate with the sales team to identify customer needs and recommend appropriate lifting solutions.

Uphold a strong commitment to workplace safety and quality standards.

Who are you:
- Can do attitude with a strong work ethic.
- Basic understanding of Australian Standards.
- Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving skills.
- Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
- Excellent communication and customer service skills.
- High attention to detail and commitment to quality.
- Current driver’s license is essential.
- A commitment to safety and compliance.

What We Offer:
- New Ford Ranger vehicle, laptop, ipad and phone.
- NATA & LEEA national certifications.
- A supportive and collaborative team environment.
- Full day induction to help you settle into the team.
- Access to employee benefits, including discounts.
- Opportunities for career growth and development.
- Uniforms supplied - feel like a team.
- Recognition programs and bonus schemes.
- 1 paid Volunteer Leave Day per calendar year.
- Parental leave top-up allowance.
- Employee Assistance Program with access to mental health and well-being support initiatives including confidential counselling services.

About Motion:
As part of the Motion Australia family, Motion (Websterbsc) is a trusted name in industrial solutions, offering top-quality products and services to keep our customers moving. This is your chance to be part of a passionate team that thrives on delivering exceptional service and building strong relationships with our valued customers.

Join us on this exciting journey of success and growth and let us help you empower your career with us!

MOTION is an equal opportunity employer.

jobs.title: Elevator Service Technician
jobs.description:
Job Title: Elevator Service Technician  
Company: BOSS Elevators  
Location: Australia  

**About the role:**  
We are seeking an experienced Elevator Service Technician with a passion for troubleshooting and problem-solving to join our team at BOSS Elevators. In this role, you will assist in maintaining and enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of elevator systems across the Sydney and Docklands Central region.

You will be directly involved with the responsibility for providing routine maintenance inspections and repairs, which factors into ensuring operational readiness and compliance with relevant safety regulations. This role requires excellent communication and customer service skills to work effectively with clients, while also being able to work independently and as part of a team.

**What you’ll be doing:**  
- **Routine Maintenance:** Perform scheduled and preventative maintenance on elevator systems meeting standards for monthly, annual, and 5-year testing.  
- **Breakdown & Troubleshooting:** Efficiently identify and resolve technical issues with components such as control systems, electrical drives, and mechanical systems; expertise in both hydraulic and traction elevators is essential.  
- **Customer Care Role:** Conducting capacity audits, maintaining clear communication, and delivering high-quality service to clients.  
- **Material & Technical Records:** Thoroughly document all maintenance work and service actions; maintain accurate documentation for compliance and future reference.  
- **Adhere to Safety & Compliance:** Maintain adherence to safety protocols and legislative requirements, providing timely, effective solutions that align with customers’ superior services.  
- **Continual Skill Development:** Participate in training to enhance skills and knowledge of advanced technology.  

**What we’re looking for:**  
- Flexible, proactive approach to service delivery and quality service.  
- Minimum 5 years of experience in the elevator service industry, with superior fault-finding abilities.  
- Qualified Electrical License is restricted.  
- Own transport is essential.  
- Working at Heights certification (favorable but not essential).  
- Certificate in Elevators (favorable but not essential).  

**Other information:**  
- Competitive Remuneration: Based on your experience and qualifications.  
- Casual Lifestyle: Enjoy the flexibility of working from home or one of the many locations across Australia.

jobs.title: Expressions of Interest - Apprentice Program 2027
jobs.location: Australia, Western Australia, Perth Region, Bayswater Area, Bayswater
jobs.description:
Expressions of Interest - Apprentice Program 2027

Schindler Lifts Australia

Perth, Western Australia

Register your interest for our Apprentice Program 2027. In August 2026 we will reach out with the application link. 

WHY join Schindler?
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on identifying potential through careful selection and constant development. We offer the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally. We launch people. We offer job stability to our employees.

About the role:
Our apprentices are trained in all the skills required to install, service, and repair elevators, escalators and moving walkways. If you join our program you will rotate across our business to help you decide what you want to specialise in after completing your apprenticeship.

As an apprentice, you will get to work closely with our customers and develop exceptional customer service skills. One of the many bonuses is that we will provide you with the chance to work full-time and study at TAFE / Trade School to receive your trade qualification in Electrotechnology Certificate III.

What’s in it for you:
- An indentured apprenticeship - 4 years
- Career in a fast-growing electrical industry
- 9-day fortnight (RDO)

**Expressions of Interest - Apprentice Program 2027 jobs in Bayswater**

- Hands-on electrical & mechanical training
- Access to testing tools at your fingertips (iPhone & laptop interfaces etc)
- Working with state-of-the-art equipment on some of Australia’s most iconic projects
- Continually changing job sites

**Our statistics:**
- Our retention of apprentices is 96% (general trade completion rate of apprentices is around 40%)
- 96 Current apprentices nationally
- Well over 300 graduations since 2000

**To be successful:**
- Completed school studies to year 10 or above
- Ideally have completed Certificate II in E
- Excellent safety awareness
- Hands-on approach to enjoy mechanical and
- An interest in working with electrical systems
- Good communication and the ability to work in
- Enthusiasm and a strong work ethic
- Previous work experience will also be highly regarded

**About the application process:**
- August - Applications open

**About us:**
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

**Further Information & how to apply:**
We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply. Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a manager.

This is a registration only - Applications open July/ August 2025 & We will reach out to you with the application link.

jobs.title: Lift Installer
jobs.description:
Lift Installer (Training Provided)  
Goingup Elevators – Burwood NSW  

Full time  No experience required  
24d ago, from seek.com.au  

Apply on company site  

The Company  
Going Up is one of Australia's largest independent, Australian-owned elevator companies — proudly manufacturing, installing, servicing, and modernising lifts along the east coast.  

With a strong pipeline of projects and a great team culture, we're expanding our Sydney installation team and looking for Lift Installers — including qualified Electricians, Mechanics, or Fitters who want to move into a specialised, long-term career in the elevator and lift industry.  

Whether you're an experienced tradesperson or ready to transition into lift installation, Going Up provides a supportive team, structured training, and a strong Sydney project pipeline. Join a smaller, Australian-owned company that values its people.  

Our worksites are located across Sydney, with our office based in Brookvale.  

The Role  
As a Lift Installer, you'll be involved in the full installation process — assembling, wiring and fitting out new lift systems from start to finish. You'll work alongside experienced technicians, developing skills in one of Australia's most stable and rewarding trades.  

Key Responsibilities  
- Install and fit out lift systems on a variety of projects  
- Learn from experienced supervisors and colleagues  
- Support senior technicians, supervisors and site handovers

- Install and fit out lift systems on a variety of projects
- Learn from experienced supervisors and colleagues
- Support testing, commissioning, and site handovers
- Maintain safety and quality standards

About You
- Trade qualified or experienced in electrical, mechanical or construction work.
- Mechanically or electrically minded, with a strong interest in technical hands-on work.
- Reliable, organised and a strong team player.
- Commitment to safety and professional workmanship.
- Willingness to learn and develop new skills
- Lift or elevator experience is great — but not essential; full training is provided.

What We Offer
- Competitive pay with opportunities for overtime.
- Comprehensive on-the-job training with experienced installers.
- Support to build a recognised career in the vertical transport / elevator industry.
- Friendly, supportive and professional team environment.
- Uniform provided.
- Immediate start and a stable, full-time permanent role.

Ready to elevate your career?

Heavy Lift Engineer
A fantastic opportunity exists for an experienced Lift Engineer to join our business.
The Company:
With over 20 years of experience, Advanced Cranes and Rigging is a national leader in lifting
solutions. We support Tier 1 government and private sector projects with a modern fleet ranging
from 3t to 800+ tonnes. Safety, innovation, and quality are the pillars of our business—
underpinned by ISO45001 accreditation and a practical, values-driven culture.
 
Role Overview:
This role requires a qualified Lift Engineer with extensive experience in the Mobile Crane industry.
In this role, you will be a valued member of the Engineering team, and responsible for lift
planning, site inspections and logistical arrangements of lift plans. 
Lift plans will be for Tier One projects, along with other infrastructure and commercial projects.
Role is based out of our North Brisbane depot.
  
Key Responsibilities:
Collaborate closely with Project Managers / Safety / Operations team and other stakeholders
to ensure seamless project execution.
Plan and coordinate lift studies in a timely fashion.
 Foundation for success:
Bachelor, Diploma, or Post-graduate degree in Civil/ Mechanical Engineering or similar
related field.
3 years’ experience in a similar engineer role (exp in rail, wind or infrastructure projects highly
regarded)
Proficient in CAD software, 2D AUTOCAD in particular, 3D advantageous.  Proficient in
LICCON Work Planner highly desirable.
Familiarity with mobile, crawler and pick & carry cranes; Liebherr and Terex/Franna in
particular.
Excellent Office365 skills (Word, Excel, MS Projects, Teams).
Familiar with safety and quality standards in the construction/crane industry.
Customer relationship management experience.
Experience in project management advantageous but not essential.
Organisational, communication and time management skills.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
An attractive package will be tailored for the best candidate. 
Apply now and join Australia’s most trusted name in lifting and rigging.

jobs.description:
