Add expected salary to your profile for insights
Posted 25d ago
Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers! At Otis, it’s
our people that make us different. 
Be a part of Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual
and the company reach new heights. 
We are looking for Construction Mechanics to be based in and around Sydney, New South
Wales, Australia.
About you 
You will be the face of the OTIS brand and responsible for installing lifts, escalators, and
travelators in compliance with the relevant codes and Otis specifications and procedures. 
These permanent or 12 month fixed term contract roles will be reporting to the Project Manager
and working closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters & Field Engineers as well as
Construction Coordinators. 
Permanent & 12 month fixed term contract roles available
Reports to the Construction Project Manager
Key Responsibilities 
Install and Modernise lifts, escalators, and travelators and work to the highest safety and
environmental standards while meeting service targets. 
Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in
coordination with the Project Manager. 
Occasional travel to regional Adelaide.
Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to
satisfy the customers of your processes. 
Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property. 
Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards and maintain
and work to the Quality System. 
Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures,
including reporting any conditions that may impact on the environment or any employee. 
What you will need to be successful 
Have a Electrical trade qualification or equivalent.
Min 5 years work experience as a Construction Technician within the construction,
engineering or elevator industry.
Have Working at Heights, Rigging and Dogging experience.
Hold a valid driver's license and has the Australia full working rights visa
Able to work unsupervised and has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information
and skills through training. 
Plan, prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner. 
Enjoys communicating with various stakeholders at different levels and working closely with
others in the field. 
Key Benefits
Industry-leading compensation and benefits per the local EBA
Be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers.
Meaningful career opportunities for people to grow through excellent Learning programs.
Becoming part of the largest vertical transportation company in the world and gaining
exposure to the benefits and opportunities offered.
Apply now and help us build what's next!
About us
We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and
service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units
worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.
We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest
transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.
Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion
We “lift” our people to their highest potential.
Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both
culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and
celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.
We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a
best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders,
sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity
of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice
feels safe, welcomed and heard.
We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career.
This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help
working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.
Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive
consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation,
gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other protected class
according to applicable law. Otis are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive place to work.
We strongly encourage applications from women, and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander
people
For more information: https://www.otis.com/en/au/our-company/diversity-and-inclusion

jobs.title: Warehouse Forklift Operative
jobs.description:
Warehouse Forklift Operator

Recruitment Alternative – Brisbane QLD

14d ago, from Recruitment Alternative

- Join a great team in a well-established & growing business
- Brand new purpose built premises
- Work for a market leader with a great culture
- Opportunities for Career Advancement

About the Role:  
Our Client supplies world leading Construction Equipment brands through sales and hire of their equipment throughout four countries in the World and in addition, they also have a comprehensive after-sales divisions for servicing and parts.

They are now seeking a driven hands-on individual who thrives working in a fast paced environment and has the ability to “get in and get things done”. Responsibilities will include receipt and dispatch of parts to customers, ensuring packaging and labelling is correct, coordinating freight and logistics using online platforms, performing regular stocktakes and keeping the stores and freight areas clean and safe. The successful applicant will need to pride themselves on their excellent attention to detail and possess the willingness to add value to their role and this growing business. LF or LO Forklift Licence is essential.

**Benefits:**
- Opportunity to get in on the ground floor with a highly successful organisation
- A Company that values and rewards their staff
- Promotional opportunities available
- Wholly Owned Family Business with long term employees

**Duties:**
- Ensure goods received and dispatched are in good condition and corresponding paperwork is correct
- Utilising ERP system - updating and understanding stock locations, goods receipting and shipping
- Utilising online systems to coordinate international and domestic freight
- Ensure stock rotation systems are maintained
- Maintaining an accurate, clean and safe warehouse
- Undertake regular stocktakes & inventory cycle counts
- Forklift operation, loading and unloading of goods

**Skills and Experience:**
- Minimum 12 months experience in a stores role
- LF or LO Forklift Licence
- ERP experience & confident utilising computers
- Professional Phone Manner essential
- Mechanically minded and/or an interest in heavy machinery, an advantage
- Positive, friendly, can-do attitude
- Hard worker, who is honest and reliable
- Excellent attention to detail

- Professional Phone Manner essential
- Mechanically minded and/or an interest in heavy machinery, an advantage
- Positive, friendly, can-do attitude
- Hard worker, who is honest and reliable
- Excellent attention to detail
- Drivers Licence and a vehicle to get to work, as there is no suitable public transport
- Physically fit, with the ability to undertake heavy lifting
- MUST be in Australia and have full working rights. This position is NOT suitable to candidates who do not have permanent working rights.

How to apply
All applications are to be submitted online – click APPLY. We will be reviewing applications as they are received so apply today! Please note only candidates that meet our client’s selection criteria will be contacted.

Goingup Elevators – Sydney CBD NSW

24d ago, from seek.com.au

Going Up is an Australian-owned independent elevator company operating across the east coast of Australia. We install, service, and modernise lift systems and are growing our Installation team.  

We’re looking for reliable, trade-experienced or mechanically minded Trade Assistants who are confident with tools and ready to work on active construction sites.  

Office based in Brookvale. Project sites located across Sydney.  

Lift experience is highly regarded — but not essential.  

Your Role  
- Assist with the installation and fit-out of lift systems

jobs.title: Mechanical Technician
jobs.description:
**Electrical Technician**  
Marble Group – Sydney NSW  

**$145,000 - $145,000 a year**  
Permanent  
3mo ago, from Marble Group  

**The opportunity and why apply**  
This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
- $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
- Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)  
- OEM Training and development on offer to become an expert in your field.  
- Working in a technical and challenging role on automated stacker cranes  

**The company**  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
- Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
- People focused management team with a strong team Orientated culture  
- Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities  

**Roster and hours**  
You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

Roster and hours

You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights, 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

- 12-hour shifts with a paid lunch and smoko
- Day shift: 6am - 6pm
- Night shift: 6pm - 6am
- Occasional overtime available on the end of your swing for those looking for more hours.

The role

As a highly skilled electrician, you’ll be responsible for all electrical maintenance and repair at one of Australia’s biggest logistic parks. All of your work will be completed at the well-equipped site.

- Electrical and mechanical fault finding, preventative maintenance and emergency repair work on a range of rail mounted automated stacker cranes
- Work on a range of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives and crane management software
- Working on variable speed drives, control logic on wiring diagrams, and off grid generator systems.

Your value

You will be a trade qualified electrician with a well-rounded knowledge of electrical fault finding and repairs on mobile plant and/or cranes.

- Electrical trade qualification (Cert III Electrotechnology)
- Hydraulic and mechanical knowledge (highly regarded)
- An excellent attitude towards work with proven ability to work both autonomously and in a team environment.
- Working knowledge and use of electrical schematics and symbols
- Must demonstrate working knowledge of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives
- Be able to pass a pre-employment medical and national police check

Acorn Stairlifts is the Australian division of the world's leading manufacturer and distributor of
stairlifts to help people with stairs in their homes. The home-care industry in Australia is currently

jobs.title: Forklift operators
jobs.description:
Lift Operators  
Qube  
Karratha, Port Hedland, Karratha & Pilbara WA  
Plant & Machinery Operators (Construction)  
Full time  

Posted 5h ago  

Apply  
Save

ABOUT US

Are you ready to be part of something extraordinary? At Qube Energy, we are revolutionising the supply chain landscape for the Energy, Mining, and Industrial sectors in Australia’s resource-rich locations. As a business unit of Qube Ports, we harness our extensive network and unparalleled expertise to deliver innovative, tailored solutions that drive efficiency and reliability for our clients.

BE SAFE is at the core of everything we do. We are committed to creating a safe working environment for our team and clients, ensuring that every operation is conducted with the highest standards of safety and care. Our focus on efficiency means we are constantly optimising our processes to deliver the best results, while our dedication to reliability ensures that our clients can count on us to meet their needs, every time.

Imagine working with a team that is committed to maximising the value of our current services while boldly diversifying into a full suite of in-house transport, and supply chain services. Our operations are data-driven and supported by cutting-edge systems and IT solutions, all managed by a high-performance team dedicated to excellence.

ABOUT YOU

You will be committed to safety in the workplace and be committed to 100% Chain of Responsibility compliance. We're proud of our 'Zero Harm' initiative and provide extensive on-the-job training to ensure you get home safely at the end of every day.

Qube is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and we encourage women and Indigenous peoples of all ages to apply.

Lift Operators  
Qube  

Qube is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and we encourage women and Indigenous peoples of all ages to apply.  

ABOUT THE ROLE  

The Lift Operator will typically work as part of a small team performing well-defined tasks where there are a specific objective and outcome. Primary purpose of the role is to operate and control heavy lifting plant and equipment, primarily Reach Stackers (3–45 tonne capacity), to lift, move, position, stack and place containers, heavy cargo and oversized industrial equipment within port, yard and warehouse environments. Ensure all lifting operations are conducted safely, efficiently and in compliance with operational, regulatory and safety requirements.  

Multiple positions available.  

LOCATION: NORTH WEST SUPPLY BASE, KARRATHA  

SPECIFIC RESPONSIBILITIES  

- Operate Reach Stackers and other lifting plant to lift, move, load, unload and stack shipping containers, oversized cargo and heavy equipment.  
- Select, inspect, test and use appropriate lifting gear including slings, chains, spreader bars, hooks and tag lines.  
- Perform dogging, rigging and spotting duties to support safe lifting operations.  
- Manoeuvre containers within operational yards and storage areas in accordance with site plans and operational instructions, including controlling the movement of loads and monitoring speed.  
- Ensure correct load calculations and safe working load limits are observed.  
- Safely load and unload trucks, trailers and other transport vehicles, ensuring compliance with axle weight and load restraint requirements.
jobs.description_summary: Qube Energy is seeking full-time Forklift Operators to safely operate heavy lifting equipment, primarily Reach Stackers, at their North West Supply Base in Karratha. Candidates should prioritize safety and compliance while efficiently managing container and cargo handling operations in a team-oriented environment.

You may recognize our products in some of the world’s most famous landmarks including the Eiffel Tower, Empire State Building, Burj Khalifa and the Petronas Twin Towers! We are 72,000 people strong, including engineers, digital technology experts, sales, and functional specialists, as well as factory and field technicians, all committed to meeting the diverse needs of our customers and passengers in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. We are proud to be a diverse global team with a rich legacy of innovation that continues to be the bedrock of a fast-moving, high-performing culture.

When you join Otis, you become part of an innovative, customer-focused team at the cutting edge of technology. You’ll belong to a diverse, trusted, and caring community where you can bring your whole self, and at the same time, appreciate the unique perspectives and capabilities you’ll gain working alongside the best and brightest in the industry. 

We provide opportunities, training, and resources for professional growth, including support for Engineering and Major Projects and our Employee Development Program, through which Otis sponsors colleagues to pursue degrees or certificates.

Today, our focus more than ever is on people. As an industry leader, we put people – our passengers, customers, and colleagues – at the center of everything we do. We are committed to doing business responsibly and sustainably through what we call our Three Absolutes – prioritizing Safety, Ethical Behavior, and Quality. To learn more about environmental, social and governance (ESG) contributions, visit our website.

Become a part of the Otis team and help us #BuildTheFuture!

Otis is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status, age, or any other protected class according to applicable law. To request an accommodation in completing an employment application due to a special need or a disability, please contact us at careers@otis.com.

jobs.description:
Lift Installer (Training Provided)  
Goingup Elevators – Burwood NSW  

Full time No experience required  
24d ago, from seek.com.au  

Apply on company site  

The Company  
Going Up is one of Australia's largest independent, Australian-owned elevator companies — proudly manufacturing, installing, servicing, and modernising lifts along the east coast.  

With a strong pipeline of projects and a great team culture, we're expanding our Sydney installation team and looking for Lift Installers — including qualified Electricians, Mechanics, or Fitters who want to move into a specialised, long-term career in the elevator and lift industry.  

Whether you’re an experienced tradesperson or ready to transition into lift installation, Going Up provides a supportive team, structured training, and a strong Sydney project pipeline. Join a smaller, Australian-owned company that values its people.  

Our worksites are located across Sydney, with our office based in Brookvale.  

The Role  
As a Lift Installer, you'll be involved in the full installation process — assembling, wiring and fitting out new lift systems from start to finish. You'll work alongside experienced technicians, developing skills in one of Australia's most stable and rewarding trades.  

Key Responsibilities  
- Install and fit out lift systems on a variety of projects  
- Learn from experienced supervisors and colleagues  
- Support senior technicians, contractors, and site handovers

jobs.title: Repairs Sales Representative
jobs.description:
Repairs Sales Representative  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  
3.6  
28 reviews  
View all jobs  

Brisbane QLD  
Sales Representatives/Consultants (Sales)  
Contract/Temp  
Add expected salary to your profile for insights  

Posted 18d ago  

Apply  
Save

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers! At Otis, it’s our people that make us different.

Join the Otis family where collaboration, innovation and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

We are looking for a Repairs Product Sales Representative to be based either in our Brisbane, Queensland or Melbourne, Victoria office.

- 12 months fixed term contract role
- Reports to the Repairs Sales Manager.
- Location - Brisbane or Melbourne

About You

As a Repairs Product Sales Representative, you will work as part of a team that supplements the wider "T" Product Sales activity of the OTIS Australasia.

You primarily focus on standard "T" products for a defined period, and where opportunity exists.

What are "T" products in OTIS?
Lift accessories that are not covered in our typical Maintenance contracts which include lift communications, dialers, door detectors, in-car lighting, safety items, pit stop switchers, top of car handrails and etc.

Key Responsibilities

- Deliver revenue performance: Consistently achieve (and exceed) assigned T Sales revenue and margin targets in line with the approved business plan, with clear accountability for results.
- Maximise speed-to-lead and conversion: Action all T leads from every source immediately on receipt, applying disciplined follow-up to maximise contact and conversion rates.

Repairs Sales Representative  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  

- Maximise speed-to-lead and conversion: Action all T leads from every source immediately on receipt, applying disciplined follow-up to maximise contact and conversion rates.  
- Qualify needs and build demand: Partner with the lead source to confirm scope/requirements, then engage the customer to validate needs, create urgency, and progress opportunities through the buying cycle.  
- Drive campaign-led pipeline: Actively participate in all T product campaigns, generating pipeline contribution and supporting the Key Account team by providing responsive, accurate quoting and commercial support.  
- Own proposal development end-to-end: Produce accurate estimates and secure required internal approvals to create a compliant Service Works Proposal, then submit to the customer within agreed turnaround times.  
- Execute a structured close plan: Proactively follow up every submission using a structured, cyclical call-closing cadence to progress objections, confirm decision steps, and secure approval/booking.  
- Build and maintain a robust pipeline: Maintain a healthy opportunity funnel that supports target attainment, including ongoing prospecting, opportunity progression, and clear next steps at every stage.  
- Forecast with accuracy: Keep CRM/sales systems current, report activity consistently, and provide accurate forecasting against plan (pipeline, probability, timing, value).  

What you will need to be successful  
- Have a degree/diploma/certificate in Marketing, Business, or a related field.  
- Minimum 2-3 years’ experience in Tele Sales, Frontline Sales, or Account Management, with a results-driven mindset.  
- Exposure to technical or solution-based sales would be an added advantage  
- Proven ability to work independently and collaborate cross-functionally, building trusted relationships to drive outcomes.  
- Strong communications – verbal and written.  
- Attention to detail and accurate record-keeping are important for success  
- Sound knowledge of Microsoft 365 and the ability to learn and frequently switch between inhouse systems/databases is required  
- A desire to learn and understand the elevator industry and the technology involved

With a strong pipeline of projects and a great team culture, we’re expanding our Sydney installation team and looking for Lift Installers — including qualified Electricians, Mechanics, or Fitters who want to move into a specialised, long-term career in the elevator and lift industry.

Marble Group – Sydney NSW

2mo ago, from Marble Group

The opportunity and why apply  
This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
• $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
• Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)  
• OEM Training and development on offer to become an expert in your field.  
• Working in a technical and challenging role on automated stacker cranes  

The company  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
• Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
• People focused management team with a strong team orientated culture  
• Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities  

Roster and hours  
You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

Roster and hours

You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

- 12-hour shifts with a paid lunch and smoko
- Day shift: 6am - 6pm
- Night shift: 6pm - 6am
- Occasional overtime available on the end of your swing for those looking for more hours.

The role

As a highly skilled electrician, you'll be responsible for all electrical maintenance and repair at one of Australia’s biggest logistic parks. All of your work will be completed at the well-equipped site.

- Electrical and mechanical fault finding, preventative maintenance and emergency repair work on a range of rail mounted automated stacker cranes
- Work on a range of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives and crane management software

About the role

Simplex Elevators, a leading provider of elevator installation and maintenance services, is seeking a skilled Maintenance Technician to join our team in Brisbane, Queensland. This full-time role is responsible for the preventative maintenance and repair of elevators, ensuring our clients' vertical transportation systems operate efficiently and safely.

What you'll be doing

1. Conduct regular inspections and servicing of elevators to identify and address any issues

2. Troubleshoot and repair malfunctioning elevator components, such as motors, control systems, and safety devices

4. Respond promptly to emergency callouts and work to resolve issues in a timely manner

5. Collaborate with the broader team to ensure the seamless operation of our clients' elevator systems

What we're looking for

1. Minimum 2 years of experience as an elevator maintenance technician or similar role

2. Strong mechanical and electrical troubleshooting skills, with the ability to diagnose and repair a wide range of elevator systems

What we're looking for

1. Minimum 2 years of experience as an elevator maintenance technician or similar role

2. Strong mechanical and electrical troubleshooting skills, with the ability to diagnose and repair a wide range of elevator components

5. Excellent customer service skills and the ability to communicate effectively with clients

6. Commitment to safety and a proactive approach to identifying and mitigating risks

What we offer

At Simplex Elevators, we are committed to the development and wellbeing of our employees. In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a range of benefits, including:

1. Comprehensive training and ongoing professional development opportunities

2. Discounted health insurance and gym memberships

About us

Simplex Elevators is a leading provider of elevator installation, maintenance, and repair services in Australia. Are you an Electrician that gets things done, likes a short work week, and would like to work in a

Licensing: A current, unrestricted Electrical Licence.

diagrams.
Aptitude: Exceptional electrical skills paired with strong mechanical "know-how."

Quick apply  

Elevate Your Career – Join Our Service Team as a Lift Service Technician  

Are you ready to take the next step in your career? Aussie Lifts is one of only a few Australian Lift Manufacturers. Proudly Aussie owned since its inception in 1990, we provide access solutions to homes and businesses. We're seeking a qualified electrician to become an integral part of our growing Service Team. In this vital role, you’ll be responsible for the maintenance, servicing, and repair of a variety of lifts, including Hydraulic, MRL, and Platform lifts. Your technical expertise will ensure these systems operate safely and efficiently, making you a key contributor to our continued success.  

Why Join Us?  

• Competitive Pay & Benefits: We offer industry-leading rates and flexible working hours.  
• Career Advancement: Grow with a company that values internal progression and professional development.  
• Supportive Work Environment: Join a collaborative team in a dynamic and rapidly expanding business.  
• Meaningful Impact: Your work directly enhances the safety, reliability, and satisfaction of our customers.

Electrician/Lift Service Technician  
Aussie Lifts Pty Ltd  

- Supportive Work Environment: Join a collaborative team in a dynamic and rapidly expanding business.  
- Meaningful Impact: Your work directly enhances the safety, reliability, and satisfaction of our customers.  

Key Responsibilities  
- Lift Service & Maintenance: Perform expert-level servicing, repairs, and routine maintenance on various lift systems.  
- Inspections & Troubleshooting: Conduct diagnostic tests, inspections, and implement repairs to ensure optimal lift performance.  
- Customer Engagement: Provide professional, customer-focused service and advice regarding lift maintenance and care.  
- Safety Compliance: Maintain strict adherence to workplace health and safety regulations.  
- Record Keeping: Accurately document all work completed, including maintenance logs and repair reports.  

What We’re Looking For  
- Electrical Licence: Essential for the role.  
- Industry Knowledge: Prior lift experience is highly regarded, but not required—training will be provided for the right candidate.  
- Problem Solving Skills: Strong technical and analytical capabilities with the ability to work autonomously.  
- Excellent Communication: Confident communicator with a focus on delivering outstanding customer service.  
- Valid Driver’s Licence: Required for travel to customer sites.  

If you’re a motivated and skilled individual with a passion for excellence and customer service, we want to hear from you. Take the next step in your career and apply now to join our energetic and growing team!

WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on supporting our employees to meet their full potential through careful selection and constant development to make them a leader in their area. You will have the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally whilst working with a great bunch of people. We offer job stability to our employees as we focus on promoting from within and foresee a growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.

About the role:

We have an opportunity for an experienced Project Manager to join our Construction team based in Fyshwick Canberra. As the successful candidate, you will be a resilient and enthusiastic individual with strong project management, leadership and communication skills. You will work within a dynamic and successful team with an impressive client list and with the support of a strong management structure. You will be responsible for managing all aspects of our new installation projects, to ensure that the project is achieved on time and on budget, whilst ensuring client satisfaction.

What’s in it for you:

- Highly competitive base + 12% Super (Depending on Lift & Escalator experience)
- $18K Car allowance
- 12% Super
- $23k Bonus structure
- Excellent opportunity for you to expand your experience at world world-leading Lift company
- Permanent full-time role
- A work/life balance organisation - culture-first approach
- A great learning environment with a local and global team
- Access to the Employee benefits discount scheme
- A wide variety of social activities - even a day off for your birthday!
- Office location Fyshwick

Project Manager  
Schindler Lifts Australia  
- A wide variety of social activities – even a day off for your birthday!  
- Office location Fyshwick  

Key Accountabilities:  
- Providing detailed schedules and contract management for projects from initiation to delivery  
- Management of our installation teams (both direct labour and subcontractors) to ensure that project milestones are met  
- Reporting on, assessing and analysing project performance and taking appropriate action to prevent overruns  
- Positive and pro-active financial management of projects to budget and delivery times  
- Effective and timely communication to all key stakeholders regarding technical information, resolution of issues and achievement of project milestones  

What we are looking for:  
- Demonstrated project management or site supervisor experience in the construction or building/services industry  
- A proven record in managing multiple projects to tight and demanding deadlines and the ability to overcome setbacks in high pressure situations  
- A strong commitment to safety, quality, environment and customer relationships  
- Previous people management and leadership skills, with the ability to adjust work and material requirements to meet schedules and customer demands  
- Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal with the ability to communicate with all levels of employees  
- Strong negotiation and influencing skills  
- Self-motivation and high levels of energy and enthusiasm, with the drive to achieve customer satisfaction  

About Schindler:  
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise transportation systems for almost every building.

Bouygues Construction – Adelaide SA  
3.1 from 17 reviews at SEEK

5d ago, from JobRadars - AU  

At Bouygues Construction Australia, we are dedicated to the design, construction, and delivery of nation shaping projects across civil infrastructure, engineering, and energy and services.  

We have proudly been working in Australia for more than 25 years.  

Our local experience in underpinned by significant global expertise and resourcing.  

The Role  

As Crane / Lift Supervisor, you will be responsible for planning, coordinating, and overseeing all crane and lifting operations across the T2D Project. You will lead site teams to ensure lifts are executed safely, efficiently, and in compliance with all legislative, project, and company requirements. Working closely with project management, subcontractors, and operators, you will develop and implement lift plans, conduct pre-lift briefings, monitor live lifting activities, and drive a strong safety culture. Your leadership and technical expertise will also be critical in identifying and eliminating potential risks.

**Responsibilities**

- Plan, coordinate, and supervise all crane and lifting operations on site
- Prepare, review, and implement lift plans, SWMS, and risk assessments
- Lead and coordinate crane operators, riggers, dogmen, and subcontractors
- Conduct pre-starts, pre-lift meetings, and toolbox talks
- Ensure compliance with WHS legislation, project standards, and company procedures
- Inspect cranes, lifting equipment, and rigging gear for certification and safety compliance
- Monitor live lifts and intervene to maintain safe systems of work
- Investigate incidents, near misses, and hazards and implement corrective actions

**About You**

- Proven experience as a Crane / Lift Supervisor on major civil or infrastructure projects
- Strong knowledge of lift planning, load charts, and rigging practices
- Hold a High Risk Work Licence (Crane)
- Current Dogging and Rigging licence (Intermediate or Advanced preferred)
- Construction Induction (White Card)
- Strong leadership skills with a proactive, safety-first approach
- Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
- Highly organised, reliable, and able to perform under pressure

jobs.title: Expressions of Interest - Office & Field Roles
jobs.location: Australia, Australian Capital Territory, Canberra Region, North Canberra, City
jobs.description:
Expressions of Interest - Office & Field Roles

Schindler Lifts Australia

Brisbane, Queensland

Apply for this job

WHY join Schindler?

At Schindler we are all about our culture! We focus on potential by careful selection and constant development. We offer the opportunity to grow and develop both personal and professional skills in each of people. We offer job stability to our employees as well as assisting the growth trajectory of our business in the coming years.

At Schindler, We Elevate…

As a leading employer in the urban mobility industry, we strive to provide a service excellence for our customers. Our goal is to have a highly motivated workforce, providing our clients with a work environment that is stimulating and attractive.

What we are looking for: 

We are currently seeking expressions of interest for individuals who would like to join our company in a field or office based roles nationally.

What’s in it for you?

Joining Schindler is an excellent opportunity for you to expand and utilise your existing skills in a large, global organisation. We have a great culture that is supportive and inclusive that offers the opportunity for growth both personally and professionally. 

About Schindler:

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

HOW TO apply:

We are constantly looking for passionate and enthusiastic people with the energy and drive to build relationships and networks. At Schindler you will find a highly motivated and engaged working environment that lets you grow as an individual and allows you to plan your career.

If you are an existing Schindler employee, please ensure you have discussed your application with your line manager prior to applying.

We support diversity and inclusion in all our work. We welcome applications from people with a multicultural background or a disability and will not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position unless you are directly employed by the company or where the recruiter has contacted a representative of the company.

Schindler Lifts Australia  
Sydney, New South Wales  

**Apply for this job**

Register your interest for our Apprentice Program 2027. In August 2026 we will reach out with the application links. 

**WHY join Schindler?**

At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on potential through careful selection and constant development. We provide the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally to launch people. We offer job stability to our employees along with the trajectory of our business in the coming years. 

**About the role:**

Our apprentices are trained in all the skills required to service and maintain lifts, escalators and moving walkways. If you join our program you will rotate across our business to help you decide what you want to specialise in after completing your apprenticeship. 

As an apprentice, you will get to work closely with our customers and develop exceptional customer service skills. One of the many bonuses is that we will provide you with the chance to work full-time and study at TAFE / Trade School to receive your trade qualification in Electrotechnology Certificate III. 

**What’s in it for you:**

- An indentured apprenticeship - 4 years 
- Career in a fast-growing electrical industry 
- 9-day fortnight (RDO)

This is a registration only - Applications open July/ August 2025 & We will reach out to you with the application link.

jobs.title: Electrical Technician
jobs.description:
Electrical Technician  
Marble Group – Sydney NSW  
$145,000 - $145,000 a year  
Permanent  
3mo ago, from Marble Group  

Apply on company site  

The opportunity and why apply  
This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
- $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
- Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)  
- OEM Training and development on offer to become an expert in your field.  
- Working in a technical and challenging role on automated stacker cranes  

The company  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
- Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
- People focused management team with a strong team orientated culture  
- Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities  

Roster and hours  
You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

jobs.description:
Project Manager  
Lift Brands – Coorparoo QLD  
Full time  
8d ago, from seek.com.au  

Why join Lift Brands?  
- Be part of Lift Brands – a global wellness leader  
- Attractive base salary + phone allowance + incentives  
- Excellent career progression and development opportunities in an innovative, global business. We’re committed to continuing improvement and have exciting plans for the future – grow your career with us!  
- Get your birthday off and up to 5 weeks of annual leave!  
- Complimentary access to Snap Fitness Clubs.  

About the role  
Reporting to the Manager – Product & Projects, this role works closely with internal stakeholders, including legal, to support franchisees, coordinate suppliers, and ensure clubs operate aligned with brand standards and proposed specifications.

- Complimentary access to Snap Fitness Clubs.

About the role

Reporting to the Manager – Product & Projects, this role works closely with internal stakeholders, including legal, to support franchisees, coordinate suppliers, and ensure clubs remain aligned with brand standards and approved specifications.

The Project Manager will contribute to the successful delivery of the club modernisation program by enabling, tracking, and supporting delivery outcomes, while also supporting supplier performance, compliance processes, and the consistent look, feel, and functionality of clubs across the Snap Fitness network.

This position works across three core areas that are critical to the Snap Fitness brand and the experience of its franchisees and members:

Projects & Modernisation

Support the delivery of the modernisation program across Australia and New Zealand through project coordination, supplier engagement, and implementation support. The role focuses on enabling, tracking, and supporting delivery outcomes to ensure clubs meet brand standards and operational requirements. This position does not directly manage construction, builders, or trades, and does not replace franchisee-appointed project managers or shopfitters.

Development & Brand Standards

Support franchisees to maintain compliance with brand guidelines and specifications, ensuring consistency across club design, fit-out, equipment, and facilities while contributing to a high-quality member experience.

Suppliers & Network Partnerships

Support the management of approved suppliers across all club-related categories, assisting with supplier engagement, performance management, and identifying opportunities to improve service delivery and commercial outcomes for the network.

Qualifications and Experience

- Tertiary qualifications in business, project management, construction, design or related field.

Qualifications and Experience

- Tertiary qualifications in business, project management, construction, design or procurement would be advantageous
- Experience in a project coordination or project management role, ideally within a multi-site or franchised environment
- Strong understanding of the fitness industry, including club operations, fit-outs, equipment and supplier models
- Experience working with suppliers and supporting commercial or preferred supplier agreements
- High level of organisation with the ability to manage multiple workstreams simultaneously
- Strong communication and interpersonal skills to liaise effectively with franchisees, suppliers and internal stakeholders
- Ability to operate confidently in a fast-paced, evolving environment

About you

You are organised, proactive, and comfortable working across both projects and relationships. You understand the importance of brand consistency within a franchised fitness network and enjoy supporting franchisees to achieve the right outcomes while protecting brand standards.

You bring a practical, solutions-focused mindset and are confident working with suppliers, navigating competing priorities, and addressing issues before they escalate. Your ability to build trust, communicate clearly, and follow through on commitments allows you to make a meaningful impact across the network.

With a strong interest in the fitness industry and a developing project management skillset, you’re motivated by seeing projects come to life and clubs operating at their best – delivering a consistent, high-quality experience for members.

The role offers an attractive salary, incentives, and benefits including complimentary gym access and extensive leave options.

About the role:

We are seeking an experienced Lift & Escalator Technicians to join our Service Team in Canberra. Reporting to the Service Manager, you will be responsible for minimising breakdowns and ensuring that all lifts and escalators perform to customer expectations.

This role covers both our lift and escalator products, we are looking for an individual who preferably has demonstrated experience in dealing with the maintenance and service of both lifts and escalators.

Benefits and Perks:

Lift Service Technician  
Schindler Lifts Australia  

- Answer all calls promptly and provide an accurate ETA, in accordance with response terms

About us:  
Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.  

Further Information & how to apply:  
If you are passionate about a career with us, please click on the “Apply” button and complete our online application form.  

We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply. Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a manager.

jobs.title: Service Account Representative - Part -time (3 days a week)
jobs.description:
OTIS

Service Account Representative - Melbourne  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  
3.6  28 reviews  ·  View all jobs

South Melbourne, Melbourne VIC (Hybrid)  
Account & Relationship Management (Sales)  
Full time  
Add expected salary to your profile for insights  

Posted 1mo ago

Apply  
Save

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different.

Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

About You

We are currently looking for a sales-focused Service Account Representative to be based in our Melbourne office in Victoria, focusing on our key accounts.

In this role, you would be responsible for selling OTIS lift and escalator range of Maintenance products and services and maintaining good customer relationships for all accounts assigned to your portfolio with the aim of growing the Service business in the respective area.

You are excited about proactively maintaining regular face-to-face contact with Otis Customers and ensuring that all selling activities, including prospecting, presenting, proposing, tendering, securing, and processing, meet the OTIS standards and customer requirements.

- Permanent/Full-Time role
- Reports to the Service Sales and Operations Manager

As a Service Account Representative, your key responsibilities are to

- Achieve Service sales targets in accordance with the individual plan and defined submission activities.
- Provide daily, professional, value-based account management to key and national accounts within the portfolio and product proposals.
- Proactively maintain regular face-to-face contact with Otis Customers.
- Provide timely, accurate forecasts and reports to management and ensure the designated service portfolio has reduced exposure to out-of-tenure agreements.
- Achieves in-warranty conversion targets and unit growth objectives to expand the portfolio unit count and revenue base.

Service Account Representative - Melbourne  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY  

- Achieves in-warranty conversion targets and unit growth objectives to expand the portfolio unit count and revenue base.  
- Organize, prepare, and deliver Customer presentations promoting the features and benefits of Otis's products and services, resulting in direct sales with new and existing customers.  
- Acts as a facilitator between the customers and the Operations team and assists with developing and implementing solutions to customers' vertical transport needs.  
- Support the wider business requirements by identifying, actively introducing, and collaborating for Modernisation sales success within the portfolio.  

What you will need to be successful  
- Have a minimum of 3-5 years’ experience in Frontline Sales or Key Account Management.  
- Those with experience in the Elevator and Escalator industry will be preferred.  
- A current driver's license.  
- Have strong business acumen and are results-oriented.  
- Able to manage several accounts, be customer-focused, and anticipate client needs. Works well in a team.  
- Has the ability to liaise and influence all levels of stakeholders within the organization and possesses strong relationship-building skills.  
- Good knowledge of using technology, including mobile applications and computer-based reporting systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet, database management) to analyze data and trends.  
- Strong verbal and written communication skills.  

Come and join OTIS today and help us Build What's Next!  

About us  
We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.  

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.  

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion

28d ago, from Astrum Recruitment  

Apply on company site  

Local Representative - Newcastle & Forster Region  
Location: Newcastle & Forster, NSW  
Position Type: Permanent  
Reporting To: Service Branch Manager, Newcastle NSW  
Package: $110k - $115k + Super + Car Allowance + Bonus  
Vehicle: Fully maintained company vehicle and fuel card provided  

About the Role:  
We are seeking an experienced Electrician/ Lift Technician to take on a Local Area Representative role within a global lift company. This role will cover the Newcastle and Forster regions.  

In this role, you will oversee field operations, customer relationships, maintenance and business development initiatives across your designated area.  

With a balance of field and office responsibilities 85% Field work on the tools 15% Office split, you'll play a key role in driving operational efficiency, safety compliance, and service excellence.  

Key Responsibilities Field-Based Duties 85%:  
- Reduce call-outs through proactive asset management and scheduled maintenance on vertical transport, lifts, elevators etc.  
- Achieve maintenance targets in accordance with contract requirements.  
- Minimise equipment downtime through timely repair coordination and estimate submissions to Sales.  
- Maintain equipment work with customers and subcontractors.

Achieve maintenance targets in accordance with contract requirements.

Minimise equipment downtime through timely repair coordination and estimate submissions to Sales.

Manage repair works with customers and subcontractors.

Build trusted customer relationships to support retention and growth.

Conduct field audits and ensure subcontractor compliance with safety standards.

On Call Work every other weekend.

Office-Based Duties 15%:
• Manage routine maintenance scheduling and meet contractual obligations.
• Monitor repair budgets and support achievement of new business targets.
• Oversee procurement and stock control of parts and equipment.
• Respond to customer queries and concerns in a prompt, solution-focused manner.
• Attend customer meetings and provide technical input.
• Assist with tenders, modernisation projects, and service-related sales opportunities.
• Provide regular reporting and identify process improvement opportunities.

What You'll Need:
• NSW Electrical License (mandatory).
• Trade qualification as an Electrician/Fitter/Mechanic. Additional studies in Electronics are highly regarded.
• Lift Experience an understanding in vertical transportation.
• IT proficiency and team leadership experience.
• Valid driver’s license and clean driving record.
• Commercial mindset and ability to collaborate with Sales teams.
• Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
• Proven ability to manage end-to-end projects and meet KPIs.
• Team-oriented with a proactive and positive attitude.

Why Join Us:
• Competitive salary package + quarterly bonuses.
• Fully maintained vehicle and fuel card.
• 12 Annual Rostered Days Off (RDOs).
• Flexible working arrangements and autonomy to manage your own area.
• Career development through world-class learning programs.
• Proud to be named in Forbes’ 2022 World’s Best Employers.

This is an exciting opportunity to join a global industry leader and drive service excellence across the Newcastle and Forster regions.

Project Coordinator  
AustLift

Wetherill Park, Sydney NSW  
Project Management (Engineering)  
Full time  
$90,000 - $95,000 per year  

Posted 12d ago  

Quick apply  

About the Role  

Australian Lifting Centre Pty Ltd is seeking a driven and highly organized Project Coordinator to join our team. This is a hybrid role requiring strong project management skills, day-to-day task coordination, and people management, especially across teams involved in manufacturing, lifting equipment, or construction environments.  

The ideal candidate will also bring hands-on experience or solid understanding of CAD/CAM, technical drafting, or similar engineering workflows to help bridge the gap between technical design and project execution.  

Key Responsibilities

Project Coordinator  
AustLift  

Key Responsibilities  

• Manage and coordinate end-to-end project delivery, including timelines, resources, and client communications  
• Assign daily tasks and priorities to workshop and site teams  
• Oversee and manage team productivity and performance, fostering accountability and collaboration  
• Liaise between design/drafting teams and operations to ensure clear understanding of technical drawings and deliverables  
• Track project milestones, risks, and variations using project management software  
• Prepare and maintain documentation including work schedules, risk assessments, compliance reports, and progress updates  
• Support procurement and inventory planning for ongoing projects  
• Ensure all operations meet safety, compliance, and quality standards, particularly in lifting or construction-related work  
• Review technical drawings and provide feedback or clarification where needed  
• Contribute to process improvement and efficiency initiatives across teams  

Skills & Qualifications  

• Proven experience in project management, operations coordination, or team leadership  
• Relevant degree in Engineering or Project Management  
• Background or strong working knowledge in CAD/CAM, AutoCAD or similar drafting software

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World’s Best Employers!

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

About you

We are looking for a Service Technician to based in Darwin, Northern Territory. In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.

- Permanent role
- Reports to the Branch Manager

Key responsibilities for YOU

- Performs Service and Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals.

- Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

• Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-roping, Hoisting, and Rigging.

• Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and /or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement.

• Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally

• Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

• Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.

• Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.

• External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers.

What YOU will need to be successful

• Have Electrical and/or Fitter qualifications as relevant to the role and Lift apprenticeship or other industry-accredited training program (desirable)

• Minimum two (2) to five (5) years’ experience within the Lift Industry.

• Hold a valid driver’s license and Electrical license.

• Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

• You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority

• Has attention to detail and results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team.

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!

We are the world’s leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world’s most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation

As a Sales & Key Account Representative you will work closely with our Key Accounts Team to quote urgent lift repairs/minor upgrades, quote proactive equipment improvements and support the administrative tasks required with our key account portals.

Key Responsibilities

- Deliver revenue performance: Consistently achieve (and exceed) assigned sales revenue and margin targets in line with the approved business plan, with clear accountability for results.

Sales and Key Account Representative  
OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY

- Build and maintain a robust pipeline: Maintain a healthy opportunity funnel that supports target attainment, including ongoing prospecting, opportunity progression, and clear next steps at every stage.  
- Forecast with accuracy: Keep CRM/sales systems current, report activity consistently, and provide accurate forecasting against plan (pipeline, probability, timing, value).  

What you will need to be successful  
- Minimum 2–3 years’ experience in Tele Sales, Frontline Sales, or Account Management, with a results-driven mindset (Preferred).

Apply today and help us build what's next!

If you are currently working in the lift industry, we will review applications even if you are not an
electrician.
About the Role
You will work across a wide variety of projects including:
Lift and escalator repairs
Installations and upgrades
Fault finding and electrical works
Working across commercial buildings, hotels and infrastructure projects
The role offers strong variety in the work, exposure to different equipment and the opportunity
to develop within the vertical transportation industry.
Work may occasionally involve:
Night works
Travel
Cleaning
Employment Structure
This position will commence on a casual basis.
This allows both parties to get to know each other before moving to a permanent full-time
position.
For the right candidate we are flexible and can initially offer trial days here and there so you do
not need to immediately leave your current role.
However, please note we have consistent work available and can provide full-time hours (5 days
per week) for the right person.
Mandatory Requirements
Applications will not be considered unless the following criteria are met:
Full working rights in Australia (non-negotiable)
Any valid driver's licence is acceptable for this position
If not currently in the lift industry – qualified electrician
Advantageous (but not essential)
Lift industry experience
Rigging ticket
Dogging ticket
What We Offer
Strong pipeline of work
A supportive and skilled team environment
Opportunity to build a long-term career in the lift industry
Variety of work across multiple projects
A company that rewards hard work and loyalty
If you are looking to join a growing and respected company in the lift industry, we encourage
you to apply.

jobs.title: Project Manager NSW
jobs.location: Australia, Tasmania, Hobart Region, Kingborough Area, Huntingfield
jobs.description:
Project Manager  
Lift Brands – Cooparoo QLD  
Full time  
7d ago, from seek.com.au  

Why join Lift Brands?  
• Be part of Lift Brands – a global wellness leader  
• Attractive base salary + phone allowance + incentives  
• Excellent career progression and development opportunities in an innovative, global business. We’re committed to continuing improvement and have exciting plans for the future – grow your career with us!  
• Get your birthday off and up to 5 weeks of annual leave!  
• Complimentary access to Snap Fitness Clubs.  

About the role  
Reporting to the Manager – Product & Projects, this role works closely with internal stakeholders, including legal, to support franchisees, coordinate suppliers, and ensure clubs operate aligned with brand standards and approved specifications.

- Complimentary access to Snap Fitness Clubs. 

About the role 

Reporting to the Manager – Product & Projects, this role works closely with internal stakeholders, including legal, to support franchisees, coordinate suppliers, and ensure clubs remain aligned with brand standards and approved specifications. 

The Project Manager will contribute to the successful delivery of the club modernisation program by enabling, tracking, and supporting delivery outcomes, while also supporting supplier performance, compliance processes, and the consistent look, feel, and functionality of clubs across the Snap Fitness network. 

This position works across three core areas that are critical to the Snap Fitness brand and the experience of its franchisees and members: 

Projects & Modernisation 

Support the delivery of the modernisation program across Australia and New Zealand through project coordination, supplier engagement, and implementation support. The role focuses on enabling, tracking, and supporting delivery outcomes to ensure clubs meet brand standards and operational requirements. This position does not directly manage construction, builders, or trades, and does not replace franchisee-appointed project managers or shopfitters. 

Development & Brand Standards 

Support franchisees to maintain compliance with brand guidelines and specifications, ensuring consistency across club design, fit-out, equipment, and facilities while contributing to a high-quality member experience. 

Suppliers & Network Partnerships 

Support the management of approved suppliers across all club-related categories, assisting with supplier engagement, performance management, and identifying opportunities to improve service delivery and commercial outcomes for the network. 

Qualifications and Experience 

- Tertiary qualifications in business, project management, construction, design or related fields.

Qualifications and Experience

- Tertiary qualifications in business, project management, construction, design or procurement would be advantageous
- Experience in a project coordination or project management role, ideally within a multi-site or franchised environment
- Strong understanding of the fitness industry, including club operations, fit-outs, equipment and supplier models
- Experience working with suppliers and supporting commercial or preferred supplier agreements
- High level of organisation with the ability to manage multiple workstreams simultaneously
- Strong communication and interpersonal skills to liaise effectively with franchisees, suppliers and internal stakeholders
- Ability to operate confidently in a fast-paced, evolving environment

About you

You are organised, proactive, and comfortable working across both projects and relationships. You understand the importance of brand consistency within a franchised fitness network and enjoy supporting franchisees to achieve the right outcomes while protecting brand standards.

You bring a practical, solutions-focused mindset and are confident working with suppliers, navigating competing priorities, and addressing issues before they escalate. Your ability to build trust, communicate clearly, and follow through on commitments allows you to make a meaningful impact across the network.

With a strong interest in the fitness industry and a developing project management skillset, you’re motivated by seeing projects come to life and clubs operating at their best – delivering a consistent, high-quality experience for members.
jobs.description_summary: Otis is seeking a full-time Project Manager for its Lift Brands division in Cooparoo, QLD, responsible for coordinating modernisation projects across Snap Fitness clubs while ensuring compliance with brand standards and supporting franchise relationships. The role offers an attractive salary, career development opportunities, and additional perks like extended leave and gym access.

jobs.title: Senior Electronic Repair Technician
jobs.description:
Electrical Technician  
Marble Group – Sydney NSW  
$145,000 - $145,000 a year  
Permanent  
3mo ago, from Marble Group  

**The opportunity and why apply**  
This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
- $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
- Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)  
- OEM Training and development on offer to become an expert in your field.  
- Working in a technical and challenging role on automated stacker cranes  

**The company**  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
- Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
- People focused management team with a strong team orientated culture  
- Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities  

**Roster and hours**  
You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

**Roster and hours**

You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

- 12-hour shifts with a paid lunch and smoko
- Day shift: 6am - 6pm
- Night shift: 6pm - 6am
- Occasional overtime available on the end of your swing for those looking for more hours.

**The role**

As a highly skilled electrician, you'll be responsible for all electrical maintenance and repair at one of Australia's biggest logistic parks. All of your work will be completed at the well-equipped site.

- Electrical and mechanical fault finding, preventative maintenance and emergency repair work on a range of rail mounted automated stacker cranes
- Work on a range of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives and crane management software
- Working on variable speed drives, control logic on wiring diagrams, and off grid generator systems.

**Your value**

You will be a trade qualified electrician with a well-rounded knowledge of electrical fault finding and repairs on mobile plant and/or cranes.

- Electrical trade qualification (Cert III Electrotechnology)
- Hydraulic and mechanical knowledge (highly regarded)
- An excellent attitude towards work with proven ability to work both autonomously and in a team environment.
- Working knowledge and use of electrical schematics and symbols
- Must demonstrate working knowledge of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives

Elevator Service Technician

Otis – Darwin NT

3.6 from 28 reviews at SEEK

Full time

22h ago. from seek.com.au

Come and join OTIS today and be part of the Forbes 2025 World's Best Employers!

At Otis, it’s our people that make us different. Join the Otis family, where collaboration, innovation, and empowerment help each individual and the company reach new heights.

About you

We are looking for a Service Technician to based in Darwin, Northern Territory. In this role, you will work closely with other Installers, Electricians, Adjusters, Trade Assistants, Field Engineers, and Construction Coordinators.

• Permanent role

• Reports to the Branch Manager

Key responsibilities for YOU

• Performs Service and Maintenance work in accordance with relevant job procedures, technical articles, and operations manuals.

• Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers.

• Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

Electrical and Mechanical training to apprentices and sub-contractors.

- Attend lift and escalator breakdowns and perform repairs and control modifications on lifts and escalators, including Re-ropeing, Hoisting, and Rigging.

- Perform Upgrades and Repair work in accordance with all relevant Codes, including SAA Lift Code AS1735, and in accordance with the relevant Award and/or Enterprise Bargaining Agreement.

- Liaise with customers and submit leads using Otis-provided applications.

- Standby and provide after-call-back coverage from time to time.

Key working relationships - Internally and Externally

- Works closely with the Field Engineers, Service Mechanics, and Branch and Operations Managers.

- Liaises with functional management at other Otis entities and headquarters.

- Represents the Company's relationships with major customers, suppliers, and the public.

- Sales Team: Sales Account Representatives, Managers, and Coordinators (where relevant).

- External stakeholders include Customers, Building Owners, Managers, Independent and Government Agencies, Unions, Consultants, and Suppliers.

What YOU will need to be successful

- Have Electrical and/or Fitter qualifications as relevant to the role and Lift apprenticeship or other industry-accredited training program (desirable)

- Minimum two (2) to five (5) years' experience within the Lift Industry.

- Hold a valid driver's license and Electrical license.

- Extensive Electrical and Mechanical knowledge across large equipment and the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.

- Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training and demonstrated fault-finding and problem-solving skills.

- Strong English language skills, both verbal and written

- Intermediate knowledge and experience using technology including mobile communications, applications, and other computer-based systems (Word, Excel, Email, Internet).

- You are a committed professional, with safety as your priority

- Has attention to detail and results-driven, curious, adaptable, and able to work well alone or in a team.

Apply today to join us and build what’s next!

We are the world's leading company for elevator and escalator manufacturing, installation and service. We move 2 billion people a day and maintain more than 2 million customer units worldwide - the world’s largest portfolio.

We can be found in many of the world's most recognizable buildings as well as the busiest transportation hubs and retail centers. We are everywhere people are on the move.

Our commitment to Diversity & Inclusion  
We “lift” our people to their highest potential.

Our international presence allows us to cultivate a strong community that is diverse in both culture and thought. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages and celebrates the diversity required to innovate, connect and thrive in a taller, faster, smarter world.

We are proud of our long commitment to diversity, equality, and inclusion. We strive to be a best-in-class employer for people of broad perspectives and experiences, cultures, genders, sexual orientation, races, and generations. We want to build a workforce that mirrors the diversity of our customers and the communities where we live and work. And a place where every voice feels safe, welcomed and heard.

We have workplace policies, practices and learning to help you thrive and grow in your career. This includes paid tertiary education and training, flexible working (role dependent) and to help working parents manage their caring responsibilities, we offer paid maternity leave.

Otis is An Equal Opportunity, Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation

Register your interest for our Apprentice Program 2027! In August 2026 we will reach out with the application process.

WHY join Schindler?
At Schindler, we are all about our culture! We focus on finding and developing potential through careful selection and constant development. We offer you the opportunity to grow and develop both personally and professionally. We launch people. We offer job stability to our employees and are focused on the trajectory of our business in the coming years.

About the role:
Our apprentices are trained in all the skills required to service and repair elevators, escalators and moving walkways. If you join our program you will rotate across our business to help you decide what you want to specialise in after completing your apprenticeship.

As an apprentice, you will get to work closely with our customers and develop exceptional customer service skills. One of the many bonuses is that we will provide you with the chance to work full-time and study at TAFE / Trade School to receive your trade qualification in Electrotechnology Certificate III.

What’s in it for you:
- An indentured apprenticeship - 4 years
- Career in a fast-growing electrical industry
- 9-day fortnight (RDO)

Expressions of Interest - Apprentice Program 2027 jobs in Brisbane CBD

Leave us your email address and we'll send you similar new jobs

Schindler Lifts Australia is part of the Schindler Group, one of the top elevator companies in the world, spanning 100 countries with more than 64,000 employees worldwide. We are one of the largest suppliers of new elevators, escalators and moving walkways in Australia today, employing over 1,200 employees who design install, service and modernise urban transport systems for almost every building type. At Schindler, we differentiate ourselves with our modern technology and innovative people.

Further Information & how to apply:
We support diversity and inclusion in all our workplaces; women, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islanders, people with a multicultural background or a disability are strongly encouraged to apply. Please note we do not accept applications from recruitment agents for any position, this includes those that are submitted directly to the company or where the recruiter has contacted a manager.
