jobs.title: Crane Electrician / Technician
jobs.description:
Electrical Technician  
Marble Group – Sydney NSW  
$145,000 - $145,000 a year  
Permanent  
3mo ago, from Marble Group  

The opportunity and why apply  
This is a great opportunity for an experienced electrician to join a well-known material handling OEM on a permanent contract with full job security.  
- $145k + 12% super + 4 weeks annual leave and sick days.  
- Ensure a fantastic work/life balance with a 4:4 roster (days and nights)  
- OEM Training and development on offer to become an expert in your field.  
- Working in a technical and challenging role on automated stacker cranes  

The company  
With a global footprint and operations on a multinational scale, this highly regarded materials handling OEM has developed an excellent reputation for themselves over the last 3+ decades. Join a business that is:  
- Heavily invested in new technology including EV fleets  
- People focused management team with a strong team orientated culture  
- Wide range of training and OEM upskilling opportunities  

Roster and hours  
You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

Roster and hours

You will work on an even time 4:4 roster rotating between days and nights. 4 days on, 4 days off, 4 nights on, 4 days off.

- 12-hour shifts with a paid lunch and smoko
- Day shift: 6am - 6pm
- Night shift: 6pm - 6am
- Occasional overtime available on the end of your swing for those looking for more hours.

The role

As a highly skilled electrician, you'll be responsible for all electrical maintenance and repair at one of Australia's biggest logistic parks. All of your work will be completed at the well-equipped site.

- Electrical and mechanical fault finding, preventative maintenance and emergency repair work on a range of rail mounted automated stacker cranes
- Work on a range of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives and crane management software
- Working on variable speed drives, control logic on wiring diagrams, and off grid generator systems.

Your value

You will be a trade qualified electrician with a well-rounded knowledge of electrical fault finding and repairs on mobile plant and/or cranes.

- Electrical trade qualification (Cert III Electrotechnology)
- Hydraulic and mechanical knowledge (highly regarded)
- An excellent attitude towards work with proven ability to work both autonomously and in a team environment.
- Working knowledge and use of electrical schematics and symbols
- Must demonstrate working knowledge of Siemens the S7 PLC, CITECT, AC & DC drives
- Be able to pass a pre-employment medical and national police check

This is a fantastic opportunity to secure a long-term permanent position. Apply now for more information or call Matt Grainger on 0448 551 ***

jobs.description:
Open app
Sign in
Lift Fitter
Falls Creek 
4.2
6 reviews
·
View all jobs
Geelong, South West Coast VIC
Fitters, Turners & Machinists (Trades & Services)
Full time
$47 – $56.88 per hour
Posted 20d ago
Lift Fitter (Year-Round)
Resort: Falls Creek
Employment Type: Permanent, Full Time
Remuneration: $47.00 – $56.88 per hour (dependent on experience & qualifications)
Agreement: Falls Creek and Hotham Trades Maintenance Agreement 2025
Applications Close: 22nd March 2026
Falls Creek is seeking experienced, trade-qualified Lift Fitters to join our Lift Maintenance team
on a full-time permanent contract. This position is ideal for mechanically minded trades
professionals who enjoy hands-on work, problem-solving, and maintaining complex lift systems
in a dynamic alpine environment.
The Opportunity
As a Lift Fitter, you will support the maintenance and safe operation of Falls Creek’s extensive lift
network, including fixed grip and detachable chairlifts, surface lifts, conveyors, and associated
mechanical systems. Working closely with the Lift Maintenance Manager, Supervisors and
broader maintenance team, you will perform scheduled servicing, fault diagnostics, mechanical
repairs, component replacements, and system testing to ensure lift reliability and compliance
with regulatory standards.
This role requires a high level of mechanical aptitude, adherence to safe work practices, and the
ability to work efficiently in variable weather conditions, including during peak winter
operations. Lift Fitters are employed under the Falls Creek and Hotham Trades Maintenance
Agreement 2025 with a base hourly rate of $47.00 – $56.88 per hour (dependent on experience
& qualifications)
What You’ll Be Doing
Perform maintenance, repair, and installation of ski lift and ropeway equipment
Conduct daily, weekly, and monthly inspections to ensure safe and reliable lift operations
Diagnose and troubleshoot mechanical, hydraulic, and extra-low-voltage control issues
Support tower maintenance, sheave train alignment, and tensioning systems
Carry out scheduled preventative maintenance and maintain accurate service records
Respond promptly to lift breakdowns and operational issues during resort hours
Operate heavy equipment and hand tools safely and efficiently
Assist with lift construction, upgrades, and decommissioning projects as required
Support snow clearing and access preparation for lift maintenance areas
Assist vehicle maintenance staff as required
The Expertise You Bring
A recognised Mechanical Trade qualification (Fitter & Turner, Mechanical Fitter, or equivalent)
Minimum 2 years’ post-apprenticeship experience in mechanical maintenance or fitting roles,
including working at heights and following safe work procedures
Strong mechanical diagnostics skills and ability to identify and resolve faults
Current Driver’s Licence
Ability to work effectively in alpine and winter conditions.

Direct Lifts Australia - Join our team

Branch Manager  
Direct Lifts  

Company Overview  
Direct Lifts Australia specialises in supply and install of modern, eye-catching lifts and elevators throughout the east coast of Australia. Working closely with architects, builders and property owners we collaborate and design lifts to suit our clients’ requirements and decor.

With a strong reputation for quality, compliance, and service excellence, we are seeking an experienced Branch Manager to lead our Installation and Service Division, based at our Kingsgrove office in Sydney.

Position Summary  
As Branch Manager, you will take full accountability for the performance of the installation and service operations, ensuring projects are delivered safely, efficiently, on time, and on budget.

Key Responsibilities  
Reporting directly to the Managing Director, you will be responsible for driving performance, profitability, and team engagement across the division. Additional responsibilities include:  
- Lead and manage day-to-day operations across lift installation and service teams.  
- Oversee budgeting, forecasting, and financial performance to meet agreed KPIs and profit targets.  
- Drive operational efficiencies and implement cost control strategies.  
- Manage workforce planning, scheduling, and resource allocation.  
- Supervise and support technicians, project managers, service coordinators, administration staff, and sales personnel.  
- Maintain high standards of safety, compliance, and customer satisfaction.  
- Manage technical lift inventory and spare parts for NSW operations.  
- Strengthen relationships with builders, developers, and residential clients.  
- Foster a culture of responsibility, teamwork, and continuous improvement.

Branch Manager  
Direct Lifts

- Manage technical lift inventory and spare parts for NSW operations.  
- Strengthen relationships with builders, developers, and residential clients.  
- Foster a culture of accountability, teamwork, and continuous improvement.

About You  
You are a confident and capable operational leader with a strong understanding of construction or lift installation environments. You combine technical knowledge with commercial awareness and people leadership skills.

Skills & Experience  
- Previous experience in lift installation, construction, or related technical industry (highly desirable)  
- Proven leadership experience managing operational teams.  
- Strong financial acumen with demonstrated experience managing budgets and improving profitability.  
- Experience using operational/service management systems (AroFlo highly desirable).  
- Experience with Microsoft Business Central (advantageous).  
- Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams across B2B and B2C client bases.  
- Strong organisational, communication, and problem-solving skills.  
- A proactive, solutions-focused leadership style.  
- High-level competency in Microsoft Office Suite.

Benefits  
- Potential annual salary of $200,000 (based on agreed targets).  
- Company vehicle and tools of trade.  
- Performance-based incentive structure.  
- A genuine opportunity to shape and influence operational strategy.  
- Supportive leadership and collaborative team culture.  
- Long-term career growth within a stable and growing Australian business.

Application Process  
If you are ready to take ownership of operations and drive performance within a respected lift company, we look forward to hearing from you.

recent.title: Vertical Transportation Team Leader - Building Services
recent.description:
Vertical Transportation Team Leader - Building Services  
CSA Recruitment  
Brisbane, QLD  
Permanent  
Full-time  
3 hours ago  

Vertical Transportation Team Leader – Building Services Brisbane, Queensland Competitive Salary Package + Benefits  

Lead the future of vertical transportation in Brisbane’s booming skyline.  
We are seeking an experienced Vertical Transportation Team Leader to head up our growing Vertical Transportation (VT) team in Brisbane.  

This is a senior leadership role for a passionate engineer who wants to deliver high-quality lift, escalator, and moving walkway solutions on some of South East Queensland’s most exciting commercial, residential, healthcare, and mixed-use developments.  

About the Role  

As Team Leader, you will drive the technical excellence and commercial success of the Vertical Transportation discipline. You’ll manage a team of engineers and designers while playing a hands-on role in major projects from concept design through to construction and handover.  

Key Responsibilities  

- Lead and mentor a team of Vertical Transportation Engineers and Designers  
- Oversee the design and specification of lifts, escalators, and moving walkways  
- Ensure compliance with relevant Australian Standards and international best practices

Support business development activities and contribute to winning new work  
Drive innovation in sustainable and smart vertical transportation solutions  
Collaborate closely with MEP, fire, structural, and architectural teams using BIM/Revit workflows  

What You’ll Bring  
- Bachelor of Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, or related discipline)  
- 8+ years’ experience in Vertical Transportation / Lift Engineering within the Australian building services industry  
- Proven leadership experience managing a team of engineers  
- Strong technical knowledge of lift traffic analysis, system design, and performance specifications  
- Experience with major projects (high-rise, hospitals, airports, or large-scale developments)  
- Proficiency in design software, including Revit MEP, AutoCAD, and specialist VT tools  
- Excellent communication, client management, and commercial acumen  
- CPEng or eligibility for chartered status is highly regarded  

Why Join Us?  
- Lead a growing Vertical Transportation team in one of Australia’s fastest-growing cities  
- Work on landmark Brisbane and South East Queensland projects that shape the city’s skyline  
- Genuine autonomy and opportunity to build and grow the discipline  
- Attractive remuneration package including performance bonus, professional development, and flexible/hybrid working  
- Supportive, collaborative culture with a strong focus on work-life balance  

If you’re a technical leader with a passion for vertical transportation and ready to take the next step in your career, this is your opportunity to make a significant impact in Brisbane’s building services sector.  

Why CSA Recruitment?  
Craig Andresen has been a professional Recruitment Consultant since 2006 and has been deeply embedded in the Australian Building, Civil & Structural Engineering industries since 2012, so he's exceptionally well-positioned to assist you with your next career move.  

If you would like to contact Craig Andresen regarding this role or to submit your skills & experience, please feel free to do so by following the details below:  
Apply Now
recent.description_summary: CSA Recruitment is seeking a Vertical Transportation Team Leader to manage and grow their VT team in Brisbane, focusing on high-quality lift and escalator solutions for various developments. This senior role requires an experienced engineer with strong leadership skills, technical expertise, and a commitment to innovation and compliance in the vertical transportation field.

recent.company: Ranger Lifting Rigging Safety Pty Ltd
recent.description: About Ranger Ranger has grown from a small player in the lifting industry to a premier supplier to the largest infrastructure projects in the country. We have our people to thank for that and of course , our loyal clients. Ranger supplies, hires, tests, certifies, and inspects lifting and height safety equipment for a variety of industries across Australia. We provide specialist lifting, planning, design, and engineering services too. Ranger is an innovative company, on the look out for people with good ideas, initiative, enthusiasm, and people who want to find a better way to do things. The Opportunity: Currently, we are seeking a dynamic mechanical engineering technicians to complement our growing team. Numerous roles will be based out of Sydney Melbourne and Brisbane. We have secured several large projects across Australia & are looking for individuals who are seeking a new challenge. In this role you will work across several areas of our operations to aid & develop your skills in our unique industry. Your responsibilities in this role include: Inhouse & Onsite work which included testing, inspection and certification of customers lifting gear, engineered and Height safety assets. Assessment of lifting gear for repair and maintenance. Installation & maintenance of ropes, sheaves & rope attachments for critical services. Design, manufacture and maintain bespoke lifting & measuring devices. Height safety inspections (including harnesses, lanyards, etc.) & installations. Lifting gear test, tag & repair Proof loading testing & reporting. Working with Ranger This role will excite you if you are passionate about the industry, are highly organised and have a strong attention to detail.  Experience in and/or knowledge of the lifting/construction industry is highly desirable. Ranger offers. ·         Career development ·         Further education and training ·         Employee Assistance ·         Supportive environment ·         Career advancement Base Salary for these roles will be between $76,000 - $80,000.00 per annum depending on experience and qualifications. Opportunities for over-time are also available. Please note that is full-time position only at this time. What you will bring to Ranger Bachelor of Mechanical Engineering or equivalent, Minimum 3 years of work experience An understanding of Australian or ISO lifting Standards High Risk Licence (Dogging/Basic rigging or higher). Natural mechanical aptitude. EWP ticket (can be provided). Working at heights ticket (can be provided). General Safety Induction in the Construction Industry Commitment to safety If you are a self-starter who is passionate about producing quality work this role could be for you. If you want to develop a career, this role will provide you career development opportunities to get training in lifting equipment inspections, engineering, and mechanical fitting. Please indicate in your response your preferred location (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane or National)

recent.title: Supervisor Lift Service  - QLD
recent.description: The role: You will be responsible for overseeing and managing elevator and escalator maintenance, repairs, and call-back activities for the Queensland market. You will ensure safe, efficient, and high-quality service delivery while leading a team of highly qualified technicians, managing workloads, and maintaining strong customer relationships. You will excel with your attention to detail, superior technical knowledge and outstanding customer service. What sets us apart: At Kleemann, we pride ourselves on allocating significantly more hours to maintenance than our competitors. This commitment to quality maintenance ensures our portfolio maintains excellent reliability, giving you the time to do the job properly rather than rushing from site to site. How You will make a difference: Living our Culture through our values of Safety, Passion for People, Trust & Breakthrough Supervising, supporting, and coordinating out service technicians and subcontractors Allocating daily workloads, including maintenance, breakdowns, and callouts Ensuring efficient scheduling and resource allocation Overseeing preventative maintenance programs to ensure contract compliance Undertaking high level fault finding Assisting technicians with diagnostics, repairs, and system resets Reviewing recurring faults and implementing corrective actions Ensuring adherence to WHS legislation and company safety policies Providing after-hours call out support About You: Enthusiastic, team focused and always wanting to deliver outstanding results. Demonstrating an uncompromising commitment to Safety. Excellent Leadership and organisational skills. Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident with valid work rights. To triumph in this role, you will need: Electrical worker licence required National Police Check required. Minimum 4 years supervisory experience required within the elevator service industry Valid driver's license White Card Basic computer skills About the company: Elevator Services Group is a wholly owned subsidiary of Kleemann Elevators Australia. We are Australia's leading specialist in maintaining and servicing Kleemann elevators, escalators and moving walk solutions for the low to mid-rise sector. We have been doing so for over 23 years. Our niche and specialty mean we have quality control over all of our works. We are a company who genuinely believes in and supports our employees and provide: A Friendly Team Culture A Safe Work Environment Development and Training To Apply Don't wait! Apply now as we will appoint as soon as we find the right person. So, submit your resume via the "apply now" button.

Responsibilities**
+ Installation of New Equipment and Modernisation lifts and working to the highest safety and environmental standards while ensuring service targets are met.
+ Ensure timely completion of defects identified during service / handing over inspection in coordination with the Project Manager.
+ Maintain company tools, equipment, and materials properly and safeguard such property.
+ Perform work in accordance with the relevant lift industry codes and standards.
+ Comply with the company's Environmental Health and Safety policies and procedures, including reporting any conditions that may impact the environment or any employee.
+ Maintain and work on the Quality System.
+ Attend customer handing over inspection/service inspections on installation and modernization projects.
+ Identify process improvement opportunities and help facilitate continuous improvement to satisfy the customers of your processes.
**What you will need to be successful**
+ Experience in Adjustment / Commissioning work would be beneficial.
+ Working with heights, Basic Rigging and hold a valid driver's license
+ Hold full working rights in Australia
+ Has relevant Lift experience and proven ability to work safely as part of a team or independently, depending on requirements.
+ Enjoys communicating with various levels of stakeholders and working closely with others in the field.
+ Solutions-oriented, independent, and well organized.
+ Willing to travel in & out of the region and able to work unsupervised.
+ Has the ability to perform knowledge transfer on information and skills through training.
+ Able to plan and prioritize tasks and complete them in a timely manner.
**Key Benefits**
+ Opportunity to work on projects of a wide spectrum.
+ Classification and hourly rate per the latest EBA
+ Opportunity to work on latest technology with the industry leader with highest safety and quality standards.

recent.title: NSW Construction Manager
recent.description: Established in 1996 following the acquisition of City Elevator, TK Elevator Australia, building on the over 200 years of global engineering excellence of our German founding companies Thyssen and Krupp, install, maintain and modernize elevators and escalators in Australia. We have a strong presence in various segments, such as office, education and healthcare sectors, and are committed to making cities the best places to live by being safety and environmentally focused while fostering a strong safety culture. We are currently seeking a highly motivated and well‑organised Construction Manager to join our NSW Branch. This role reports directly to the Branch Manager and is responsible for overseeing all Lift Modernisation and Construction sites within the region. The successful candidate will have extensive experience in the Construction industry, with demonstrated capability in project management, scheduling, and financial reporting. Prior experience within the Construction sector is essential. You will also need strong communication and computer skills and maintain a professional presentation at all times. We offer excellent working conditions and a negotiable salary package reflective of the successful candidate’s experience. The key responsibilities of the successful applicant will include but not be limited to: Labour control, project financial control & forecasting Direct site management and site meeting attendance Programs & planning using Microsoft project and excel Contract administration Providing all site & company documentation Estimating / calculation of project variations Maintain an excellent team culture Conduct toolbox talks in conjunction with the field supervisor Conduct site safety leadership audits The successful candidate will also need to possess the following skills and attributes: Minimum 5 years proven experience in Project Management Ability to read, plan and execute “scope of works” in accordance with specifications and contractual obligations Ability to read and understand engineering drawings Microsoft Office proficient in Word, Excel and Project Well presented Excellent attention to detail and accuracy Strong communication skills Ability to build a strong rapport with clients and colleagues alike SAP knowledge and experience will be highly desirable What we offer you At TK Elevator you will be able to extend your horizon – thanks to flexible working time models, international teams and a wide range of individual training courses.  A collaborative approach and treating each other with respect have been central to what we do for more than 200 years. If that's as important to you as it is to us, then apply now! All suitable applicants should apply in writing to the HR department. To be eligible, you must submit a cover letter outlining your skills and experience in line with the role and a current CV. Please Note: Only successful candidates will be contacted We value diversity and therefore welcome all applications, irrespective of gender, nationality, ethnic and social background, religion and beliefs, disability, age, or sexual orientation and identity. TKE Australia is an ISO certified Company.

recent.title: Senior Project Manager (Lifts / Elevators)
recent.description: We are seeking a dedicated, highly organised Project Manager to oversee lift projects and ensure smooth operations from completion of sale to handover. This senior role requires interaction with multiple internal teams, engagement with external stakeholders, and managing several moving project parts simultaneously. Key Responsibilities: Project Coordination: Schedule and manage lift projects, ensuring seamless coordination of works and materials with the scheduling team. Team Management: Lead installation teams, ensuring optimum efficiency while maintaining high morale and productivity. Procurement & Logistics: Work closely with the procurement team to ensure products are available on time and in full. Deadline Management: Oversee the progress of projects, adapting work plans as needed to meet deadlines and ensure quality delivery. Client & Stakeholder Communication: Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and internal teams (Installation, Sales, Procurement, Accounts & Senior Management) Quality Control: Review and sign off on building works before installation, maintaining a high level of quality control throughout the project lifecycle. Documentation & Reporting: Oversee all incoming and outgoing project documentation, and prepare progress reports for senior management. Process Improvement: Continuously optimize processes and approaches to enhance efficiency and quality. Safety & Compliance: Ensure installation teams adhere to company OHS policies and maintain a safe working environment. Growth & Development: Identify opportunities for growth, new projects, and initiate actions to expand business opportunities. Required Personal Qualities: Professional "can-do" attitude with excellent communication skills. Ability to manage high-stress situations while maintaining clear, calm leadership. Strong multitasking skills with the capacity to handle multiple project components simultaneously. Leadership ability with a big-picture vision and strong attention to detail. Conflict resolution skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving. Experience 3 years plus experience within in the Lift / Elevator industry Managing multiple projects, ideally in construction or engineering or residential build projects Proven leadership in budgets, and project timelines. Experience with installation projects and construction-related work and understanding of building processes and safety protocols. High level of familiarity with project management or CRM software for scheduling and reporting. Experience in managing project budgets and working with procurement teams to ensure timely material availability. Ability to identify risks, adapt project plans, and solve issues to keep projects on track. Skilled in managing client relationships, communicating effectively, and ensuring project satisfaction. Experience enforcing safety protocols and ensuring OHS compliance on-site. Strong communication, conflict resolution, and leadership skills to maintain team morale and resolve issues. What We Offer: $130K base + Super + Car $5K bonus per quarter based on successful install KPI's achieved ($20K per annum) Opportunity to work in a senior, dynamic role within a fast-growing company. Exposure to a wide range of projects and the chance to lead teams to success. A collaborative, positive work environment focused on professional growth.

recent.title: Inspector - Crane / Lifting and rigging equipment
recent.description: Lifting Equipment Inspector Lucon Industries | Full-Time | Williamstown / Port Melbourne About Us Lucon Industries is a leading specialist in industrial maintenance and repair, providing services to major ports, Quarries and industrial facilities across Australia. We pride ourselves on delivering high-quality solutions for our clients, with a focus on safety, efficiency, and reliability. We’re expanding and looking for an experienced Lifting Equipment Inspector to join our expert team. About the Role As a Lifting Equipment Inspector at Lucon Industries, you’ll be responsible for inspecting and testing a variety of lifting equipment, ensuring compliance with Australian safety standards. This is an exciting opportunity to work across different industrial environments and collaborate with a highly skilled team. Key Responsibilities: Perform inspections and functional tests on lifting equipment (chains, slings, shackles, hooks, cranes, hoists). Inspect flexible steel wire rope systems on various types of cranes and equipment Utilize start of the art NTD tooling and reporting software Ensure all lifting gear meets safety standards and regulatory compliance. Log, track and share inspection reports. Analyze data and trends to identify potential issues and ensure continuous safety improvement. Coordinate with maintenance teams to schedule regular inspections. Prepare and submit detailed inspection reports with recommendations for repairs or replacements. Maintain accurate documentation and equipment certification records. Skills & Experience: Previous experience in lifting equipment inspections is essential. Dogman/ intermediate rigging tickets Proficiency in using computer systems for managing records, inspections, and data analysis. Strong report writing skills with attention to detail. Ability to analyze data to identify trends and ensure equipment safety. Excellent organizational and time management skills. Ability to work both autonomously and within a team. Knowledge of relevant safety standards and industry regulations. What We Offer: Competitive salary package. Overtime and weekend work available Opportunities to travel interstate and internationally Ongoing training and development opportunities. Supportive team environment with a focus on safety and quality. Exposure to a wide range of high-profile industrial projects. How to Apply: Ready to join a dynamic team at Lucon Industries? If you meet the above criteria, apply now with your resume and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience.

recent.title: Offshore Wind Install/Service - Heavy Lift Supervision
recent.description: About Us Cosmic Group Services Pty Ltd is an experienced provider of wind turbine installation and services across all Asia Pacific, with the resources and expertise to deliver efficient and cost-effective mechanical and electrical solutions. Our goal at Cosmic is to provide project based Heavy Lift support in a safe and harm-free manner, working closely with global leading turbine manufacturing companies to deliver high-quality, cost-effective, and efficient services. We offer a range of exceptional career opportunities and are proud to be a company invested in its people — encouraging individual development, focusing on employee engagement, and creating a learning environment. We always aim for the Golden Tower… The Opportunity Due to continuous growth and upcoming projects across APAC, we are opening an Expressions of Interest campaign for experienced Heavy Lift Supervisors to join our team. If you're a Heavy Lift Supervisor with wind industry experience, we want to hear from you. Extensive heavy lift supervision experience within wind turbine environments Proven experience supporting Main Component Exchange (MCE) operations Strong crane coordination and rigging oversight capability Experience supervising large nacelle component lifts (generator, gearbox, hub or blades) Strong understanding of lift planning, load management and exclusion zones Ability to lead multi-disciplinary teams during critical lifts Excellent communication and safety leadership skills Experience working under OEM procedures and engineered lift plans A bit about you You will be a key member of our Offshore Install and Service team, supporting turbine installation, inspection, and servicing on a variety of client turbines. You’re a safety-first technician with a track record of delivering quality work at height. You must hold: Valid GWO Basic Safety Training (BST) – in date Proven heavy lift supervision experience Recognised Lifting Supervisor or Appointed Person certification Working at Heights certification LOTO competency Rigging or lifting qualification (preferred) Medical fitness for turbine climbing Valid passport / working rights for deployment region As part of the recruitment process, you may be required to undertake pre-employment checks including police clearance, medical assessment and/or drug and alcohol testing. We are an equal opportunity employer that supports your career, growth, and development — and we can’t wait to welcome the next group of high performers into the fold.

recent.title: Installations & Service Technician
recent.description: Acorn Stairlifts is the Australian division of the world's leading manufacturer and distributor of stairlifts to help people with stairs in their homes. The home-care industry in Australia is currently going through a continued period of growth, making it a very attractive career choice. Acorn, as a leader in this field, continues to drive forward its growth throughout Australia. To jump on this growth and our further plans for the future, we are looking for additional capable and enthusiastic Installations and service Technicians to support our expanding Operations team. This fantastic opportunity will involve installing, servicing and repairing our stairlift installations in the Newcastle and central coast area. There is also be the need for regular regional travel and interstate travel for training. This is a Full time role with lots of overtime possibilities and you are paid door to door! Technicians work an average 43 hour weeks. You work from your home at various work sites across the Newcastle/central coast area. About You The ideal candidate will: Must have current Full Australian working rights Clean Australian Drivers licence and Police check Mechanically minded Good physical fitness and able to lift heavy items on a regular basis. Good attention to detail. Reliable and punctual. Knowledge and experience in using powered tools desirable. Able to work autonomously A background in stairlifts is not essential as full training will be given. The job requires work in the homes of our customers, many of whom are aged and therefore the successful candidates will need to be well presented, tidy, honest and be able to demonstrate pride in a job well done. Why join us? Competitive salary package including fully expensed company vehicle (van) and mobile phone On the road, change of scenery everyday! 2 - 4 weeks of PAID training Get paid from when you leave home to return home, Door to Door! Be part of a fun and enthusiastic team! Put your technical abilities to good use helping other people live safely at home. World market-leading company Interesting and varied role Potential for interstate travel Your skills and customer service will be acknowledged and rewarded How to Apply : To apply for this role, please apply now via SEEK. Any questions please email: recruitment@acornstairlifts.com.au For more information about Acorn please visit www.acornstairlifts.com.au Please note: Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

recent.title: Elevator Service Technician
recent.description: The Company Going Up is one of the largest independent Australian-owned and operated elevator companies, manufacturing, installing, servicing, and modernising lifts along the east coast of Australia. We have a great community of team members and are looking for skilled Elevator Service Technicians to join our team. As a Service Technician, you'll have the opportunity to apply your technical expertise in a friendly and supportive working environment. Sites are located across Sydney , with our office based in Brookvale NSW. The Role Conduct routine maintenance, servicing, and repairs on elevators to ensure optimal performance and safety. Diagnose faults and carry out repairs efficiently. Install and modernise lift equipment as required. Coordinate with the service team and management for job scheduling and reporting. Maintain compliance with all relevant safety and industry regulations. Ensure high-quality standards in all work performed. Full-time, permanent role. About You Qualified or experienced in the elevator or mechanical trades industry (Cert III in Electrotechnology or equivalent preferred). Strong problem-solving skills with a mechanical and electrical aptitude. Previous experience working with lift systems is highly regarded. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Organised and self-motivated. High standard of workmanship and attention to detail. Commitment to workplace safety and compliance. Benefits Competitive wage. Company vehicle and phone. Ongoing training and development opportunities. Full uniform provided excluding boots. Work within a supportive and dynamic team environment. Immediate start available. If you are ready to take the next step in your career in the elevator industry, this is the opportunity for you! Apply now for an immediate start and become a valued member of the Going Up team. Apply via Seek. No recruiters. Please note this is a full-time, permanent role.

Exclusive partnership with a single supplier, ensuring top-quality products. Exceptional installation support and training. Join us in revolutionising the elevator industry and enjoy the financial rewards that come with it. Elevate your career with us today!

recent.title: Electrician (Elevator/Lift Installer)
recent.description: Are you an Electrician that gets things done, likes a short work week, and would like to work in a niche industry? If so, keep reading! We are a small, high-performance team based in Brisbane, Queensland , specializing as the preferred subcontractors for several elevator companies. Our reputation for excellence means we are in high demand across the Greater Brisbane area, the Gold Coast, Sunshine Coast, Toowoomba, and beyond. What We Are Looking For: We need someone who takes pride in their work. You’ll need: Licensing: A current, unrestricted Electrical Licence. Safety: A valid Construction Induction (White) Card. Technical Literacy: The ability to accurately read and interpret layout drawings and electrical diagrams. Aptitude: Exceptional electrical skills paired with strong mechanical "know-how." Standards: A commitment to neat, tidy, and compliant workmanship. Bonus Points (Advantageous Licences): While not mandatory, we value candidates who bring extra skills to the table: Dogman’s or Basic Rigger’s Licence. Material and Personnel Hoist (HP) or Basic Scaffolding Licence. Forklift Licence. Blue Card: Working with Children Check (Required, or must be willing to obtain). Industry Experience. What You’ll Get: We believe in rewarding high-quality work with high-quality benefits. Joining our team means: Above Award Pay: Compensation that reflects your specialized skills. 4-Day Work Week: Reclaim your long weekends and enjoy a genuine work-life balance. A "Test Drive" Period: Start in a casual position so you can "try us out" first. Enjoy the flexibility of seeing if the industry is the right fit for you before committing long-term. Niche Industry Career Path: Expert experience in a specialized field with massive growth potential. Full Training: We provide all the specialized industry training required for the role. If you're an Electrician ready to move into the elevator industry (or already in the industry) and enjoy more time off, we want to hear from you. If you're happy with all the above, please apply (also email your resume/CV with a brief cover letter to admin@capstoneelectrical.com.au )

recent.title: Crane & Heavy Lift Engineer
recent.description: Cranecorp Australia is seeking an experienced Senior Heavy Lift Engineer to lead our engineering function and play a critical role in delivering safe, innovative, and commercially sound heavy lift solutions across Australia. This is a key position within the business, ensuring exceptional customer service and best‑in‑class safety outcomes that consistently exceed client expectations. About the Role Reporting to senior leadership, you will work closely with Operations, Business Development, and project stakeholders to deliver complex heavy lift and transport solutions. You will also contribute to business growth, technology advancement, and the development of industry‑leading engineering standards and tools. Key Responsibilities Collaborate with project managers, engineers, and clients to develop compliant heavy lift plans in accordance with relevant Australian Standards Communicate strategy, planning, outcomes, and issues clearly to senior management and the wider business Build and maintain strong working relationships across the business as a key member of the Technical team Evaluate and implement new and emerging engineering technologies related to cranes, rigging, load monitoring, and heavy lift operation Develop, coach, and support team members to enhance capability and succession planning Develop tools, systems, and training to ensure compliance with world’s best practice and to set new industry benchmarks Provide engineering support outside normal business hours as required Ensure compliance with the latest Australian Standards and Codes of Practice nationwide Support Business Development with technical input for tenders, proposals, and client presentations Assist with engineering design including lifting devices, load support beams, stools, transport equipment, load restraint diagrams, lifting studies, and alternative lift and transport methodologies About You You are a technically strong and commercially aware engineer with a passion for safety, leadership, and continuous improvement. You thrive in a dynamic environment and are confident working with clients, stakeholders, and multidisciplinary teams. Essential Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical or Structural Engineering Minimum 3 years’ experience working as an engineer (heavy lift or related industry preferred) Strong background in construction, cranes, lifting, and transportation Proficiency in AutoCAD or similar engineering design software C‑Class Driver’s Licence National Police Clearance Ability to pass a pre‑employment medical and Drug & Alcohol screening Why Join Cranecorp? Lead complex, high‑profile heavy lift projects across Australia Work with a respected and growing industry leader Opportunity to influence engineering standards and innovation Supportive leadership team with a strong focus on safety and excellence If you are ready to take the next step in your engineering leadership career and make a real impact in the heavy lift industry, we would love to hear from
